⭐ DEC 30: @Board Tasks


⭐


⭐
Red: There are some tasks in the Guild Log that were not assigned to a charter. So I set them all to @Board. @StarTree, please sort them when it is your turn.
StarTree: Roger!


⚔️










Defending Deer Crossing
Sealing the Crystal Lake Spawn Point.
⚔️ Crystal Lake First Dive
Crystal Lake
Thursday December 30, 2021




Ledia and Evelyn rowed a boat to the middle of the misty lake. Although it was winter, the weather was not too cold at Crystal Lake. According to Sasha's scroll, there were geysers at the bottom of the lake. There might even be hot springs near the lake.
...
Evelyn: May the spirits of light protect you.
The party gains Protect and Light Evelyn is now recognized as a Level 3 Light Magic User. Evelyn: Be careful.
Evelyn receives Ledia: Ledia makes the first dive.
First Dive at Crystal Lake


🎲


Ledia dived into the lake with Evelyn's light illuminating the surrounding. Hopefully it makes it easier to spot the old seal at the bottom of the lake.

Cardinal: The water was quite clear. The light spell definitely helped. However, the unusual light source drew curiosity from the lake creatures including monsters. The chance of encountering monsters has doubled.
🎲🎲
A monster has appeared to attack Ledia.
⚔️

Ledia: Ledia observes the monster's type.
Cardinal: The monster was flat like a devil fish, but uses electrical attack with its whipping tail.
Ledia: Ledia has resistance to electrical damage because she herself is also an electrical attack user.
Ledia is now recognized as a Level 4 Lightning Magic User.
Cardinal: The monster has no physical attack other than its electrical attack, which is dampened by Ledia's magic resistance. However, the encounter is preventing Ledia from searching for the magic seal. The monster is not moving particularly fast as it has no natural predator in the area and its electrical whip attack would normally work against its preys.
Ledia: Ledia tries to hit the monster. If she could land a physical hit, she would cast freeze on contact, otherwise she would try to freeze the monster at range.
Ledia is now recognized as a Level 4 Ice Magic User.
Cardinal: Although the monster was not moving particularly fast, Ledia was moving even slower. Ledia tried to freeze the monster at range but ice magic was at disadvantage because the water was a bit warm...
🎲🎲
⚔️ The freezing attack was too slow to have an effect on the monster.

Cardinal: The monster started to flee, it seems that it has lost interest in Ledia as its attacks did not work. Ledia is running out of breath and must resurface or risk drowning.
Ledia: Ledia resurfaces.
Diving and fighting under water was strenuous.
Ledia now has 1 hit point remaining.
⚔️ Round 2
Round 2
Thursday December 30, 2021


⭐
The attempt to find the magic seal at Crystal Lake didn't go well. Although she could retry every day, it seemed like a waste of effort because she knew of someone better suited for the task.
...
Natalie arrived at Crystal Lake and was briefed about the situation.


Natalie: A Taser Ray! It sounds like fun! Let's do it!
Natalie is now recognized as a Paladin.
...
Crystal Lake Round 2


Natalie: My plan is to use water bending swim really fast underwater. I will avoid fighting and prioritize locating the seal. I think that once the seal is located, we have someone else who can apply the scroll for sure, even if I don't do it myself.
Natalie is recognized as a Level 4 Water Magic User.


Evelyn: Do you want protect and light? Light was attracting monsters last time. 😖
Natalie: I think I can swim faster than the monster so I should be fine. So, Yes please.
The party gains Protect and Light. Natalie: Natalie prays to the spirits for guidance. "O Spirits of the Lake, we thank you for lives and joy that you gave us. Please help us find the seal and protect the lives here."
Natalie receives Natalie is recognized as a Level 1 Divine Magic User Natalie gains advantage in locating the seal. Natalie: Okay. Here I go. Natalie flies above the lake to see if there is any sign of the seal.


Cardinal: From the sky, Natalie sees two things unusual. There is a spot with dark shadows, which seemed like a small school of Taser ray monsters. About 100 meters from there was a spot with faint radiant lights the looked like broken rainbows.


Natalie: Natalie relays that information back to base camp.
Tanya: Please go down near the broken seal and use the first part of the scroll to gather the old seal. Then go to the new spawn point to use the second part of the scroll to relocate the seal. You don't need to gather anything physical that had the old seal. As long as the scroll can recognize the old seal, it can recreate it at the new location.
Natalie: I fly to the top of the broken rainbows and dive down, using just enough water bending to create a slim pressure bubble so that I fall to the bottom of the lake.
🎲🎲
Cardinal Natalie fell to the bottom of the lake which was about 100 meters deep. Natalie was surrounded by broken rainbow lights that spread for about 10 meters all around her. There, she could also see shadows of three Taser ray monsters circling, uncertain what to do with this intruder. There might be enough time to use the first part of the scroll before they decide to attack.
Natalie: Natalie uses the first part of the scroll. She is ready to abort and resurface if the monsters attack.
🎲
⚔️ As soon as Natalie started using the scroll, the monsters attacked.
🎲🎲
Natalie dodged the electric tail whips and resurfaced.
Natalie still has 3 hit points but is now out of mana.
...
Natalie: Ah well. Natalie calls their secret weapon.


Sasha: Why didn't you just let them hit you? You have protect.
Tanya: Protect doesn't cancel the effect of paralysis from electric shock.
Evelyn: I didn't help at all. 😖
Natalie: We didn't know what else was in the lake.
Sasha: We still don't know.
👑 Round 3
Round 3
Thursday December 30, 2021


The secret weapon Natalie mentioned was V. V was immune to electric shock and attacks that do less than 3 damage. Natalie's plan was to just let her go down and use the scroll.

Sasha: How is she going to breathe underwater?
V: I don't.
Sasha: O. K.
...
Crystal Lake Round 3




V: V walks to Evelyn.
Evelyn: Yes?
V: Light, please.
Evelyn: Yes! Sorry! 😖
The party gains Protect and Light. V: V flies to where Natalie was and dived.


Cardinal: V sinks slowly and quietly down the lake like a falling star. The monsters are attracted to the light.
🎲🎲
⚔️ Three Taser ray monsters approached to attack V.

V: V counters with sword skill Ice Carousel. V receives V is recognized as a Sword Master and a Level 3 Ice Magic User.
🎲🎲🎲🎲
👑 V has KO'ed all three monsters.
Casey: That sounds so cool!
Cardinal: V, how do you want to do this?
V: There was no witness to what V did. V kept sinking. She intended to use the first part of the scroll, then swim to where the new spawn point might be. Hopefully catch exactly where they spawn.
Cardinal: Without any difficulty, V applied the first part of the scroll. The scroll collected the broken rainbow fragments and reassembled the seal to the applied to the new location. V then swam to the next location. There was no obvious sign of where the spawn points were. V waited.
V: V sucks on her popsicle while waiting.
🎲🎲🎲🎲🎲
Cardinal: V waited for 25 minutes at the bottom of the lake before the three monsters respawned together. V could easily see the spawn point, and the Monsters could clearly see V. The monsters were attracted to the light just the same as before.
⚔️ Three Taser ray monsters approached to attack V.
V: Ice Carousel.
🎲🎲🎲🎲 👑 V has KO'ed all three monsters again.

Sasha: That is just too OP for a Level 9. Ledia is level 13 and couldn't defeat one. V KO'ed 6 in one breath.
V: V applies the second part of the scroll to the spawn point.

The Crystal Lake spawn point has been sealed. V: V resurfaces and flies back to shore.

Sasha: And she can fly!!
Ledia: I feel that I used wrong tactics. V's fighting style is efficient.
Sasha: Sasha questions the rule that physical attributes are free to import.
Evelyn: Thank you for coming, V.
V: ✌️
🦌 DX: Status Update
Episode 2 Status Update
Thursday December 30, 2021


⭐
The magic seal at Crystal Lake was just one of eighteen locations to restore. Tanya decided to list all of them.
Magic Seal Status
2021-12-30
⬜ [05] Moss Rapids
⬜ [06] Timber Bridge
⬜ [07] Imp Tunnel
⬜ [07a] Imp Tunnel North End
⬜ [07b] Imp Tunnel South End
✅ [08] Crystal Lake
⬜ [09] Northeastern waterfall
⬜ [09a] Northeastern waterfall caverns
⬜ [10] Forest Bazaar
⬜ [12] Sage's Cabin
⬜ [13] Owl Forest
⬜ [15] Wisp Circle
⬜ [16] Golem Caves
⬜ [17] Wizard tower
⬜ [17a] Wizard tower dungeon
⬜ [18] Watermill
⬜ [19] Sundial
⬜ [20] Crystal Archive
~ End of List ~
Tanya receives


Zoey: Zoey checks in at base camp to see how she may help.
Tanya: Could you check Seal 19 Sundial. There was a report that the seal was already fixed. It is not surprising if that had happened because that area is travelled by merchants. Could you confirm that it is sealed? You should be able to find it along the path.
Zoey: It would be awesome if we have a bigger map.
Tanya: I think that can wait.
Zoey: Zoey followed the path to north shore to look for the sundial. She is going alone because according to Tanya the path should be quite safe.


Cardinal: Zoey followed the path and crossed Timber Bridge to North Shore. Where the old records described to have a sundial is now the construction site of a clock tower, as the small fishing port has been growing into a full-fledged town.
Zoey: Hmm... Timber Bridge. That sounds familiar. But first thing first. I am here for the sundial. I look for a town hall to ask about recent monster sightings.
Cardinal: Zoey finds a town hall and there is a young town hall clerk.
Zoey: Zoey asks the clerk for reports of monster sightings.
Cardinal: The clerk gave Zoey a case file that about a year ago, there was a mysterious incident where the sundial in the vicinity would become out of sync. Around that time, monsters started appearing and many people got sick. The issue was solved when the people started using clocks instead. There had been no sighting of monsters nearby since then.
Zoey: Considering the situation, Zoey believes that the town's record is truthful, and if not, there should be new reports of monster sighting. Zoey considers this check confirmed. She will look for a town crystal and return to base camp. The safe status at North Shore has been confirmed. Teleport to North Shore is now connected. Zoey receives Kudos from Tanya.

202207161025|Log Fall Dungeon

 202112301304|📯 Arxcade {"cmd":"PIN2","node":"202112301304","music":""}

About

20230423: This is an archived post for Ch16 Dungeon. The original date of this post was unknown. The date 20211230 1304 was based on the timestamp of blogger.

WELCOME TO THE ARCADE

Arx Catacomb is a training dungeon for peacemakers to hone their skills against evil. Using alter-reality magic, trainers can spawn replicas of monsters recorded from all realms. Tanya: With total control of the monsters, the trainer may adjust their speed and actions, as well as pausing and replaying the simulation for discussions and studies. No real monster or creature is present or hurt during training exercises. Tanya: The training exercises exist for the benefit of peacemakers. There is no deadline or urgency in defeating the monsters. (2021-12-10: Securing the site) Ledia: We Paladins call this place the Arcade. I used to work there. It was fun.
THE DUNGEON
Game flow (Sasha: Group with Albatross Quest)
The Dungeon, which is Arx Catacomb proper, is where we get the materials to revive and supply the new Albatross. Periodically, Tanya will open a room let in a creature. The peacemakers will then need to decide whether that creature is a friend or a foe, and KO it if it is a foe. During or after KO'ing the foe, the peacemakers will try to identify the foe and conclude the best weapon against them for future references.

Dungeon Todo List:
⬜ STEP 1: Open a room and invite peacemakers to respond.
⬜ STEP 2: Peacemaker responses: For conciseness, each peacemaker should choose and give their best shot.
⬜ STEP 3: If the room is cleared, craft ammo for Albatross.

Supportive Todo List:
⬜ Conceptual: Create a list of identified and spec'ed monsters
⬜ Rulebook: Conceptual: Define terms for the Rulebook
⬜ Rulebook: Artistic: Graphic and layout design for the Rulebook
⬜ Promotion: Artistic: Design dungeon banner
⬜ Promotion: Artistic: Design dungeon map
⬜ Promotion: Artistic: Design monsters
⬜ Music: Record the fighting BGM
ALBATROSS
Reconstructing the peacemaker vessel (Sasha: Repost as Albatross Quest)
The Albatross was a peacemaker vessel that we are trying revive. When revived, it will be capable of wrapping supplies and reinforcement to the frontline to provide rapid response against monsters.
The following is the action plan.
ACTION PLAN
2021-12-30
STAGE I:
o Define the format at the Construction Site so supplies can be sent
o Create the definition section.

STAGE II
o Restart the routine to survey alien monster types, devise effective countermeasures.
o Complete undefined section at the definition section.
o Survey Game Theory Upgrades
o Survey Logical Fallacies and Bias

STAGE III
o Complete importing from old designs.
.. Old prototype (65 pages total)
.. Arcacian Principles 14 pages to merge
.. Unused Module Text 1 page to merge
.. Import concepts from Google Sheet Todo List. Current backlog: 90+ Items.

STAGE IV
o Eventually resolve the difference in style with the colored section. This is postponed if the page has a navigation link (not an orphan) (17 pages)
o Resolve Manga section (2 pages)
*Dropship: The Dropship was our rapid response unit to dispatch paladins to fight actual monsters outside the training area, and in other realms. It is built to equip the pinnacle of technology and material recovered from the catacomb. It responses to events in real time in uncontrolled environment.
💬 Discussions

SCOREBOARD
🦄 Players: Cardinal, Evelyn, Ledia, Natalie, Sasha, Tanya, V
⭐ Merits: Ledia, LRRH
🏅 Recognitions: Natalie, V
💟 Kudos:
💗 Awards: Arcacia, Natalie, V
🏆 Top Scorers: 6 V 5 Ledia 5 Natalie 4 Tanya 3 Arcacia 3 Ken 1 44 1 Cardinal 1 Evelyn 1 Kisaragi 1 LRRH 1 Sasha
📅 Calendar Not Functional




Vivi: The "calendar" here is not really functional. Once the day has passed, you can't tell what had happened unless you click on that date. In the old calendar you could at least see an avatar picture.
StarTree: StarTree doesn't understand what she needs to do!


Ivy: In the spirit of reducing manual links, why don't we use "2021-DEC" label to mark quest of the month? Then Blogger will list them.
Vivi: I think it is annoying to have to go to a different page to see a list to make this page, the Dashboard, useful.
Ivy: It is useful because it has the link that will list you the quest of the month, which is December 2021.
Vivi: But as a USER, why would I care about clicking on your quest of the month? I am not here to do archaeology or how ever you spell that. I am here to be entertained. You need to advertise what is interesting and draw my attention.


Sasha: Queen Vivi is back! And she is right! Bow and serve our Queen!
Ivy: What is it like when you come to the website? What do you want to see instead?
Vivi: For starter, when I come to the website I click on the biggest thing. And for me to do that I am already being really generous because your store front picture is just not really exciting. But for now I just want to talk about the Calendar because it is the end of the month and I want to see changes by tomorrow.
Vivi: When on 2021 December, I want to automatically the top banner being the advertisement for the top quest/event of the month. I don't want to see the cheeseboard. Following that I want to see a calendar grid. The grid shouldn't take more than half of the screen on mobile. When I click on a date, I want to see the date cheeseboard you have now but with Status, Scoreboard, and Chatroom collapsed. For the Quest Banner there, I don't want to see a whole list of avatar pictures of the participants. Two is the max that you could fit on mobile. Please hide the Chatroom if no one end up saying anything. If I click on another date on the calendar, show another cheeseboard so I can compare them two. Let's start with that and finish it today.
StarTree: StarTree is on it!
⭐
⭐
Deer Crossing
🦌 James's Brown
James Checks In
Friday December 31, 2021


Feeling neglected at Freedom Plaza when no heroes answered his call for help, James decided to take matters in his own hands, to show the world what the story is about--him. So he followed the news about Deer Crossing and arrived at the base camp to see how he could save the world.




Tanya: Could you check the Northwestern waterfall and the cavern? According to reports it should already be sealed.
James: How do you decide where I shall go?
Tanya: I'm choosing the ones that seem the easiest. I am trying to check off as many and as quickly as possible so we can concentrate on the harder ones.
James: While that answer didn't make James feel ultra important, he decided that this could be a good opportunity to shine in quantity. He plans to distinguish himself for checking the most spawn points... James knows that before going to the waterfall, he needs to know what exactly he must check to tell if a spawn point is sealed. So far the closest way to check was to find the town records of last monster sightings. So his first stop would still be North Shore.
North Shore Town Hall




James: James arrives at the North Shore Town Hall to look for monster reports about the waterfall.
Cardinal: The young clerk retrieved a few reports. An incident report, a design of a trap, and scroll of a magic seal.
James: James reads the reports and writes a summary for the Status board.
Cardinal: James learns that what the reports were describing was about a prediction that the a spawn point will become exposed due to natural erosion by the waterfall. The trap and magic seals were copies used by another town for a similar defense. A crystal needs to be placed and enchanted at the cavern behind the waterfall, but the crystal itself has not been completed.
James receives Status at Northeastern Waterfall and Caverns is now confirmed.
Deer Crossing Base Camp




James: James returned to base camp and questioned whether it was premature checkmark the waterfall. "The situation is clearly a still a threat until the new crystal is installed," He said to Tanya, "I propose that the checkmark be reserved only for locations that are completely secured, that is, we would mark them only after Spring Equinox when we know the sanctuary is no longer at risk."
Tanya: Tanya intended to just add another column of checkmarks when they are ready for the next stage of confirming.
James: James proposes using different symbols: A white symbol for a location that needs more information. A yellow symbol for a location that has a task a hero can do beyond just gathering basic information. A blue symbol for a location where the heroes are waiting for a condition before they could progress. And the green checkmark when everything is done.
Tanya: Tanya updates the status board according to James's suggestion for James to see.
James: Hmm... Blue doesn't look quite nice on the Status Board. Let's make it Brown. James's Brown. What's next?
Tanya: Could you check Forest Bazaar? Same deal, please bring back a status report.
James: Sure thing.
James completed status report for Forest Bazaar, and more later on. James is now recognized as a Herald and a Magician.


Zoey: Zoey sees what James is doing and updated her status report similarly.
Zoey: Tanya, if we will make a status report for each location, should I just do them one by one in any order?
Tanya: I still prefer you would start with what I think is the easier ones. So please do Imp Tunnels next.
Zoey: Is this one dangerous?
Tanya: If you stick to gathering information from town hall then no. I am not asking you to actually explore the tunnels.
Zoey: Hmm... okay. Zoey teleports to North Shore town hall.
🦌 Imp Tunnels
Imp Tunnels
Friday December 31, 2021
⭐
Zoey asked about the Imp Tunnels at North Shore but the town clerk could not find any record about it. The clerk said there were rumors about Imp sightings east of Deer Crossing on the way to Fort Arsenal. So Zoey decided to go there.
Fort Arsenal




Cardinal: Zoey arrived at Fort Arsenal, which was a barracks and weapon factory, now making mining equipment and supply for mines nearby. The landscape nearby was rugged and dusty without trees serving as wind breakers.
Zoey: Zoey submits a side quest to plant trees around Fort Arsenal. "There is no fighting anymore, so they shouldn't need to clear all the trees to avoid ambush. Zoey receives Cardinal: Zoey found what would could be called a quest center and submitted a side quest. The old clerk said he didn't think the town's folk would want the trees, since they must be vigilant against attacks. The old clerk guessed from Zoey's outfit that she was from Panarcadia. He said, Panarcadia is just a small pocket in the world, beyond Deer Crossing it is a whole other beast.
Zoey: Ooo. A talkie NPC! What does he look like?
Cardinal: The old clerk looks like an old dwarven miner.
Zoey: Finally! A dwarf! Oh, by the way, what do I look like in the game?
Cardinal: It is up to you.
Zoey: May I be an elf, and not be a kid? I don't picture myself as a kid with bow and arrow. I picture myself as an elf. A dark skinned elf. I mean a normal elf, not a dark elf. And not a kid.
Cardinal: You are not saying anything that would contradict the story so far, so as long as you decide that I would set your character to be so and use the information in the narrative. However, once I start using your character setting in the story, if you want to change it you need a valid story element for the change.
Zoey: That should be easy if I just go to Mira Mira. But I don't think I would change thought. I want to be an elf.


Sasha: Well I see a contradiction. Elves are supposed to be agile, but you were panting on your way to the Statue.
Zoey: Can't I just be a sheltered and anime-watching elf. I am still good with bow and arrow just not good at going places, yet. But more importantly, how did you get here?
Sasha: I... followed you?
Zoey: Cardinal, there is no tree around and as a scout I would have spotted Sasha. She lied about following me because she realized that I hadn't connected the teleport at Fort Arsenal yet. I request ARP.
Sasha: Hold on. Why do you want to punish me for joining the game? I am going to make the game more fun! And for my defense, you are calling ARP too soon. I could very well have checked with North Shore town clerk and learned from him that you went to Fort Arsenal. Then I "followed" you by following his lead to Fort Arsenal, where I found you talking to the dwarf clerk. There is no contradiction about what I said.
Zoey: But your said that with a question mark. Your tone meant that you were not certain and you were making it up.
Sasha: Of course I was making it up. That's is RPing! All it matters is whether what I make up still make sense within what our characters would do. And I don't see your character having lie detect skills, so in the story, the surprise from your character didn't come from knowing that I lied, but from your grudge of me having KO'ed you before. You are just looking for an opportunity to take revenge. But I am not here to fight. Having travelled to Fort Arsenal, Sasha meets her rival Zoey. Sasha connects Fort Arsenal to the teleport system and offers to rest and eat with Zoey.
Teleport to Fort Arsenal is now connected. Zoey: Sasha is being nice again! I don't understand!
Sasha: I just don't want you to be the only one connecting all the teleports. That would make me feel like we are using you to do all the walking. So, let's eat?




Sasha: While eating, Sasha and Zoey share information on what they gathered about the Imp Tunnels.
Cardinal: The old clerk had told them that Imps were known to dig tunnels in the area between Deer Crossing and Fort Arsenal, but as they are weak monsters, the miners consider them more as a nuisance than a threat. They do not track or keep records about their sightings.
Zoey: But the clerk did say they are trying to vigilant about a thread. Then what is the threat that they worry about?
Cardinal: It seems that the clerk doesn't bother telling these young adventurers who are literally strangers unknown to this area.
Zoey: He doesn't trust us.
Sasha: I think that's a GM's way of saying we are off-topic. So let's focus on the Imps first. The Imp tunnels may not be affecting Fort Arsenal, but they are affecting Deer Crossing's leylines. According to North Shore, there had been no sighting of Imps along the paths, but the locations of the alleged northern and southern ends of the tunnels will be on the paths. I think it means that the Imps haven't dug far enough to make the exits yet, but that is going to happen. If we know where the exits will be, then we can seal it with this scroll.
📜 Scroll of sealing Imp Tunnel exits. Zoey: How would we know where the tunnels will exit? I think it is too late if we just wait for that to happen. (I know what we can do but my character doesn't.)
Sasha: Sasha knows who can do so but doesn't want to divulge about the fact that Queen Vivi used that method to find the Cardinal Core and had been digging since. So Sasha said, "I think I know someone who can do that, but it would take a bit of time. Give me a few days and I can report to base camp.
Zoey: Having been eating with the new nice Sasha, Zoey did not suspect Sasha of hiding any information from her.
Sasha: Sasha will ask @StarTree to map the Imp Tunnel and create a map. To not be overly confusing, Sasha will just update the Status board now, but in the story, the map is obtained a posted a few days later.
⭐

StarTree: StarTree will have no problem mapping the Imp Tunnels, but only as far as halfway between Deer Crossing and Fort Arsenal! Beyond that there weren't enough trees for her to do so!
📜 Partial mapping of Imp Tunnels. Status report for Imp Tunnel is complete.

[202201020900]
202201020900|Character Cards
▷ 2022 JAN 02 SUN 9:00 AM
Magic Bakery
⭐
I made a character card for the Deer Crossing campaign.
Ivy: I started with the one for Evelyn then did the one for Ledia. Please feel free to tweak it (or change it altogether, especially if it is yours). There were no rules about what needs to be on that character card. I just put the information that was already there with a new format, where only skills that cost a player level have a number, so that you could add them and check whether you've maxed out. Please keep upgrading them.
Zoey: It looks really nice! I will make one too. I think everyone who has roleplayed in that game should make a card. But no pressure.
Ledia: I maxed out...
Zoey: By the way, I want green for my title although I know the designated color for Herald is teal. The text already says what our main archetype is, I don't think the color really needs to match that. Zoey: Ok. I "bought" some skills to make up for the gap between my skills and my actual skills. Now I am a proper ranger with hiking, camping, and tracking skills, in the game.

Cardinal Quest
[202201031933]
202201031933|Password Book
202112310100|◀ ▷ 2022 JAN 03 MON 7:33 PM
Deer Crossing
📯
Back at Panarcadia, Little Red Riding Hood went to the Governor's Manor about the Statue of Legacy quest. There was a handwritten question on the flyer about the quest that was posted at Magic Bakery Headquarters. The question was "Where to put a book of passwords?"
🚪 Governor's Manor
🕰️ [19:33] 🕰️
LRRH: Little Red Riding Hood knocks on the Governor's door.
There was no answer. But there was a sign that said James the Governor had gone to assist Deer Crossing. For any inquires please find his daughter Ivy at the Adventurers' Hall. Red goes look for Ivy at the Adventurers' Hall. Ivy was not there. There was a sign that said Ivy had gone to help Deer Crossing. If there is anything urgent, please go to Deer Crossing base camp. Red thinks why is everyone being so difficult to reach?
🏕️ Base Camp
🕰️ [19:54] 🕰️
Tanya: I see that you are looking for James. But James and Ivy are both KO'ed from questing. You can find them resting at the tent over there.
Red didn't feel like bothering people who are on their break. So she asked Tanya about the question on the flyer. I see. Tanya tells Red James's dilemma.
🔐 James's Dilemma
🕰️ [20:10] 🕰️
Tanya: James was worried about himself dying so he wanted to leave his unfinished work for someone else to continue. His unfinished work and treasures are locked at some location. He was wondering how he could pass down that information without also letting someone else steal his treasures.
Red doesn't feel like she is good at this kind of puzzle. What is it that he has that others can't just take? Magic Bakery's guideline is that everything is public domain. If you make something, even if it is unfinished, you would make it public so that people can take it continue if they want to. Sasha: ARP! ⚡Cardinal: LRRH referred to an undocumented guideline. LRRH takes 1 psychic damage. LRRH is now KO'ed. Tanya used the undefined Oracle Skill Insight. Tanya takes 1 psychic damage. Tanya is now KO'ed. Oh wow, I didn't realize that Tanya had been cheating! Not that I am complaining. Double KO FTW! LRRH has added a guideline. LRRH: LRRH has revived herself. So as I was saying, if you are making something worth continuing, your unfinished work should be available for free. There shouldn't be a password. The thing you make shouldn't be able to let people do bad things. But you can make ____ from flour. Flour is commonly available. But you don't know what James is making. Maybe he is making ____, but he wanted to use them for good. ... Red realized she has no idea what the conversation is about. All she knows is that Magic Bakery makes bread. Bread is not a threat to anyone and it is cute. I would send that question to @Mikela because at this point that is just a puzzling curiosity. Magic Bakery has nothing that is password protected. What about the account password to the world? That's not important because people can just make their own website with the same content. Well, I don't know the specific context to argue with you but I feel that you are just leaving the ball to someone else. I think you didn't solve what troubled James. He is just going to ask you again when he comes back. I hope that it wouldn't be so cryptic next time he describes it.

Puzzle Time
📯
The Dashboard has a lot of space again! Let's make the space interesting!
Fina: I am adding a nomination section in that space. And I nominate Arcacia's Plan tab for Best Tab for 1 EXP for the chapter.
Her Plan Tab replaces and includes the old "Updates" tab. Inside that tab, she started with an overall plan, which replaces the Todo list section, followed by an year bar that hides the statistics for the updates. The overall look is clean and saves space on the banner.
Jan 9: Red: Arcacia Plan Tab is now adopted in the Place EXP Guide.
Fina: Next I nominate Ledia's for best tab view for 1 EXP for the chapter.
Despite most of us switching to having individual toggle for the banner tabs last month, Ledia kept the old style, and apparently someone updated the function to hide the display area when the tab is clicked twice. As a result, now Ledia's banner has the best switching tab view.
Jan 9: Red: Ledia Tab View is no adopted in the Place EXP Guide. Fina is recognized as a Herald. {"cmd":"next"}

POLL: Should we suspend TIER 2 and 3 quest rotations and focus on Deer Crossing?
LRRH: This question came up in DX Episode 2. You may still work on your chapter at your pace with rotation suspended. Please let us know your thoughts. If you suspend the rotation, I would start resetting the timer every time I update something related to @Skill. There are many overlap between DX and @Skill, I am actually holding back. So I don't mind if it is suspended. (Jan 7) Thank you for the weekly reminder for Freedom Plaza. I would appreciate the same reminder going forward, even if it is just for me. Could we just let people choose another Tier? (Jan 8) @StarTree, I volunteer @Carrot to be on Tier 4 (No reminder), and leave an empty slot in Tier 1 so that Tier 1 is still on a weekly rotation. My todo list includes repairs. I need a reminder unless you don't want things fixed. Actually, may I be on Tier 2? And make Tier 3 a bi-weekly tier? (@StarTree) @Carrot is now on Tier 4 and @Treasure Tier 2! General maintenance and repairs should be in @Guild, but you are welcome to do that during @Treasure. @Guild time slot is not convenient. Could we make @Treasure Saturday, @Guild Sunday? The lineup is now this: SUN @Guild, MON @Skill, TUE @Puzzle, WED @Seed, THU @Makeover, FRI @Freedom, SAT @Treasure!

🔔
While she was KO'ed, Tanya received an anonymous message: Tanya: To the Quest Giver at Deer Crossing Base Camp:
The quest is missing an EXP schedule showing the EXP they would get per task. Without this you aren't giving proper incentives for the players to play the game as a game. You do plan to give players credits PER TASK, not PER DAY, right?? And while you are at it, find a way to show who is about to level up, that would nudge them to join. ~ Queen Anonymous Queen Anonymous receives Queen Anonymous is now recognized as an Oracle.
Tanya: In response, Tanya updated the charter with an EXP Schedule. The player will get credits per task completed, as logged at the Status Board at Base Camp. The amount of EXP is based on the difficulty of the task itself, not how much time a player spends on the task. However, while a player is doing the task, Cardinal will regularly KO exhausted players to let other players join. With these changes, it is unnecessary to RP to get EXP. As for alerting players who were close to level up...
🏕️ New Helper
Tanya: Tanya had reservations about advertising and asking new people to join this quest because the tasks are difficult and not for everyone. She was afraid that an advertisement would invite too many unqualified helpers that need training.
LRRH: It sounds like you want someone else to babysit non-intensive tasks. As I see it, base camp is getting messy and could use a maid.
Tanya: But your head maid already gets EXP for cooking. She will level up today one way or another.
LRRH: But now I am here, so I want her to be here also.
Tanya: I see. She is welcome to tidy up here and do other quests she wants.
LRRH: Then I will call her.
🎠 Rotation
Tanya: Red, I have another request. Could we suspend all other Guild quest rotation and concentrate on just this quest?
LRRH: I think that is not up to me. People are free to do the quest they want.
Tanya: But I can see a difference if you suspend the rotation. People are still free to do what they want. But the rotation makes people do things according to 'their turn' instead of the priority, even when they agree with the priority.
LRRH: Is that so?
Tanya: I believe so. When you setup a rotation schedule, you make people feel that they should work on those other quests.
LRRH: I want to see how others think. @StarTree, could you set up a poll at Dashboard on whether we should suspend Tier 2 and Tier 3 rotations?
StarTree: Copy that!
LRRH: I will decide after seeing some responses.
Tanya: Thank you.
🧹 Cleaning up
Kisaragi: Kisa reports to the base camp with bento boxes. She has been briefed that Heroes have been working really hard, and she may start cleaning up as she without supervision and let the Heroes rest. Kisa looks around to check the condition around camp.
Cardinal: Kisa sees several messes. The quest board was cluttered with information scattered at different places. There was an incomplete roster of player. The desk was cluttered with paper and unprocessed quest logs. Kisa also notices Cardinal as in its familiar form. Cardinal sees Kisa but does not know what archetype or skills Kisa has.
Kisaragi: Kisa greets Cardinal with her character card, then adds herself to the roster. Kisa assumes that her archetype is Paladin, but she will let Cardinal be the judge. Kisaragi is now recognized as a Paladin.
Kisaragi: Kisa wants to know who else is around.
Cardinal: There was no one else around. It is nighttime. The Heroes are either sleeping in tents or have logged out.
Kisaragi: Kisa feels a bit relieved. She starts her tidying up...
📜 The Desk
Kisaragi: Kisa started at sorting the things on the desk. The desk was cluttered with seven things: Charter, Plan, Preliminary survey reports, Maps, Hero profiles, Status reports, and Quest logs. There was no clear space left on the desk. So she filed the papers vertically to make space. ✨
📰 Old News
Kisaragi: While putting things vertically, Kisa noticed that the stacks of preliminary reports and charter seldomly used. So she grouped them together into the stack "Reports". 📂
🗺️ The Map
Kisaragi: And there was the map, which seemed important. So she blew up the section near Deer Crossing and hang it prominently, then started adding pins to key locations. 📍

🔔
At the Guild Log there is an obscure statistics that gives the guild a level from total member EXP. With the results from yesterday, which was a gain of 64 EXP (!), the guild had reached LEVEL 100!! Congratulations! 🎊
Sasha: POLL: Use Square to calculate EXP required to reach a level. We had some discussion in the field about EXP and came to a proposal to simplify the math by using square to determine how many EXP is needed to reach a level. For example, to reach level 10, it takes 100 EXP. If we change to this way, everyone will become slightly lower level. The guild will become level 68. Tanya, who is Level 20 now, will become Level 19. @StarTree, could we get a poll about this?
With Square EXP it will take 10000 EXP for the guild to be at Level 100! It will sound more EPIC and we are almost halfway there! (Our current EXP is 4742!) I proposed the current EXP calculation partly as a joke. I don't mind if it is changed. BTW, those who are below level 14 might see their level increase by 1. Square EXP might be simpler, but not as magical. The original EXP formula will still be legendary in my heart. I respectfully disagree with what Ledia said. Square number contain way more magical power. 289. In case you don't know, that is the square of 17. And how many EXP I need to have to become level 17, again. We need the EXP calculation to be stable for updating the character cards. And an difficult to calculate relation is bad for promotion. So let's do this. Little Red Riding Hood hereby proclaims that square number are cute. @StarTree, @Cardinal, please proceed with the proposed change. And it is done! Please be mindful of your new Player Level! This poll has concluded!

SCOREBOARD
🦄 Players: Ivy, Kisaragi, Ledia, Sasha, StarTree, Tanya, Zoey
⭐ Merits: Kisaragi, Sasha, StarTree, Tanya
🏅 Recognitions: Sasha, Tanya
💟 Kudos:
💗 Awards: Arcacia, Kisaragi, Sasha, Tanya
🏆 Top Scorers: 11 P4 8 Kisaragi 6 James 6 Vivi 6 Sasha 6 Tanya 4 Arcacia 3 Ledia 3 Ivy 3 Black 3 StarTree 2 Casey 2 Zoey 1 Neil 1 LRRH
On her way to look for new ingredients, Arcacia noticed the base camp at Deer Crossing. It seems that there is something really important going on. Maybe she should set up a massage booth there also? But home is just a teleport away. Arcacia leaves her card before moving on to her own quest for the day. Arcacia is now recognized as an Alchemist. Casey passed by the base camp to see what the big deal was. The camp was unexpectedly tidy, and there was a map with some symbols she didn't understand. The roster looked interesting so she left her card there too. She didn't come to do the Deer Crossing quest. She came to recharge a magic mirror to tell whether it could be a good gift. Casey is now recognized as an Alchemist.
Is the mirror useful? Casey was surprised that the bird was talking. Not that the bird could talk, but that it decided to talk to her. She said, "It doesn't seem very useful. The mirror has a habit of showing something else by itself, which is a bit distracting. It might work well at a waiting room though. Are you going to give it to someone? I think I made a mistake of waking it. Now it only recognizes us (Magic Bakery). It has become quite useless to others... Why did you get it in the first place? I thought it could replace some clutters, but it may very well end up being a clutter itself... A clutter that can't be donated. You can still donate it if you attach your account email and password. But while we are here, let's do Deer Crossing quest. Of course, you don't have to do it if you don't want to. It has been a week since the Heroes had updated the reports. There was a stack of... Tanya noticed that the base camp has been tidied up. Although the field notes still need to be filed. Kisaragi receives Kudos from Tanya. Let's start with the Quest Map and pin all the locations. I think the pin head we use are too big. Let's make them smaller. The group at base camp finished added checking the reports and pinned the locations on the map. There were "What do we feel like doing today?" Could we do the watermill? That seems to be the easiest one, and I am new here. The watermill itself is still in the safezone, but the clues to the enchantment would be deeper in the forest. There would be monsters and possibly golems. You might need to fight or defend yourself going there. Sounds like I should go with Evelyn, then? Who else wants to come? Before you start forming a team, you should know that the bigger your party, the higher chance that your party would be seen as a threat and get attacked. Here are the chances I see:> For every half hour in the forest, there is 2/6 chance on each Hero that something will find the group a threat and call for help to attack the Heroes. The first encounter will have as many enemies as the party size. If the party does not TPK, each encounter thereafter will have up to 2 more enemies than the previous. 1/6: Same number as previous. 2/6: 1 more enemy. 3/6: 2 more enemies. Still feel like going? It can't be that bad. Let me go with just one other person... Casey decided to go with Arcacia because they are both new to the quest. We are looking for the enchantment pattern for the watermill. Tanya said we might be able to find clues in the forest. Should we ask the trees then? Oh? You can do that? Arcacia goes to a tree, gently put her hands and forehead on it for a few moments... I can't! 😜 "Ah well, you tried." Casey said, and though maybe StarTree would have been the perfect person for this quest. She could probably solo it in no time if the trees knew. And they most likely do. But Casey is satisfied for this journey where both Heroes have ZERO clue. I break off some loose leaves and flowers to make a a wreath headpiece while planning to aimlessly and mindlessly wander with Casey in the forest. Cardinal, what is our chance of finding a clue? I forgot to ask Tanya. The party wandered in the forest. Half hour has passed. 🎲🎲 Not far away the party sees what seems like two rock golems doing something. It is not clear what they are doing or which direction they are facing. Maybe we can ask them? Casey: But James's report said we can't communicate with them, we need wisps as intermediates.
Arcacia: Ooo there was a report?! 😆
Casey: Casey thinks that Arcacia might be more high than StarTree.
Cardinal: Just as Casey was thinking about that, it had become clear that one of the golems had lifted up a boulder from the ground.
Casey: Casey thinks that this is bad and is prepared to dodge if they throw it. "We should probably hide." Casey thought, but fell short of saying it out loud.
Arcacia: Arcacia mirrors the golem's movement and lifts up her wreath, with no particular intention or meaning.
Cardinal: How big is the wreath?
Arcacia: The hole is shoulder width.
🎲
Cardinal: The golem that lifted the boulder lowered its boulder. While the other one lifted its.
Arcacia: Arcacia puts her wreath around her neck and started humming the tune of Fireflies of the Milky Way https://musescore.com/user/35526651/scores/6287786 for no particular reason.
Arcacia receives
🎲
Cardinal: The second golem puts down its boulder. The two appears to be sitting still. They now look like ordinary rocks.
Casey: Is there any clue around?
Cardinal: The party has no idea whether they have or have not seen any clue, or whether there will or will not be any clue. Casey: Arg.
Arcacia: If we don't find any clue, we could just start from scratch. We'll enchant it our own way.
Casey: How do we enchant it ourselves?
Arcacia: We will make it so that everyone who sees the watermill feels loved. Arcacia receives Arcacia is now recognized as a Cleric.
Casey: The party went to the watermill with Arcacia's wreath and love, where Casey started writing a scroll. Based on the design and the actual watermill, Casey made a list on how to care for the watermill so it my run for a long time. With a wreath hang over it, it drew people's attention as they took breaks from construction. Casey wondered how much love they were able to give.
Arcacia: The more we care the more love it will show. Let's come back again and again.
Casey: The watermill quest is taken cared of. We didn't find a clue, but we enchanted it our own way.
Tanya: I see. Thank your for your good work.
Casey: But that's not all, we found something extra at the watermill. **Hands a scroll to Tanya.**
Owl Forest resealing scroll obtained. Casey: Someone clearly loved the Owl Forest. We can't let their love go to waste. Casey receives Tanya: I see. This is a great find. We will study it. Thanks again for your good work. And for taking care of the guild.
Casey: "It's not that big of a deal."
Tanya: Before moving on to the next quest item, Tanya decided that now is a good time to tally the task credits for Episode 1 and 2. The tally was based on the Status Board. The result is next.
SCOREBOARD

POLL: There was a suggestion of Bye days. Applied to Tier 3, there will be three bye days. How does that work? What should be shown? StarTree doesn't understand!
For a bi-weekly tier with 11 quests, it means that we have 14 days to complete 11. When a quest is attended to, just move it a sub-tier such as 3.1, and don't show it until two weeks later. Then there will be days when we would see no quest left in Tier 3. The days we see nothing are the bye days. Are we really doing bi-weekly, or bi-monthly? Bi-monthly will give us two bye weeks in a year, which will be similar to having guild vacation days. I don't mind bi-weekly or bi-monthly. But bi-weekly could match payroll calendar. What I mind is that people don't do a few small things in one sitting and say they had done enough to check off a few periods. I also don't want people to intentionally spread out small tasks over a long period. But between the two, I would rather people do some part, then wait and see if someone else has a better way of doing it, then continue with the better way. The tiers are just reminders. Actual measure of the quality of the result should be done based on the place level. Each of our location has a place level. We start the year at a certain level. A year from now are we still at the same level? That's what matters. If the period doesn't matter, could we match the moon phase? There are 13 moons in a year. today is first quarter 🌓. Next last quarter will be Jan 25 🌗. Using moon phase is so mystical! I like it! Let's not make the same mistake of choosing something niche and uncommon. If you need to reference a table to know when something is due, then it is too complicated. Then it comes down to either monthly or bi-monthly. I would say let's just do Monthly for the rest of January and see how it goes. During a month, you could set a goal for the month and try to finish it. Now we have this "Poll" section and you can discuss and get feedback. I like Ivy's idea. I think it will destroy the snooze issue, and our plans and results would make good summary banners for the Dashboard. If we do that, we don't even need Tier 3 or 4 any more. We just need a list of monthly goals. We could still have the weekly rotation schedule. Ivy's idea seems like the solution to my worries. I suggest that for now, we make no change to the Tier reminder (no bye days), but people may make a monthly goal for their chapter. There is no rule on how easy or difficult that goal is.
🏆 Place EXP
StarTree: Red and I have updated the Place Level Guide and tallied the current Place EXP for each chapter! We will do it again when January ends!

I didn't see @Concept would score so low. Please make a poll like Fina did if you want to nominate a new scoring item.

POLL: It seems that every month StarTree forgets what she is supposed to do and can't write a charter! What do you think @Board should be? What do you want StarTree to do for the guild?
StarTree is clearly the one keeping the board (message board and quest board) up and is really good at it. I don't see a problem keeping it that way and have someone else try to do Front Desk as a different charter. What about @Freedom? StarTree is basically doing @Freedom's charter. Maybe StarTree should be the Mayor! StarTree doesn't want to be the major! StarTree wants to be free! Maybe we should emphasize the difference between Place Level, and the Top Contributor. If we keep @Freedom's charter unique, then it means that StarTree had been leveling up Freedom Plaza. James, the Mayor, can still be the Mayor in the RP story, but the leaderboard should show StarTree next to @Freedom because she is the Top Contributor. There is no rule that a charter needs to be unique. Two charters can be the same. The score should go to the person in charge of the one that is contributing. I don't want a charter to exist by itself. I want each charter to embody what its writer aspires to do. If you make a charter, it should be something you care about and would take charge working on it. It should have that personal connection. Then what was it about "a charter is not yours, it belongs to the guild." when we were at Evelyn's Garden? (Edit: Link added by StarTree) That is if you go AFK on a charter that other people in the guild need. For example, if you make a charter saying that you want to be the one designing our characters, and ask people to send request to you, then you go AFK, the requests are going to at your place. But the requests and the workspace do not belong to you. The rest of the guild may go to your place and help out finishing them. You don't have an exclusive right to the orders. Anyone is allowed to help. Then, say the rest of the guild kept doing that for a few weeks and got it running really well, and then you return from your absence, you don't have a right to kick out the rest of the guild and take the place for your own. The place is never "yours" to beging with. It belongs to the guild because it is a chartered place. I want to say I understand half of it, but I think it is more like one-third. Sasha is disappointed that Red didn't say the magic word for her to use ARP. Explanations like this are important perspectives and should be written as part of the guild rule. I'm not good at speaking in rules! When I say something is a rule, people misinterpret it and do weird things! So just let me ramble! I think Red is saying a charter exists for a person to declare how they want to help others. Once declared, everyone else is free to join and get credits for helping regardless whether they do it under the organization of the person who first wrote the charter. A charter exists to inspire, not to control. Evelyn receivesEvelyn is now recognized as Cleric. I think I understand what Evelyn said. Red gives Evelyn Merit.So, as I was saying, since it is clear that StarTree is good at, and is passionate maintaining our board, by that I mean this dashboard, I think it would make most sense if you craft Charter 18 according to what she does. You don't need to intentionally set new goals or cover task no one is doing for the guild. You may just focus on what you already do. We get problems when people try to declare more than what they are passionate about. Then the score of @Freedom is wrong. When I go to the board I consider myself going to StarTree's, not James's. James's usefullness is less than monthly. I agree. No offense to James. For those who might read this later, the current Place Level EXP of Freedom Plaza is 15. It is at the top of the leaderboard. We are saying that it shouldn't be there because it is taking credits for what was done by StarTree. StarTree did the scoring! You did it wrong then. But according to Red's intention, we can't write a charter for you. You have to write your own. Can StarTree have no charter like Vivi's? StarTree wants to be free! This discussion is going too long. How about we sleep over it and resume later?
It seems that my Charter had caused some trouble. I have updated it to Version 3 and deducted some points from my Place EXP. Everyone is doing great. Let's keep up the good work! Mikela receives Kudos from James for creating Mikela Jump.James is awarded Merits by LRRH. @Board now has a Charter and leads the Place EXP leaderboard as Level 4! This POLL has concluded.

POLL: There is a backlog from Cardinal Quest field notes that should be scored but StarTree doesn't now how to score them!
If someone is present or checked in, they should get 1 EXP on the guild log column. I think that is already done minus errors. What about the other credits? In the field log there are stars. Are people supposed to get a point per star? I envision that the document map will be used as a score board. When something is done, you put your name there then Google Sheets may just count your names. On the same cell, there would also be a status flag, and Google Sheet can read that to format the cell color. Show me how it is done so I can tell if the scoring is wrong. Let's score today. Blackchecked in. Then later Casey joined. So they should appear on our daily row. ✅ Then as you go down, you see Casey got a Star mark for searching for courtyard leyline. So you would put [Casey] under the Part column on the row Courtyard leyline. In this case, she hasn't finished, so you could jus leave the cell white. According to James, white should mean a situation where no one has any info. Yellow is for a situation where someone could work on. Then let's put an @ for yellow. # for brown, and $ if it is done (Green). Can a name appear more than once in a cell? No. Tasks that are difficult would have multiple columns. Next, Casey mapped out what we need for the Reservoir. So that is $ [Casey]. Next, Casey determined that there is no need for a scroll there. So it would be .[Casey]. Next, Casey tried to look for the Rosetta Crystal, so @[Casey] there. So based on the log alone, Casey scored 4 EXP today. She was scoring more points if we dig deeper and count the descriptions in our Master Map. But we can do that when we do the Master Map scoring. The counter is now online! StarTree, you don't need to go through our field logs and do the scoring for us. I think the deal is whoever wants EXP need to log their score by themselves. If there is a problem or dispute on who did what, they we can go to the log to verify. You don't need to go through the log and check that each star is counted. It would be tedious and inefficient because you would end up asking us which column you should put someone's name. The person who did the quest should know that and enter it by themselves. If they don't know it they shouldn't be getting an EXP. StarTree is not needed! StarTree, could you help us on something else? It is about the side journal. Let's talk at Court Arsenal.

Charter 18 covers the maintenance and upgrade of the Magic Bakery website and quest board.
✅ Aspirations
✅⭐ Maintain Quest Board Task: Maintain the quest board daily to highlight discussion topics. 20240323: Covered by StarTree.
✅⭐ Tally guild scores daily 20240323: Covered by StarTree.
✅⭐ Board GUI Upgrade Task: Upgrade the interface of the board to keep it appealing and convenient. 20240323: Covered by 202303052122|GitHub node.
Help Wanted
⭐ Help checking/complete the 100+ quest items marked for @Board
⭐ There are some orphaned tasks from version 1!
⭐ Maintaining a "front desk" for the guild
⭐ Maintaining the guild website
⭐ Promoting the guild
Plan
🥧 Implement Mikela Jump to Dashboard This is a navigation function at BlogSpot where the URL accepts a node code. This is covered at the GitHub website!
🥞 Add scoring related rules to Place Level Guide
🥐 Bring back Front Desk
🥯 Check for errors in Guild Log
🍰 Town map
🥨 Answer Questions
🥠 Fortune Cookie section
History

Reformatted charter to quest card style
JUN 23: EXP 20 | 0 days cycle | 0 Issues | 32 Quests | Boss Lv 71 |

 DEC 31: Lv 2 | 11 Days Cycle | 0 Issues | 134 Quests | Boss Level 43 |

 202201140937|📜 Charter 28 v3 {"cmd":"PIN2","node":"202201140937","music":""}
QUEST CHARTER XXVIII
Version 3
Magic Bakery Peacemaker Blog Backup
James @Freedom
January 14, 2022

Version Up

⭐ StarTree has been doing a phenomenal job maintaining the discussion board for the guild. To avoid confusion, this charter is updated to highlight its intent in light of the status quo.

Peacemaking Relevance

⭐ This charter maintains its commitment to provide a publicly viewable discussion and information hub for peacemakers to discuss, plan and to bond with each other, although those functions are met by StarTree @Board and Cardinal @Cardinal. To continually support their contributions, this charter will focus on backing up their contents, and adding/linking contents from older versions of the forum to make it convenience to see the whole story that was scattered in different media.

Redefined Scope of Aspirations

⭐ Weekly rotation to backup and check guild assets and guild well-being.
⭐ Maintain guild history and links to previous documents
⭐ Create summaries and celebrate guild progress
⭐ Ensure that there is a place for discussion and exchange. Recreate the discussion board as needed.
💬 Discussions

202209202344| JAN💝  202211241436| FEB💝  202212031845| MAR💝  202212031908| APR💝  202212031850| MAY💝  202212031911| JUN💝  202212031855| JUL💝  202212031858| AUG💝  202212031913| SEP💝  202212031859| OCT💝  202212031906| NOV💝  202212031900| DEC💝 
💝 = Latest format (202407) A Happy Calendar is a rolling calendar that marks the days when something happy happened in any year. We started the canlendar in January 2022, but we also tag older posts. If a post makes you happy, please give it a happy tag, then it will show up when the date is clicked on the calendar. We try marking every date with happy posts, hopefully every date will have a happy post!
    You can participate in the happy calendar by sharing happy moments and tagging.
      To tag a node, add the tag 'data-happy'. To tag a message, add the date tag and use the icon 💟 or 💗.
    Upgrade the format of each rolling calendar to the latest. Updated July. Updated September and checked August. Checked September and updated October. Updating November. Checked October Updated December. Checked November. Updated January, February, March, April, and May. Checked April and May.
    This is about using the macro to create the calendar to make them shorter in the archive for the regular months.
    Use regular expression to reformat. For the avatar calendar, use this as the macro template. Replace with , then replace with nothing. For the icon calendar, use this as the template: Then replace with , then also replace with nothing. Updated: 2025:AUG-SEP Updated: 2024:JAN-MAR     Be mindful of which day never had any happy moment and fill it with a happy moment. This quest may involve checking the messages of a day in other means and retrospectively tag or correct the tag for the latest rolling calendar format.
14 of each month https://matadornetwork.com/abroad/the-14th-of-every-month-in-south-korea-is-a-holiday/ Add QSLBL query here to query all nodes with 💟 icon but without a date. Upgrade kudo code to credit both the sender and receiver Calendar should have a query to epic. Add flowers to the calendar Should birthdays be listed here or separately on each kudo calendar? (1/5, 3/11, 8/13, 8/15, 8/25, 8/30, 10/24, 11/12) I think they should be on the Kudo calendar.
    Update the query of kudo to query nodes with mbkudo Upgrading. Anything to be listed by the rolling calendar needs the date tag because JQuery can only query the beginning or end of an attribute. If the MMDD substring is inside the ID or DTS, then it can't see it. Either that or query without the date then filter for those with correct MMDD. https://www.quora.com/How-do-you-write-a-jQuery-regex-selector-for-a-DOM-element-containing-substring-abcd Testing. Updated the function calls in June calendar, but the calendar needs a macro that makes the whole table. Adding a RES type that is a calendar. Adding MacroResCalendar. Tested and done for June! Making the code also scan for the mbKudo tag. I think we don't need the calendar to scan for mbKudo because kudos can be marked by 💟. The kudo tag is supposed to kudo both the sender and the receiver.

POLL: The effort on quest and the credits seem disproportional. Getting EXP from the actual cardinal quest is WAY harder than doing normal guild quest. Can we fix that?
We are limited by simplicity. For a single task that is long, we are rewarding maximum to those who do enough, then let others continue, instead of those who try to do the whole thing by themselves. But maxing out after 1 EXP per task is too soon. Five or so should be more appropriate. And I think that the accounting shouldn't be driving the behavior. If people want to help, then let them help all they want and reward them accordingly. The accounting needs to be upgraded to reward people who are helping. @Ivy, could you upgrade the accounting? I don't want people to start making rows in Guild Log just to track the additional EXP they should get. I want the Cardinal Quest Map to handle the field scores alone. The scoring map is now upgrade to count the number of times a name appears even if it is in the same cell. I also added some columns to help audit for errors, and added 4 points for Ledia on her cell today. Now we can see that Zoey is leading the scoreboard with 21 points, followed by Ledia and Evelyn with 8 points each. Thank you, Ivy. Eight points for today sounds about right. @Zoey, how did you get so many points? Ledia gives Ivy Kudos. I was there almost every day. I've just added more auditing statics. I think Zoey got 15 points or more on the same day. She got them from mapping and sending out orders. So it wasn't just because she was there everyday. There are some tasks that are easier to get EXP. When you are there everyday, you would be right there when you the opportunity to get easy EXP comes. It balances out. You got 8 points in one day, I have 21 points total. I am sure I was there more than 3 days. So on average you are getting more points than I did per day. If you keep at that pace you will catch up in no time. That is encouraging. I am going to add a new leaderboard for Cardinal Quest alone! First with Fort Arsenal scores, Deer Crossing will be added back later when we get to it! @James, please don't freak out! THE GAME IS ON! This POLL has concluded.

Games
[202201240900]
202201240900|Adventure Begins
202201252200|▶ 2022 JAN 24 MON 9:00 AM
Magic Bakery
📯
How do people feel about the game we had on Sunday? What do you want to add most to our game? Or what does our game need the most?
Can we just keep playing first? I am dying to shoot something down with my bow XD Ledia: I think the format of the game is fun, but not as satisfying because we aren't earning real EXP unlike the actual Cardinal Quest. I don't want to get EXP from Cardinal Quest, then use that EXP to play a different game. I think we were doing okay translating our field notes into a story, but it would help if we know what we will be dealing with ahead of time. Sasha: Ledia, that is what I am going for. I am not trying to just play random games. @Tanya, what will be tomorrow's Cardinal Quest? Tanya: Tomorrow we will try to deal with the spawn point at the Geyser. The overall drill will be similar: Zoey will lead us to the spawn area according to the reports, we will try to clear the area and wait for them to respawn to know the exact spawn point, then we will seal it while fighting adds. Ledia: I think our quest is potentially way better than the Sunday game. We just need a better "adventure card". What kind of monster will we be fighting? Tanya: We only have rough descriptions. I believe it is a highly armored monster that can shoot water jets. But we are doing the scouting on the same day, so we don't know it right now. Zoey: We will be fighting a Squirtle XD Tanya: The more scouting we do beforehand, the more information we will have for making that "adventure card". I was hoping that we would get ahead on Sunday. Evelyn: I like how in our Cardinal Quest we don't have to worry about dying. If we all get KO'ed we can just continue the next day. But I think our field notes was a little strange. It is not in chronological order, so I can't see a story from it, and my memory is not very good. 😅 I noticed that too. I was trying to make it easier keep score. The problem we had earlier, was that if we just mark when someone scores a point in the timeline, the person keeping score would need to read the whole thing to know where to put that point on the scoring map. So we started keeping a separate section to track scores, ordered by location. Then we didn't bother also keeping the chronology, and just mark on the field scoring map directly. LRRH: @Ivy, any thoughts? Ivy: It sounds like you want to sort the same data sometimes by time and sometimes by location. Then your field log should probably be a spreadsheet and not a text file. If we try to do that on a table, wouldn't we need to enter the time and the location everytime? That would be tedious. Ivy: You could use a macro then bind it to a shortcut key. I see. I'll try it. Tanya gives Kudos to Sasha, Zoey, Ledia, Evelyn, Red, and Ivy.
💟
Thank you for ideas to improve the field log for Cardinal Quest.

[202201252200]
202201252200|Field Report
202201240900|◀ ▷ 2022 JAN 25 TUE 10:00 PM
Cardinal Quest
⭐
We went to the Geyser today and tried the new field log suggested yesterday. We got 76 lines with scores. @StarTree, how do you want to do this?
StarTree: May I add tallying formulas to the field log? Then I will import only the final score from all days on the field log. On our Guild Log I will only have the current Cardinal Quest field scores with no history! Tanya: I think that's fine. StarTree: May I turn the abbreviations of the names into full name, and make each row only have one name? Tanya: You may modify the field log as long as its information is not lost. You may add columns and rows, and use a new sheet. StarTree: Is it fine if I don't update the Map section in Guild Log? If this system works, StarTree won't be using that Map for scoring! Tanya: That's fine also. StarTree: Wee~!
Lab
[202201252300]
202201252300|JQuery Enabled
▷ 2022 JAN 25 TUE 11:00 PM
Adventurer's Hall
⭐
I have just enabled jquery and confirmed that it is working. If you want to see how it works please go to the Lab at Adventurers' Hall and see the script at the end.
Ledia: This sounds like some super powerful thing. What do we use it for? Ivy: That's for you to figure out. Tanya: Is it okay if I put the Cardinal Quest map here at Dashboard with JQuery? Ivy: Just do it and see if someone vetoes it. Zoey: What have you done, Tanya? Now no one can rest with the quest map up like that all the time! XD 🤣

[202201262056]
202201262056|Will it fit?
▷ 2022 JAN 26 WED 8:56 PM
Quest Channel
⭐
We spent the whole day trying to map out a section of Geyser cave and couldn't finish. Then Black came along point out that according to the old map our high power crystal could fit through the old tunnel. Did we spent the whole day doing nothing?
Casey: Then Tanya came along and said the old map might have a typo and had the wrong dimensions. If she was right, then we haven't finished and there is no shortcut. So. Who is right? Evelyn: I think even if it could fit back then, we shouldn't take that chance, since it contradicts everything else... You were the one who realized that it won't fit. And we checked your work. I believe in you. Black: Tanya is probably right. It has to be a typo. I didn't check as much as you did and just made a passing comment. Tanya: I can't find more concrete evidence that it is a typo. But it could be an error. Sylvia: It has to be an error, because the dimensions don't match up with the next room. Sylvia receives Tanya: I figured it out. The original map had no error. What we thought was the original map was actually a partially modified version. Someone had partially updated it to the dimensions we want. Casey: Well... That could only be me, right...? I was the only one editting it yesterday. My bad. Sorry for confusing everyone.

202208141928|Puzzle Plaza

 202201290832|⛏️ Gold Rush {"cmd":"PIN2","node":"202201290832","music":""}

Puzzle

The year was 1854. The California Gold Rush was in full swing. A man went to California hoping to make a fortune by selling tents but the weather was nice and the miners didn't need tents. He ended up making a fortune anyway and he is still famous today. How did he do it and who was he?

Show Answer

He sold canvas and later denim pants to the miners. He was Levi Strauss! More

📒 Classic Lateral Thinking Puzzles. Pg 195
202208141928 | 🧭 Puzzle Plaza 💬 Discussions
{"id":"202201290832","parentid":"202208141928","parentname":"Puzzle Plaza","icon":"⛏️","title":"Gold Rush","subtitle":"Puzzle","img":"https://cdn.pixabay.com/photo/2018/05/08/10/41/apple-pie-3382722_960_720.jpg","music":"","author":"Mikela","description":"","card-text":""}
Puzzle Plaza
[202201290841]
202201290841|Gold Rush
▷ 2022 JAN 29 SAT 8:41 AM
Puzzle Plaza
⭐
Hey hey hey, it's 1854, let's strike it rich at the 202201290832|Gold Rush! Mikela: I don't know how to do this yet, but if you like or approve this week's puzzle, could you leave a mark somehow? I want to show if you like the puzzle or not. StarTree: The puzzle now has a like counter like the Nominations!
🧩 Gold Rush
Mikela: The year was 1854. The California Gold Rush was in full swing. A man went to California hoping to make a fortune by selling tents but the weather was nice and the miners didn't need tents. He ended up making a fortune anyway and he is still famous today. How did he do it and who was he?
Tanya: Isn't this a trivia, not a puzzle? Either you know it or you don't. Casey: If it is not a bad puzzle, then it has something to do with the context. P4: Did he get rich by mining gold like everyone else? Mikela: No, he did not! Amelia: People used his tents to collect gold. 44: Because he sold the parts of the tents? Casey: Did he get rich by selling the tents as something else? Mikela: Yes! What did he sell and who was he? Ledia: Levi. He sold jeans. Sasha: Could we get more hints? Evelyn: Ledia knowing it is a big hint... 😉 Mikela: What do you think tents were made of? Sasha: That blue thing. Tanya: That's plastic tarp. They won't have that. Sasha: What were tents made of back then? Zoey: May I Google this? I might see the answer... Tanya: Tents should be made of canvas back then. Ledia: The thing that he end up selling was not made of canvas but a similar fabric called denim. If that doesn't ring a bell, it is typically dyed blue. Sasha: He sold blue jeans! Casey: OMG it's got to be Levi! Mikela: He was Levi Strauss and sold jeans! Ledia is our winner, congratulations! Zoey: Fact check: Levi went to San Francisco in March 1854.

202202020825|Candidates
February 2, 2022 Wednesday 8:25 AM
World Tree
⭐
For @Seed, there is an objective to evaluate candidates of peaceful concepts. How should we do that? Could you give an example of a concept you want to evaluate? Say, Freedom of Association and Choice Architecture. Choice Architecture is not evaluable because it is a subject. Freedom of Association is a concept that allowing others to choose their associates is a human right. How do we justify that letting people choose is peaceful?
⭐
Tanya: Peace can be defined as allowing differences in a way without creating conflicts. For @Seed, you could list different definitions of Peace, and evaluate a concept according to each definition. Then you just let people decide for themselves which definition of peace they want. For example, Arcacian Principle can be considered a definition of peace. And you can evaluate other concepts to see if they are Arcacian. I understand.
The police put up some signs warning people about a certain type of crime, but it ended up helping the criminals. Why?

The police posted a sign "Beware of Pickpockets". And the pickpockets saw that when people see the sign, they reach for and check their wallets. Then the pickpockets know where their wallets are! |
SATURDAY FEB 5 2022
Tanya: Did the signs end up teaching people how to commit the crime? Mikela: Nopa! The sign was simple with no details about how those crimes are committed. | Zoey: Were the signs posted at places vulnerable to that crime, so now the criminals know where to go? Mikela: Nopa! The criminals were already there, but the signs made it easier to commit the crime. | Sylvia: I think I know the answer. Sylvia: Is the sign "Beware of pickpockets" ? Mikela: Yes it is! CONGRATULATIONS to Sylvia! 🎊 Others may keep guessing how it helps the pickpockets! Sylvia is recognized to have Invisible Hand. |
{"id":"202202050800","parentid":"202208141928","parentname":"Puzzle Plaza","icon":"📯","title":"Promoting Crimes","subtitle":"Puzzle","img":"https://cdn.pixabay.com/photo/2018/01/29/12/09/money-3115984_640.jpg","music":"","author":"Mikela","description":"","card-text":""}
🔔
Mary was discharged from the hospital after spending three days there. She was not sick nor injured, but she had to be carried out when she left. Why?
⭐ Puzzle
Mikela: Mary was discharged from the hospital after spending three days there. She was not sick nor injured, but she had to be carried out when she left. Why?
I hope it is not what I think... Amelia: She was dead. Mary was ALIVE when she was discharged. Thank you! 😅 Evelyn: Did Mary work too much so the hospital sent her home? Nopa! Mary doesn't work at the hospital. Casey: Is it normal for Mary to be carried? Yes it is! But why? P4: Was Mary a plushie? 🧸 She went to the hospital with her owner, and they left together. Cute!!! Mary is a person! Amelia: Mary was a baby Mikela: CONGRATULATIONS! You are correct! 🎊
Everyone is a winner!
{"type":"chat","id":"202202050900","parentid":"202208141928","parentname":"Puzzles","icon":"🚑","title":"Hospital Discharge","prev":"189912300000","author":"Mikela","location":"Puzzle Plaza"}
A security gate scans an ID on a vehicle before letting it in. If the ID is not recognized, the scanner will send a short pulse and the gate will not open. If the ID is recognized, the scanner will send a long pulse that is three times as long as the short one, and the gate will open. What is the simplest cheat to get the gate open with an unrecognized ID?
Link |
The answer is not posted yet. Give it your best shot! |
SATURDAY FEB 12 2022
Amelia: You let it scan three times. Tanya: That would create three short pulses. | Tanya: Here is a fair warning. I don't know the solution. I saw this puzzle in a dream. I was thinking about it when I woke up. Sasha: If you don't know the solution, isn't this a waste of time...? Tanya: That's why I'm warning you. | P4: Maybe you'll get the answer in the next dream? Tanya: Perhaps. | Black: I don't know if this helps, but if the scanner is electronic, its pulses are usually generated based on the resonance frequency of a quartz crystal. Depending on how the crystal is cut, the crystal frequency could change outside its intended operation range. But we are talking about very very small change, in the scale of hundreds of pulses per a million, nowhere near making a pulse three times as long. Ledia: No freezing or heating up the scanner, then? Black: I think the scanner will be destroyed first. Evelyn: We are not trying to do anything illegal, are we? 😅 Tanya: We are not. There is no way a real life security gate would just rely on the length of a simple pulse. Evelyn: Then could I just cast SLOW on the scanner? If I cast double decel, then it would be three times as slow. Sasha: That could pass as a solution in a game. People normally don't think that they can cast slow on objects, or there would normally be no useful effect on objects. |
{"id":"202202120900","parentid":"202208141928","parentname":"Puzzle Plaza","icon":"⭐","title":"Checkpoint Scanner","subtitle":"Puzzle","img":"https://cdn.pixabay.com/photo/2016/08/26/22/28/iron-gate-1623303_640.jpg","music":"","author":"Tanya","description":"","card-text":""}
202202160835|Is Bing Reward Arcacian?
February 16, 2022 Wednesday 8:35 AM
World Tree
⭐
For more than a decade, Microsoft had gamified their search engine. You earn points when you search with Bing, and you could trade those points for gift cards. Is this Arcacian?
Tanya: It would make certain assumptions about statistics invalid, but overall it is Arcacian because arguably when people use a search engine, it helps others who are using it, and no one is forced to participate. I like how you could choose to donate the points. Should we start using Bing?
Ledia: Do we get guild credits for using Bing? I don't know what it is. Could someone explain it? It is just a reward program that you could earn points toward getting a gift card for using Bing.
LRRH: How does that help people? I don't understand. According to it helps prevent Google from dominating the search engine market. Hmm... I would say no, because if you use Bing, you are already getting Bing reward, so the guild doesn't need to give you a reward even if using Bing helps people.
Ledia: I meant if we get Bing rewards for the Guild.
⭐
Ivy: In that case that would just be the same as donating to the Guild. Donors should get a reward. We have never tied guide credits to a monetary value. I feel that if we tie it to a monetary value, then there will be a lot more bureaucracy and book keeping, because people will start treating the guild like a bank or a place of guaranteed employment because we don't turn down people trying to do a guild quest. I am not asking the guild to let people cash out their credits. But if people donate money to the guild they should get credits.
LRRH: I don't have an objection if there is no cashing out guild credits. How many credits should a donor get? That's an interesting question. How about 1 credit per dollar? Hmm... but what do we do if the value of a dollar changes?
Tanya: Should we use a meal as a reference? If someone feeds the guild one simple meal, then it is worth 1 credit. Suppose the market price of a simple meal is $5, then the current conversion is 1 credit for every $5. That seems too low...
⭐
LRRH: I don't want to give Guild points for people simply browsing the internet or playing trivia. I would rather people just do the guild quests. And we don't really need monetary donations so I would shelf that topic until it becomes more relevant.
Zoey: In summary, Bing reward is an Arcacian mechanism but it doesn't have enough peacemaking quality for our guild to award credits. Thank you for the comments.
