202203300654|Endless Work
March 30, 2022 Wednesday 6:54 AM
Puzzle Time
⭐
What work can a person never finish?
(This puzzle was originally posted as "What work can a sculptor never finish?") Tanya: Any work beyond their ability to finish... Logically right but not the answer to this puzzle! Casey: This has to be one of those puzzles with word play. The words 'work' and 'finish' are suspicious. Is the work really a sculpture? The work is not a sculpture, and many people try to do it! I'd consider that a bad puzzle, since it is misleading. Zoey: Is it their autobiography? Congratulations! You are correct! Bad puzzle. I'd consider Tanya's answer to be the best answer against this bad puzzle. Congratulations! Tanya is the winner! Can we give star ratings to puzzles? I want to give this 1 star out of five. For puzzles, please only include yourself in the heart list if you like it. But It seems mean to not give a heart. How about you choose the icon for this puzzle? The chick in eggshell icon. Congratulations! You changed the icon! Why not just change the wording to "What work can a person never finish?" I agree that would be better. Then it is changed! The Puzzles are for you!
202203300737|Ghost Town
March 30, 2022 Wednesday 7:37 AM
Quest Channel
⭐
Is it okay if I open a quest channel every day? There is no guarantee that anything exciting would happen. 😅 If it ends up being exciting, we would just change the title to be more exciting. Hey, it turns out Puzzle Time is useful! Just to give @Mikela some credits. Thanks, @Mikela. Mikela gets Kudos from Casey, Zoey, and Evelyn for having Puzzle Time.
🕰️ [10:01 PM] 🕰️
Evelyn: I think today went slightly better than expected. 😊 There were some good surprises, even though the overall outcome is unknown yet. We were at the forefront of it. 😊 That means we were the guinea pigs! XD 🤣 Could we not use animal testing references? 😅
Ledia: Could we get a more quest-like summary of what happened?
Tanya: Basically, a new seaside settlement was planned without thorough survey and ingredients for its barrier were apparently lost in miscommunication. So the settlers couldn't take care of the spawn points and called the Heroes for help. Casey looked for alternative ingredients, while Zoey charted the spawn points and updated the maps. There were many confusions, and we decided that the original barrier planned for the settlement was too strong. With Casey's resourcefulness we got what we need to build a weaker but sufficient barrier. At this moment, everything seems good except we got banshee sailors getting through the barrier. That was unexpected and even the original stronger barrier wasn't designed to block wraith-type monsters.
Ledia: So there is a ghost lurking in town. Lurking is not the right word. It is pretty obvious, actually. 😅 Apparently the design of the barrier drew them from their domain. If you try to cast summon-type spell, you would get the banshee along with what you summoned. The banshee doesn't hurt anyone but its aura makes what you summoned invisible. I hope that this is an easy fix. 😊 The sages already said they don't know why it happens yet, so the settlers would have to live with that for now. XD 😆 That's sounds annoying. When people teleport to town they become invisible. It is not scary once you know it happens. 😅 It is a ghost town XD Tanya is recognized as a Storyteller. Tanya receives

202204012235|Weekend Plans
April 1, 2022 Friday 22:35 PM
Quest Channel
⭐
Today we held the line again. The backlog for Cardinal Quest did not increase. Of the 107 quests in category A, 59 were for Deer Crossing. I hope we could clear them. Most of our "progress" so far this past two days were from crossing out quests that we had already completed. The remaining will require substantial effort. Just do them one by one. Before you know it, it will be done. I think we should try to finish as many easy ones as we can first. Reducing the list will make it much easier to manage and prioritize. Good point. How should we mark the ones we are skipping? Let use a single slash / for low priority (blue), and double slash // for pending (purple). @StarTree It's done! Let's see how far we can go tomorrow.

202204030941|Treasure Hunting
April 3, 2022 Sunday 9:41 AM
Treasure Islands
⭐
On this fine Sunday we go to Treasure Islands to look for exotic upgrades and treasures. Have we decided whether to donate the old camera? We shouldn't take too much time deciding that, so I put it back to the shelves. I think we should explain to the reader that by Treasure Hunting we really mean thrift store shopping. And often times we also donate things. Sometimes we donate back the things we bought there... XD The old camera wasn't from a thrift store. You could just keep it if you want. It is not like we have to donate everything we don't use. I have lots of stuff I don't "need" and I'm keeping them. I know you can keep it, but what is the significance of this camera? It was the camera Magic Bakery used to publish a photo book. I see it as a witness... or perhaps an author. Little Red Riding Hood now designates the camera as a Baker and Treasure of Magic Bakery. It is part of Magic Bakery family and it shall be displayed along our book. Thank you. Tanya receives LRRH receives Tanya thanks Red for the decision. I like this ending. 😊
⭐
Tanya: On the other hand I still think we have too many board games. There is no 'We', those are mine. So mind your own business. They are yours, but they are also in the guild. I'm trying to make games for us. Then why is this Zombie game here? It is cheesy and graphical. I just thought we could use the board for our own game since the game looks SO BAD I am sure no one would be sad if we destroy it. In case you don't know certain someone here is against breaking a game and taking just the components. If the game is complete and good, someone else could have enjoyed it. It is a waste to break it apart. That's why I got this. But it is... new. The components aren't even open. That is evidence that NO ONE in their right mind would want to play it! We don't know that. If you want it just for the game board, I would rather make you one than knowingly destroy a new game. Bad games just sit on the shelves until the staff takes them off and put them in garbage! You are so afraid of destroying it but that will be its fate anyway! We are saving it! Boardgamegeek gave this game a rating of 4.4/10. Just saying. I have an idea. How about we don't get a game just for its components unless it is already on the shelves for more than 2 weeks. The price tags have dates we could check. How about we ask the staff how long they let a game sit on the shelves? Yes.
⭐
Evelyn: It turns out that there is no rule and many of the price tags don't have a date. 😅 You're making too big of a deal out of it, since it must be really cheap when I got it. Hey, I found what we came for. We just got here. 😅 There are even different colors for us to choose. XD I don't mean we have to leave. This is really common and I expected to be able to find some. So now let's see if we can find something 'Rare'. Do we get points for finding something? No, this is considered "entertainment" and you are not the entertainer. What if I get it for the guild? Not really. Normally I already got the point for suggesting that we need something. Then, when I get it, I simply complete that quest. But I already got the credit. That doesn't sound quite right. I think it is fine. If you discover something good you can still get credits. What if there is wish list and I just run the errand to get them? Do I get points or not? You get points for shopping for supplies. I think for non-supply items, you don't get points for shopping because you could have just ordered them online. But we don't just order them online if we know we could get them cheaper elsewhere. So I think shopping for those things should worth some guild credits. I think Ledia is onto something. The guild didn't recognize some tasks to be credit worthy when they should be. If the guild gives credits, should it be when someone searches for it or when someone buys it? I think both. No credits for shopping, because people tend to shop and browse for too long. If your idea is good and you get credits. It doesn't matter whether you need to buy something or you could make it work with what you already have. What if someone has many good ideas but don't make them happen? They already got the credits for suggesting it, they won't have the incentive to make it happen. I'll do something if people abuses that trust. As Casey said, the assumption is that if you make a good suggestion in good faith, you intend to fulfill it if you can. If you can't do it alone, then you would have created a proper quest and those working on it would get credits. This is not really different from how StarTree gets credits for upgrading the board. Once it is logged, someone else helping to fulfill it could get credits also. So do I get credits in this case because we as a team came to get something, and we as a team got the thing we came to get? No I classify this as entertainment. I don't want to give a point for everyone who tags along in addition to the point you already get by participating in chat. I still think there is something missing in the rules. I'll think about it.
Tanya: @StarTree, did Casey get a point for getting the leyline material? Casey got a point in the generic "Shopping" category for today! I'm totally fine with that. I already have many points and I don't really use them anyway. Casey has the third highest score in the guild! And Casey has just got another point in the "Good Deed" category for helping another shopper! Casey receives That was another three points from Arcacia, in case it wasn't obvious! I am okay with being seen as coming for entertainment. I'm not here "to get points". But points should be given when a person does something good or help other people. In our scoring there is no rule to deduct points when someone is being bad or inconveniencing other people. But I believe that if you get too few points, Arcacia would settle the difference. Then when Arcacia gave Casey points, it could be interpreted as she agreeing that Casey had been getting too few points. I wouldn't draw that conclusion. Especially when you didn't get Arcacia Award for saying that. Shopping in general takes too long. That's not the purpose of the guild. You are paying too much attention to it. You are not going to get dozens of points just by shopping for the whole day compared to what you would get from Cardinal Quest. We do get points shopping for Cardinal Quest. But I don't think there is a difference in principle. The only difference is that we got used to logging tasks during Cardinal Quest. Then, since Cardinal Quest log gave us a point, we got used to getting a point when it is convenient to log it. The difference in scoring between the two--Guild and Cardinal Quest--is not based on principles, but on the convenience of scoring. People aren't getting points unless the task is logged. In cases like this, we aren't logging, so we are losing all kinds of points. If you think you should get a point, then you should log it. But why would I log it if I know that the guild doesn't intend to give a point? Even if you log zero point, you would still create a record of your effort. Then retrospectively if the rules are corrected you would get the points back. But that's so... inconvenient.
⭐
Sasha: There is no good game here today. I'm done looking. What about this Monopoly-ish game? It has been here for a long time. But it is complete. But our conclusion was if it had been here for a long time, its next destination would only be the dumpster. That was my argument! Then are you agreeing or not? I am so confused!! @Tanya come make a reading! I'm right here... Tanya, will this game go to the dumpster if we don't get it? @Cardinal. Tanya doesn't have a skill that can predict the game would go. Aren't you the Oracle? Not in this sense. We can't easily tell the outcome unless we get the staff to tell us if someone buys it or if they discarded it. We can't even do a skill check on it. Can't you just pretend and roll the dice anyway? ... If that's what you want. Actually we could roll on whether the game will still be here the next time we return. But that's assuming that we don't buy it right now. I don't see the purpose of this guess. It is just meaningless gambling. If you roll and it says it won't be here, wouldn't that mean someone bought it? No it wouldn't. The staff could have just cleaned up and removed it. About that... I think if we come often enough, we can tell the difference between an item is gone because it was sold or discarded. If the staff cleaned up, many things would be gone. No you can't tell. You can't tell if a boardgame fan just happen to buy lots of them. Without asking someone who knows what happened to it, you can only tell if you happen to catch the moment when it is bought, or when staff puts it in the clean up cart. I think you are right. Then I ask a question. Why do you want to get it in the first place? To be honest I don't really want to get it except the tokens are cute, especially the bicycle one. For the reader, we are talking about this game. The tokens are so small. And I don't foresee us making any game that wouldn't use tokens that are ourselves. I didn't say I would use it for a game we make. I just said it is cute. By itself. There is no end if we try to get every cute thing. But it is small! It won't bother anyone. But it is also useless and meaningless. It is not relevant to Magic Bakery. If we have a bunch of different tokens, it would be interesting, although still irrelevant. I don't think we should get board games just for its part thinking that they would be useful for making our own games without first knowing what game we would most likely make.
Sasha: I did think about it. If we directly make an Arcacia Quest game, it would have a world map and the purpose of the game is to use your resources and opportunities to gain members and try to be the best country. That was Chapter 23 Philosophy Arena. XD We haven't finished it. Then components wise you only need a board, tokens to represent people, and some cards to represent you, your principles, and world events. Component wise we have enough because we already have wooden tokens from Risk without the rest. If the game is good and you want people shaped tokens we could just get meeples by themselves without breaking a game. I think it is better if we keep the game components simple and readily replaceable so that people could just print their own and play it with whatever tokens they want, and, since they can print it by themselves, they may scale it to the size they want or depending on the size of the tokens they use. I like that idea. Maybe we should continue the Philosophy Arena game? That game doesn't actually need a game board. It just needs tokens to represent followers of a philosophy. But I want to have a face for each philosophy, so I want to keep Fina, Natasha, and Alice. Should we make LEGO people for them? Then we can just take pictures and post online. I second that idea. What should we use as tokens for the followers? Other LEGO people? We do have a lot of LEGO people. I guess we would just make the leader of each philosophy taller by having them stand on a stand. So. The course of action: 1) Build Alice, Fina, and Natasha as LEGO people. 2) Restart the game by posting photos. I wonder if that would be too offensive? 😅 Actually I would be surprised if we get any attention. We have a long way to go.
🕰️ [6:29 PM] 🕰️
Casey: And so we made LEGO Natasha (Red), Fina (Green), and Alice (Blue), and also Mikela as the host.
⭐
Mikela: Hey Hey Hey! Welcome to Philosophy Arena Reboot! Here three philosophies will compete in their number of followers. To participate, please endorse one of the philosophies. You may change your endorsement until the game ends, The winner is the philosophy with the most endorsements! Let's start by letting each philosophy introduce itself!

202204042155|MagicBakery.org
April 4, 2022 Monday 21:55 PM
Magic Bakery
⭐
What should we do about the domain MagicBakery.org? I believe the plan was, and still is, that once we have added all the links there here, then we would just make MagicBakery.org point to this blog.
⭐
Ledia: If that is the plan, why wouldn't you just add a link to this blog there, and add a link to that page at this blog. I do not object. Does anyone have any problem if we link the two? But wouldn't you say that this blogger site is still in alpha stage? We don't even have a greetings page for visitors. If we link the two, then we could just make the old page the greetings page. At least temproarily. I don't have a problem with that. It is up to you. @StarTree, could you link them?
StarTree: They are now linked! MagicBakery.org and panarcana.blogspot.com are now linked! It is a little unsettling that the chapters that weren't finish are still unfinished. I think we are moving away from the book/chapter format. Albatross will be the format going forward.
202204042234|Properties
April 4, 2022 Monday 10:34 PM
Magic Bakery
⭐
About Albatross, on Twitter I am discussing about the definition of Property, as in Private Properties versus Public Properties. Could we make a page about it so I could link to it?
What is there to discuss? It seems pretty self-explanatory. Someone said that "Private Property" is an oxymoron because all properties are private. That makes no sense. I don't think we can debate about it unless we have a definition of what a property is. Something that belongs to the public is public property, Something that does not belong to the public but belongs to specific people are private properties. What does belong imply? It means you can move it around or decide what to do about it. For example, if a house is my property, then I can decide what to with it by myself. If a house is public property, then the public decides what to do with it, no one may decide by themselves what to do with it.
⭐
Tanya: In the context of describing decision rights, an object X is referred to as the Property of Y when Y has decision rights on X. That sounds like what I just said. I was just translating your example into a definition. Red needs a definition. I think both are important. Example and definition. It this definition correct? If by "correct" you mean whether it covers what people normally mean, then I believe so. And, this might not be what you need, but I want to draw a difference between "property" and "custody". In general, we say that X is in Y's custody when Y is does not have the rights to harm or destroy X. But we say that X is a property of Y when Y has the rights to do so. This might come in handy if we could get it on a page... @Zoey, could you add it for us?
Zoey: It is not that hard to add to the page. Why don't you do it yourself? It is better if the some person is writing it. My voice is cold and mean. Umm... Okay, but please think about how you would write it. I don't think my writing is that different, and Grammarly is built-in. You may check by yourself. XD
⭐
Zoey: I made the page. Is this what you want? Thank you! Let me see where the debate at Twitter got to. Um... Why are you debating with that Tweeter? He sounds nutz XD Are we sure we don't sound the same to others? Conceptually, if someone's logic is bad, it should be easy to show their flaw right? Could we show their flaw concisely? We don't need necessarily need to identify flaws. We could just compare. For example, his moral view suggests that properties are defined for its economical value. But in Stewardism, properties are defined to describe freedom and rights.
Tanya: In Stewardism, the more abundant or unimportant something is, the more freedom a person has in owning it. Things that are important and scarce falls are subjected to public decisions. For example, when there are plenty of spaces for house, everyone is free to build and own their own house. But when there isn't enough living spaces, then the living spaces become rationed. The more scarce something important is, the more it becomes an object of custody instead of property. I think we should add that too. Tanya, do you want to try adding it yourself? If I add it for your, it would just slow down everything. You have a world to save. I'll try.
💟
Tanya: A practitioner of Stewardism aims not to have possessions, but to take care of the world and make it a better place. Tanya receives Good job, Tanya!

202204052045|Sudden Collapse
April 5, 2022 Tuesday 8:45 PM
Puzzle Time
⭐
A healthy man got dressed. Then he collapsed and needs to be taken to the hospital. What happened?
(Original wording of the puzzle: A healthy man got dressed. Then he lay down and died. What was going on?) Evelyn: I hope this is not an execution. 😅 Congratulations! It is not! But still very far from the answer. Casey: This is not one of those puzzles where 'dressed' means 'marinated', is it? But I guess if he was eaten by cannibals, that would count as being executed...? Dressed means he put his clothes on and such. And his death could be considered an accident. P4: Did he get bitten by a spider when he put his clothes on? Congratulations! There was a spider in his shoe! I think the tone is a little strange because the man in the puzzle died. Could we not have puzzles in that tone? 😅 If I change the title and puzzle from 'death' to 'getting hospitalized', the guesses so far won't make sense. It is okay! StarTree can fix it! Thank you! Mikela gives Kudos to Evelyn and StarTree.
202204052130|Medical Vacation
April 5, 2022 Tuesday 9:30 PM
Puzzle Time
⭐
An American flies to another country to find a cure for his illness, but not because the treatment was unavailable or unaffordable in America. Why?
Evelyn: Is it because he has friends and family overseas that could help take care of him? Very thoughtful but no. The country he goes for treatment is foreign to him and he knows no one there. That sounds stressful...😅 Is it important that he is from America? He doesn't have to be from Americas but the difference between the country he is from and where he goes is important for the puzzle. Sylvia: Is he a substance abuser? So he goes to another country where those substances are not available? Congratulations! He went to a country that bans alcohol to cure his alcoholism! Fact check: Countries that ban alcohol
202204052217|Quest Counters
April 5, 2022 Tuesday 10:17 PM
Cardinal Quest Debriefing
⭐
Today Tanya suggested that we need to pace ourselves to clear the 300 quest backlog. So we focused on Fort Arsenal and cleared 30. The backlog is now at 270.
Zoey: In case people are curious, we have seven field quest backlog counter for Cardinal Quest. From 300 to 270 is just one of the counters. Yes, and for today we managed to hold the line. Every counter is either held the same or reduced.
Ledia: The "Homework" counter is almost clear. Why don't we clear it? The remaining one was from Casey. It says, "In general, don't store things in the field because the mist is dirty. Get crates for field storage." I don't know what to do about it. 😅 It literally asks for crates. But I don't think there is space for crates. @Casey?
Casey: I haven't found a suitable crate yet. I want a small one. I think it is better to cross it out from field notes and move it to the treasure hunting list. I agree, that's where we should see it. And now the homework category is cleared. 😊
Sasha: I don't get it. You didn't finish anything. You just moved it. Now the treasure hunting list is longer. We did do something. We moved it to where it should be to get fulfilled. But in terms of number, it is the same. We don't track how many things are in the search list. It is not really urgent.
Tanya: We are saving time by removing tasks we don't need to review every day. The homework tag is doing its job. We use it to tag tasks we record on the field but should be moved off the field notes. If you say so.

April 6, 2022 Wednesday 8:56 PM
Mira Mira
⭐
We have changed how we post things a few time. Not to burden everyone about it, I am taking the liberty to import our old profiles from Google Slides as screenshots.
Thank you! Little Red Riding Hood thanks Ledia. I look so big on the screen it is embarrassing. 😅 And the drawings look really nice. I miss 3B. Should we start importing the rest of the manga page? I thought we were saving those scores for her. If she got too few credits, we should just add it back and recalculate it. We shouldn't need to save up a chore for her to do to get credits that she should already have gotten. @StarTree, how many credits should 3B have? StarTree has no idea! But 3B drew or translated more than 300 pages of manga! StarTree receives Wee~~!! @Tanya, does it sound about right that each manga page should worth 3 points?
⭐
Tanya: Concept/drafting should worth 1 point. Outlining should worth at least 1 point. Shading should worth 1 point. Uploading it would be another point. Translating is another point. We are not accounting for the Arcacia Award points that she would have gotten when she was making them. How about 5 points per page for the 250 pages? 1250 points on top of her current points. Any objections? That sounds about right. She started a year before we started, I think? @Arcacia? Little Red Riding Hood receives It is approved. StarTree please update her score.
⭐
StarTree: @3B's score has been updated! 3B is now taking the lead with 1415 points at Level 37! Justice.
3B: I'm sorry for having been gone so long! Just let me know if you need something drawn! 🙏 Welcome back, 3B! 😃 I'm going to start importing your manga pages to this Blogger if you don't mind. Of course not. Sorry for falling behind. I don't think you are responsible for posting it again when the blog changes. No one has such responsibility. But I can get credits for doing it. Yes you are right. 3B, some of us have added daily routine. Do you want to add a daily routine? I guess I could draw something simple everyday, but wouldn't that just take up space and pollute the internet?
⭐
Zoey: How about we try a game where, on the same page, we all draw something on it? Then at the end of the month, that would be the picture for the calendar. I like that idea. 😃 Zoey receives Are you sure that's a good idea? It would just look very messy. I can't picture it until we try it... 😅 And, if you start drawing before the month ends, you won't now what is important in that month to draw something representative of the month. Then should we draw for the previous month? Bingo! I admit that seems like a better idea. Sasha receives I don't think the drawing needs to be a summary of a month. It by itself is also what we do that month. It would be like a month-long conversation, but not in words but in pictures. Evelyn receives Let's try it and see what happens.

202204101024|Authoritarian
April 10, 2022 Sunday 10:24 AM
World Tree
⭐
What is our definition of Authoritarian?
⭐
Ledia: Authoritarian means people are commanded on what to do instead of choosing what to do. It is the opposite of being autonomous. If a restaurant offers three choices of meals, but someone would punish you if you don't choose Meal A, then that someone is being authoritarian. If you are allowed to choose the meal you want and no one would punish you, then it is autonomous. What about laws that disallow stealing? Is that authoritarian or autonomous? What do you mean by "disallow". If the law simply states that "People who steals are allowed to be stolen from," then it is not authoritarian. Treating people the way the treater others is not authoritarian. You don't need to make a law to tell people to retaliate. People would autonomously choose to retaliate if the law allows. Does that make stealing legal or illegal? In terms of guild membership, that would be illegal. If you steal from inside or outside a guild, the guild will kick you out. If you steal from the outside, and the guild does not kick you out, the guild would become a target of retaliation. And a guild having its own membership rules is not authoritarian? It is not authoritarian when guild membership is a result of mutual agreement. Both sides need to agree. A person can't demand a guild to accept them, and a Guild can't just say something is their member without their consent. I think we are getting somewhere interesting...
Zoey: What about a law that says people can't have more than one home? Is that authoritarian? It depends on what the law actually says. If the law says, "Our system only registers one home per person. Unregistered homes are considered available for someone with no home to register as theirs." then it is not authoritarian. You mean... someone without a home can then just register that home and be a rightful owner? Yes. By the way, I am not arguing whether such law is morally good or bad. I am strictly commenting on what makes a law authoritarian vs autonomous. For my understanding, what would make that law authoritarian? If the law says, "If you own more than one home we will put you in jail", then than would be authoritarian. What if the law says, "If you own more than one home, then we consider you an object that can be stored in a storage room." ? It is authoritarian because the subject is no longer the home but the person. What about, "If you claim to own more than one home, then we recognize that you are capable of splitting up into as many parts as the number of homes you own and simultaneously occupy them." That would be autonomous, but doing so does not give the law the right to separate the person into parts. The person would have to separate themselves to demonstrate legitimacy. Can we... not talking people separating into parts? 😅 But P4 can do that. In that law, would P4 be allowed to own four houses while others may only own one? Her split is not permanent. So her legitimacy of owning four houses should also be temporary. What about StarTree? StarTree can clone herself by budding. You don't need extraordinary examples, human can have kids... the kids can have their own home. The difference seem superficial. I don't think people would accept this difference. The difference is subtle in words, but not so in how the society is organize.
Ledia: An authoritarian society may ban or require carrying of personal protection weapons, where as a voluntarian society would let each individual decide by themselves. In a voluntarian society, individual groups can still define their membership conduct. What if a guild decides that it is legal for its members to bully others? Would gang violence be legal then? To that guild, yes, it would be considered legal. But so would be another guild that declares that it is legal for its members to protect people who are bullied, and such a guild may have a guild rule so that if you don't help the guild protect innocent people, you cannot be part of the guild. The members are allowed to leave the guild. What if the guild has a law that it is legal to hunt down deserting members? That type of law is authoritarian because when it denies members the right to leave. But they might have signed a contract and agreed on being hunt down if they desert the guild. In voluntarism, such contracts are not enforceable. When someone tries to hunt you down for deserting, from Paladins perspective it is the same as they initiating an unprovoked attack on a free person. Unless the person being hunted voluntarily allows its former guild mates to hunt them down, then it would be considered a consensual interaction. How do you put that concisely? What doesn't voluntarism cover? Voluntarism only covers the case when all parties maintain consent for the continuation of the interaction, with the concept of implied consent where a party is allowed to be treated the way they treat others. So in the case of a member deserting a guild, the guild is only allowed to desert the member in return. It doesn't give the guild a right to hunt down the ex-member unless the ex-member is hunting down the guild. Ledia receives May the guild hunt down an ex-member if they possess sensitive information that would threaten the personal safety of its members. No. Voluntarism only covers the case if the guild divulges personal information if the ex-member divulges theirs. If a guild starts hunting others, regardless whether they were ever a member, just because they have the ability to hurt them, then anyone may also hunt down that guild just for having the ability to hunt others. Paladin guilds don't hunt others just because others can hunt. Intention to initiate a hunt is also required to justify preemptive strike.
🕰️ [11:18 AM] 🕰️
Zoey: How should I name the difference between these two: 1) An interaction happens due to mutual agreement, 2) An interaction happens due to a party acts on another without their consent, and the other retaliates under implied consent.? I think (1) is mutualism, (2) is voluntarism. When you retaliate, you are still acting from your volition. Voluntarism includes situations where a person initiates a voluntary attack. If voluntarism just means people act on their volition, then we need a word that means mutualism plus implied consent. What about reciprocationism? It means treating other the way they treat others. It covers mutualism when people reach an agreement before acting. Yes, I think we need that word. Zoey added Reciprocationism to Seed of Peace.

202204122111|Spy Exchange
April 12, 2022 Tuesday 9:11 PM
Restaurant
⭐
In World War I, two spies often ate at the same restaurant to exchange information, but they never sat together nor used any code words. How did they pass information?
Evelyn: Did they leave a letter for the other as they pass by each other? They did pass letters to each other, but they never pass by each other. What did they do? Ledia: Did they pass it at the cloth rack? They leave the letter in their coat, then take the other coat as they leave. They left it in their similar hats! Congratulations! It's too easy! More please!
202204122123|Special Feature
April 12, 2022 Tuesday 9:23 PM
Farmland
⭐
For about 100 years, a feature in The Old Farmer's Almanac made it much more popular that all of its rivals in rural US. What was the feature?
What... is... an... Almanac...? It is a calendar for farmers marking when to plant. 😅 What feature could they have added that no one else could copy for 100 years? Click me again if you want a hint! Mikela: It has NOTHING to do with its printed content! *Shocking* Ledia: Did people use it as toilet paper? That is part of how an Almanac was used! What made it stand out from the others? Ugh. I need a puzzle that is less gross. Casey: Did it have a string for people to hang it? It has a hole for it to hang on a nail! Your guess is close enough! Congratulations! One more please!
202204122144|Stuffed Cloud
April 12, 2022 Tuesday 9:44 PM
Cloud Nine
⭐
A meteorologist needed to be replaced due to a stuffed cloud. What is a stuffed cloud?
P4: Is that a mascot? Cute !!! Cute but not in this case! The stuffed cloud killed the meteorologist! What is it? ...😅 Sasha: Is that another name for poison? A cloud of poison that suffocates people? The cloud is not poisonous, it is stuffed. What does that mean? You mean "cloud" actually means a cloud? Yes! And stuffed actually means stuffed! Ledia: Is that a cloud with ice in it? You are somewhat on the right track and somewhat not! The thing in it is much more impactful! Evelyn: I hope it is not another plane in the cloud...😅 Equally tragic but no! ... What else can be in a cloud? Hint: P4 and Vivi would not face that problem! P4: Is it a tree? Think bigger! The World Tree? Sasha: Is it a mountain? Congratulations! A stuffed cloud is a cloud with a mountain in it! Let's have a moment of silence for the unfortunate event as we remind ourselves the hidden forces of nature. 🙏

202204131146|Crystal Lake Cave
April 13, 2022 Wednesday 11:46 AM
Crystal Lake
⭐
Legends has it that in Crystal Lake, there is a cave full of treasures. I've just gotten a key to the cave. Who's with me?
Tanya: We are in a hurry. We haven't had a proper Cardinal Quest for so long. I think if we keep the chat short and elaborate on it later, it will be fine. I agree. 😊 We've never been there before, let's gather some information. The inn lady said the cave has two entrances. This is the key for the west entrance. She said the entrance probably has a sign. It can't be that hard. So the inn lady has never been there? Probably not. But it is not like the key is fake. People go their with the key and comes back. She keeps the keys. Let's ask someone who had actually been there. Tanya found a sailor who had been there. He confirmed that that was the key. However, just as the party were to head to the cave, they said, "by the way, if the key doesn't work, you need to shake it." Is this one of those ghetto quests?
Cardinal: The party arrived at West Shore beach, where the alleged entrance was supposed to be. There is a cave with trees in front of it. Do I see a sign? You don't see a sign. Do I see a sign? You don't see a sign either. However, you see that across a bay where there was a giant turtle, there were at least two more caves. Protect. The party now has Protect. Should we just try it? Maybe there is no sign. If it works we don't need to go around the turtle. ... I try opening it with the key. The key gave out a glow as you entered. But the entrance did not open. I shake it. There seems to be no difference. I use far-seeing to look around the other caves. Tanya sees that they other caves had signs leading to elsewhere. By the process of elimination... Did I just not shake it hard enough? I shake it harder. There seems to be no difference. Should we call... James?
James: James comes to the rescue! Evelyn gives James the key. 🙏 James uses the key. The key glows, but the entrance does not open. James shakes the key. There is no observable difference. The entrance is not open. James shakes it as hard as he can. James shakes the key as hard as he can, and... Loses 1 stamina in his attempt. The entrance remains closed. Hiding his utter disappointment, James proclaims that if he shakes it any harder, the key is going to break. I'm sorry! Thank you for helping us, we know there is another entrance, we will try that. 😖 I found the sign. P4 points at it. From her vintage point, P4 could see the sign hidden behind the overgrown vegetation. The sign indicates that this is the entrance. I don't think the sailor was stronger than James. Maybe he shakes it in a different way. We need a demonstration.
Tanya: Long story short, the Sailor was the only one who could open the entrance with shaking. But we couldn't tell the trick to the shaking, so... I asked the Hospitalian Knights to change the entrance. I got what I was looking for, and found that we are missing two things, presumably somewhere at the bottom of the lake. I suppose that would be a story for another day. We had to end the storytelling because it is getting past bed time. Good night everyone! 😅

202204152139|Weekend Plan
April 15, 2022 Friday 9:39 PM
Treasure Bay
⭐
Hey, what's our plan this weekend? I want to go look for the pink hair for Arcacia.
Tanya: I think you should just buy it. The last time we checked, there is only one. Yes, but what about the adventure? I am not against adventures, looking for things we know we could get is not much of an adventure. Are we talking about getting a pink LEGO hair for Arcacia? Yes. I am okay with getting it online and have more family time. 😅 Family Puzzle marathon? I'm okay with that 😃 @StarTree, could you look for the Pink hair at BrickLink? It is found! I'll see if it also have other pieces I want...
🕰️ [10:48 PM] 🕰️
⭐
Tanya: Other than shopping, what is our weekend plan? Albatross quest please. We are behind putting concepts up for Albatross. What is our stance on this Risk boardgame that has been here for weeks? @Sasha? 😅 It is for us to play, eventually. I don't think we will play war games. But wars are real. It even has Kiev in it. And I got it when it was on sale. But I don't see it happening in this guild. We are a peacemaker guild. Why would we play a war game for fun? Maybe it would be educational? Evelyn, I see a curse affecting Sasha. Could you de-curse her? I don't see what you mean. 😖 She got this game for some reason. She might not even know the reason, so she is making up reasons to justify her impulse. There is a curse on her. Sasha, what do you like about this game? Hmmm... It has cool components? These crowns, these castle cities, and different miniatures for each faction. I don't quite get it. 😅 Sasha, I think we talked about not getting games just for their components. And the majority of the components in this game are soldiers. We can't have a situation where we imagine commanding them to fight each other. That should be against guild rule. It is against guild rule. I put it on the page already. But we played Adventure Begins. We fought there also. An all out war game is definitely a step in the wrong direction. In Adventure Begins, we were playing ourselves and it was cooperative. I don't want to play a game where we fight each other, especially when it is by sending others to fight. 🙏 Hold on there. In this game, you DON'T have to fight. You could just defend your borders against others who try to fight you. You could win by just earning crowns without attacking anyone. Then it is better than I imagined. But large scale fighting is still a tragedy to imagine. But wouldn't you want to test how peacemaking methods work if OTHERS are trying to take over the world? Shouldn't that be the end goal of the guild? I find that argument quite compelling. If you want peace, prepare for war. Someone gets it. I am surprised that Tanya didn't get it. Not all peacemaking guilds are the same. This is not a paladin guild. Simulation is not entertainment. I agree with Tanya on that also. But then how does the guild rule "If it ain't fun, change it" work? Simulation is not fun. Therefore we need to make it fun. If it is not fun thinking about how to defend the world from someone trying to take it over, then we need to make it fun thinking and preparing about. But this is warfare. 😅 No it is not. It is a war *game*. We aren't destroying any pieces. They know that it is a number and dice game. If their number is not enough. They are "defeated" and stay outside the board. No one is actually hurt unless you WISH to imagine that people are getting hurt and such. I didn't ask you to wish for that. I'm presenting you a GAME with defined game rules. If you WISH to imagine people are getting hurt, that's on you. I find that to be a very compelling argument, if the players simply do not mention or refer to anyone being hurt. So from this number and dice game, we can PLAY and experience the other issues such as diplomacy and alliance. It is educational. Even if you do so, the game will not be played because we have more direct and faster ways to imagine and discuss diplomacy and alliance, than to spread a game board and move game pieces around. That might work for you, but for some the visual aid will make the discussion more attractive and memorable.
🕰️ [11:57 PM] 🕰️
⭐
Sasha: At this point, if I ask you whether Adventure Begins or Risk Europe is a more relevant game for the guild, you should conclude that Risk Europe is better. And us playing it would lead us to Arcacian Quest constantly. We will learn and grow together and it will be fun. I think Sasha's argument is compelling, especially when I am responsible for imagining suffering when there is no need.

202204171326|What is Freedom?
April 17, 2022 Sunday 1:26 PM
World Tree
⭐
I'm making a post at Seed of Peace about our definition of freedom. How do we want to use/define that word?
Tanya: I would first define Ability. Ability means a person being able to do something. Freedom is different from Ability. Freedom means whether a person is allowed to do something without retaliation.
⭐
Ledia: How about defining freedom as the actual result/condition describing whether some can do without resistance? For example, a powerful villain that without meaning opposition is said to be "Enjoying their freedom to do evil". That doesn't mean they have a right to do evil. It just means no one has the means to stop or restraint them. Then in your definition, "Freedom" by itself is a neutral word. It does not imply whether a person is allowed to act or not. That would contradict with the intent behind terms such as "Freedom of Speech". That implies that the speaker is not retaliated against for speaking. To describe that villain, I would say, "their violation of other people's freedom is unopposed."
Black: I want to distinguish between "Freedom" and "Liberty". I want Liberty to mean what a person or society allows. Freedom to describe a situation to act without restrain. If we make that distinction, then we can evaluate different "freedom" and refer to the ones that we subscribe as "liberty". I agree with that. The word "Freedom" is also used to describe evil behaviors. And yes, "free" means being unrestrained, which also means without being retaliation. But it doesn't imply that the reason is people "approve" or "allow" it. Freedom is something you can define by yourself and claims that others are violating. Liberty is not something you can define by yourself because it is given. The meaning of Liberty would be similar to "permission", except a permission is something you need to ask others to get, while liberty covers everything you are allowed to do without asking for a permission on a case by case basis. I see.
Zoey: Then what is a right?
⭐
Black: I think as this point I want Right to mean something that is morally right and a person shall be given the liberty to do. Liberty refers to what a person or a regime allows others or its people to do. Rights refer to what those people and regimes should allow people to do. So when a country recognizes a right (a human right), the country would craft its laws accordingly to support it. The set of freedom allowed by law is the liberty that the country offers. If the country's legislature is outdated, the liberty it offers may be insufficient to protect a person's human rights. Let me write it up first so we could comment. XD
🕰️ [2:36 PM] 🕰️
Zoey: What do you think about this? 👍 👍

Magic Bakery
⭐
There are only 8 quests under the "Guild" category. Could someone help me finish one of them?
Sasha: @Cardinal, roll a die and tell us one of them please. Cardinal: D8=2. "Guild member nomination and recognition instead of employee evaluation." What is that supposed to mean? Unlike a company that may have employee evaluation, our guild should have nominations and recognition of guild members. Don't we already have that? If a member wants a recognition they would just ask for one. I think that is different. And there is the leaderboard that shows the highest score member. But when you look at the leaderboard you can't tell what they are especially good at. Is that something you want to see at a member's profile? Maybe ? The quest is ill-defined XD The last time we had a nomination, it was Fina nominating posts for having the best tab style. But that was about things we make, not we ourselves.
⭐
Evelyn: I think I prefer awards based on what we do instead of about ourselves. For example, I would be more comfortable having a statistic on "who helped the most people" instead of "who is the nicest". I can see that.
⭐
Vivi: I want to see how each member gets the majority of their scores, and for each "chapter" I want to see the highest scorer. I am fine with those statistics. StarTree doesn't know how to do that automatically! But we need that functionality. Otherwise it would be hard for people to see the fairness of the scoring. Red, is that something you want to do? I think it can be that. Let's start from the top. 3B is the highest scorer at the moment. And we know that most of her points came from drawing manga. How do we want to put that on her profile? As of April 17, 2022, 3B has 1424 points. 1328 of which are from Manga charter. That is more than 93% of her score! So I would put "Score Leader of CH 12 @Manga." This recognition is unlikely to change anytime soon. What you said is correct, but that's not what StarTree said. StarTree said 3B got 93% of her score from @Manga, not that 3B contributed 93% of the scores of @Manga. Ok. My bad. StarTree does not know how to tally the highest scorer for each charter because a scoring row could have more than one participant! I don't think we need to be that accurate. I think it is less about accuracy but automation. Accuracy would come as a bonus if we enable automation. I am fine with what Ledia would put for 3B. I want to know Tanya's score distribution. Of Tanya's 992 points: 379 are from Cardinal Field Log. 366 from Guild Log, and 217 from Daily Log! Tanya's 366 points Guild Log scores distribution is: Concept 111, Board 77, Guild 45, Cardinal 32, Whale 31, Vacation 23, Seed 9, Skill 8, Paladin 6, Wish 6, Freedom 4, Music 4, Puzzle 3, Happy 2, Treasure 2, Arena 1, Dream 1, Makeover 1 That looks like a quite balanced distribution. Is Tanya the score leader of @Concept? The top scores of @Concept are Tanya 111, LRRH 95, Zoey 23, Fina 19, Vivi 12 I would put Tanya is a contributor in many areas and the score leader for Cardinal Quest and CH 16 @Concept. I don't care how Tanya's score compares to other members. I just want to know where she got credits. An audit column is now added to the charter list in Google Sheets! I want that information to be available at the profile so people don't have to go to Google Sheets. I am okay with the kind of information Ledia puts. More detail is fine also. Who's third? Evelyn is third! I... I'm third? 😳
StarTree: Evelyn passed StarTree TODAY and becomes the third highest scorer in the guild! 🎉🎉🎉 Congratulations! And what are her scores? Cardinal Field Log 274 I'm fine if you just copy and paste that to her profile. That is super dry. I think it should be hidden behind a click. Is Evelyn the score leader of @Happy? Yes she is! And she is leader of receiving Arcacian awards in Daily Log! Every time I sing I get Arcacian award. 😊 When you sing during Cardinal Quest, you get 5 points. 1 in the Field log, 1 in the Daily log for singing, and 3 for Arcacian Award. 3B gets 5 points for drawing and translating a page of manga. It took her hours. But singing only takes a minute. I can't tell if it is fair or not 😖 We need bards during the quest for mana regen. I don't see a compelling reason to adjust the scores. We don't get many points for discussing. We get points for generating and presenting ideas. We are not awarding points based on effort but on need. If we discover how to better use 3B's manga, then 3B can get more points. But we are using avatars that 3B draw every day. So are most of the concepts that we define. I also don't have a compelling argument.
Ledia: Red, does this take care of the Guild Quest? Maybe ? You can't clear the quest when the quest objective is undefined XD Is it okay if we just do three of them? I would just update it as part of @Makeover. Okay. Then I'll say it is done for @Guild. LRRH gives Ledia Kudos for taking the quest.

⭐
@Whale has about 24 quests remaining. @Cardinal could you roll the die? Cardinal: D24=14. "Add a section on how Black fails." That's long over due. Let me look up the last time we got a FAIL on our field log...
Black: The most recently discovered FAIL was from last week. I sent out a crystal that didn't work. I didn't test it because I didn't have the mana type to power it up. Ghetto. Do we have a mean to check before sending out the next time? We can't power up the whole crystal, but we can do enough to see if it doesn't power up at all. I didn't think of that way before. But you still didn't do it after you know that you can do it. But now we know why it didn't work. Plus I didn't really have time powering it with alternative mana. I think you are overestimating the time it takes. I see an interesting Oracle moment here. Suppose we try and it works, then we have wasted time testing. But that is part of our responsibility. If we don't test it, we are wasting someone else's time. No. If we don't test it AND it fails, then we are wasting someone else's time. If we don't test and it works, then that is the optimal outcome.
⭐
Evelyn: How long does it take to test it? Maybe an hour. Is that something I can do? Yes, but that's not your responsibility. But we are in the same team. 😅 I haven't checked recently, but it was monster infested the last time I saw the mana well. You might need help getting to it. Sounds like an adventure. When would be a good time to go? The first thing we should do tomorrow is to check the Crystal Lake gate seal. Hospitalian Knights are going to remove the barrier and let the water back. We need to check before that. A reasonable time to check the crystal would be tomorrow evening, Thank you. I'll plan for that.
Evelyn: Did anything go well today?
Tanya: Casey was resourceful. She quickly found an alternative when a bridge broke. But I realized that there were two other FAILs. I marked them on the field log for next time. 😅

202204191954|Essential Space
April 19, 2022 Tuesday 7:54 PM
World Tree
⭐
Tanya suggested that we should work on @Seed daily. Let's see if we can keep this up. @Cardinal, could you roll the die? Hold on. Let's work on putting up Stewardism. That's the current topic. Okay.
Zoey: In Stewardism, how do we define extra vs essential land in the context of personal dwelling?
Ledia: I feel that that's a scientific question.
Tanya: The essential dwelling should have enough space for a person to sleep comfortably and to store essential supplies such that it is sustainable. The more convenient the locale is, the less space is essential for storing supplies and tools. So it depends on the locale? Yes, and the individual. I have an idea. it is probably wrong, but I'm just going to toss it out.
Casey: If A has housing but B doesn't, when A needs to give housing to B, then A should give B the size of their largest bedroom. My reason is, that is how they would treat themselves. Then why just the bedroom? Why not their largest plot of extra land?
Tanya: We need to know what is essential to tell what is extra. But if their dwelling is big and continuous, then we need another method to determine what is essential. The method that applies to continuous space would also apply to disjointed space. So Casey's method won't work for a continuous large space. People would just argue that none of them has acquire enough essential space, so no one needs to share anything.
Ivy: Essential space should have enough space for a person to rest and meditate, with access to natural clues for they circadian physiology. A person should have enough space to stretch in place, and be able to see the sky. Along that line of thinking, it should have access to clean air. Yes. Essential space should allow their personal caretaking tools to rest also. That would include protective equipment, and clothing.
Ledia: Should essential dwelling include a restroom and kitchen? That doesn't seem essential unless a person is diseased. According to universal precautions, you would assume everyone else is diseased. So a person dwelling should allow them enough space to shelter in place with personal protective equipment off. How long should they be allowed to shelter in place? it would depend on the local threat. If we just consider quarantine, maybe 2 weeks. This means people don't need to go elsewhere for quarantine. They may self quarantine.

202204231547|Lost in Time
April 23, 2022 Saturday 3:47 PM
Time Travel Hub
⭐
On September 9, 1752 in London, there was no birth, no death, no crimes and no celebrations. What happened?
Casey: Is that referring to a work of fiction or a poem? Nope. It is very real! Real as if I look it up online I can find what happened? Yes! But then I would find what happened. Yes! And you would find that nothing happened! What...? Zoey: Was there a strike? Like all the reporters were on strike in London? Nope. There was no strike! Ledia: Was there a political scandal that was covered up? There was no cover-up! Nothing actually happened! This is mind boggling. Sasha: Is it just due to chance? That day just happen to have no birth and no death? It is not due to chance! What does that even mean?! You're saying that not only no one died, but no one could die on that day! In London, at least. Evelyn: Did London not exist on that day? Like it used to have a different name, or its name was temporarily changed? 😅 Nope, London was called London before and after. But you're on the right track! Right track...? 😅 P4: Did people evacuated on that day? So no one was in London? No one evacuated, but nothing happened! Were there people in London on that day? That turned out to be a very philosophical question! I have no idea how to answer that! You mean you don't know whether there were people there? I don't know how to answer that question! You could discuss that after you know the answer! Evelyn still has the closest answer! Sylvia: Did they used to have a leap day on September 9? Then changed it to February 29? It wasn't a leap day! But this is EXTREMELY close! So I'm going to tell you the answer: 44: Is it related to change of calendar scheme? Britain changed its Calendar format! September 3 to September 13 1752 did not exist! Fact checked XD
202204231708|Secret Interview
April 23, 2022 Saturday 5:08 PM
Noisy Office
⭐
At a noisy office, five job interviewees were instructed to wait until they were called. A few minutes later, one of them got up and entered the inner office, and subsequently got the job. What job were they applying for?
Ledia: Was it a sales job? If you wait to be called you'll never get the sales. Very insightful but not the answer to this puzzle! The instruction was literally legit! P4: Were they called? But only one interviewee heard it? That is part of it! What job were they applying for? What job requires you to know you are being called in a noisy office? 44: Something to do with eavesdropping? Nope. But alertness is required. 44: Bodyguard? Very good guess but not for this interview! Evelyn: Were they applying to be nurses or secretary? Nope. Here is a hint: At the interview, they were not called verbally! Zoey: Were they called by a sign? And there were supposed to be able to see it? So it was testing their eyesight? They were called by sound, but the call was not verbal! Tanya: Were they radio operators? And they were being called by their callsigns? Congratulations! It was the 1800s! They were applying to be telegraph operators and the interviewer was calling them in Morse code! Tanya receives Arcacia is calling us. 😊 Evelyn receives
202204231810|Moral and Policy
April 23, 2022 Saturday 6:10 PM
World Tree
⭐
Is moral compass necessary in discussing policies?
Tanya: If you propose a policy, you should have evaluated whether it is morally sound. And if possible you should also evaluate its effect and impact. So if you propose a policy, it is fair if others ask you to show that it is morally sound. If you cannot show that, then you haven't done the prerequisite due diligent for others to pay attention. Then you are not proposing a policy, but asking for help in evaluating your policy.
Zoey: What about the argument where people with different moral compass may still agree on the same policy, so it is not necessary to talk about moral compass in the discussion? Then you are describing a case where no one happens to question the moral validity of the policy. If no one questions the validity and no one shows its validity, then the discussion group is at risk of Group Think. They are at risk to be ALL WRONG together because they didn't vet the policy at something fundamental. That is surely one way for a group to end up deciding to do something morally wrong.
Zoey: What about the argument that even if you know that your proposal is morally sound, in order to get acceptance, you would present it the way the opposition believe would benefit them. I don't think one could normally pull that off because people are opposed to changes. People can quiet easily tell or form an opinion on how a change would impact them in the short term and oppose it. So if you just give them short term benefits with strings attached, you could potentially wait until they get used to the benefit and are willing to compromise to keep the benefit.
Zoey: For housing, we know that Stewardism would say something like "If a place is unoccupied, it is not wrong to take shelter there," and the corresponding Arcacian policy is, "You may get credits for building shelters for others." For Arcacian credits, people would say that is taxation through inflation. They would say those credits have no value because one would need a lot of them to trade for something, if there is anything that they may get with it. We don't need to spend Arcacian points for day-to-day things, because the provider get credits. Correct, as in the case of sustainable abundance. People are helping each other and don't have to worry about whether they have enough credits to get what they want.
Zoey: Is Arcacian economy a type of gift economy? Yes, as opposed to transactional economy. But not all gift economies are Arcacian. Arcacian has the requirement of helping. And you can't define helping without define what moral compass is in use.
Zoey: How do we counter the perception that if someone talks about moral, they must believe that there is only one moral compass? That is just wrong. People with different moral compass can discuss the differences and identify what policies would fit both compasses.
Zoey: What is the difference between Moral View and Moral Compass? A Moral Compass is a rule or a set of rules that helps a person test whether something is morally right. A Moral View is more fuzzy and general notion about what is good or bad, but without the precise guidance. A Moral Compass is a test to help a person determine objectively whether an action violates a Moral View.
Zoey: In what moral view would people discuss/draft policy by starting with moral check? Hospitalian Knights do that. It is part of the legislative process. Each law must be grounded in protecting basic rights. If you can't connect the law to it, then there shouldn't be a law about it. What about dress code? How does that tie to protecting basic rights? The dress code is not a law. It is part of a Guild's code of conduct. There is no Hospitalian law saying that a guild member needs to adhere to the guild's dress code, and there is no law saying that a guild can't require its members to follow the dress code. There is no laws about it because having such laws would violate freedom of association, since an entity is free to define its code of conduct, and individuals are free to join. No one needs to join a guild. Why is the requirement for the Law different from guild code of conduct?
Tanya: The law is about when a person may act in defense of someone's rights. Those actions would normally be disallowed if someone's right was not violated. The Laws are all for defining when the Paladins can mobilize and use force to right the wrongs. Tanya: Violating a guild's dress code is not a situation that warrants Paladin deployment. Exactly. There is no dress code law. *Look at me* Ledia is not in Hospitalian Knights. That's correct. I am in Arx. That's a different guild.
Zoey: Okay. I wrote something about it. Is this correct? According to that, Arcacianism does not recognized self-help. Forbidding someone from helping themself is not a moral violation? That's is not what Arcacian principle means.
⭐
LRRH: A person can "self-help", except in Arcacian terminology, no one is ever alone. You are always with your better self and Arcacia is with you. Little Red Riding Hood receives In Arcacianism, there are no singular persons. Every person is a plurality. So please change the wording. You can "help yourself" in Arcacianism and others shall not stop you from helping yourself. Corrected.

202204241321|Cleric Protect
April 24, 2022 Sunday 1:21 PM
Adventurer's Hall
📋
It was postulated that in addition to Protect against physical attacks, which would be classified as a Paladin skill, there is a also a Cleric class Protect. Do you agree? Do you see a difference?
⭐
Evelyn: The Cleric peacemaker archetype is makes peace by mediating ideological conflicts. The Cleric Protect skill lets people with conflicts to discuss peacefully and find constructive solutions and agreements. And how do you cast that spell? I would keep the discussion oriented in addressing the problem, highlighting common values, avoid accusation tone, let people feel welcomed as a contributor. Could you give an example of each? It is difficult to imagine examples... 😅
⭐
LRRH: Say someone is petitioning for the city to rezone a piece of farmland for business because the petitioners don't want to commute elsewhere to work. The opposition says that it would reduce farmland and cause traffic congestion. How would you Protect the discussion?
Evelyn: If I am the host I would start by requesting both inputs and solutions.
⭐
Mikela: Hey hey hey! Let's make it fun and have a mock discussion! Mikela casts special event flare. Mikela is recognized as Herald.
Fina: What is the purpose of the business? It is for high tech research. Let's say it is not safe or practical to do in someone's home. They need a separate facilitate for that. Are there are location willing to let them build? Hmm... Yes. Then what is the issue? Why can't they build there and move there? Let's say they don't want to move because they grew up here. If they build locally, they would need to reimburse the other local people for the impact they cause. But they are doing the research for everyone. Why do they need to leave? Isn't there a Stewardism rule that local should take care of them? High tech research doesn't necessarily benefit the local. What if they do? But the other local wants to exploit them by kicking them out so they don't need to bear the congestion during the research, which will benefit them. That doesn't explain why they would refuse to move when another location already wants them there.
Ivy: The purpose of this discussion is not to actually find a solution, but to demonstrate what Cleric Protect is. So please be mean and attack each other. Little Red Riding Hood doesn't want to be mean because that's not CUTE! It is more like you CAN'T be mean because you have no moral ground in this argument. You are at the mercy of the local letting you be there because you aren't willing to compensate them for the lost of environment and convenience. You are trying to sneak by without paying your fair costs. But I'm doing research that will benefit you. Not unless I ask for it. You can't show up to someone saying you will clean their house then demand you have a right to the house unless they let you clean it. But the result of the research is informational. Once it is out you would get the benefit for free. Then come back when it is out and when I am using it. I thought Stewardism doesn't have a concept of intellectual property. It doesn't. That's why I can use the tech without paying royalties But you are saying that if you use it, then Red has a right to live there. That's not about intellectual property rights. That is for her being an intellectual contributor to local Stewardship. Each location would rank how much that contribution mattered. Something is off but I can't pin point it.
Vivi: In Stewardism, who has a right to decide whether the facility can be built? It is not about authority but sustainability. When there are not farmland available for the facility, there is no reason for the researchers to convert the farmland. Their request out of their convenience is unreasonable. To pay for the decrease in sustainability, they would have to pay it back in sustainability, which means coming up with another piece of farmland. If they can't create farmland, then they cannot afford to take away farmland. They are at the mercy of caretakers taking up their slack. What if there is too much farmland? In Stewardism, too much means reducing farmland would increase sustainability. But that's not the case here because there is an alternate site for the facility. What about the commute? They can move near the facility that they will build. I can't find a problem. But the exercise is failing because we couldn't demonstrate what Cleric Protect is.
Evelyn: In conclusion, it seems that we reached an understanding that without adding back farmland, building at the non-farming site is the most sustainable option. The researchers might need to leave the local town to avoid commuting, but their successful result would grant them a right to live in any community their research benefits.
⭐
Ledia: It seems the researchers are getting a bad deal. Even their research benefits every community by a small amount, they would end up having not enough credits in each community to get a place to live. Then they are just being exiled. If the research is worthwhile, they should have enough credits to live in many places, because once a community have taken care of caretakers, the remaining living space is prioritized based on total credits, that's when a small benefit to many people counts. But no matter how many total credits they have, they can't display people who are physically taking care of the location day to day. That's the bottom line. Okay. That sounds like a fair tradeoff.
Ivy: I added Cleric Protect at @Skill. But I don't feel that I can recognize anyone to have that skill until it is used. If you see it in use please nominate for recognition.
202204241922|Clothing Design
April 24, 2022 Sunday 7:22 PM
Mira Mira
⭐
Who wants a new costume or a makeover?
Tanya: Albatross doesn't have an avatar yet. I thought the Albatross is an aircraft, and I heard rumors that Casey was going to build a mini one with LEGO. I see.
Zoey: Could you draw me as Elf? What hair style? Curly and athletic. Zoey thanks Ledia for drawing.

202204292251|Oracle Speech
April 29, 2022 Friday 10:51 PM
Magic Bakery
⭐
What do we feel like doing this weekend? I worry about Forest Bazaar. I think we should investigate.
Casey: I want to do some treasure hunting. Same routine. It shouldn't take long.
Ledia: I'm will Tanya if we Cardinal Quest it.
Evelyn: I have a question about hate crimes... but that can wait. What is your question? Should hate speech be legal?
⭐
Tanya: In Hospitalianism, bullying is illegal. What people commonly refer to as hate speech is therefore covered in anti-bullying laws. Where bullying is defined as actions and treatments to make someone feel unwelcomed at a place they can rightfully be. Bullying is considered anti-autonomy because it attacks a person right to be at public places. Therefore, if Elon Musk claims that Twitter is a de facto public town square, then either he protects people from being bullied, or Twitter is not to be treated as a public town square. Hospitalianism allows people to create sub-environments where people are hostile to each other as long as the public is not forced to participate.
Zoey: Hmmm... bullying is legal in private settings? If you are bullied in a private setting and you do not leave, then it is considered a mutual interaction. If you are prevented from leaving then it is imprisonment, which is anti-autonomy and illegal. Ok.
Tanya: Evelyn, did that answer your question? I'm relieved. 😊 Then what is free speech in Hospitalianism?
Tanya: Publicly legal speech does not include those that harm autonomy of others, unless it is done to protect autonomy in response to oppressions. There is no Hospitalian law that says every thing spoken is protected. And remember that Hospitalianism is an Oracle based world view. Words are the most powerful things, as spoken by Oracles. Tanya receives Then there is a Ministry of Truth? No, Oracle work is a service. Anyone can practice it. The medium and channels are open for anyone.
