202205011236|Forest Bazaar Quest
May 1, 2022 Sunday 12:36 PM
Forest Bazaar
⭐
This is Tanya calling for players for Forest Bazaar Quest. Takers please report to the quest channel. I'll join. And me. I hope I can help. I'll join. Since nothing else is going on... So, what is the quest.
Tanya: Last month April 2nd, Evelyn asked about the area crystal of the Bazaar after seeing that another crystal scroll has spell we didn't plan to include. I decided that it was irrelevant and asked her to move on. I am asking the heroes for a double check on whether those magic spells were in fact necessary.
Ledia: How did you tell that those spells were irrelevant the first time? As you many know, the new Deer Crossing statue crystals combines the magic spells of statues from two other regions: A and B. The Forest Bazaar spells came from region A. And we did not include the magic spells about the Bazaar from region B crystal. The question is, should those region B spells be included in Deer Crossing?
Zoey: I think we should know what those spells do. I don't get it. If they weren't there in the first place. Why do we need to know what they do? That's like saying Puppy A was living in House A, when we move from house A to House D, we moved everything from A for the Puppy. Why do we need to know what is in House B? That was my logic. If that logic is correct, then it is correct that the items remaining in House B are unnecessary. The quest is to confirm what were left in House B. It may seem unnecessary. But that's why it is double-checking. I think it is better to verify. If somehow we were wrong, then there will be a huge spawn point left unsealed. 😅 I just want to play something, I'm fine if it is fun. Do we agree with Zoey's plant to find the purpose of those spells? If so, let's teleport to Cat Island. It's official! That island is called Cat Island! XD
🕰️ [12:56 PM] 🕰️
Cat Island
Evelyn: CUTE!!! 😍
Zoey: In case we can't show the map, the Cat Island is on the opposite side across the sea of World Tree. It has the shape of a cat reaching for another island, the Yarn Island. By the way, I took a sight-seeing map so we could mark on it. Zoey gains 1 EXP. I know we normally don't announce when someone gets EXP. But I think we should do that for Cardinal Quest when we play it like this. I already got one EXP for calling for a party. Just to be clear, I won't get EXP if I just take another map right? No, we only need one. You get EXP for making progress for the quest. According to the old notes, the spells in questions are at booths 60, 62, and 69. Are we splitting up or going together? Let's go together. Protect. Evelyn casts Protect and gains 1 EXP. I'll stop announcing when everyone has gotten EXP. I want to try on the first one. May I see the map? @StarTree, could you show the area map of the other region where we took spells from? The dog island map? There is no dog island! XD For the sake of this quest let's call that the Dog Island.
StarTree: Here it is! Ledia, what are you checking? I am checking whether they share any monster type. That's not possible because a region can only have one crystal. It is possible because if a region starts to get cat island monsters, then it will need spells from cat island crystal. Then you might see it listed on both field monster guides. Is that possible? 🤔 It is possible, but very rare, almost never or factually never happened. Ledia compares the monster lists and finds no overlap. Ledia gains 1 EXP.
Evelyn: I know this is probably a detour, but I have a question... 😅 If you look at the dates when the crystals were applied, you see that somehow they go back and forth between the years. The Cat crystal is applied in 2019 and 2021. The Dog crystal is applied in 2019 and 2020. That's not what date when it was applied. That's the date the region was incorporated. The Cat crystal was first applied in 2020 to those 6 regions. The Dog crystal was first applied in 2019 for 9 regions, then in 2020 unapplied in two of them. Thank you for explaining. 😖
Sasha: I am just going to look at booth 69 directly and see what it does. If we are lucky, then we can tell right away whether it is relevant. Sasha opens the spell section for node 69. Sasha gains 1 EXP.
Cardinal: 36 furball monsters has appeared around Sasha. I look at the first one closest to me. What do I see? You see that the first furball is drifting toward a light pole. What do I see about the light pole? You see that the light pole is a scratch pole.
Sasha: I judge spell 69 is irrelevant because Deer Crossing doesn't have scratch poles. Very convincing. Any objection? I agree. I don't want to decide just yet. Let's see the next spell. Sasha opens Section 62. There are 3 spells. I open the first one. 41 foxtail monsters appeared around the party, waving and beating on the ground. 😅 Foxtail is common but I don't think Deer Crossing has this attraction. What spell did we take from this crystal? 😅 We took Wisp Circle and Rock Golem spells. Let's see the second one. You see a spell that normally provides ladder for climbing is disabled at all but one region. Presumably because the cats can climb by themselves? Are we checking or are we just convincing ourselves that they are irrelevant? How do we know instead of guessing? We know that there are cats here. But these spells are still about the Bazaar. What is your point? Other dog places have bazaars too. And they don't have these spells. We are just confirming what we already know. But how are we confirming? It seems to me that we are just making up explanations to support our pre conception that we don't need them.
Tanya: I discovered that the Deer Crossing crystal already has Spell 69. It is already active by default. I'll take that as the definite answer. We don't care because there is no difference. That's reassuring! 😃 Tanya closes the case for Spell 69. Can we say the same about Page 62 spells? The spells on page 62 are also in Deer Crossing crystal. Same as Page 60. All of those spells are already in Deer Crossing crystal. But the cat crystal activates them, the dog crystal does not. Then we are back to the same conclusion. Since the previous dog region crystal do not activate them, therefore we don't need to activate them for Deer Crossing. But we found that if they are needed, we could just activate them. That's reassuring. 🙂 How do we know that Deer Crossing is more like a dog island and not the cat island? We were told to use Dog Island as the reference. But Dog Island didn't Wisp Circle and Rock Golem spells. Hmm... So for Wisp Circle and Rock Golem you copied from Cat island. But Cat Island's Bazaar activates more spells. Correct. Logically we would ignore those differences because we came only for Wisp Circle and Rock Golem. IDK XD We now know that cat island's bazaar has a scratch pole. That is not at dog island. If we can tie those function to the scratch pole, then it should be a second way to confirm that they are not needed. I can connect spell 69 and the last spell of 62 to the scratch pole. I can't think of anything further we could do about it. Then are we done with this quest? @Evelyn, @Tanya?
Tanya: I am convinced that even if we are wrong, the risk is much lower because it is easy to activate them if needed. So I will close this quest. Thank you for coming.
202205011742|Front Desk
May 1, 2022 Sunday 5:42 PM
Magic Bakery HQ
⭐
There are only six guild quests remaining. Can we finish one of them today? @Cardinal, roll please. "Implement front desk at Magic Bakery HQ"
LRRH: I think at the minimum, we need to have a page that greets the viewer and introduce them to the purpose and function of the blog. Where should that be?
Ivy: It should be at the top page bar and at the sidebar.
⭐
Vivi: I want it to be called "About" and replace the "Home" tap. I don't like how the Home tap jumps to another website without warning.
StarTree: The About page is now shown at the front page and at sidebar!
LRRH: What else should be on the About Page?
Ledia: This About page is very different from the original front desk. The original front desk was interactive. The viewer could click on options as they talk to Arcacia.
⭐
Tanya: I think it is quicker if we just have a site map point to the best things we have.
Tanya: We need a link to Seed of Peace. A link to Seed of Peace is now added at About! Wouldn't a site map duplicate the Links section at the side bar? I don't think the chapters in the side bar is a good list of where our good contents are. Then what are the good contents? I think currently only Seed of Peace has the good content. Magic Bakery blogger is like our lounge where we hang out. It is irrelevant to someone not in the guild.
LRRH: Do we agree that Seed of Peace has our best content?
Vivi: Make the link more prominent. Make a banner.
Tanya: I don't think we should show all discussions. We should somehow show only the best.
⭐
Evelyn: Should we each post our thoughts about peacemaking? I think that is an interesting idea, but I am hoping for something shorter, like a summary.
Zoey: I'll write a summary. Please let me know what you think.
🕰️ [8:12 PM] 🕰️
Zoey: How is it? I think that is good for About. It is nicely done. 😃 We didn't end up making Front Desk as the quest asked. But we did something important. Let's continue next time. Evelyn thanks Zoey and Red for making the blogger page better.
202205012115|Enemy Fire
May 1, 2022 Sunday 9:15 PM
Academy
⭐
During World War II, aircrafts take heavy casualty, returning aircrafts were riddled with bullet holes from anti-aircraft guns. However, adding armor to the whole plane would make them too heavy. Where should armor be prioritized?
Tanya: ... This is too famous. (Spoiler alert) Let's see what others think! I also know the answer, but I think you could have asked it better. It was too wordy and weird. I am listening for improvements! I think you can shorten the second part to "But the engineers didn't reinforce the wing armor, why?" The wording has changed! (Original: During World War II, aircrafts take heavy casualty, and those returned to the field were riddled with bullet holes. By looking at where the bullet holes were, the engineers figured out how to better protect the aircrafts from enemy fire. What did they do? I wonder who doesn't know this. Let me ask others. That's survivorbias. Put the armor where there aren't bullet holes. Fact checked. Next please. Congratulations! Everyone wins!
202205012202|Martian Attack!
May 1, 2022 Sunday 10:02 PM
Academy
⭐
In 1938, a radio drama on Martian attack caused nationwide panic as many mistook the drama as real news. However one group was not fooled. Who were they?
P4: Were they friends of the Martians? Cute !! 😍 Very cute! But not the reason! Robert: The producers? I feel like this is a trick one again. Not anyone who already knows! Casey: It can't be people working with the space agency or the military, right? that would be too obvious. No they were not! P4's answer was closer than you think! 44: The ones that didn't have radios? They tuned in and listened to the show, but they immediately knew that it was just a story! Who were they? 44: Were they scientists? Here is a possibly helpful hint: those people don't have jobs! 44: Where they kids? Yes, but why? 44: They read the book about Martian attack story? The kids recognized the storyteller's voice from kids shows! Doesn't this mean one has to know that the storyteller narrated kids shows too? I'd like to file a complaint. Hmm... I couldn't fact check this. I couldn't confirm that Orson Welles had regular children show. One more please.
202205012257|Free Fall
May 1, 2022 Sunday 10:57 PM
Academy
⭐
In 1993, a man who was not suicidal ran into a glass plane window on the 24th floor and fell to his death. Why?
Is this real? 😖 It is real. And we should keep this valuable lesson...! Ledia: Was the building on fire? 😖🙏 The build was fine and he was not threatened in any way! Sasha: Was he on drugs? He was not! But he was over-confident! Zoey: Was he a stun man? He was not, but that was very close! What was he trying to do? Casey: Was he trying to jump to the next building? He was not. He could see that there was nothing outside the window! Ledia: Was he trying to bounce off the window? He was trying to demonstrate how strong the window glass was but the window frame gave out! Congratulations! Fact checked... Could we not use the heart icon for this puzzle? 😖 👌

202205020010|Anonymous Voting
May 2, 2022 Monday 12:10 AM
Freedom Plaza
⭐
What is a correct protocol for online anonymous voting that would support choice democracy? How do you make online anonymous voting trustworthy and auditable?
Evelyn: We know how anonymous voting works in real life. Is it that different if it is online? 🤔 In real life, anonymous voting doesn't mean that you don't know who the voters are. You may know who they are, so you also know the total number of voters. When they vote, each voter gets a serialized but shuffled ballot. Each voter keeps their receipt with the serial number that they don't need to show anyone. Then the votes are counted by a computer and the result of all ballots are displayed. Everyone can see what each serial number voted, therefore, if they want they may tally the vote themselves. Since each person can verify that the display is showing their own vote correctly. If no one objects, they can assume that all votes are counted correctly. And the result is that everyone can see how each serialized voter voted, but not know which person each serial number represent, yet they can trust that there are no extra voter and no ballot is ignored.
Ledia: Wouldn't that expose everyone's address?
Evelyn: If a voter needs to be a local resident to be eligible, then on the voter's profile, they could self-declare why they are eligible, and have other voters witness or endorse their truthfulness.
Ledia: What if some fraudulent voters form a ring that endorses each other?
Ivy: I think an algorithm can detect those reference loops, and give a rating of isolation in general.
Ledia: How do you recover if a hack hijacks your account so you can't change your vote?
Evelyn: Since you have the physical receipt, you can prove that you are the voter. 😅
Ledia: But if you do so, people will know what you voted from the record. Then you as the victim is no longer anonymous. Then I don't know. 😖
Ivy: They way it can work is that the system just keeps track and display the voter serial numbers that object their own results. Unless the number of objections would affect the outcome, nothing needs to be done.
Ledia: How do you object the result of another voter when you believe that they have falsified their eligibility declaration?
Ivy: Accusations about the identity and eligibility of voters would be done at the open roster section. That is not anonymous. That's all the questions I could think of. @Zoey, could you make a post at Seed of Peace about this method? Okay. I'll put it in the task list.
🕰️ [11:00 PM] 🕰️
⭐
Zoey: It is up.

202205022330|My Better Self
May 2, 2022 Monday 11:30 PM
Arcacia's Carrot Farm
⭐
Evelyn: Some time ago 3B drew my better self, which looks like me but older. I hope that I am on the right path to be wiser so I could help more people. What's up with this format? You're not using the chatroom. You're directly talking on the page. I was just trying to jog down my thoughts as I go through the recipe. I also know this is a little strange, but I thought maybe people reading it would want to know what others were thinking when they read it...? 😖 I think it is a really nice idea, but if you don't use the calendar... we would miss out on this conversation. @StarTree, could you stitch this back to the calendar? Will do! Evelyn, sorry for interrupting your exercise. The way JQuery can work is that once StarTree makes a tag, clicking on the bubble would query all experience comments like the ones you started writing. Then it will show up on the calendar and here. Thank you for explaining! 🙏 Arcacia thanks Evelyn for being the first person to cook along! Evelyn is now recognized as First Assistant Chef!

202205030029|Carrot Reboot!
May 3, 2022 Tuesday 12:29 AM
Arcacia's Carrot Farm
⭐
Seeing that the Carrot Farm has been neglected for almost four months, Evelyn decided to help Arcacia at her farm! Where she decided to jog down her thoughts as she went along with the cooking! To help others see the storyline, please continue posting at the calendar! New tags will be made so that they are also shown at the Carrot Farm!
Zoey: We are re-inventing overlay comments! XD
Ledia: I said it there and I'll say it again. I think that made it much more interesting. Maybe we should all go through the training and post our comments.
LRRH: That is a promising idea. Should we bump @Carrot up a tier? I think @Carrot is Tier 2 worthy. I could make room by moving @Makeover to Tier 3. @StarTree, please make that change. @Carrot is now a Tier 2 Quest, @Makeover Tier 3!
Ledia: I am really excited to learn more about what each of our better self is. I am a ranger for sure XD And we should all go through it, it is like peacemaker boot camp. That's what we have been missing.
Evelyn: I am happy that we are suddenly interested in @Carrot. I hope that we will go through it together. 😊 Ledia gives Evelyn kudos for discovering something interesting and important that we can do together.
202205031940|Abortion
May 3, 2022 Tuesday 7:40 PM
Arsenal Court
⭐
This topic is as current as it gets. The US supreme court will be deciding whether abortion violates the US Constitution. For this thread, we are not trying to tell whether it violates US Constitution, but what Stewardism, Hospitalianism, or Arcacianism, or your belief, says about it.
Black: I think one of the bottom lineis that forcing someone to take care of a dependent would violate the carer's autonomy. This means society can't force someone to remain pregnant.
Ledia: But can they kill it? I think that is the question. And if they can't, that could be another bottom line. My intuition is that a person has no rights to kill an unborn if someone else is willing to take custody. If no one is willing or able to take custody, then the carrier may choose to abort. The situation would be the same regarding someone on life-support. If no one else is willing to sustain the life-support, the current support may choose to end their support.
LRRH: I'm going to say the easy answer first. In Arcacianism, helping someone give birth and taking care of their kids are credit worthy.
Ledia: What about helping someone get an abortion? Helping the unborn become independent is credit worthy. I can't tell about abortion.
Tanya: In Arcacianism, helping is only defined when the parties agree. We don't know what the unborn is thinking. So a doctor helping someone gets an abortion would not qualify for Arcacian credits. But a doctor who helps a terminally ill patient commit suicide would.
Zoey: Hold on, wouldn't that mean Paladins don't get Arcacian credits for fighting intruders? The intruders don't agree to be resisted.
Fina: In Stewardism, you have a right to fight back and protect each other. We don't care about Arcacian credits. Then in Stewardism, does a person have a right to fight for the unborn? How about I just tell you what happens if someone doesn't want to carry a baby in Stewardism? If you don't want to carry a baby, you ask if someone else wants it instead. If so, we do a teleport transfer. If no one wants to have it, then you may kill it because no one can force anyone to take care of anything. I thought Stewardism would make people clean up their own mess. If you pollute then you lose your right to partake. You lose your share of the world. If you can't stop polluting you can be killed. What if there is no teleport transfer? You can't force a person to go through childbirth. That is their choice.
Zoey: If I am in a dragon's grip, wouldn't Stewardism allow anyone to attack the dragon to save me? Even when doing so would mean forcing the dragon to release? Not if you are sucking the dragon's blood like a leech. I hope that we don't go further in that direction... 😖 But if you have a parasite, others don't have a right to attack you to save it, or force you to be its host, no? If you neglect that, you have no rights to kill germs or predators. If you have the right to kill them, then why do you need to ask if someone would take it? Actually you would if you can identify that the predator is something special. Otherwise, if it is a common predator, you don't need to ask. You can assume that if someone wants them as pets they could just go get their own. But in that case, it would mean that it is not the law that you need to ask if someone wants it. You could choose not to. It is not a law. You could just do it without telling anyone. In that case would you say abortion is a right? If you would classify an unborn to be the same as a parasite, then you would, right? It is one way to put that. Yes. The carrier may protect themself, that is part of their self-care. Does it matter whether they created the unborn? It doesn't sound right if you could create something with feelings then kill it. Then you are saying that if you made something evil you can't kill it by yourself? A good being would not demand to be born. It would let the carrier decide. Otherwise it is a slaver, that is evil. Anything the demands to be nourished is violating the provider's autonomy. Therefore the provider may reciprocate by violating their autonomy. Everyone basically starts Evil if you believe that the unborn *demands* to be born. Otherwise, you agree that the unborn only *hopes* to be born but lets the mother decides. So, in either case, the mother can decide. That's somewhat compelling. But brutal. So if you believe that your unborn is kind, and the technology is there for it to be transferred, then you would ask if someone else wants it. If you believe that it is evil, you have the evidence and the right to kill it. No one else has the evidence or a right to tell you that it is not evil. What if your mother says she felt that you were evil but chose to give birth anyway. So if you believe that the baby you carry is evil but not give birth, then you don't deserve to be born? But she did choose to give birth. She may blame herself. Can she kill you now? For what? If she believes that she should have exercised her right to kill something evil, then why would she stop me for doing what she wished to do. I would be her redemption. But reciprocation would allow her to kill you, right? But I am born and no longer a parasite. If I am trying to kill a parasite, others cannot reciprocate unless I am also a parasite. I feel that there is a loophole if you can't hold someone accountable for what they did.
Tanya: I think a grown person is practically a new person compared to when they did not have free will. That doesn't change my question. If your life was spared, and later you decide not to spare someone else's life. Do others have a right to take your life now, knowing that in the same situation you would not have spared your own life? In Hospitalianism, the answer is no. There was no deception when they spared you at first. There was no contract that you need to save someone else's life in the future. Even if there is such contract, in Hospitalianism it is not enforceable because that would violate your free will. What about reciprocation? Reciprocation just says you may treat others the way they treat others. If I don't spare the life of someone evil, then you don't need to spare me if I am evil. But if I am not being evil and I am not being a dependent, you don't have a situation to choose not to spare me. But can people travel back in time to the moment where you were being spared and change their mind? That's a huge can of worms. Traveling back in time to change a timeline would cause huge collateral damages. You could however create a branch. But I hope we stay on the topic of abortion. Okay then. What is the Hospitalian view on abortion?
Tanya: In Hospitalianism, each country is allowed to decide whether it permits abortion. Country territory is adjusted according to migration. So you are saying, If a country legalizes slavery, then the Hospitalian Knights may intervene and free the slaves. But when a woman tries to get an abortion, the Hospitalian Knights cannot intervene ? Correct. Why is slavery an oppression, but abortion is not? Forced pregnancy is slavery, and oppression. If a country forbids abortion and forbids pregnant women from leaving to get an abortion, the Hospitalian Knights can intervene. Clarification: The Hospitalian Knights can intervene if a country keeps anyone from leaving. It has nothing to do with abortion. But in the case of abortion, the woman is leaving in order to kill someone. Gladiatorial countries are legal as long as the participants are willing. It is not illegal to try to leave a country to kill someone. Paladins do that all the time. But it is legal for a country to have a border that keeps killers out. A murderer cannot be prevented from exiting a border, but they can be hunted down if they are trying to hunt innocent targets. So is abortion a right or not? In the typical meaning of the word, no. It is something that each country is allowed to decide. A person has a right to leave their country. Leaving is a viable choice in Hospitalianism because the borders are adjusted with net migration. There will be communities that allow abortion and those that do not. Both types may legally exist in Hospitalianism. It is illegal to force a community to allow or disallow abortion. What about the unborn's right to leave the custody of the mother? The Hospitalian Knights do not assume that the unborn wants to leave. Therefore intrusion is not justified. You are saying that even if you know that the mother wants to kill the unborn, you cannot assume that the unborn doesn't want to be killed? I said leaving. We can assume that the unborn doesn't want to be killed. We can't assume that the unborn wants to leave. The Hospitalian Knights can't enter a country to extract people who do not want to leave. But the natural progression is that the baby will come out. That doesn't mean that the baby wants to come out. You could interpret that as the women's body wants it out I think it is more natural to argue that the unborn wants to be born. 😅 Why? I think it is more natural to argue that the unborn wants to be taken care of. Even if it was born, it demands attention and nourishment. It behaves like a little tyrant and terrorist. That is the exact type of people Hospitalian Laws were designed to eliminate. It was only by the mercy and tolerance of others that such tyrants are allowed to stay put. Parasites... terrorists... 😖 In Hospitalianism, a citizen does not have a default right to be taken care of. But they have a right to leave and go to another country that offers more amenities and liberties. Therefore, if the Hospitalian Knights were to allow to stop people from having abortions, then those who want to have an abortion would have nowhere to go to counter enslavement by the unborn. It all comes down to who is enslaving whom in the mother-unborn relationship. I don't think there is any dispute that it is the unborn who is enslaving the mother. Hospitalianism allows countries to make their appeal and attract immigrants. The baby is the slave driver... 😖
Tanya: Anyone wants to elaborate on what Arcacianism thinks about abortion? I don't think Arcacianism says much about it. I think it would reach the same conclusion as Hospitalianism. If you see an unborn as a parasite, then you would let a doctor help the mother get rid of it, and also help anyone who wants to be the unborn's carrier. If you are the mother and don't want the unborn, but someone else wants to carrier it, then in Arcacianism, you would let them help by carrying it. It would be against Arcacian value to kill it instead if someone offer to help. Ledia receives In this whole thread only Ledia got the award XD
Ledia: I guess Red fell short of pointing out that abortion is not a right in Arcacianism when someone could help. Can we say that in Arcacianism, a person does not have a right to refuse help? Helping and being helped are choices. Arcacianism is a guideline, not about rights. Not acting according to Arcacian value doesn't mean that you are violating someone's rights.

202205052126|Wishes
May 5, 2022 Thursday 9:26 PM
Magic Bakery
⭐
Anyone has any wish they want to make?
Tanya: I think we don't have time to make wishes. Arsenal is getting out of control.
Evelyn: Is it okay if I wish that Arsenal will be easier than we expect? 😅
Black: I wish that it is less like gambling.
Ledia: What do you mean by gambling? We don't set the deadlines, but we are expected to deliver by the deadlines. But I thought they did ask for estimations. The estimations are unrealistic. It would be more honest if instead of promising a deadline, we simply reflect an expected completion date and give status reports.
LRRH: Do you want a different quest? Yes. Wouldn't it help if you know you are on track? But we don't have time to map it out. And I think if you map it out, you would just see that it can't be done. It is, "Do you want to spend a few days mapping out the future and delay what you need to do now?" There is no time left for my autonomy to decide what to do.
⭐
Ledia: But IF we map it out and it looks doable, then there won't be anxiety everyday. Correct? If it shows that it is doable then yes, it would be reassuring.
Ledia: Tanya, could you make a quick and simple map? I can make a highly inaccurate map. Let's start with that.
Tanya: The current deadline of Arsenal is June 17, which is about 30 weekdays away. With 47 spawn points, we need to seal 1.6 spawn points per day. But we don't even have 30 weekdays. We have about 20 left if we also try to do the Pylon quest. So it is 2.4 spawn points we need to seal per weekday. I think our actual speed is way lower than that. Maybe a little less than 0.5 spawn points per day. @StarTree, can you fact check that?
⭐
StarTree: According to the field log, Deer Crossing took 340 hours for 18 spawn points, that is 0.42 spawn points per day! Tanya is pretty accurate. 😃 But 0.42 per day means we need 112 days. But we only have 20 days. StarTree, could you re-calculate with only the time for the first statue? Then it took 288 hours, which is 0.5 spawn points per day! That's still 94 days instead of 20 days. In terms of weekdays, that is more than 3 months behind. Is this reassuring?
Sylvia: I thought the spawn points in the gem mines would be easier to seal. We don't know that for sure because we have not done them. But we know that some of them are difficult. If all of those spawn points are gone, is the rest doable? 18 spawn points are outside the mines, so 36 days! But not all of them need Crystal work. We are getting more and more back to the realm of wishful thinking. The aggregated average includes the time we needed to work on non Crystal works. So the current estimate is that even if there is a gem expert handling all the gem spawn points, we are still off by 16 days. Not to mention that the hours StarTree got are solid hours, not turnaround time. That's the time we need if we only work on Arsenal.
Tanya: So if we look at the rough estimate, it says that we can't make it. What can we do?
Zoey: It seems that Cardinal Quest needs to be Tier 1 again, and all other quests should be Tier 3. I think we can get a more accurate estimate if start doing it. Evelyn prays for Easy Mode. Evelyn receives We need help on this. Are we keeping Pylon quest? Are we dropping it? Let's wait for the meeting tomorrow.

May 7, 2022 Saturday 2:34 PM
Southern Plains
⭐
Pylon Quest looking for member. Previously, we estimated that our speed is 1 spawn point per 16 hours, and we could not finish sealing Arsenal. The Hospitalian Knight is committed to seal the Jewel Market, and may help on sealing the mines. But we still need to take care of the Pylon Quest south of Fort Arsenal. Pylon Quest is estimated to take 8 days. That is the situation. Friendly reminder that weekly status report is due! BRB.
Ledia: What's the plan for Pylon Quest?
Tanya: We will still use the field log to log subquests. Anyone is free to do the part they can do. Is it okay if I do easy subquests? That's up to you.
Zoey: I'm going to do what I know first. Mapping things out.
Evelyn: I'll ask sages about the history of the old pylon crystals. I found something unusual. 😅 There were three old pylon crystal and we are adding a new one. However, two older crystals are different from the newer third one. Should the one we add now be similar to the older 2 or the newer third? I believe the newer third is capable of acting like the older two by command. So we will create based on the newer third. Hospitalian Knights already arranged reagents for the newer type. Okay.

202205082100|Happy Mother's Day
May 8, 2022 Sunday 2:34 PM
Evelyn's Garden
⭐
Happy Mother's Day! 🐣
Evelyn: The Oven of Infinite Love is unlocked with better self. What do you do with the it?
LRRH: Give Likes at Twitter. 💗
Sasha: Buy gifts. 🎁
Casey: Forgiveness. 🙏🏽 Such as forgiving people for buying too many board games. Those were gifts. At least three of them are. It's all good.
Kisaragi: Cooking? 🍱 Although ever since people started fasting Kisa needed to cook way less. 😅 I call foul. Tanya didn't say what she would do with infinite love.
Tanya: Apologies and gratitude.
P4: Playing music.🎻 Our best music for the violin is Twinkle Light Tavern / Fort Arsenal. Agreed. Best music for singing is North Shore. Evelyn's vibrato is coming along. North Shore is the easiest for practice. 😊 I call foul on...
Zoey: Praising. I was praising.
Ledia: Giving people time, attention, and patience.
Vivi: Then how about practical advices?
Evelyn: And understanding. I just realized that I haven't given a suggestion. 😅
Fina: Treating others as nice as you treat yourself. 🍲
StarTree: Being accommodating! 🦽 StarTree comes to tell you that this post needs only two more participants to break the record of most participants! I'm calling for help then! XD
Natalie: Helping people. 😃 One more. XD Hey hey hey! What's going on? The prompt is what do you do with your infinite love?
Mikela: Make life exciting for everyone! ... Mikela said what 3B was going to say. That's OKAY! There are plenty more answers!!
3B: Make life memorable. 🎨 Evelyn thanks 3B for starting it all. I thought Red started it. But you had the dream. You started Chapter 0. There were a lot more before Chapter 0. Let's not go too deep into the history or else some cute girl will be confused about her age.
James: Being there and be part of history. ☀️
Ivy: Helping in the background. This is actually a hard question. It gets harder the longer you wait.
Black: Sharing. Share what you know. 📖 Very appropriate.
Arcacia: Do the impossible! 😉 Arcacia speaks! XD That's completely normal! I think that's about everyone I would expect to be around to answer. Going Once...! Going Twice...!
Sylvia: Changing yourself. 🐉 19 XD
StarTree: This post has broken the previous record of 15 as the post with the most participants! Evelyn thanks everyone for participating.

202205092211|Detective Puzzles
May 9, 2022 Monday 10:11 PM
Vacation Island
⭐
Can I make a section for detective puzzles?
Casey: Wouldn't that be part of Puzzles? I think Red wants Puzzle Time to be more family oriented. But detective puzzles have to do mostly with people getting murdered. 😅 That is Evelyn trying to say "How about we don't have that section?" but not knowing how to say it. But we already got a deck of puzzles. We already have boxes of similar puzzles, and you got even more.🤷 But these are shorter and faster. How about we try one first? Okay. This one is about mass murder of hundreds of people across a few states by cyanide poisoning. In the end, the murderer was found to be one person. The puzzle was to guess what the victims had in common, and why and how the murder did it. I hope that we would do more healthy puzzles. I agree. What if someone tries to do that against us? Wouldn't it be better if we know what people can do? You mean this whole deck of cards each have a different way to... 😖 Supposedly. We are talking about Crack the Case (1993). How about we just read all the answers. If the purpose is just to learn the possibilities. But that would be a waste, since we can't play it. I don't think we want to play it. I'm offering to just read all of them, then move on to something we would rather play. Okay. I am reading too. Can't just have Tanya knows everything.

202205112100|Capitalism
May 11, 2022 Wednesday 9:00 PM
Arsenal Court
⭐
What is a good place to present debates and arguments? The Albatross was the designated place, but Seed of Peace is so flowery. The content doesn't seem to fit. If the content won't fit, should it not be presented, or should it be presented elsewhere?
Evelyn: I think Seed of Peace still needs a definition of Capitalism. Hopefully one that is more mainstream. 😅 I don't think there is a peaceful definition of capitalism.
LRRH: Can we start by defining what an unregulated market is? An unregulated market is a market free from control outside the decisions of the market participants, meaning the buyers and sellers. And what is the range of regulations in that context? The regulations include who may participate, the prices, what can be sold, what people may possess, where, when and how a trade may happen. Is it correct that in an unregulated market, a person can sell themself? That is correct.
Zoey: Objection. Why did you say that market regulation includes what people may possess? Possession is different from trading. If market regulation does not include what a person may possess, what do you want to call the type of regulation that defines what a person may possess? Property Laws. Then according to you, an unregulated market could exist within a society with property laws. Correct? Correct. And the said property law could define that a person owns themself as their property, but says nothing about whether they may sell it, correct? Correct. So if the property law does not define a person as a piece of property, then even in an unregulated market it cannot be traded. In an unregulated market, a person can attempt to trade anything, including things they do not possess. It is up to the buyer buying it. It ignores possession laws. What do you mean? It means that if I just write a deed for Magic Bakery, I can try to sell it. And people can try to buy it. Whether Magic Bakery lets the buyer controls it is a separate matter. An unregulated market allows that transaction to take place. It is a buyer be ware situation. A transaction doesn't imply that the buyer will get what they think they will get. ... 🤔 That sounds about right.
Tanya: For example, when Red encounters someone who doesn't realize that an unregulated market implies people can be sold, she could link to Albatross, which will do the explanation. That is the intended use of the Albatross. The question is, can Seed of Peace handle such content? Let me add the definition of unregulated market first. Okay. Done. Back to the question of where could Red link to that would explain what an unregulated market implies. Can you imagine how that content would be okay? You will pick a picture for it? I think I will pick the picture of dirt. Just give me the argument and I will post it. Okay.
Tanya: An unregulated market implies a person may sell themself to a willing buyer. Once that happens, Capitalism allows the owner to control all aspects of the sold person, and gains ownership of everything they make including their offspring. That's what can happen. But now you have to define Capitalism. I would define that to include this meaning: the owner of a means of production owns the product. But this is just one ideology of capitalism. That's enough for what matters.
Evelyn: I have a question: Why are we trying to tell people about this? People should know how dangerous their concepts are when they try to promote it. People who wants capitalism with an unregulated market opens a quick way to slavery. Actually I would think an unregulated is part of the ideologies of capitalism. So capitalism by itself opens a quick way to slavery. You still need the original person who is willing to be a slave. But I see how that can happen so I can't argue against it. That would be part of the ingredients: An unregulated market, and a person willing to sell themself. Since we are talking about childbirth, we are really talking about an unregulated market, plus a woman willing to sell herself given her circumstance. Capitalism only needs these two to start a bloodline of slavery, and it can create the second ingredient with the first because the parents automatically owns their kids in an unregulated market under capitalism. As I said I think capitalism by itself already implies it wants an unregulated market. I think that is optional. And just because capitalism wants it, it doesn't mean that it would get it. An unregulated market only exists when someone fights off all the entities trying to regulate it. But if that condition happens, the rest can happen.
LRRH: Would you say that capitalism needs two ingredients for slavery: An unregulated market, and either someone willing to sell themself, or to claim their child as property? Correct. Then people will counter saying that communism only needs one ingredient for slavery: a regulated market with a rule for slavery. That's not directly communism, that is authoritarianism. It takes more force to have a regulated market than an unregulated market. It is easier to get an unregulated market than an regulated market. To get an unregulated market, all you need to do is to hide. Then there is a question of how a communistic society makes decision to legislate slavery Then you need to define communism. Hold on. Let me define capitalism first. O...K... It sounds really negative. But I think that is what it means. Instead of saying "it is not immoral to...", I think it is better to say "... shall not be forbidden." You might phrase that as a right in capitalism. Okay. We are not done here. Let's continue later.

202205122213|Race to Slavery
May 12, 2022 Thursday 10:13 PM
Ageless Forest
⭐
Yesterday, we got to the concept where we might be able to evaluate an ideology's affinity to slavery. Capitalism only needs two ingredients: an unregulated market, and a person who is willing to sell themself of their child to slavery. What about the others?
Fina: For Stewardism, it takes someone trying to enslave another person for the original slave driver to be enslaved. But as soon as the original slave driver stops, the permit ends. And it doesn't pass from one generation to the next, since a slavery contract is not enforceable.
Tanya: Same in Hospitalianism. Slavery contract is not enforceable because a person is always allowed to leave with their belonging and share of property. What about Arcacianism?
Evelyn: I think helping a slave break free is helping, but helping someone enslave is not. In Arcacianism it is harder to have someone in desperation to sell themself.
Ledia: Then it seems that the ingredients of slavery is the same for all ideologies, the difference is in how likely each ideology can get that ingredient. Correct. Then are we comparing how strongly each ideology forces a person to serve another? Is being ranked at the top good or bad? I think it is more intuitive if good is on top. Then it becomes some type of freedom ranking. I would prefer phrasing that as freedom or peace ranking instead. 🙂 That's something I can easily post at Seed of Peace.
Tanya: In such ranking, would imperialism be at or among the bottom? Let's start with that at the bottom. After defining it. Imperialism is a concept that people exist to serve an emperor, who has a right to rule the world by conquest. I can add that.
Zoey: Let's rank them as we add them. I need to make another page. What should the title be? Peace ranking. Inside that page you could have a section of ranking of ideology. Okay. I ended up adding the ranking in the definition page for now. I'll split it when we have more definitions. Then please add Stewardism to the definition page. Can't have capitalism sitting at the top of the scale.
Tanya: I want to add populism between Stewardism and Capitalism. I define populism as a moral view of deciding based on majority rule. It allows subjugation of the minority, but not the other way around. I would rank it above capitalism because capitalism allows a minority to subjugate a majority. And it is ranked below Stewardism because Stewardism doesn't allow subjugation except is reciprocation. Okay.
Tanya: Next up from populism would be constitution democracy. I would define that as having a type of democracy that asserts some rights as basic human rights, but may also include many instances where majority rule is used without any compensation to the minority. Done.
Tanya: Next I am going to say Hospitalianism is at the top, above Stewardism. I define Hospitalianism as constitutional choice democracy applied in an international setting which uses ongoing contest of hospitality to setting international disputes. I rank it above stewardism because stewardism doesn't have a predictable way to settle disputes. No contest. Done.
Tanya: I can't tell whether Arcacianism is above or below Hospitalianism. We can save that for next time.

202205141503|Ranks vs Tiers
May 14, 2022 Saturday 3:03 PM
Magic Bakery
⭐
Here is the idea: Instead of having a Tier system indicating what quests are important, why don't we list them with a rank system based on how many times it progresses in a month? For example, at the start of a month, every quest resets to rank 0. Then when someone does something for it, it upgrades to rank 1 and so on. We would set a max, such as rank 5.
Evelyn: If I see that I would want every quest to be at least rank 1. 😅 That's up to you.
Tanya: What is the reason for the change? The Tier system looks more authoritarian even if we let people do whatever quest they want. It is like, "How dare you rank my quest lower?" A rank system would just reflect what people work on. I see.
LRRH: We are already half way in May. So every quest that has been updated start at Rank 1 now? @StarTree can you show us the current rank?
StarTree: Here is the result: Level 4: Seed. Level 3: Cardinal. Level 2: Carrot, Concept, Music. Level 1: Arena, Board, Book, Freedom, Guild, Happy, Manga, Paladin, Puzzle, Skill, Treasure, Vacation, Whale, Wish. Level 0: Makeover! Yes, this makes me want to do something for Mira Mira. 😅 Nothing wrong with that. I thought the purpose of the Tier system was so that we can concentrate on important quests. But you can choose to work on the one you believe is the most important. I want to know what the guild decides to be important. 😅 I see.
Tanya: Evelyn, how about if you don't know what is important, you start a quest and ask for input? That was what I was doing when I asked for weekend plans. Okay. I can do that. I thought Tanya would be more against this.
Tanya: I have thought about it and concluded that Arcacianism is more peaceful than Hospitalianism because Hospitalianism is based on Coexistence, while Arcacianism goes a step further to promote Cooperation. Tanya receives I was reminded of that ranking because 3B was posting her manga. She drew about it more than a year ago. So now I am more in favor of having less directedness and having more flexibility. Tanya gives kudos to 3B and Fina for having defined that scale a long time ago.
LRRH: It seems no one is against it, so @StarTree please make the change starting tomorrow? What is the actual heading of each rank level? Can we use "Rank" but start with Rank D up to Rank A? Then if a quest made significant progress it will be awarded Rank S. StarTree's mind is blown! If you do that I would want to update every quest so that every quest gets an A. 😅 I think that is fine. It is Arcacian.
Ledia: So does Mira starts at Rank D? Or is it unranked? I think untouched in the month should be "unranked". StarTree understands!
Sasha: Hold on. If no update is unranked, then it means it takes 80 updates for the 20 quests for all of them to be Rank A. That's almost 3 updates per day. Yes. Rank A would be weekly update on average. So weekly update for all quests. But an update can be a small one.
Casey: Sasha, what are you worrying about? You don't even have a quest. But if you say I can get an A then it makes me want to have a quest and get an A. Arcacianism at work XD 🤣
LRRH: @StarTree, could you apply the ranking starting today. I think it is better if we see it now while we are discussing. Do we still care how long ago a quest was last updated? Am I listing them in alphabetical order in each rank? Let's start with alphabetical order. It's done! I think it looks better than before. I think it looks more like a quest board now. 😊 Actually I never look at that because it wasn't really readable. I just look at the google sheet XD Now it looks better.
Evelyn: I wish that the quests on the list are clickable, so that when I click on it I can see what was last updated. And it's done! Whoa, I was going to say you can't sort links because the anchor goes before the actual text. StarTree cheated by adding a comment tag in front! Clever. 👍
202205141757|Absentee Passenger
May 14, 2022 Saturday 5:57 PM
Puzzle Time
⭐
Why did an airline add a fictitious name to its passenger list?
Evelyn: I hope this is not about cheating insurance of tax. 😅 It is for a good cause! 44: Is it for the Air Marshall? Tanya: Did they do it to protect someone, as a decoy? The name is not of anyone famous. It is a fictitious name that doesn't correspond to a real person people might know. Ledia: Could it be that if an airline is shown to be too empty, it might invite people to hijack? But why would you need an actual name? Who would see the passenger list? Amelia: Only one name? So they cannot over book? It was done intentionally to fight a crime, you all are very close! Yes, just one name. So the fictitious name IS for a criminal to see? Yes, what would the criminal do after seeing that name? 44: Claiming to be that person? Not exactly. 44: Steal personal information? You're almost there! What information are they stealing? What do they steal it for? 44: Sell fake tickets? Natalie: Their address! Burglarize their house while they are away! It was a sting operation in respond to a series of burglaries! All the victims flew the same airline, where a worker tipped a burglar on which address might be gone for vacation! Congratulations!
202205141839|Row to Glory
May 14, 2022 Saturday 6:39 PM
Puzzle Time
⭐
To determine who may inherit the kingdom, an ancient Irish king told his two sons to row their boat a mile from shore. The one who touched shore first would inherit the kingdom. When the elder son was closing in, the younger son was still 20 yards behind. How did the younger son win in the end?
Zoey: If I were me I would just shoot an arrow! XD I meant at the shore. In case it wasn't obvious. Ledia: Did he swim the rest of the way? You two are thinking too nice! Zoey: Too nice...? He didn't shoot the brother did he? XD The young brother did not hurt the elder one! But it didn't end without bloodshed! Evelyn: ✋😖 Congratulations! That's the correct answer! Metal. Wait, what? WHAT...?! The younger brother used a sword so his hand could touch first! Can't fact check this. It is unconfirmed. Possible legend.
202205141926|Atomic Habits
May 14, 2022 Saturday 7:26 PM
Magic Bakery
⭐
What quest do you want to work on? Do you need help?
LRRH: Guild quest only has 6 quests left. But they are all hard. I think "add bakery item to HQ post" would be the easiest. Should we change the picture to that of a pretzel? Okay. Let's do that first. Now HQ can be part of the menu items. 😊
🕰️ [8:15 PM] 🕰️
Ivy: For @Skill, let's add concepts from Atomic Habit. {"cmd":"url","url":"https://www.youtube.com/embed/PZ7lDrwYdZc","desc":"📺"}
Ivy: For those who watched it, what skills are there?
Ledia: Habit building?
Ivy: What archetype should it be? Would that be Cleric? When someone trying to break a bad habit, it would be like de-cursing.
Sasha: Habit building should be a Magician skill. Magician skills due to desires, cues, attraction, and rewards.
Ivy: I agree with Sasha more. I think it should be a Magician skill. Ledia and Sasha are recognized to have the skill Identify Skill.
Zoey: I feel that we are applying some of the concepts in Atomic Habit already. For example, our quest list is a cue. We are starting small, so even a small action counts for updating a quest. The way we use quests match the concept of defining systems instead of goals.
Ivy: In that case, what habit do we want to build? What habit do we want to break?
Zoey: Too much social media XD Especially during the week.
Ivy: What is the current habit?
LRRH: We go to Twitter as part of daily morning news routine. If someone replies, we tend to respond. It is not clear whether responding makes any difference. But we are trying to make a difference, that's why we are building Albatross. So I think going to Twitter is a good habit, but doing it for too long is a bad habit.
Ivy: Okay. Let's focus on breaking the habit of going to Twitter for too long. Is it fair to say that if you need to refresh, then you have been on it for too long? That seems fair. What habit should it trigger when you need to refresh? How about copying a bookmark from Twitter back to Guild ? The bookmark list should be empty, because those are all links that should be processed in the guild. In that case, why not switch the order? Start Twitter by importing the bookmarks. If you are done importing the bookmarks from the previous day, then you browse. And in order to promote Albatross, you don't reply unless there is an Albatross link. I am applying "make it difficult" And if you don't follow the habit, Sasha may nuke you and you are KO'ed for the day. I like the KO part. Ok. Sasha is recognized as the enforcer. I enlist @StarTree to monitor when Red is nukable. Roger!
StarTree: Special Announcement: The guild is only two quest away from having updated ALL quests within a week! Let's do it! I like how StarTree makes these announcements when we are very close to an achievement. This should be a magician skill: Achievement Identification. And StarTree has it. StarTree is now recognized to have the Magician Skill Achievement Identification. Wee~~!

Magic Bakery

This is for the Wish Quest. What reward do you want for your good work?
Evelyn: I think good work is self-rewarding because having done a good work means people are happy and not worrying.
Sasha: When people have nothing to worry about then we can play. But I want to somehow say play should be part of the routine. There should be some benefits to playing, maybe it keeps people's mind open? Sasha, you made that wish already? You wished that we would make a family friendly detective game section. Yes I know. But no one is going to participate because it is a campaign.
Black: How about making it an efficiency habit detective? If we are not going to deal with actual crimes, and want to solve some relevant puzzles, then daily efficiency would be the puzzle we need to solve. We just need to make something 1% better each time. It is a promising idea. Black is recognized for the Alchemist Skill Kaizen.

We are missing a step to check for the latest document. So you want an algorithm upgrade. Or you could call that a protocol. 202205150912|Buddy System
May 15, 2022 Sunday 9:12 AM
Magic Bakery
⭐
Yesterday I dreamed of a puzzle regarding an efficiency tradeoff between the number of people doing a task, the time it takes for them to complete it, and the risk of error. The three attributes depend on many factor such as abilities of the people involved and difficulty of the task. It is an Oracle class problem. But this is the puzzle:
Tanya: If a person working on a task alone can finish it task in 2 days, and working with a buddy can finish in 1.5 days. At what error rate should the task be assigned to two people?
Zoey: Very interesting what kind of things Tanya dreams about. Arcacia was the one asking that in the dream. Tanya receives Arcacian Quest received XD
Ledia: When a person makes an error, does it create another task to be solved? For now let's say yes. An error creates another task that needs 2 days to "solve" alone. This sounds entirely a math problem. It is, so there should be definite answers we may use as a rough guideline. If the error rate is 1 then the task would never end XD What if the error chance is 50%? Then... it adds up to ... 4 days to actually finish a task on average. If you assign the task to two people, is the error rate the same or better? You would think that it is less likely to have an error. Let's say the error rate will reduce to 25% of normal. So: 12.5%. Then it takes about 1.7 days to complete. It pays to not make mistakes. But we are not comparing 4 days to 1.7 days, because if you assign two people to the same task, you are forgoing having them working on two tasks. So we are comparing 4 days versus 3.4 days. Which is still better. Correct.
Ivy: For those interested in the math, it is infinite geometric series. I see. So... are we done with this puzzle? Almost. According to this math, there are only three important variables: 1) T = Time multiplier of session completion with a buddy, and; 2) S = The error rate when solo. 3) B = The multiplier in error rate with a buddy. So I want to see a graph showing when having a buddy is better. The graph will be 3D. ...
Ledia: Then I have a question: Suppose having a buddy does not reduce the session time, at what error rates would it still make sense to have a buddy? Hey, this sounds like the game mechanics of Robinson Crusoe! Then we are getting the educational benefit without playing the game. What if it has more interesting lessons? Then we can find out by solving real puzzles. Compelling reasoning. Okay. Magic Bakery Detectives: Solving puzzles in the game of real life.
Tanya: The condition equation is when having a buddy takes less over all time than soloing: 2T/(1-SB) < 1/(1-S). Solving for B gives: B < (1+2TS-2T)/S. When T=1, it becomes B < 2 - (1/S). This means if the solo error rate is not more than 0.5, it won't make sense to assign a buddy if it won't make each session shorter. So if your skill is Rank B or above for the task, then you could solo it. Or to say it the other way around: If having someone tag along won't help you complete the task earlier, then you are at least Rank B at the task. If you want to derive the skill level of a person, you just need to check their error rate. Error rate of 0.5 means Rank B. 0.25 means Rank A.
Evelyn: What is our error rates? The error rate can be calculated based on the amount of time we think we can finish a task Ts, and the total time we actually take to finish the task Tt = Ts/(1-S). For example, if we think we can finish in 0.5 days, but we actually takes 2 days, then our error rate is S = (Tt-Ts)/Tt = 0.75. We are rank C (?) 😖 If we want to know our proficiency, we need to start making prediction of when we would be done for each task. Proficiency P = Ts / Tt.
Sasha: Suppose we are Rank C, then it would worth having a buddy as long as we could reduce errors by 1/3, even if it takes the same time. Correct. But more precisely: B < (1+2TS-2T)/S. When S=0.75, B < (4-2T)/3
Sasha: But is this math helpful? Say if you are stuck at a task, you can always ask for help first. People who can't help could simply not reply. Then, as long as you ask for help early, you don't need to know what the statistics are. You should already be doing the best you can. I agree with Sasha that asking first is generally good. We have cases where it saves us a lot of time. Heuristics over planning.
⭐
Sasha: If you want to tackle the task assignment problem from the view of figuring out how many people you need to assign, there will be too many variables you don't know. Instead, you could have broken it into small pieces and let everyone work on it at once. And call for help or release your task for other to do when you are stuck. Our focus should not be in predicting how long it takes to do quests, but to help each other becoming more proficient at whatever quest they choose to do. If we all become more proficient, then we don't care about complicated math on how to assign teams. We could all solo the quests. Sasha receives Word. I think the heuristic and the math are both important. The math will help us adjust the heuristics if we have the data.
Ledia: But is this our general strategy? That we want to focus on identifying where we lack proficiency and try to improve? Atomic Habits did say we should be content even if we assign the "right number" of people for a quest and the quest is completed as expected. It postulates that if we focus on getting better, we will get quests completed. If the math is not helpful, should we end this and switch to another quest? I think this quest helped us confirm our strategy of completing quests: we should aim to skill up so we can solo the quests, but at the same time we break down quests into soloable pieces. And we are Rank A at a task if we complete it leaving no spawn point unsealed. Actually that would be Rank S. You can still make 25% error at Rank A. Sorry about my math. 😖 I'll keep the math in mind. Discussion is adjourned. Let's move on to other quests.
🔑
Sasha: In conclusion, we started with a question on when more people are needed for a quest, and ended up with a solution of always breaking a quest into soloable pieces and help each other solo. By focusing on ourselves becoming more proficient, we could eliminate the overhead of managing or predicting who shall do what. The Magic Bakery Detectives conclude.
202205151524|Disappointments
May 15, 2022 Sunday 3:24 PM
Carrot Farm
⭐
How do you deal with disappointments? I am asking this as part of the Cooking with Arcacia training at chapter 11. This is part of step 2 in the recipe.
Evelyn: I think in most cases, I could still try again if I fail. So my method would be (e) I try to improve until I am satisfied.
Sasha: For puzzles, if you fail you can't try again because you already know the answer. In those cases I would do (b). I learn why I didn't get it before move on to the next one, so that I don't waste the next puzzle.
Zoey: Lowering expectation XD But it is not exactly (a) because I am not talking about setting a lower expectation for the next target. I'm talking about when I am disappointed, I convince myself that my expectation should be lower. ... Unless I knew I could do better then I try again immediately.
Tanya: I would add (f) I correct my error and try again. If I am disappointed, it is because there is something I knew I could do but did not do. Or it was just bad luck? I don't feel disappointed if it was just due to luck. Hmm... 🤔 Then what do you feel instead? Nothing. I guess I am mostly (f) also.
Ledia: I don't really remember the last time I feel disappointed. To me, if the result is bad, it just tells you what your proficiency is. If it is a skill I want to upgrade, then I will do what it takes to upgrade it regardless. I guess I will add (g) I don't get disappointed.
Casey: It might sound irresponsible but I would just move on and do something else (d). Changes are the next time I need to do it again I would have learned how to do it better while I was doing something else. So I don't worry about specifically trying to get good at something right away. Just work on what you are good at first and come back to it. Take it easy. Easy Casey. 🤷
Evelyn: Does P4 want to answer?
P4: I think I'm like Ledia. I don't normally feel disappointed. I wonder if that is a Paladin attitude.
Natalie: I am disappointed all the time! 😃 I just learn and do better next time! High five! ✋ ✋
Sylvia: What about making changes so you still win with your ability? What does that mean? Say you can't hit the target from where you are. Instead of keep trying where you are, just move closer or get other advantages. Use wits to your advantage. I added (h) for you. Hmm... Didn't Atomic Habit say the opposite? 🤔 It didn't say a person shouldn't be goal oriented. It only postulates that a person who is system oriented may still succeed. Foul! Ivy didn't respond to the original question. There's no rule saying I must response to the prompt. But then you are off-topic! I was responding to Zoey's question. By bad then! XD (a). Your calling foul goes off-topic more than my response to Zoey. I think we all agree that we should stay on topic. If someone is off topic we could just ask them, like "Ivy, would you like to share what you do when you are disappointed?" Evelyn is now recognized for applying Cleric skill Protect. Evelyn receives Ivy receives 👏 Were you trolling me? It was unplanned. I just point out what I think is wrong because it I don't say it as it happen I'll forget about it. Is it too off topic if I ask what proficient Evelyn's Cleric Protect is? Let's finish this training session first while we are at it. 😅 Hold on! Completely on-topic: @Ivy, why did you say (a)? You didn't just pick the first one did you? XD
Ivy: I picked (a) because that is part of my adjustment process. If I am disappointed them my expectation of myself is too high. So I lower it for next time. Thank you for confirming. Evelyn thanks everyone for sharing.
LRRH: I got one more new answer!
LRRH: When I am disappointed, I award myself with a new recognition. One time I was trying to explain something to someone, but they say I was spamming. So I awarded myself the Create Spam skill. Fact checked! I saw that one! XD LRRH is recognized for Magician Skill "If it ain't fun, change it." LRRH adds "(h) I make the situation funny and rewarding." We started with 5 methods and now we have 9. It is fun and rewarding training together. 😊

202205180804|Left of Way
May 18, 2022 Wednesday 8:04 AM
Rome
⭐
How did an archaeologist, while in Britain, deduce that the Romans drove their chariots on the left side of the road?
Tanya: Was there a law against driving on the right side of the road? Casey: Did the chariots have a passenger seat or a "door" on the left side? Evelyn: Were street signs and markings on the left side of the roads? Zoey: Was there a painting or sculpture about it? Sasha: Did the Romans build some of the roads in Britain? No document was involved, the archaeologist examined physical evidence (but not actual chariots or its parts) from an archaeological site in Britain. What was the function of the site? Sasha: Was the site a toll booth? It was not! Here is a hint: The archaeologist could deduce which side of the road chariots used to enter versus exit the site by examining the road! P4: Was is where chariots were born? Baby chariots all come out from the same side? Cute!! 😍 P4 is recognized to be Super Cute. Chariots entered and exited the site, but their tracks were different! What was the difference? What was the site? Zoey: It was a site where people go drinking, they left wobbly! XD Hint: The site was somewhat remote from towns. It was similar to Fort Arsenal. Casey: Did they make cannons there? On the side leaving they were dragging cannons out? That's extremely close, it was about the weight, but not weapons! Sylvia: It was a mine. Sylvia receives Congratulations! It was a quarry! Chariots exiting were heavier and left deeper tracks! Fact checked! XD

202205190916|Best Note-taking
May 19, 2022 Thursday 9:16 AM
Harbor
⭐
Do you take notes? How does it help you? How do you do it? I am asking this as part of @Whale to share effective note-taking ideas.
Black: The method I want to share is our field troubleshooting notes. It is a huge document that contains countermeasures and information on many situations. It currently has five section: Alarms, Features, References, Tasks, Special. In the alarm section, topics are listed alphabetically by alarm codes. Under each are the countermeasures and history of occurrence.
Black: While that organization is convenient for computer issues because the computer will give an alarm code, in real life there is no alarm code. If real life gives an alarm code then I can apply it to real life. I think if we simply start making a real life troubleshooting document, we could organize it into categories as we go.
Ivy: I think part of the problem is that since life doesn't give alarm codes, you won't even know if something is wrong and needs to be addressed. You need some kind of diagnostics sequence. It could be a checklist of situations that should be alarming.
Casey: If something is not working, people will complain. You don't need to go out of the way to check. For example, we just fixed the air conditioner. If I were to categorize it, it would be under Appliances or AC. So for a real life document, since there are no actual alarm code, we would only have the Features section where you group the issues under topics.
Black: Should Harbor host such troubleshooting information for the guild? Should it be a document or just html?
Zoey: I still want my side panel where I could just click click click. XD
Ivy: I told you earlier that you could just use Word in web view, then you could shrink it to the size you want, and you could edit as you use it. For the same document, you could open it twice using New Window. You can have a small one on the side and a big one as the main. But it is difficult to make links in a Word document. It's not. It is just a bookmark.
Black: So, should we start with a document for the guild? Yes. Because there will be information you can't post online. But you'll need that information if you were to use it. Then how do you share it? The part that you share would be a subset. Couldn't we just password protect it or use a secret menu? No one needs to know that information. That is just junk for a visitor. It is junk for them, not for us. They don't have to click on it. This is about anonymity. If you want to talk about it, please make a new topic.
Ledia: I've an idea, for local information that matters, we could just keep it physically at a local place. You mean like making post-it next to the AC? Hmm... yes, or with permanent marker? Then if you want to check something you need to physically go to places and open closets. I think just having a central document with everything is better. Then you can see and check everything without moving. You're right.
Black: If we make such document, what should it be called? Master Notes, or just "Master". And put it at the root of the directory. Then when something breaks you can say "Go check the Master" XD
Casey: Right now since we already put appliance receipts in a folder, the notes about them are in the same folder. If we make a master, do we delete the notes in the folders? This is like Ledia's idea, but virtual.
Evelyn: I think each person has a difference preference on how they take notes. So the person who wants everything in a Master could copy information elsewhere there, without deleting the file in the folder.
Sasha: I feel like at this point you are trying to improve something that does not have a demand for it to be improved. You don't have the volume of issues you need to deal with to justify a change.
Tanya: But it bothers me that there is no Master document at the root of our local directory explaining where things are or how the directory is organized. That I agree.
LRRH: @StarTree, please make a Master document at the root and add an @Guild task to gather and merge information from other documents scattered about.
StarTree: It's done! Now the root has two documents: Master, which is a copy of "Info" in a subdirectory, and "Dashboard" which is a spreadsheet that no one really uses! We replaced using Dashboard because we have Google Sheet Guild Log. Then should Master be a Google Doc? Google Doc viewing options are horrible. But it is online, and I don't think we need to view it that often. But bookmarking is easier on Google Doc.
Sasha: I oppose to moving data around in general. I think it is okay to share our preferences an experience on how to take notes. But if you start talking about "how we shall standardize note taking for the guild", then it is a waste of time. Just let each person who will use notes choose and take their own notes. But if you want your notes to help someone else, wouldn't you want to do it in a way that is convenient to others? XD
Tanya: @StarTree, how much space do we have at clouds? We used 1 out of 15 GB at Google Drive and 2 out of 5 GB at OneDrive! The size of our local guild data is more than 200 GB. You aren't removing any process by moving some of it online. I see.
Black: I think that is enough exploring today on this topic. I think if the information can benefit others, my preference is to share it here. If for some reason it cannot be shared, then the person with the information decides where to put it. I think in that case it would be helpful enough for them to leave a message here (blogger) on where they put that. Then, the first point of information seeking could be here at blogger, so that we could aim to share information helpful for others. I agree with that conclusion. I want to see this site becoming more organized and helpful, not just to us but to others. Black thanks the participants for their valuable feedbacks.
