202206050816|Abbreviations
June 5, 2022 Sunday 8:16 AM
Court Arsenal
⭐
This week we adopted abbreviation to replace the alphanumeric code we used for field logging. Previously, Tanya introduced the enumeration code of a letter followed by two digits. B was for Boss fights, and S was for spawn points. But as time went on, no one could remember the code. Looking it up was annoying and took time. So we changed the code for field logging to a mostly two letter abbreviation. For example, Twinkle Light Tavern is abbreviated to LT instead of I-don't-remember-what-it-was.
Evelyn: Yes I think it is easier with abbreviations.
Tanya: I was more concerned that we would miscount and miss a spawn point. So they were numbered sequentially. And that was before we gave a name to each of them.
Tanya: And there is another purely numeric code that we need to assgin in the registry. The alphanumic code has a direct translation to that code.
Zoey: But in practice, virtually no one needs to know that numeric code. For logging we need to location code, that we use every day many times. As far as I understand, the numeric code in the registry has no significance other than listing the spawn points in an order. I think we shouldn't even record them anywhere else, so that if someone decides to arrange the order at the registry, they could just do that and nothing else needs to be changed. I see. Although I have already started another numeric convention for the registry. Code 1000s are for spawn points that need crafting materials to seal. Code 2000s are for those that only require magic spells. Code 3000s are those that required neither, or only procedures and instructions. I did that so when we list them on our field map, they can be listed in the approximate order of when their sub quests should be completed. You won't need to individually filter which spawn points have material gathering subquests. That does sound convenient, but if someone tries to change the order at the registry, they would have to change the code on our field map. I think we almost never change the registry code. An alternative is to just ad a sorting code on the field map that is unrelated to the registry code. Then you can sort the field map all you want, and other people can change the registry order all they want. I'll consider it. But it is a little unsettling when there is no unique code specifying which field map item corresponds to which registry item. I think it is not too hard to tell from the text title. And in the field map, we have scroll codes, those are unique and we can't change those. That is the cross-checking you could use to avoid errors. I see.
Zoey: I do like the 1000s 2000s 3000s numbering convention. I think it will look more organized. I see.
Sasha: And so the team will probably make further tweaks looking for ways that are easy to remember, easy to sort, and easy to change. Will the team find another breakthrough? We shall see when that happens! Sasha thanks the team for sharing experience on making it easier.

June 5, 2022 Sunday 9:18 AM
Owl Forest
⭐
Zoey arrives at the Owl Forest for Cardinal Quest. Is it too early? XD
Zoey: I thought we were done here. What do we need to do?
StarTree: There is supposed to be an owl registry list, but it is missing! I'll look for it. XD
Cardinal: With her masterful elemental trace skill, Zoey found the lost registry in no time. Zoey, you are are already Rank S in elemental trace. Otherwise, I would ask you to roll for skill upgrade. I maxed out. Maybe I should have let someone else try. XD Zoey has completed 2 quest hours. The blue text for effects is too bright, could we make it darker? What color would you like? Actually, why aren't we using the complete tag, which will be green? XD Good catch. 👍 For the blue, please try darkblue. Zoey is recognized for Herald Skill Keen Eye. Owl Forest subquest is completed for today. Next. XD
202206051005|Serpent Cave
June 5, 2022 Sunday 10:05 AM
Serpent Cave
⭐
Zoey teleports to the Serpent Cave, the location for the second set of subquests for today.
Zoey: What is the quest here?
StarTree: The Serpent is a new monster type to the heroes party. The party needs scouting and possibly a trial fight. Tanya allocated 14 quest hours for this quest due to unknowns. It sounds too hard to solo. XD The subquests are all meant to be soloable! But you said Twinkle Light Tavern was soloable, we almost got a TPK. XD According to Tanya, all subquests are soloable! I want to see Tanya solo it! XD
Zoey: For the Serpent I will do some scouting. But before that I check the records. The Serpent is new to us, but not to the region. There should be information in the archive.
Cardinal: Zoey found monster type information about the Serpent but not exactly for this location. Zoey also found information that seemed relevant to the caves around the backup barrier generator. I add notes for the backup barrier quest, and make copies of the Serpent attack pattern. This is going to be intense XD Zoey completes the subquest to obtain Serpent attack pattern.
Evelyn: Evelyn checks in for quests.
Ledia: StarTree, status report please.
StarTree: Zoey completed 4 quest hours this morning! With 236 hours remaining, the sealing date is still July 16!
Ledia: Then should we do Geyser?
Zoey: I didn't finish Serpent Cave, it still has 12 hours remaining XD
Ledia: What is left for Serpent Cave that we can complete today? I prefer to do a subquest where we could complete within today and reduce the quest hours.
Zoey: I can't promise we can knock off any quest hour, but I am about to physically scout the Serpent Caves. I got clues about where magic circles were set in another area. Now I need to scout and look for those place in our caves.
Ledia: If we succeed, how many hours can we complete?
Zoey: Finding where to set the magic circles is worth 2 hours. Complete the magic circles is another 4 hours.
Evelyn: Is it important for the magic circles to be set at equivalent locations? I thought they can be set anywhere within the aura of the area crystal.
Zoey: Zoey shares the six old locations she found earlier.
Evelyn: I think the first two are fixed. The last two are arbitrary. I am not sure about the middle two. I add the two arbitrary locations and confirm that our area crystal understands the two fixed locations.
Cardinal: Evelyn opens a giant spell book. Is this going to take a while? It should be quick. 😅
Cardinal: Evelyn, roll with advantage please. Hold on. I got these dice. I hope it makes the game simpler. Are these FATE dice? They are not. They are Caledea dice. That game is so ugly I don't feel bad taking its dice I hope no one is offended. 😅 These dice only have one plus and one minus on each die. Wouldn't that lower our chances? I thought about it. Just roll a bunch of them at once and ignore the blanks. If you are right handed, just roll toward your left, and count the ones with plus or minues from the your left. Okay I'll try. So I grab... 7 dice and roll... Evelyn: Two Plus and Five Blanks. Is that good? 😅 That is a succeed even without advantage.
Cardinal: Evelyn flipped through thousands of pages but quickly and skillfully confirmed the first two locations are fixed relative to the crystal. That leaves 2 locations uncertain to the heroes. Actually this is kind of fun XD I think I just like rolling a bunch of dice! I like how the colors are bright and happy, and it is easy to see the result... since there are no numbers. 😊 But you can't cry "Natural 20!" anymore. Cardinal doesn't have the concept of natural 20. If you roll and bunch of dice and the first two are plus, that is 1/36 chance. That's more rare than a natural 20.That's what Evelyn just did. Yes, that's pretty hard! I haven't gotten one yet XD You don't have to try it. I already told you the probability. You didn't tell me what my luck is... OMG Evelyn GOT ESP! XD 🤣 Evelyn is now recognized to have Scroll Reading skill of Rank B. Evelyn redeemed herself XD
Tanya: I think it is within our ability to scout the caves and confirm or set the two remaining magic circle locations. Without further delay, I'm going to expand this sister crystal and study it.
Tanya: Zoey, could you get the raw form of the sister crystal so we can compare?
Zoey: Yes but no. I maxed out on my elemental trace skill, if I keep doing it, no one else can level up XD Our priority should be efficiency.
Ledia: How about you show us once, then we know how to do it?
Zoey: It is not that hard. Here it goes XD
Cardinal: Zoey skillfully identitied the raw crystal, and discovered a guardian crystal of the sister crystal that might also have important clues. That was a pretty complicated process. It is pretty easy conceptually XD but there are also some details here and there... Zoey, you are Rank S at this skill. You internalized many details and do it as second nature. It is not easy for someone who hasn't done it. It is Alien, to be honest. I guess not... But if I am the only one doing it, they we aren't really trying to solo anything are we? A subquest being soloable doesn't mean it is efficient for anyone to randomly solo any quest. It is more efficient if each person solo the part they are good at then hand the next part to someone else. But in case no one is available, the subquest is still soloable if the quester takes the time to learn what is going on. That would just take longer. I kind of understand what you mean, but somehow it is not satisfying. We have different strengths, we could all solo the part we are good at. We don't need to team up. That was the conclusion from the "Buddy System" post. Which you can find at Magic Detective. But that is somehow not satisfying.
Tanya: I came because I am familiar with this part. I am going to show the differences so we can mark the changes we need for our magic circles. Marking is not difficult. Judging might be more involved. Illuminate Crystal.
Cardinal: Tanya illuminated the crystals, projecting 241 differences surrounding the party. Are we marking all of them or only the ones related the Serpent? I suggest marking all of them in the first round, then check what are relevant in the second round.
Cardinal: Tanya, Zoey, and Evelyn started marking the difference. I will join. Since nothing else is going on...
Cardinal: Ledia and P4 join marking the difference. It will sure go faster if we all do it together. 😊
Sasha: I don't understand. Aren't you trying to find just two locations? Why do you need to mark everything? I bring up the parts of the scroll related to the locations in question.
Cardinal: What does Sasha conclude?
Sasha: I see nothing suggesting that they aren't arbitrary. I add them to the location list to clear those two quest hours. Remaining Quest Hours is reduced to 234.
Ledia: Um... do we stop?
Tanya: No. Please continue marking all of them. We are doing this to prevent mistakes when we transplant relevant changes to our crystal.
Ledia: Sasha, is what we are doing necessary? Got any Clever shortcuts?
Sasha: I don't know if it is necessary, but when we judge whether a change is relevant, we need to put our conclusion somewhere. The way they are marked now makes it easier to put those comments.
Cardinal: Sasha joins the team effort.
Casey: Might as well.
Cardinal: Casey joins the team effort. 😊
Sasha: I know it is almost over, but I have an efficiency idea: If you see a cluster of changes that are close together, you could just mark the whole cluster together. Because chances are they serve related function, so if we don't need them, we don't need the whole group.
Tanya: It might be okay for this comparison, but please avoid doing that in general.
Sasha: Why not?
Tanya: When the raw crystal spell is upgraded, it is easier to merge the changes if we only mark exactly what changed. It is finished. The heroes obtained Marked Sister Crystal.
Tanya: Now let's mark the Sister Crystal's Guardian Crystal. Is everyone still good? It is not too much work when it is spread out. 😊 It is just a little weird that right after Tanya's speech about soloing and seemingly trying to suggest that we should all get out of the room and leave her alone, we were actually working together. XD I didn't ask anyone to leave. But your mood was so hostile and repulsive XD I'm sorry. I didn't mean it that way. I know XD We did this together for almost every crystal. Of course I know. XD Well, that explains the strange dynamic. I seriously thought Tanya wanted us out, but Zoey and Evelyn stayed without a word. I'm sorry. But here is the second crystal. Illuminate.
🕰️ [8:44 PM] 🕰️
Cardinal: Illumination shows 7 differences between the Guardian Crystal and its raw form. 7 XD This feels like a joke!
Cardinal: The heroes finished marking it in no time.
Tanya: Let's judge the guardian crystal first. Its changes are probably irrelevant. Then we don't have to worry about it. We will all look at it at once. If you can tell what it is for then say it.
Ledia: The first one who understands get the EXP?
Tanya: Sure. Here is the first one.
🛎️
Zoey: It is related! Not to the Serpent, but to the Barrier Backup Generator!
Tanya: I agree. Here is the second.
Zoey: I can't tell 🏳️ XD
Ledia: How do we tell if it is related?
Tanya: We don't have a procedure.
Evelyn: I read the change comments but it was not obvious. 😖
Sasha: How about we look at all of them to see if there is any pattern?
🛎️
Sasha: The last change is related to Barrier Backup Generator!
Tanya: I agree.
🛎️
Evelyn: I think the remaining changes are for shutting down leylines to the cave when the Serpent is detected. Evelyn receives
Tanya: I disagree. I think they are also for the Backup Generator. Here is the evidence.
Cardinal: Tanya shows sage comments about the Backup Generator in Elvish. I'm sorry. 😖 It's okay. Apparantly the sister crystal has two more hovering barriers, which we don't have.
Ledia: I thought we don't need any magic circle for the gravity guards.
Tanya: And now we see seeming contradicting evidence. We need to investigate.
🛎️
Sasha: There is no evidence that we need magic circles. If we disregard the other two floating objects we don't have, the remaining changes in the guardian crystal add two leylines to keep something afloat. Our Backup generator is based on another area that is more similar. If we want to check, we should check that crystal, not this one. So I would conclude that none of these changes are relevant or are good references for us.
Tanya: Zoey, could you identify what area our backup generator was based on?
Zoey: Found it XD. That area uses a standard guardian crystal.
Tanya: We should probe further to confirm that its main crystal does not need changes. Otherwise I will just add 4 quest hours for a subquest to confirm later.
Zoey: I say let's do it. XD I am about OOM for this type of task. I don't think I can help much but I'll stay. I think in that case it is better to just full rest. There is nothing urgent going on. Even if we find something you can do, you can do it tomorrow. Alright then. Tanya thanks all for helping.
🕰️ [9:57 PM] 🕰️
Tanya: We checked the history and trials of the fhird sister crystal. We found no evidence that there is a magic circle for the backup generator.
Tanya: This means we don't need to modify our guardian crystal. The next step for Serpent Cave is to judge relevant changes in its main crystal.

202206072017|From scratch
June 7, 2022 Tuesday 8:17 PM
Serpent Cave
⭐
The trials for Serpent Cave has been taking longer than anticipated. We are essentially stuck. We checked all the changes but there is still about 1/3 that we did not understand. And for the part that we understood, the existing spell seems convoluted
Ledia: So I propose we make our own streamlined spell. Unless someone has a better idea.
Tanya: I believe we only need to add some key symbols. The overall spell will still be the same unless we try to be very different, which I don't recommend.
Ledia: I just want to make sure that everything we add make sense to us, so that when someone goes wrong at least we know what we did. I don't feel I understand what is going on if I just try to copy the old spell. I see.
Ledia: So do I get the bless from the group to go forward?
Tanya: You have already done the spell crafting for the Tavern. It seems more efficient for the same person to craft the whole thing. You have my blessing, please proceed. Thank you.
Ledia: I will explain all the details in the field Master, so if you want to check my design you could check it there. Thank you.

202208141928|Puzzle Plaza

 202206090900|👑 Truth, Lies, & Dice {"cmd":"PIN2","node":"202206090900","music":""}
Your adventuring party stumbles upon a tea party of three goddesses. Truth always tells the truth. Lies always lies. Dice tells the truth or lies randomly. The goddesses challenge you to identify who is who by asking only three Yes or No questions. You may direct each question to any single one of them. What should you ask?
🎨 Art Request
Cover Picture: Please show Vivi, Arcacia, and Patricia at a round tea table. This picture can be used for presenting the puzzle and for @Puzzle title page.

202206102234|Hall of Zombies
June 10, 2022 Friday 10:34 PM
Arx Catacomb
⭐
There is an interesting idea going on at Arx Catacomb, can we make it happen?
Sasha: By my conscious decision, @Detectives does not keep a todo list. Every week or so I try to report what issue we solved or look for something with an issue.
Sasha: This week I see Tanya tried to update Arx Catacomb. There is a really interesting idea there, representing each logical fallacy has zombies, and imagining the viewer is going through a dungeon.
Sasha: But I imagine that it will be really hard to pull off. Unless we have actually played it.
Sasha: So I propose that we actually take some time to design that game and play it. And by doing so, we could review some concepts we learned from reading FATE Accelerated and FATE Core. What do you think?
Tanya: I am glad that you like the idea. Help is welcomed and requested. I am stuck trying to present it as a story. I was trying to avoid simply listing the fallacies because it would be too dry. But it was still too dry when I tried to present it as a story.
Sasha: I think you should first make a dry list, then we can discuss what zombie or monster the fallacy should be. Since the list has 24 fallacies, if they are all zombies might be too repetitive.
Sasha: So I propose these steps:
Sasha: 1) Make a section in Arx where you just list all the fallacies with a link to what they mean. Then...
Sasha: 2) We'll discuss what zombie/monster they are.
Sasha: 3) When we have a good number of them defined, we will start an actual game going through the dungeon.
Zoey: Question: If we all understand the fallacies, then we are already trained. Then in the game, we won't be using the fallacies, how do we give examples?
Tanya: There are plenty of examples if we go online.
Zoey: I'm fine if if we don't have to use fallacies just for demonstration XD
Sasha: Someone is really confident.
Evelyn: On the other hand I can't gaurantee. 😖 May I see the list of fallacies?
Zoey: We are talking about this list of 24 logic fallacies.
Sasha: Timeout. Let's list all of them in Arx first, before continuing the discussion.
Evelyn: Okay 😊
🕰️ [11:44 PM] 🕰️
Sasha: Step 1 completed. Sasha is recognized for the Magician Skill Timeout. I set it to Magician. Not trying to hijack your thread. Please continue. Congratulations to me!
Sasha: Any idea on the monsters?
Zoey: I see Special Pleading as a ghost type monster. They keep dodging, it is hard to hit. I agree. I'm adding that note as comments in the html code for now because other it will look super messy without proper formatting. I think you can show it in parentheses. It is easier when we think about it after the discussion. Good call.
Ledia: Then Loaded Question should be Jack-o'-Lantern that can explode. Super appropriate! I'm really liking this theme. It can be our Halloween Special. XD It sounds really fun. 😊
Tanya: Then can Strawman be a Shadow Reaper? Instead of attacking the hero, it can deal damage by attacking the hero's shadow. Sounds like a Boss. I like it. 😁
Sasha: I see we lost the original zombie. How about Band Wagon is the Zombie. And it comes in a horde. I second that, seems fitting.
Evelyn: How about Appeal to Emotion be a crying girl? They lure the heroes into traps. Good one!
Sasha: Should we keep going or should we stop?
Evelyn: @Ivy would you like to pick one?
Ivy: I would see Fallacy Fallacy as a moving suit of armor that is overly strict. I can picture it.
Sasha: Should we continue tomorrow when there are more people online? It is late and I think it might be a good idea if everyone who remotely wants to play gets a chance to define a monster.
Evelyn: That's a good idea, maybe Sylvia would be interested.
Zoey: We have plenty options left, should I cast a flare tomorrow? XD
Tanya: We need a proper URL to cast a flare.
Zoey: I'll do that tomorrow. Consider it done XD
Tanya: Thank you all for helping. It was very helpful.
Sasha: Thank you for the great idea! Tanya thanks the group for making the game together.

202206110934|Fallacy Monsters
June 11, 2022 Saturday 9:34 AM
Arx Catacomb
⭐
Yesterday we stated a thread discussing what monster would be fitting to represent each locial fallacy. is updated to show the list. Let's continue the discussion.
Ledia: So if I have more ideas, can I say it or should I wait? I have no idea. I just post things XD @Sasha, @Tanya?
Evelyn: I think it is better to wait. It is hard for new comers to add a suggestion if everything is already defined. 😅 If you do that I am being kept at 8. 😁 I'm at 7. 😄
LRRH: I want to add a pudding monster.
Zoey: There goes the theme! XD
Evelyn: I think it would be fun if you summon puddings to fight monsters. So puddings are on our side. Okay. Nice save, Evelyn.
Sasha: Did Zoey just use Slippery Slope? Yes or No?
Ledia: I would say... Yes? For all we know, a pudding monster could have a halloween theme. And halloween is somewhat about sweets, if we go for a more E rated theme with lighter tone.
Evelyn: I think that is a good point. Candy monsters would actually fit and it would be cute. 😃
Zoey: 🤔
Sasha: Cardinal, Activate Catacomb Protocol! Zoey used Logical Fallacy: Slippery Slope and takes 1 damage.
Cardinal: What monster is Slippery Slope?
LRRH: Can that be the pudding monster?
Ledia: I was thinking it would be a slime monster because it is slippery, but I can also see slime, jelly, and pudding being the same monster type.
Tanya: I think the situation is not whether the pudding can be the monster, but that it was the monster that Zoey fell for and took 1 damage.
Zoey: It is official! I got attacked by a pudding. I added it to the list. XD
LRRH: The pudding monster looks firm, but once it is hit it turns runny makes it really slippery and heroes would fall into traps. It is a bait XD It is cute, I like it. 😊
Tanya: Should I change @Concept to @Catacomb? I think that would be a more fun charter code.
Ledia: Then what about @Dungeon? If we are going to lighter tone. @Catacomb is darker. I think @Dungeon is more fun.
Tanya: @StarTree, could you make the change to @Dungeon?
StarTree: It's done! Please update the charter when you have time! Thank you.
Sasha: @Cardinal, @StarTree, want to define monster?
StarTree: StarTree is now thinking!
Cardinal: Vampires for Genetics. Perfect choice!
StarTree: This is too hard for StarTree!
Evelyn: StarTree, what monster do you picture in the dungeon?
StarTree: Fungi and spores! They hallucinate the heroes slowly so the heroes have no idea!
Tanya: Maybe Appeal to Nature?
Ledia: That seems like the best fit. But for Appeal to Nature I would picture a mana fountain that is actually a foutain of poison.
Sasha: That could be the result of hallucination.
StarTree: StarTree doesn't have good ideas!
Evelyn: I think that was a good idea. Nothing else so far would be constantly there and become unnoticed. That is unique.
Sylvia: What about a mimic? The treasure chest monster. The heroes might get used to looting and seeing treasure chests. But they are not all good.
Tanya: What fallacy do you want that to be?
Sylvia: Composition/Division.
Ledia: I can sort of see that.
Evelyn: Now I also have a second idea. 😖
Ledia: About something new or something that already has a monster?
Evelyn: Something new. 😖
Ledia: I think it should be fine, I am out of ideas anyway.
Casey: What are you all doing? Who wants to go treasure hunting?
Evelyn: We are coming up with monster ideas to represent logical fallacies. Would you like to add an idea?
Casey: How about False Levers that look like they are for the door but sets off traps? For False Cause.
Ledia: No dungeon is complete without a lever or two.
Zoey: But is a lever a monster? 🤔
Casey: Does it have to be a monster?
Tanya: That's Zoey's prompt. I think anything that can defeat the heroes is okay.
Zoey: Added, but I'll leave the title for now because I don't know what to change it to. XD
Evelyn: Could we continue after treasure hunt?
Sasha: We don't need to define all of them before moving to Step 3. I think we have enough ideas, so we should fresh out what the game is. When we come back, could we move on to Step 3: Starting a game? We can still take further ideas as we play.
Sasha: I want to at least start a game before the weekend is over.
Evelyn: I'm fine with that. 😊 Zoey thanks all for the lesson XD
202206112107|Context
June 11, 2022 Saturday 9:07 PM
Arx Dungeon
⭐
For the first training exercise, my plan was to go online and spot examples of people using a logical fallacy.
Tanya: The purpose of this training is to be aware of them so we don't use logical fallacy. Then we can train on what to do when it is used against us or against someone else.
Evelyn: Does the context of the argument matter?
Tanya: That is up to you.
Sasha: Hold on a second. I had an idea on how we present this so it would be fun to read. On this channel, we could just talk and discuss without RP. But this shouldn't be the main story.
Tanya: In my vision we are doing this mainly for us. My priority is that we are trained and we have fun doing it. Whether someone will have fun reading it is secondary.
Sasha: I don't want to prioritize them. I think it is better if it is also fun to read, and if we are clear about that goal, even if you call it secondary or a bonus, then we could train in a way that will get both done by going through the exercise once.
Zoey: I think if it is fun for someone else to read then it will be fun for us to read later. I want that if it is not too hard to do. Is there a plan for that?
Sasha: Can we fix the context of the training? In the story, we are peacemaker heroes, and we are going through the Arx Dungeon. Do our hero characters know that they are fighting monster that represent logical fallacies, or are they trying to clear the dungeon of monsters?
Ledia: My undrstanding so far is that our heroes are there to train on how to fight monsters. Whether those monsters are dummy monsters or actual monsters infesting the dungeon is up to the GM.
Tanya: That was my intent. Our characters are training to fight monsters. The context has nothing to do with logical fallacies.
Sasha: I think if we do that we will miss out too many dialogue about logical fallacies. The story will become a generic dungeon crawl.
Evelyn: I cannot really picture what it means if it is not a generic dungeon crawl. 😅
Sasha: How about we start the story as a generic dungeon crawl, where the character believe that they are to clear monsters. But as they progress, they would discover the monster's weak points if they understand their connection to logical fallacies?
Tanya: I think that is not necessary because we defined the monsters using analogy, and the reader already know the relation. For the first training, we are just trying to spot the monsters. For future training, when we try to do something about them, only effective response in real life against those fallacies would give an effective result. We will not be rolling dice to determine the result. We will response in real life, and let what happens in real life be the result.
Tanya: And when it works, the player character is recognized for their skill that works, and gets a treasure item.
Zoey: I got a Fact-check bow. Sorry I forgot what its official name was. XD
StarTree: It was a quiver of fact checking! On Decemmber 9th 2021! 6 months ago XD
Evelyn: And I have a Sanctuary Staff. It was anti zombie, but now zombies are... reapers.
Tanya: When the Sanctuary Staff is activated, there is an aura of light so the heroes would have no shadows for reapers to attack.
Sasha: Okay, so we have two characters who already have been to the dungeon and had gotten treasures: A bow and staff. What is Ledia's story? Did Ledia work at the dungeon?
Ledia: In real life yes. In the story maybe. It is hard roleplaying experience when I have no experience about this game dungeon.
Sasha: Actually how about you tell us a little more about the Arx Dungeon in real life? What were you doing there? What was the purpose?
Ledia: The gist is that the Arx Sealing Team seals monsters and evil beings at its vault. We clean them up section by section. There is a huge backlog but it is has many layers of barriers. When a team is ready, we unseal a section and clean it up.
Ledia: Because of the barriers, it is relatively low risk even if we fail. So practically the whole vault is training ground. It is not particularly stressful working there.
Sasha: It sounds like a huge prison.
Ledia: Yes, except sealeing being are not exactly "alive" while they are seal. They are in the same condition when they were sealed until they are unsealed. All they know is that they were fighting Arx Knight in the field, the Arx Knight casts a spell, and the next thing they know is future Arx Knights waiting for them in full health.
Zoey: Then in this case, it wasn't really an evil wizard trying to invade the catacomb.
Ledia: It is not. We put them there.
Evelyn: Could we use the same context? If it is easier for Ledia to keep her story?
Tanya: That is fine.
Sasha: Then the rough context is the newer peacemakers Evelyn and Zoey are going to the Arx for training, where Ledia is their guide. Who else is going?
Tanya: The original concept, and the context was that as Queen Vivi tried to excavate the Cardinal Castle buried underground, Hospitalian Knights joined force with Arx on a mission to clear the closest catacomb site to Cardinal Castle to strengthen defense against Queen Vivi.
Tanya: We don't want Queen Vivi to take that site and unseal monsters there as her minions.
Tanya: So the Hospitalian Knights are in charge there calling for help from known heroes to clear that site.
Ledia: Do you mean Arx is in charge of the site, but HK is in charge of calling heroes from Panarcadia?
Tanya: Yes.
Ledia: That arrangement might make sense because Arx doesn't normally open their vault for others to level up. And adventurers typically won't accept the terms and conditions there anyway.
Evelyn: What are the terms and conditions?
Ledia: You have to link your soul to Arx Core, so that the Arx and monitor you as you fight the monster and learn your skills. When you fight there you are training yourself and Arx Core. Arx Core has a copy of your soul. It uses that to raise people who dies fighting in the vault.
Tanya: I think we are close to the limit of being E rated.
Zoey: That doesn't sound like a bad deal if that makes you practically immortal.
Ledia: If we are at the E rating limit, I have to think about whether I can comment on that.
Sasha: In the story, Panarcadia heroes can already be raised, so that is not something we need to talk about. We can just assume that every heroes can be raised. There is no motive to join Arx just to be raised.
Ledia: Agreed. And I am not recruiting here.
Tanya: Back to the context. In terms of number of players, since the arrangement is that Hospitalian Knights will call on heroes for each chamber they unlock, any hero may just drop in if they think they have the suitable skill to deal with the particular monster.
Tanya: In the story, the main purpose is to clear the dungeon, not to train the heroes. For our players at the Blogger Guild level, this story exists to train us.
Sasha: That is somewhat confusing. We should clearly mark which goal is the non-RP guild goal and which goals are the RP goals.
Tanya: Noted.
Sasha: But overall that is a good description. It should be summarized in the story. My earlier point was this discussion should be a footnote, not what a read will read first when they arrive th Chapter 16.
Sasha: Let's continue tomorrow with spotting a monster.

202206120921|Frugivorous?
June 12, 2022 Sunday 9:21 AM
Arx Dungeon
⭐
The Hospitalian Knights is continuing their mission to clear the Arx Dungeon closest to the buried Cardinal Castle. At the command post there, Tanya looks for the next monster to clear. For this thread, I want to try a convention where we use the bubble only for non-RP comments. Please use chat mode only for RP content.
Evelyn: Evelyn reports to the Arx Dungeon to help spot the next monster. Are we going to Twitter to look for a discussion where a logical fallacy is used? Yes, but it doesn't have to be from Twitter. Any source is fine. Red argues at Twitter so there should be many examples. I found something, is it okay if I link to it? I think that is fine. @LRRH? It is okay linking to Twitter when we are not hurting anyone. How is Red related in the story? Little Red Riding Hood scouts and routinely sends information to the alliance. So in this particular case, why did you bookmark it? I wanted to know whether humans are "Frugivorous" and not "omnivorous". Evelyn, LRRH, in the story, if you are doing this, you are already in an unsealed chamber. You are exploring the dungeon to tell if there is a monster. Do you want to RP that context?
LRRH: When Evelyn arrived Little Red Riding Hood was already exploring the dungeon and found what looks like a treasure chest. So she opens it. Little Red Riding Hood is the kind of character who would just open chests without defusing traps when she is alone. Because she has Auto-Revive, so in general it doesn't matter if it sets off a trap. If you do that in the story I think it is better to roll the dice. But my priority is to get to logical fallacies, so the chest opens without issue.
LRRH: Inside the chest, there was a scroll. The scroll claims that humans are "Frugivores" which are different from "Omnivores')". Is that the truth?
LRRH: Red thinks that wouldn't that be convenient if that is the truth? Many animal will be spared if humans learn that they are not supposed to be eating meat.
Evelyn: From the command post, Evelyn saw that Red had already checked in and caught up to Red.
Evelyn: "I think we should ask Ivy or Zoey to check the authenticity of the scroll." Question: Did Evelyn just use Appeal to Authority?
Evelyn: "I want to confirm the meaning of the term. I am not sure what 'Frugivorous' means. Also, the list show many characteristics, are they the full range of the characteristics in each category, or did the maker of the scroll hide the truth that each category have a wider range of characteristics?" I wanted to know whether that list was cherry-picking, but we don't yet have a monster for that. 😅 I think since the game is on, if you have an idea for a term that doesn't have a monster, you could just define it even if you had defined some before. I don't have an idea for cherry-picking, but I thought Minions could be the monsters for Appeal to Authority. Bananas? I meant gargoyles, demon hounds, and other familiars commanded or controlled by some authority like a wizard. I am looking up what is going on.
Zoey: Zoey arrives at the dungeon and learns about the situation. I added Demon Hounds as the monster type because "minions" is not specific enough. The long description includes your other examples. Thank you. Any idea on what Cherry-Picking would be? How about Skeleton Archers? If you fire an arrow at them, the arrow tends to just go through them, and the pick up your arrows and fire it back. That's a pretty good match. Added. And Evelyn didn't use Appeal to Authority. She was just asking for the definition of the terms which presumably both the speaker and a competent listener would agree on. Sasha, do you agree? I agree. In the story you could say you have fended off some demon hounds.
Ledia: Ledia arrived at the chamber and saw red growing eyes of demon hounds down a hallway. She skates down the hall at a surprising speed to the hounds and KO'ed them easily.
Evelyn: That surprised Evelyn also. She had not seen Ledia move like that.
Ledia: "These were my skates when I was working at Arx Vault. We used them to skate down long halls of cleared chambers when there were no teleports in between. It was fun."
Ledia: Ledia was wearing her Arx uniform, which was described as a battle nun uniform with light armor pieces. Okay. Fact 1: Frugivore refers to animals that primarily eats raw fruits. It doesn't mean that the animal cannot digest other food, or other foods are harmful. What about Omnivorous? Fact 2: Omnivores refers to animals that are capable of digesting and surviving by eating both plants and animals. Omnivore describes a being's physiological capability. Being an Frugivore describes a being's diet preference, which might be situational specific to the evironment they live in. An Frugivore can either also be a Herbivore or an Omnivore. And I found an article discussing specifically this topic and refers to the memes. Zoey, how would you describe what your character is doing in the dungeon?
Zoey: Zoey arrives with another scroll she retrieve from the archive and started comparing the two.
Zoey: "The scroll I brought is long. It might take some time to understand it."
Ledia: "Why don't we skip to the conclusion of the scroll?" Ledia thought and read ahead. The Long scroll made that conveniently possible without disturbing Zoey.
Ledia: The conclusion of the scroll Zoey brought stated that humans benefit the most from a mostly plant-based diet, but not strictly herbivore diet. Although their most recent ancestors are frugivores, humans are better adapted for higher starch and higher vegetables diet than higher fruit diet. So what do we make out of this information if it is true? The Twitter post originally claimed having a carnivorous diet is not healthy and not natural for humans. And this scroll doesn't say that humans are Frugivores. I think so far it is clear of any logical fallacy.
Evelyn: If the scroll Zoey brought is truthful, the one Red found seems to be authentic. But it doesn't say what Red thought it meant.
Evelyn: The scroll didn't say humans are frugivores.
LRRH: What do you call a diet that is high starch and high vegetables?
Zoey: I can't find a name for that.
Evelyn: I think as long as it is reasonably affordable, it is better to minimize harm to other beings. So even if human are evolutionarily carnivores, if we should choose to harm no one if we can.
Evelyn: If we choose to harm others just because we are evolutionarily adapted to doing so, that would be an argument of Genetics. That is Vampirism. So far this section is clear. Evelyn, do you want to conclude it?
Evelyn: Satisfied that the scroll Red found was harmless, Evelyn blesses it with an anti-vampirism spell.
May our conscience guide us to goodness free from inherited habits.
Evelyn is recognized for Cleric Skill Anti-Vampire Enchantment. We didn't identify an actual fallacy. But we did vet a piece of information and prevented misuse of it. We are preventing ourselves from arguing veganism from an evolutionary point of view. I think that is a good result. I think as a result, the enchantment Evelyn put on the scroll is worth more than the scroll itself. I think the information from the scroll is interesting and informative also. An I am sure people put a lot of effort to do the survey and science to obtain that comparison. 😅 But in terms of a logical argument for veganism, the focus on not hurting others is more important than what we are made of. Yes. If anyone here tried to conclude that "Because humans are closer to frugivores therefore humans should eat fruits", then it would either be Appeal to Genetics or Appeal to Nature. Evelyn protected the group before the group was attacked. Clerics don't get enough credits when they have no idea what issues they prevented with just words. Evelyn is worth more than she thinks of herself. That's very kind of you two. 😊 Evelyn thanks the party for the lesson.
202206121500|Music thingy
June 12, 2022 Sunday 3:00 PM
Music Room
⭐
Ivy said that if we want to include music in our normal post, we could add a music thingy. What is a music thingy?
Ivy: I meant you could make a list of div. In each divyou put the code you want that can play the music, and tag each div like we tag posts. Then when you to display them, you can JQuery them as a list. From a post, you could make a function such as PlayMusic("Fort Arsenal") that would do "the same." What you could do further is that PlayMusic JQueries and starts playing music at a specific div at the side panel. But I think for it to start playing, your div objects need to contain links to MP3, not musescore. It is just musescore, the user will still need to click on play. In that case it won't make a lot of use to pin it at the side bar.
P4: P4 will start with making a list. Where should the list be? Is it a post or a page?
Ivy: It doesn't matter. Wherever you put it, only the function needs to know where it actually is.
P4: Actually, where can I upload mp3?
P4: I want to try this, but it doesn't work if I put the mp3 in Google Drive.
Ivy: I guess you could try Github.
P4: It doesn't work?
Ivy: Add "?raw=true" at the end. ref 💟
🕰️ [5:01 PM] 🕰️
P4: We got banned from Musescore again. ╰(*°▽°*)╯
Ivy: It is probably automatic if you refresh the links too often when you test the page.
P4: I asked Musescore to unblock us and promised to be good. 💟 ... by not embedding so many scores on the same page.
P4: Since we can now post mp3, I posted some old ones.

June 13, 2022 Monday 9:39 PM
Mira Mira
⭐
What are must-haves on our profiles here? Or, what do you really want to know from other's or your own profile here?
Vivi: I want to know how they get their credits.
Ledia: Is it that important? You asked. So I answered. Fair enough.
LRRH: I want to know people's wishes.
Ledia: That was on the membership form.
Ivy: I think the profile should have the levels the character has. And those levels shouldn't be higher than then overall level. You did that for the profile cards. They should be on the main profile.
Ledia: I could start with their contributions, then their wishes, followed by their level allocations. This has no resemblence to FATE.
Ivy: I think only you know FATE here.
Vivi: I only care about the scores. I thought you were going to update a person every week.
Ledia: We already did the top scorers. Who else do you want to know?
Vivi: @StarTree, who is the next top scorer that we hadn't profiled?
StarTree: It is StarTree! Ranking 4th in the guild! Score distribution: 38% Daily Quest, 33% Guild Quest, 29% Cardinal. StarTree is the score leader of @Board!
Vivi: I want something more helpful. What is your average score per day in June?
StarTree: It is 8.3!
⭐
Vivi: In that case, I want you to put three stars for Daily Quest and Guild Quest, and two stars for Cardinal Quest.
StarTree: Like this?
Evelyn: Aww~~ It's cute!! 😍
Vivi: Yes. And I want a short description when I click on each row.
StarTree: Done!
Evelyn: That's so cute!! Wee~~!
Ledia: Yes it looks really nice! And motivating.
Ivy: I want to give StarTree an Admin badge at her profile.
Vivi: That doesn't help me, unless you want StarTree to handle all administrative tasks.
Ivy: It is not about authority but recognition.
Vivi: I think I got what I want for now. So I won't comment on what you want.
LRRH: StarTree, I think I asked for your wish before but you didn't have one. Has it changed?
StarTree: StarTree doesn't have a wish!
Evelyn: But I really like partying with StarTree. StarTree is kind, helpful, and funny. 😊 That is so true.
LRRH: Should there be a fellow member comments section?
Ivy: There shouldn't be, otherwise you would have to write something nice for everyone.
LRRH: Why is that a problem?
Ivy: When you run out of genuine comments, it will show and it will make people feel bad.
Evelyn: I think we should aim to have fun and make fond memories together. If we can see who is left out, then it make us want to include them and do something for them.
Ledia: I think that's the way to go. I'm not contesting.
Evelyn: Should we have a fond memories section for StarTree?
Ivy: How about calling that funny moments or highlights?
Evelyn: I hope that wouldn't create too much expectations. 😅
Ledia: Should we JQuery every story moment with StarTree?
Evelyn: I think that would be a good start! Then we can review and choose. 😃
⭐
Ledia: Let's call that section "StarTree moments".
Evelyn: That's a very good name! 😄
Ledia: I am adding the tag "data-moment-StarTree". Evelyn: Okay. 😊
Evelyn: This turns out to be a pretty hard task because we have so many posts. And I remember there are many fond memories at Google Slides that we haven't imported. 😖
Ledia: I guess we should have a habit of marking tagging them from now on...
Ledia: Anyway, this is what we have so far.
Evelyn: StarTree, we love you. 😊 Evelyn thanks StarTree for all the wonderful moments.
StarTree: This makes StarTree feel like this is her birthday! Wee~~!
StarTree: StarTree will also start tagging posts for happy moments for everyone!
Ledia: Let's all do it.
Evelyn: Yes, I like this a lot. 😊 Ledia thanks everyone for huge breakthrough for the profiles.

202206142047|Championship
June 14, 2022 Tuesday 8:47 PM
Ageless Forest
⭐
For Philosophy Arena, I want to setup a championship. The contestants would be different philosphies, and each contestant may post a situation for other philosophies to solve. Each contestant can propose their best solution for the situation. Then other contestant may find flaws of their solution, for example, when they propose a solution that uses something contradicting their principle.
Fina: In this post I want to discuss the scoring in order to determine the champion.
Fina: About the scoring: A contestant gets one point for providing a solution according to their principles to the prompt.
Fina: Next, the solution gets extra points based on the ranking scale at Seed of Peace. That is +0 for Conquest, +1 for Control, +2 for Compete, +3 for Coexist, +4 for Cooperate, +5 for Celebrate.
Fina: As you see, the scale is set to favor Celebrate as the highest ranking. Those who want Conquest to get the most points could make a different ranking.
Vivi: What constitutes a valid solution? If the prompt is: "A neighboring country is attacking your country", and your answer is: "we celebrate the invasion" does that answer score 5 points?
Fina: No, because that reaction does not stop the conquest, so the resulting solution type is still conquest. That is +0.
Vivi: If I say: "My country will defend itself" then what is my score?
Fina: For what reason are people in your country defending the country? For example, if you say they must fight or be executed, then your solution is still +0. That answered my question.
Evelyn: I think this is easier to understand than what we started in the manga. 😃
Sasha: But if each contestant may only have one question, it will be really hard to pick a question to start.
Fina: Each contestant can propose multiple questions, but they may only choose one for the scoring. So when they think of a situation where they can score higher, they would write it up and swap the prompt. If they are already at +5 for their prompt, they can try to come up with another prompt where other contestants will score lower.
Sasha: That's a good method, there's no need to hold back posting new prompts. 👍
Fina: Naturally, I will be a contestant for Stewardism. I am not going to ask anyone else to participate. Buf if no one else participates, Stewardism will show as the sole champion.
Evelyn: In the manga I played for communism, but I don't really understand it. 😅
Fina: If you want you can play as "What Evelyn thinks Communism is" until a more knowledgeable contestant takes your place and answers the prompts for you.
Zoey: Or you could cite arguments people make when they promote communism. XD
Evelyn: I think I should first consider whether I want to roleplay that at all. 😅
Tanya: If our aim is not to rank existing philosophies than it shouldn't matter whether communism is a contestant. I will compete as Hospitalian.
Zoey: Then we are fighting among ourselves. XD
Tanya: Not necessarily. In this scoring system, we could draw. Tanya receives
Evelyn: Maybe I should play as Arcacian? 😅
Fina: Yes you should. Arcacian Ambassador.
Zoey: The nomination was a setup for the challenge! XD 😄
Fina: So who wants to set the stage at Chapter 23?
Zoey: Nothing gets done at Chapter 23 when Fina can't boss 3B around XD
Evelyn: I guess I could try formatting the page. Although I can't promise that it will look very good. 😅
Fina: If it doesn't I'll let you know.
Evelyn: Okay. Thank you. 😅
Zoey: It should be the other way around. Fina is so ungrateful. XD
Fina: I am being grateful enough for coming once a while. I don't need to be here at all. That's ... true. 😅
Evelyn: I'm sorry, Fina! We've been slacking so much and didn't follow through with what you taught us since... Chapter 4. I will start making the Arena right away. Thank you for your invaluable guidance! We're very grateful for your visits! 😖🙏 That's more like it. OMG I feel sorry for Evelyn. XD Then help her build Arena. This is your guild, you are from the same team. It is so mean but kind of right at the same time XD
Fina: It is completely right. I expect visible result for us to comment on in this thread.
Zoey: I feel like now I know what happened to 3B XD
Sasha: I want to laugh but... but...
Fina: It's too late. Go help your team. And that other cat who is still reading this.
Tanya: This is not my forte. I also need help at @Dungeon. Excuse accepted.
Sasha: It's so unfair!
Vivi: It's okay Sasha, I'll laugh in your place. Mhahahahaha!! XD 🤣
Evelyn: Fina, how is this? We added numbers on the left for the scores. The next step is to fill it with principles and post challenge questions.
Fina: Good. Arena Quest "Format new arena page" is complete. Arena Quest "Define scoring rules" is complete. Fina thanks Evelyn and Zoey for taking care of the page. Seriously it is getting so cozy here, it makes me want to just update this blog. XD 😄 It is kind of exciting seeing this. I look forward to seeing the challenge questions and answers.
Fina: I nominated Chapter 23 for best scoreboard. What is the highlight of the nomination?
Fina: I nominate it for the brilliant style combining score, title, participants and a bright color code. We had not have a team scoreboard. This is the first. Copy! Which team shall I join? XD Fina thanks all for the attention.

202206152050|Step by Step
June 15, 2022 Wednesday 8:50 PM
Magic Detectives
⭐
We are missing something that show dependencies of subquests for Cardinal Quest. So I propose adding something in the planner to show dependencies. However, I forgot what it was that I saw to have such dependencies. Anyone remember what it was?
Tanya: Are you talking about Sapphire 898? We might need to craft that into a shield. Yes. That, and more.
Casey: Don't ALL of them have dependencies? We are waiting for many crafting materials.
Sasha: There are two kinds of dependencies. The ones where we wait for something done so we can finish the quest, and the ones where we need to tell someone else to do something so they can do their part before we can finish the quest. I was talking about the second type.
Casey: They are both important
Tanya: We need to tell others when they are behind, and we also need to tell ourselves when we are behind. It is the same problem. Technically I don't care when someone else is behind. But I get your point. And we should care.
Tanya: Let's redo the back calculation first because the one on the planner is outdated.
Tanya: The updated handover date is July 22. How did we get a month extension? XD With help from Hospitalian Knights. Hospitalian Knights--Our Deus Ex Machina. XD
Ledia: Hey, if we are playing FATE, "Hospitalian Knights saves the day" would be a good Aspect for Tanya. It is not the time to celebrate yet.
Sasha: No that won't be good enough because it is not double edged. It should be something like, "We are in debt to Hospitalian Knights." or something. That does sound better. It is more playable. Order! Order! XD
Sasha: Okay. So by the grace of Hospitalian Knights, the handover date is postponed to July 22, and I edited the planner with new dates for the quest hour bins.
Casey: I heard that some of the crafting materials won't arrive until July 20.
Sasha: This is beyond what I wanted to talk about originally, but let's first put all the known material arrival dates on the planner.
Sasha: Now, on to what I wanted to talk about. Should we go through each spawn point? Let's start with the Sapphire Shield, then maybe the Ruby 948 if we have time left.
Tanya: For the Shield, we are waiting for a template, and we need to learn how to enchant. We also need to know how to enchant for Exchange and Shaft spawn points.
Sasha: Okay. Please wait while I summarize the steps on the planner.
Sasha: It is not too hard having it somwhere on the planner. Should I spread it out on specific dates instead of having it outside the schedule?
⭐
Tanya: How about make a dummy bin after this Friday and put them there. Then it is close enough so we will see it every day, and easy to move sub quests we don't finish this week. That's a great idea!
Sasha: With that, the Planner became slightly more useful!
Zoey: Sasha, I've just realized that the planner is really a Quest Journal.
Sasha: I can see that somewhat, but it would need to look much nicer for me to call that a Quest Journal. Right now it is more like a quest list.
⭐
Casey: I am planning to upgrade @Treasure to have information and stories per location. I could do Arsenal mines first.
Zoey: I think that will be useful.
Sasha: Go for it, Casey! 👍 Sasha thanks the team for the discussion.

202206162302|Dungeon Cleanup
June 16, 2022 Thursday 11:02 PM
Arx Dungeon
⭐
Tanya arrived at Arx Dungeon when Red had already opened another chamber. This thread continues the convention that CHAT is for RP and BUBBLE is for non-RP.
LRRH: Little Red Riding Hood found poison spores! This shows people drinking milk from a cow and implies an argument that drinking cow milk is bad because it is unnatural.
Tanya: Yes. Clearly. Little Red Riding Hood is recognized for Detect Poison.
Tanya: This chamber has many more monsters if someone else wants to come spot them. I don't know if we should link there. In that thread, there are bad words if you scroll down. 😅 @Red? Evelyn, what should I do instead? I wanted to have a link so people may see example in its context, and the other examples in the thread. Perhaps we could just paraphrase the argument? Regardless whether we will remove the link after discussion, paraphrasing is good practice. We don't know whether that link will work in the future, and it saves them from leaving the page. I am against external links in general because for almost everywhere on this blog, if you jump to another page, you can't jump back to where you were. Very compelling. Will it be okay if every external link comes with a pop up? The pop up will warn you that you are leaving the site. It tells you the link points to, then if you want you can click on it or open to a new tab. That is probably better. @Ivy, @StarTree, can you implement? If you want to include an external URL, please use function "ExURL". We'll add the message in that function later. StarTree finished with confirmation message and showed Vivi how to test it! Much MUCH better. Now if you could protect all the existing links. There are 1866 instances to check! I'm sorry. I'll make sure my chapter uses ExURL. 😖 There shouldn't be that many to fix. The template must be using a lot of jumps. Does StarTree not need to guard jumps within the blog? Please start with external jumps. They should have the "mbbuttonEx" class already. 290! I can't believe we did so much already... 😖 This calls for Regular Expressions. StarTree is almost done changing them. 😭 I don't know whether it will work. Please finish it this time. I will ask someone who knows better. For next time. StarTree is done! But... StarTree doesn't know how to replace the existing archive without deleting it...! 😭 Arg... That sounds like a bad idea. Let's replace them page by page like Evelyn said if you can't wait for a verdict on regular expressions. I'm sorry for going off topic and for the trouble. 😖 Evelyn thanks the group for taking her request seriously. Let's continue next time.
Tanya: RP OFF: In the discussion above, we decided that external links should use the new function "ExURL" which will invoke a popup message instead of jumping directly. The popup allows the viewer to see the target url and cancel the jump.
Tanya: The reason of this change is to accidental jumps, especially on mobile because it is not obvious where the jumps are, and our site is missing the function to undo a jump to return to the same view.

202206172005|FATE points
June 17, 2022 Friday 8:05 PM
Cardinal Core
⭐
We have been reading the FATE game system for a while now. One of its main concept is fate points. To summarize, fate points are used like mana to power up skill and change the course of the story. Your earn them by letting your character get into complications. That system sets FATE apart from other systems, and Cardinal Core doesn't have that concept. Should Cardinal Core have that concept?
Ledia: To me, fate points are like the rage bar in a fighting game. When your character takes hits, their rage bar charges up, then you can unleash super moves. It is a mechanism that allows the underdog to overcome, thus creates dramatic results.
Evelyn: It feels strange if we intentionally put ourselves in a pinch. 😅 You also get fate points for being in-character. No, I think only when your being in-character leads to a complication. If you are just being in-character in a good way, you don't get fate points.
Ledia: For example, when Evelyn enters a dungeon she casts Protect. That is in-character but she won't get fate points for that, unless we were told we shouldn't cast protect but she did it out of habit anyway. Then if she accept that she would mess up in that situation, she gets a fate point.
Evelyn: I think if we simply play in-character, we don't really need fate points to be in-character. We would already mess up naturally. 😅
Zoey: Like that time when Ledia tried to cast chain lightning in Arsenal. 😉 But I didn't get anything for being entertaining. But I think it was funny because it was genuine. You weren't trying to create drama. It was you. 😊
Evelyn: And for being entertaining, you would get a moment for your profile. True.
Sasha: So I see one against fate points. Is there anyone FOR it?
Ledia: In boss fights, it is easy to get into a situation where the heroes are low on mana. so the end of the fight simply drags on, as we only have basic attacks left. The end of a fight is usually, "That was a long fight, thank god it is over."
Ledia: Fate points is a way to change that. If we don't use fate points, what else can we use?
Tanya: For the Hellapagos boss fight, there was a round limit. If the heroes couldn't finish the boss, the boss escapes and the heroes lose. At the last round, the heroes would just have to invent new ways to cooperate and stack up their advantages to make the final blow.
Tanya: I think it did fine making it a team effort.
Zoey: The way I remembered it was we tried to be too dramatic but the dice didn't work out. It might be easier if we just hacked on it with basic attacks instead of trying to combo. XD
Ledia: 3B was trying to draw lightning to nuke the boss. I don't remember exactly why that didn't work. I don't remember how the fight ended.
Zoey: It was the dice XD
Tanya: I think what happened was, when the boss is close to KO'ed, Cardinal followed the dice more faithfully. The heroes need to stack up the advantages more.
Zoey: It's too long ago I don't remember XD
Evelyn: I remembered Vivi comboed with light for Shadow Bind. Evelyn receives
Ledia: Yes that was awesome.
Sasha: So do we have a conclusion about the rage gauge?
Tanya: My conclusion is that the players need to remember that if they don't think hard, the drag at the end will happen. That drag is realistic. Everyone is tired and out of mana. How do you keep the opportunity and end the quest? That is part of the challenge to the players.
Tanya: If we go by a rage gauge mechanism, then Evelyn won't need to cast Protect and we would all be more reckless than who we really are. We'll all be out of character.
Evelyn: I think if we are playing in-character, our character won't be able to consider or talk about "fate points" because they don't make sense inside the story. I can understand it if I helped someone and they gave me magic item that I can use in a pinch. I can't understand if I need to put my character in a pinch to gain an advantage later. There is no causality in the story. 😅
Sasha: Ledia?
Ledia: I do enjoy the fact that we are already playing in character. I don't think fate points would make us play out of character because you can only get fate points by being in character. I guess what I might be asking, is that good roleplayers get an advantage or bonus until they use it in the session. Then when it comes to a fight, they can use those bonus when their die roll is bad. I understand that this has nothing to do with casuality.
Tanya: Instead of simply rewarding drama, the GM could reward relevant behavior. For example, an observant character can see where they can get an advantage in the surrounding. And they become recognized as an observant character by paying attention and be observant during other parts of the story. Then their leveling up their observant skill and their being able to use that skill to get advantages would make sense.
Ledia: That's quite compelling. I don't have further arguments
Sasha: Okay. So far we are mostly against using fate points because it breaks causality. Should we keep reading fate? we are at about page 80/300.
Ledia: I think the parts about aspect is helpful.
Evelyn: I think we can skim through many parts because we don't need to know how to play FATE if we aren't adopting its mechanisms. I think our dice mechanism with advantages is simpler.
Sasha: I think that's good enough for now. In the meantime we could look for the next thing to read. Sasha thanks the group for feedbacks.

202206212041|Special Quest
June 21, 2022 Tuesday 8:41 PM
Magic Bakery
⭐
The guild has a situation. It is not exactly an emergency, but some of you want to be in the know, so this is the thread.
James: Here is the situation. Someone stopped paying rent since May. By July it will be the third month. So we are about to start the first step of an eviction process, which is a formal notice to them that they need to pay or we might evict them. 😖
Tanya: Why didn't they pay rent?
James: They say their relative is trying close a property sale, so they are waiting for a big sum of money before they can pay. I don't understand this. Are they buying a property or selling a property? Does it matter? It is not an emergency. They did it out of greed or convenience. They are taking advantage of other people. They have no excuse.
Fina: It is not charity if someone cheats out of greed and you just forgive them.
James: To answer Tanya's question: I think the context is that their relative is selling a property. They will get a large sum of money when the sale closes. In the meantime there may be legal fees and realtors they need to pay. I see.
Evelyn: I know it is late, but if they had asked would we have lent them money for closing the sale?
James: Probably not. We're not in a business of lending money.
Zoey: I agree with Fina that this is not okay. It is not honest if you help someone by cheating someone else.
Evelyn: If they pay after receiving the notice. Can they stay?
James: It might depend on how they pay.
Sasha: If they were willing to cheat, there should be better renters.
Evelyn: I feel that if you serve the notice, it would be a huge escalation.
Tanya: But the notice doesn't actually do anything.
Sasha: It is the opposite. They escalated. We are showing them how to de-escalate.
Evelyn: But if they are telling the truth, then they have no money to pay in the short term. This will create a lot of panic. Little Red Riding Hood has just served the notice. OMG! XD 😖 How did it go? They say they will shoot for paying within three days, but probably next week. I see. Emergency meeting is adjorned. Please resume your other quests. Little Red Riding Hood thanks all for the discussion. Are we ignoring the "fact" that the notice should be served by an adult? Don't go there, Ledia! XD

202206220916|Housing
June 22, 2022 Wednesday 9:16 AM
Arena
⭐
How does your philosophy deal with housing shortage? Does it try to house everyone? How does it do so? These questions is for the challenge question at Arena. I hope that the format is correct. I am posing it for Arcacianism.
Evelyn: When someone needs housing, Arcacianism reimburses and rewards those who choose to help. The result is that the person in need gets housing without being in debt, and the helpers get rewarded with credits that they may or may not choose to exchange for other rewards at quest centers. What rank are you aiming for? Celebration. Arcacianism celebrates those who help others. If you help people by building houses for them, you get many Arcacian points. Your good deeds are on public record.
Fina: Stewardism simply keep access open so people who need housing may build by themselves. They could get help if they want, but there is no system to promote help. Stewardism does not keep record of people's good deeds, but it reacts against those laying waste and blocking fair access. I'd rank Stewardism at Coexistence for this question. I don't get it. Why didn't you just say Stewardism celebrates those who can build their own houses? Then you'll get 5 points. Stewardism by itself doesn't say that being self-sufficient is something to be celebrated. It is anti-villains but does not promote heroism. You're going really soft against Evelyn. Then come join the fight.
Vivi: I want to join as a contestant. What do I need to do?
Fina: What philosophy are you representing?
Vivi: Dictatorship, World Domination.
Fina: Any difference to imperialism?
Vivi: That's for you to decide. In Queen Vivism, the world belongs to Queen Vivi. Queen Vivi has the final authority to decide who deserves to have a house and resources allocated accordingly to her liking as the world celebrates her infinite wisdom.
Vivi: I propose this as a celebration rank solution. I dare anyone to disagree.
Zoey: I disagree. Isn't this the definition of Conquest rank solution? You just said you decide who deserves to have a house. So, what do you do when you decide that someone who has a house doesn't have one?
Vivi: Queen Vivi does not feel a need to reply to non-contestants.
Fina: Zoey come join Stewardism.
Zoey: I feel that I should be neutral for now.
Sasha: Why would anyone want to join Stewardism when your answer is only mid-ranked?
Fina: This is Stewardism to Vivism: Stewardism does not take away livelihood of neutral beings. That is the requirement for a philosophy to reach the rank of Coexistence. Vivism as described violates Coexistence. It is at most competition rank.
Vivi: Objection. I never said I would take away livelihood. I said I get to decide who deserves to live in a house.
Fina: You are not denying that you would not take away people livelihood as you see fit. Unless you declare that Queen Vivism does not give Queen Vivi the authority to make such decisions, your objection is meaningless. It is implied that you also have that authority even if you don't say it explicitely. Sasha? Sasha can't think of a comeback against Fina's argument. 🏳️
Vivi: This is Queen Vivism to Stewardism: Does Stewardism declare that they will never do anything that harms or ends the livelihood of another neutral organism for their own interest? Do you hunt indiscriminately all living things that hunt others? Or do you play favorism and let some of those hunters off the hook?
Fina: Stewardism doesn't force anyone to enforce Stewardism values. If you don't fight for the rights of other being, that just makes you less of a Stewardist.
Vivi: I claim that Stewardism also violates the requirement for Coexist unless it declares Stewardism bans pulling weeds from a garden and building roads over vegetations.
Fina: Stewardism follows sustainability, and that is better than Vivism which would not only pave roads over plants but dead human rebels.
Tanya: Vivi, you can't win arguments against Fina. I'm speaking as an oracle.
Vivi: Queen Vivi does not response to non-contestants.
Tanya: Hospitalianism promotes necessary housing by letting hospitable places get more resources based on net migration. When people with no housing migrate to a place they find more hospitable (such as a place the provides the kind of housing and community they want), their resources rights travel with them to the new place.
Tanya: Hospitalian solution to housing is cooperation ranked.
Vivi: Vivism to Hopistalianism: How is Hospitalianism not ranked at Competition? That housing solution does not promote different states to cooperate. It is promoting them to compete against each other.
Tanya: Hospitalianism promotes people in a country to cooperate with people trying to migrate to their country. People who share the same problem of needing housing
Vivi: That doesn't change the relationship among the countries. They are clearly in comeptition against each other.
Tanya: @Fina, how is competition rank defined?
Fina: Compeition rank is defined as solutions that allow losers to lose their livelihood. It is defined for individuals, not organizations. There is no problem "killing" an organization when it lacks members.
Tanya: Therefore, Hospitalianism is above competition rank. Unlike Stewardism, Hospitalianism has an incentive for people to be hospitable to others.
Vivi: I contest the conclusion that Hospitalianism promotes people helping each other because the added resource follows the migrant. The local helpers don't actually get anything.
Tanya: That is not how it normally works. The local helpers are not providing hospitality entirely free of charge. They could have a tax or duty schedule where a migrant is allowed to join only if they pay a due. But the migrant is free to choose any country to join or be independent. Most people would rather pay a fee for a comfortable and safe life than to invent and defend their life all by themselves.
Tanya: Hospitalianism is not much different from choosing which hotel you want to stay in, when you also have a choice to camp. You don't have to choose a hotel.
Fina: Without further arguments, the scores for this challenge question are: Arcacianism 5 points, Hospitalianism 4 points, Stewardism 3 pts, Queen Vivism 0 pt. With this scoring system, won't the score stay the same for every question?
Fina: That's for you to find out.

202206231035|Board Updates
June 23, 2022 Thursday 10:35 AM
Magic Bakery
⭐
What changes would you like to see at Blogger or @Board? There are 57 items for @Board in the guild log but StarTree doesn't know which one to work on!
Ledia: I think this might be a bad practice if we log items at the guild log, but we actually ignore them when we have time to do something. StarTree is doing it wrong! 😭
Ledia: What is the oldest quest logged there?
StarTree: Red logged something about Big Bad Wolf on August 23, 2021!
Ledia: That has nothing to do with @Board. Red was asking the question what constitutes bullying and how should society react to it. I can't tell exactly where it should go. Maybe @Seed or @Arena or @Paladin? But it is definitely not an @Board quest.
Fina: @Arena can take that as a challenge question. Thank you!
LRRH: @Board has unrelated quests because at one point the quests did not have proper chapter assignment. So they were all set to @Board to be assigned. That explains it.
Ledia: We categorized the remaining quests at @Board. The categories are:
Ledia: Coding: Where someone needs to learn more about coding to upgrade the blog.
Ledia: Style: Where someone needs to figure out what changes can make the blog nicer.
Ledia: Archive: Importing content from old Google Slides.
Ledia: Celebrate: Celebrate most liked posts at Twitter.
Ledia: Guide: A guide for ourselves on how to write our own chapters.
Ledia: Scoring: Rules on scoring for the guild
Ledia: Milestone: A quest on publishing a newsletter.
LRRH: @Guild will take Scoring and Milestone quests.
Evelyn: I think @Freedom can take the Archival quest.
Ledia: That leaves tracking most liked posts at Twitter to be an odd quest for @Board.
Evelyn: I think @Happy can take that quest.
Ledia: Now the quest list of @Board has a clear focus on the blog. It collects ideas on good styles, it upgrades the code, and it shows the rest of us how to upgrade on own chapters.
Ledia: I think the Guide quest is the easiest to clear. We should clear that first, so everyone knows the known ways to make their chapters better, then do Style. Style Quests will tell us what code we need to upgrade.
LRRH: Sounds like a great plan. Ledia receives
Sasha: It think this turns out to be a great topic for @Detective. So I am tagging it. Ledia is recognized for the skill Battle Master.
StarTree: Thank you! Now StarTree knows what to do next! StarTree thanks the fellow guild members for guidance!
