202206260936|Treasure Hunt
June 26, 2022 Sunday 9:36 AM
Magic Bakery
⭐
I think I am aiming for something really awesome, but my vision is a little cloudy. Could someone help me figure out what to do with Chapter 21 more Questy?
⭐
Tanya: @StarTree, could you take a screenshot of Chapter 21? Done! Aren't we confident that after discussion it will be shockingly better? So we need a before and after comparison? XD It is also good for the context if we read this later.
Casey: My rough plan is to show Cardinal Quest content by the treasure related to them. So when you click on it, you should see what the treasure is, how it was obtained, and who has it. I see. What do you need help with? To make it more Questy and exciting...?
⭐
Ivy: You could present the treasure as it own game item card. You could see Chapter 20\Archetype\Herlad\Flare for example. Nice!
Casey: What should I really put for the rarity?
Tanya: Rarity has no meaning in Cardinal Quest. Instead we should know how much it costs to use it.
Ledia: Should this shield block magical or physical attack? If it blocks physical attacks, how is it different from an ordinary shield?
Tanya: It blocks physical attack, but it absorbs more impact the more it blocks the same attack.
Cardinal: Normally blocking takes 1 stamina. If the incoming attack is notably impactful, then it becomes a contest of strength between the attacker and the blocker, assuming the shield doesn't break.
Cardinal: For this shield, the property would be after the first block against an attack type, there is no strength check against that same attack type for subsequent blocks. Is it per fight or permanent?
Cardinal: It is permanent per user. So what happens if the user is KO'ed and someone else tries to use the shield? The new user cannot take advantage of the KO'ed user's experience.
Ledia: But does the shield now start leveling up with the new user and remembers the level with the old user?
Cardinal: It remembers. It works with summoner skills so that you could summon another hero's experience for the shield to block for you. I like that idea. Even though I have no summoner skill. You can learn it XD
Casey: Even if we don't try to add a rarity label, the way Cardinal described it would make the shield unique. Each shield has it own memory.
Tanya: But it is irrelevant whether "Sapphire Shields" are rare, but the one you used is unique. I don't have a better word for it.
Zoey: Could we call that type of items legendary? If what makes it special is the experience it has. I'd take that.
Tanya: I think you need a section to define what "legendary" and "gear" mean, as opposed to other keywords.
Casey: I think they can go to @Cardinal I think it is more relevant here. How about both? With JQuery? Alright. But not now. I have another question.
Casey: So we have a card for the treasure.I think something should be done about the quest to get that treasure. How do we get that treasure? We will know when we actually get it! XD
Tanya: @StarTree, could you list field log entries we did for Sapphire Shield?
StarTree: Done! There is a key material, the Sapphire, that had not been obtained because it is not clear if enchantment are compatible or need to be merged!
Evelyn: That reminds me that we need to ask the sages something about it. 😖 I already asked the crafters guild about it. They said they are looking into it. I think we should also ask the elves about it. It is the other side of the compatibility question. You were asking whether sapphire enchantment is the only thing we need. I am asking whether we could de-enchant an existing enchantment at the slot so we don't need to merge them. Ok. Ok, I asked the question.
Casey: Can we represent the requirement in simple Quest terms? Such as go to location X, defeat Y, obtain Z, then craft W ?
Zoey: Well, we know the location. That is Sapphire mine shaft 8 level 9 chamber 8.
Casey: And what is there?
Zoey: It is a whole dungeon there XD
Casey: What monster type is the boss?
Ledia: It gave us so much trouble it better be something big.
Evelyn: But we haven't seen it yet. It could be something innocent. 😅
Casey: Who has that skill that can identify what monsters are in a dungeon? Isn't that Ledia? I have monster recognition. It means if I see a monster during a fight I can tell what elemental affinity it has. I can't tell what monsters are in a dungeon. I think that would be an Orcale skill.
Sasha: I've got just the right thing! Oracle cards! And I see... A Blue Dragon that is immune to percing and lightning attacks!
Evelyn: I hope that we are not attacking creatures at their home for no reason. 😖 Then let's say it is evil. I hope that we are not making creatures evil just so that we can fight them. 😖 Evelyn, you're making this really difficult.
Tanya: It is a challenge. We have to show our worth then be rewarded with the required sapphire. I like that better. 😊
Ledia: But once you get the sapphire, you can pass it to anyone? That doesn't make sense.
⭐
Tanya: The creature can see how it is used and revoke its enchantment I'm okay with that.
Casey: It is much better than before. @StarTree, screenshot please. Done!
Zoey: This style setting is great, it looks good on both desktop and mobile.
Casey: I can't wait to for us to complete some treasure quests so we could do the story content.
Zoey: I nominate that to be best layout to show a card.
Zoey: Actually, maybe also for best About tab. Casey moved the charter down. That kep the Start tab more interesting.
Casey: I thnk Enemble has the best format right now. On desktop, my Start section would have a large blank space. Maybe I should move charter back. Oh wait. I can't. My avatar is transparent. It will look strange. Chapter 21 is now listed for Best Card Display!
Tanya: Do you plan to let a visitor play the challenge? Or is it for story only?
Casey: I only plan for showing the story so when we play the game we have a reference to look up what the treasure and dungeon was about. I see. For Arx Dungeon I want it to have a quiz feature. But I think that should be another thread. Casey thanks the team for the help.
202206261919|Arx Dungeon
June 26, 2022 Sunday 7:19 PM
Atx Dungeon
⭐
After some clean up at the command post, the Arx Dungeon is open again for training. The convention of using CHAT for RP and BUBBLE for Non-RP applies to this thread.
⭐
LRRH: Little Red Riding Hood opens a chamber and sees monsters, but can't identify what they are. I'm referring to this twitter post and its replies. "Shouting down a speaker at a designated event isn't exercising "free speech" in the same way that punching someone whose opinions you dislike isn't free speech. It is instead a coercive method to stifle discussion among people exercising their rights."
Ledia: Ledia arrives at the chamber. In the first post, comparing shouting down someone to punching someone was an exaggeration and probably not a helpful analogy. But is it at fault of any logical fallacy? Maybe Appeal to Emotion? The exaggeration was meant to connect something that is unknown (shouting down someone) to something that is clear (punching someone). Then somehow use that association for the argument. False Analogy is a type of fallacy. Anyone wants to define what monster it is? How about Twin Jesters? They look the same but they are actually different. If you can tell them apart then you can defeat them. How about an Evil Twin? You know one of them is correct, the question is whether the other is also correct. How about a Doppelganger? Who is impersonating one of us? I like that idea, but how exactly do we decide what we are seeing right now? I think it comes down to, do you want the situation to be us, the heroes seeing two things that just look similar, or seeing something that looks familiar but might be different? I think Ledia's idea is more interesting. What is the difference betwen "Evil Twin" and "Doppelganger"? Well an Evil Twin is evil. A doppelganger could just be mischevous. Sasha pleads to Tanya with a note written on a small piece of paper. Tanya reads the note and says, "I don't want to do that." Why not? It feels too intrusive. Doing so will make someone uncomfortable. She'll be fine. What is it? I guess I can't say it because the GM doesn't OK it. Question: is Tanya violating Sasha's freedom of speech? That's a good question. I would say no. Tanya didn't forbid Sasha from telling anyone. Tanya is just saying she won't adopt Sasha's idea. But Tanya's disapproval made Sasha unwilling to tell others. I'm just saving the idea for a plot twist if I get to GM about it. Do you mean that if Sasha were the GM right now, we will get to know what the idea is? That depends on whether Sasha decides to go ahead after knowing my opinion about it. I think... I would just ask the actual person about it. But you already know she won't say no if you ask her like that. She would comply even though that would make her uncomfortable. Umm... Is this about who I think we all know who it is about? If that's the case, I agree with Tanya. I'm not interested in Sasha's idea. I am guessing that Sasha will push too far. You are judging me by anecdote. That's a fallacy. That's not a fallacy when I am admitting that I don't know. But more importantly do you deny that you won't be pushing what is acceptable? ... Tanya, I think your convention is not working. If we keep going, the entire post will be in BUBBLEs. Okay. I'll move the plot along.
Tanya: Little Red Riding Hood, Ledia, and Zoey gather at the chamber and saw a strange sight. Down the hallway stood what they recognized to be Sasha. I knew it! Back at you! XD That wasn't the twist. My twist was kind of cute. GM, what is that Sasha doing? I'll let Sasha answer.
Sasha: That Sasha appears to be fighting monsters and getting EXP, doing the normal dungeon crawl.
LRRH: Little Red Riding Hood thought she was the first to unseal the chamber, but perhaps there was another entrance. Her suspicion has vanished. XD GM to players: suppose you see someone uses false analogy, how would you proceed? Referring to the post, it said that shouting down someone is a coercive method to stifle discussion. Whether it is "coercive" is debatable, but in that situation, it is undenably stifling discussion. So even if it used a false analogy, I wouldn't focus on it. Shouting down someone strifles discussion. That's a fact. Is strifling discussion the same as violating free speech?
Ledia: Ledia observes carefully to see if that Sasha is actually defeating monsters, and not an illusion that will lead the monsters to attack them from behind if they turn their backs.
⭐
Sasha: Sasha looks further down the hall and sees a pudding and nukes it. The post there says if freedom of speech is treated as a personal right, then whoever speaks first may speak indefinitely, and no one may interrupt. That is slippery slope argument. Something that is a personal right doesn't mean that they have a right to do so in ways that prevent others from also doing so. Sasha KOs the pudding and gains a sack of gold coins.
⭐
Ledia: Following Sasha's lead, Ledia caught up to her and attacked the next monster, a succubus. Here, ad hominem was used: "You sound like the kids who flunked out of my undergrad law classes." GM to Ledia: Suppose you see someone using ad hominem, what would you do? I think I would just ignore them because if they start doing that it is no longer a discussion. Then what did you mean when you said you would attack the succubus? Good question. Ledia swung at the succubus, but the succubus entangled her sword and drained Ledia of 1 hitpoint. Umm... aren't succubus supposed to target men only? Not in my dungeon. That answer... A succubus is just a demon type humanoid monster that has a charm skill. To be honest I'm glad Evelyn isn't here for the awkward moment. XD So is Ledia charmed? We'll see.
Zoey: Zoey sees the situation and wants to break the succubus's hold on Ledia, by drawing Ledia's attention away. I hunter mark the Ghost at the adjacent hallway for the group. Here the OP tried defending her original post by saying that shouting down someone and punching them are really the "same way" if you only consider its effect on suppressing speech. That is special pleading for the readers to ignore the obvious difference and error in the original analogy. The OP was moving the goal post instead of admiting that it was a bad analogy. The Ghost is Hunter Marked. The succubus's hold on Ledia has weakened.
⭐
Evelyn: Evelyn arrives at the dungeon after getting an alert that a party member has been attacked. If I see someone moving the goal post, I would probably allow them to make the amendment and continue the discussion if it still has merit. Misunderstanding is forgivable.
Evelyn: I cast Ascension. I tap in to understand the ghost's circumstance and let it pass on. Evelyn, roll with advantage for skill ranking placement please. I'll use the happy dice 😊 Evelyn: ➖➕ Evelyn, your ascension skill is rank B and you succeeded. How do you want to do this?
Evelyn: A warm light surrounds the ghost, releasing it from its chains of negative feelings. The ghost ascends to a happy place. Evelyn, you may draw a loot card. What is this? 😳 This is from Dungeon!, courtesy of Sasha! Thank you. 😊 The ghost leaves behind a silver cup. Evelyn is recognized to have cleric skill Ascension Rank B. What am I supposed to do with it? 😅 You are a cleric, so when you get 10K gold you win. The silver cup is worth 1K, so you just need 9K more. Ok 😅 Is that official? What happens if we win? We'll see. But that game mechanism isn't really cooperative. Should I have tried helping Ledia? XD I think we can just collect loots and win together when we have enough as a team 😊 I'm fine with that XD What class am I? You are considered a cleric also. Okay. When Sasha KO'ed the pudding, why didn't you ask what she would do against slippery slope arguments in real life? That was a Level 1 monster. Spotting it is enough. Then can I collect loot also? Now that we have evidently and completely ignored the succubus?
Tanya: As the heroes regrouped, Ledia discovers a treasure chest near where the succubus was. Ledia, you may draw a Level 3 loot card. I draw. Ledia obtains a Gold Cup. At this rate it will take us a long time to get enough gold. The dungeon is on-going.
Zoey: I want to get some treasure also XD
LRRH: Let's look for one more before going back. What about the Doppelganger at the beginning? We ignored it but didn't get any loot. Your character didn't identify any doppelganger.
Zoey: Zoey asks Sasha if she would join their party.
Sasha: Sasha declines, "It is probably faster if I solo and keep my loot for myself."
LRRH: Sasha, how much gold point do you need from the dungeon?
Sasha: I am wizard, so 30K.
Zoey: Sasha is never leaving the dungeon! XD
Sasha: I nuke Zoey. Because she was using slippery slope argument. That was a joke! XD I would allow it for training purpose. XD
Tanya: Sasha nuked a group of puddings that were spawning near Zoey. Zoey was caught in the AE attack. Zoey takes 1 damage. Sasha gains another sack of gold.
Ledia: Sasha, I'm just curious. How did you get in before Red unsealed the chamber?
Sasha: I didn't. The chamber was unsealed when I entered.
Zoey: What time did you check in, Sasha?
Zoey: Zoey asked this question because she knew that there is a check-in process for the dungeon, everyone can see who checked in before them. And from the record, the check-in order for today was Tanya, Red, Ledia, then Zoey.
Evelyn: Evelyn felt that the atmosphere was a little strange and started healing Zoey and Ledia back to full health. 😖
Sasha: I was here since yesterday.
Zoey: But you said you entered after Red unlocked the chamber.
Sasha: She already unlocked it in March. Three months ago. Sasha said and showed them the date.
Zoey: 🤔 Zoey knows that this Sasha must be lying somehow. Why would the dungeon leave the chamber unsealed for 3 months?
Sasha: I nuke Zoey. I nuke her for using Personal Incredulity. Even if that is abnormal, that doesn't point to my lying. It just means that the dungeon was mismanaged. Sasha, what monster do you choose for Personal Incredulity? Devil on the shoulder. It promotes a person's unjust thoughts. Sasha KO's a demon spider that had found its way to ensare Zoey. Zoey takes 1 damage. Sasha gains a third sack of gold. I am a monster magnet... XD
Evelyn: I heal Zoey. 😅
Ledia: Ledia knows that that's not what the date means. The date Sasha showed was the date the monsters were sighted before they were sealed. But even if Sasha misunderstood about that date, there was no contradiction yet.
Ledia: Sasha, did you check-in yesterday?
Ledia: Ledia asked that because if she did there would be a record.
Sasha: Why are you asking me all these questions? Are you suspecting me of something? Why didn't you suspect Evelyn? She is not casting Protect like her usual self, and she got a new skill out of nowhere.
Evelyn: 😖
Zoey: OMG No way! XD
Ledia: Ledia spots a Stone Gargoyle coming to life and pulverizes it. Sasha is avoiding the question by asking questions. Ledia destroys the Stone Gargoyle, which drops a Magic Sword.
Ledia: Zoey, could you check the check-in record from yesterday?
Sasha: So what if I didn't check-in? I don't have to tell everyone everything I do.
Zoey: I think I spot Scarecrow near Sasha. I release barrage in Sasha's direction. Do you mean to hit Sasha in the AE attack? No, I'll be nice. For now. You may draw a Level 1 loot card. Zoey KO's the Scarecrow, which drops a badge for moving through all secret doors. Finally! XD
Ledia: Two things. 1) If you didn't check-in, it is within protocol to detain you for further questioning before you can exit the dungeon. 2) It should be virtually impossible to sneak in without checking in. This is not the Arx Vault, but security is still very high. You are unlikely to have the skill necessary to sneak in. There is reasonable susipcion that you are from the dungeon.
Sasha: That makes no sense. If I were a doppelganger and wants to get out, why won't I copy one of you who checked in?
Ledia: Maybe you didn't know about the barriers. It is getting late. Can this game continue later? Then are we all locked inside the dungeon? I'll think about what happened until the next time. Or I can...
Sasha: Cardinal. Pixie.
Cardinal: All players are exhausted. All players are KO'ed to be respawn at their home point. Arx Dungeon is now closed for the day.
Zoey: That is so Sasha. Can a Doppelganger mimic so much? XD If they did that would be a correct analogy, not a false one... Good night everyone. 😊 Tanya thanks the players.

LRRH: Under the hood we are carrying the changes that Black made for a new Harbor master notes, and changes Cardinal started making. Harbor master doesn't use flex yet. 202206300849|Monster Hunt
June 30, 2022 Thursday 8:49 AM
Fort Arsenal
⭐
Currency Exchange and Shaft Elevator have the same underlying issue. We have tried questing at it for a long time but have not been making any progress. What is the problem? What are we fighting? Tanya receives
⭐
Ledia: Aren't you almost there? I'm trying to zero in on the monster, but haven't succeeded yet.
StarTree: It's a hit! Tanya, you finally got a hit!
Tanya: This is some progress, but still a long way from what we need.
Ledia: I thought you only need one thing and the quest is complete if you want to stop there. Anything else is really not necessary.
Tanya: In that case, what monster or what issue are we dealing with?
Ledia: The issue is, for some reason someone who should know something is not telling us. So we are left having to figure it out by ourselves.
Evelyn: I hope that we are not asking for something too simple. I hope people are not mad at us for asking. 😖
Ledia: That was what Vivi was saying...
Sasha: Queen Vivi was right. All hail to Queen Vivi! Are we RP-ing or not in this thread? I made it as a non-RP thread to discuss what we should do in the RP.
Tanya: Based on the result of Arx Dungeon, I want to extend the effort of personalifying issues as monsters to help us deal with them better.
Ledia: This is much easier with the logical fallacies. Here we don't even have a word for the situation we are in.
Evelyn: I think this is a quest where something is hidden and we are trying to look for it. Once we find it, we can share with others.
Ledia: Buy in reality we can't just share it with anyone. Or can we?
Tanya: We'll respect the regional request.
Ledia: Just to be clear, we could have just said Currency Exchange and Shaft Elevator are both spawn points. They spawn possibly different monsters, but the key to address both is the same. And the key item we need is lost and we need to find it by doing a lot of investigations.
Tanya: Yes.
⭐
Ledia: Then in FATE terminology, what we need to overcome are challenges relevant to Lore, Investigation, and Rapport skills. In Cardinal terminology, if we are struggling, then our skill ranks are about C. Or maybe even lower. That's solid! Nice job promoting FATE concepts! I also think that is a good representation of the situation.
Tanya: I see. How many skills are there in FATE?
Ledia: I think 18? Athetics, Burglary, Contacts, Crafts, Deceive, Drive, Empathy, Fight, Investigate, Lore, Notice, Physique, Provoke, Rapport, Resources, Shoot, Stealth, Will.
Tanya: I see. Any suggestion on what the key item we are looking for?
⭐
Zoey: It is ancient dwarven runes translator. There were runes along the elevator shaft, and Currency Exchange square has the same style of runes. It gives us unexpected results when we tested our spells. I find that to be very accurate, and I like the theme. 😅 Yes this is it.
Ledia: So we need Investigation skill to track down its meaning, Lore skills to digest the text we do find, and Rapport skills to try picking the brains of those people who should know what they mean, if they weren't occupied or had forgotten them.
Evelyn: Someone must know something, but they aren't telling us. We should study the text. I think we are bothering them. 😖
Tanya: Then this is what I see as the quest:
QUEST: Dwarven Runes Quest
Location: Currency Exchange Square and Elevator Shaft
Brief: Spawn points at those locations are spawning monsters with Accel from the ancient dwarven runes, making them very difficult to defeat. The plan is to patch the runes so that their powers are not used by monsters.
Skills: Investigation, Lore, Rapport
Boss: Pick pocket, something that levitates.
⭐
Zoey: Maybe the dwarven runes aren't just ancient, they are a type of thieves' cant or are being used as thieves cant. We aren't going against a single boss monster, but two thieves guilds. That's why no body is talking. Zoey receives
Ledia: Then, what we see as monster spawn points are really distractions for the thief guilds to operate?
Sasha: Great Aspects about the quest. 👍 Aspect is a FATE term.
Evelyn: I hope that it doesn't get too complicated. 😖
Ledia: But we know that their network runs deep. Those runes have ties to many store fronts.
Evelyn: I don't think they are all Thieves Cant. It could just be a common motif for decoration. 😅
Ledia: But it could be.
Sasha: Should Sylvia take this quest?
Zoey: Phantom Thief vs Thief Guilds XD
Ledia: What if she goes back...?
Evelyn: 😖
Tanya: Let's consider those ideas. Cardinal needs an upgrade to show stories per quest. This post will go toward that. Tanya thanks all for the discussion.
202206301013|Daily Quests
June 30, 2022 Thursday 10:13 AM
Magic Bakery
⭐
June is almost over, would it be have a small summary for each daily routine quest?
Ledia: Positivity time and Paladin Training are really the same thing. We are using the chant, "Everyday in every way I'm getting better and better." We use that for counting sets. It works much better than using numbers.
Zoey: Won't miscount XD.
StarTree: StarTree will turn each item into a clickable thing to show details!
Sasha: I think quest open and close aren't really a thing. They should be part of Cardinal Quest.
Zoey: Why are we combining all of them all of a sudden?
Sasha: Doesn't that list look too long to you?
Zoey: Not really, although a litte disorienting after seeing the new layout at Chapter 27.
Zoey: Actually, what is the difference between the list of Daily Quests at HQ and at the calendar?
Evelyn: I like the one at the Calendar/sidebar because it shows what we are doing in the month.
⭐
LRRH: I think we want to keep the one per calendar so we can see changes over time. The one at HQ might be more general and overall, and may have an overall leaderboard. The one at calendar will reflect actual participants in that month.
Zoey: For the record, the study group finished reading about Silva at the beginning of the month, then had been readint FATE since. We finished reading FATE accelerated quite quickly, then moved to FATE core. We are about halfway in FATE core. So that will probably continue for half of July. And we are looking for something else to read. Thank you for the summary!
Sasha: Reading and lunch hour are basically the same activity.
⭐
Ivy: I think at Calendar we can combine them to reflect when we do it. At HQ they can be more distinct so that it is easier to see what the quest is by itself regardless how we combine them when we do it. 👍
Ledia: If we combine them, then we have morning, lunch, and the rest of the day: three sections?
Evelyn: I want to add a daily quest for tidying and important old content to the blog.
Ledia: About when would those activities be?
Evelyn: Maybe night? They don't take a lot of thoughts, I think. But we should do some everyday.
Ledia: We should have some good trigger for the habit to stick.
Evelyn: For tidying, how about when we leave or return to the guild house? And archiving will be triggered with importing field scores.
Tanya: For archiving, how about every time someone makes a post, they must archive something?
Ledia: That's a rather interesting idea.
Evelyn: What about days when we don't post anything?
Tanya: If you would still archive something on those day, maybe you would end up posting something.
LRRH: Little Red Riding Hood is slightly against Tanya's idea. If someone wants to discuss something, they should be able to just start a thread. You need to know a lot more on what's happening in the archive to be able to archive something.
Evelyn: Maybe for those who do know how to archive can archive before posting?
StarTree: Should StarTree do that? StarTree already makes the Status post everyday!
Tanya: I think that should be exempted. On the daily log, StarTree would get 1 point for making the status post. Anyone else who wants a point can also get one by importing a post. We don't need two status posts, so that column only has StarTree right now.
Evelyn: By if StarTree wants to import another post, she is not getting credits if it is just the same column.
LRRH: StarTree please add a column for Import. This will replace logging import activities in Guild Log.
Ivy: What if someone imports many posts in one sitting?
LRRH: How about if you import more than one thing in the same day, then use guild log for the extra? That shouldn't be very normal. Just trying to maintain that the task is not tied to anyone and it is optional. If you want to do it, there is a convenient way to log credits. But you don't need to do that to make a new post.
Ledia: That scoring doesn't take advantage of how "if you don't do it today, you miss the opportinity". For PT, if you don't go, you can't do more just to make it up.
⭐
LRRH: I think importing is different, the thing isn't going anywhere. I don't worry anyone trying to "procrastinate" and import a lot at once. That's okay if that is more efficient. If someone wants to do that every day, the scoring should be easy. So it is a choice. If you want to do it, you may do it the way you want. The scoring at the end is the same. The scoring is not nudging you to favor either style.
Ledia: Very compelling explanation.
Tanya: Agreed.
⭐
Vivi: StarTree, can you automatically tally the number of credits a person gets for that particular daily quest? If they do it once a day, it should be 30 for June. Roger! LRRH receives StarTree thanks everyone!

202207011914|FlexShow
July 1, 2022 Friday 7:14 PM
Whale Nursery
⭐
Based on comments yesterday, I added a function FlexShow in the javascript. There are some added effects that I want people to try. I think it is better if the behavior is consistent across the blog.
Black: Effect 1: Objects in a flex container are normally split into three columns as shown on desktop. The width is enough to show a card. On mobile, those objects will show in one column.
Effect 2: When it is clicked, on desktop it will move up above the separate columns and expand to full width. This is the main feature that makes it functional on desktop. On mobile, the content will just show in-place.
Effect 3: By our convention, if a 100 pixel square thumbnail exists, it will shrink to 50 pixel square when the content is expanded. When the content is collapsed again, the thumbnail will not restore to 100 pixel. This is intentional to show that the content has been visited.
Black: You may try it at .
Tanya: This is very nice, thank you.
Vivi: For the Guide section, could you add an avatar to the content once expanded? I want to know who is speaking.
Black: I added 100px for step 1, 50px for step 2, and kept step 3 to have none for comparison.
Vivi: 100px.
Tanya: Black, are you going to import the story?
Black: When I get around to it next time.
Tanya: I want to see how you would format it.
Vivi: Your guide section is too nested. The steps shouldn't be in another cheesboard.
Black: I am planning to merge Problems and Failures to the same tab and see how that goes.
Vivi: I'll wait.
LRRH: Can Chapter 22 get some love and get this formatting for story posts?
Black: I don't imagine it to be any different.
LRRH: But for stories, the actual content is in the Archive. Then the user needs to click it twice?
Black: I think in that case you would want to write a synopsis. So at the uppermost level, that would be the Quest. The reader clicks on that and it expands and shows the Quest synopsis. If they want to read the story, then then click on the Query link to load the links.
LRRH: Right now at , the synopsis is already part of the upper button. The viewer clicks once to load the query list, then click again to view the post.
⭐
Ivy: Black, I think they are asking for a function that does what your function did, but also queries a list and list them inside while expanding at the same time.
Black: I can kind of see that, but I feel I am OOM.
Ivy: Then I'll do it. If you don't mind, I might just modify your FlexShow function so that it will also run a query if it exists in the structure. Then we don't have to remember what function is for what. They are all the same one function, it does everything depending what is around it. Ivy receives
Black: It's all yours. 👍
Ivy: My plan is to store a query string in the same button that the viewer clicks. Then the function will load the content to the last div inside the sibling. If someone who has stories to post wants to vet it first.
Tanya: So you are asking whether Querying is the last thing of the expanded content.
Ivy: Essentially yes.
Tanya: That seems to be the convention so far. But I think we are missing the previous and next buttons. After reading a section of the story, the viewer can't continue to the next segment.
Ivy: How about I try to make it so that the function scans the content of the sibling and looks for a tag with a command to query. For each one it sees, it does the query and replace the tag with the queried content?
Tanya: I think that is what we need.
LRRH: Is this difficult?
Ivy: It shouldn't be too difficult. I'll start now.
🕰️ [10:07 PM] 🕰️
Ivy: @LRRH, @Tanya, it's done. Have fun! Little Red Riding Hood loves you.

202207031944|Universal Access
July 3, 2022 Sunday 7:44 PM
Philosophy Arena
⭐
In Stewardism, there is a concept of open access to basic needs. This means that people have a right to reasonable share of natural resources, and do not have a right to destroy resources that are needed by others.
Arena: On the other hand democratic socialism has the concept of Universal basic Income. Open Access and UBI are not the same. Or are they? Who wants to come duke it out? Fina's got to fight XD
Evelyn: I'm interested in this discussion but I am speaking from some one exploring Arcacianism, not socialism.
Tanya: Fina, what is your challenge question?
Fina: Universal Access: Yes or no? What does your philosophy have about people trying to make their own living?
Evelyn: The word "Universal" is ambiguous. It can mean too many things for people to attack by misinterpretation.
Fina: In Stewardism, there are private properties and public properties. Public properties are numerous and watched over under stewardship principles. Whether something can be a piece of personal property is a matter of abundance and necessity. Things that are necessary fall under open access stewardship. It means that you may use what you need. You may not harm the sustainability. If you keep others away from fair sharing, you lose your right to access that resource.
LRRH: This might not be entirely related to what Fina wants to discuss here, but I want to reply to someone taking about UBI here.. Summary of that thread. The OP said UBI and automationa are not compatible because the more high-tech jobs become, the more complicated new jobs there will be for people to work on. Then I replied that UBI and automation are compatible within the context of distributing basic things the automation can afford. Then a third person says nothing is automatic and that is utopia. They are using some kind of logical fallacies... Although that is off topic. Fina, what do you want to discuss?
Fina: Let's reply first. This has nothing to do with Arena, but let's see how has the best reply.
Evelyn: I think the third tweeter is worried about inflation. If basic necessities are sufficiently and affordably produced by automation, then they would be free. You wouldn't need to pay people UBI for them to extra for what they need and is automated and affordable, but also things that are not automated and in scarcity. That reply would be too long for Twitter. Sorry. 😖
Ledia: Is length all the matters? How do we want to judge what reply is the best? What effect should it have?
Fina: We can use the same scale where Celebrative reply has 5 points. It is pretty hard to be celebrative without sounding sarcastic 😅
Sasha: How does UBI prevent people from not doing their own fair share?
LRRH: It does not. You are allowed to slack off. You don't need to do anything for things that are free.
LRRH: For example, sunlight is free. You may get as much sun tan or sunburn as you want.
🕰️ [7:44 AM] 🕰️
LRRH has replied to twitter.
LRRH: "UBI is compatible with automation means that if you want to take over the world using automation, having a government that implements UBI is the next best thing to permanent government subsidies. 👑" Sorry for going off-topic. LRRH thanks for the discussion.

QUEST ID: 202207041052

202207041108|Book of Life
July 4, 2022 Monday 11:08 AM
World Tree
⭐
World Tree is formally commissioning heroes assistance in crafting the magical directory, dubbed the Book of Life to assist the World Tree Root Quest to survey the realm. This thread adopts Tanya's convention to use Chat for RP and Bubble for non-RP.
Zoey: This is Zoey the Ranger from World Tree Quest Center. For the quest charter, please go to World Tree Quest Center and look for Quest Charter ID 202207041052 in the Root Quests section. I like where this is going, Chapter 13 is looking really nice! 😊
Evelyn: Evelyn checks in to World Tree Quest Center and read the Quest Charter: "World Tree Book of Life. Objective: Create a magical directory so that quest doers of the Root Quest can report the findings." What about the Albatross? I feel like building a directory here because everyone is here and I want to be useful and part of the story.
Tanya: Tanya asks Zoey, "We created the Albatross to shield us from direct attacks. Do you intend to drop that project?"
Zoey: The Albatross is still being built and maintained as a Leaf Quest. It is concurrent to all other quests. By the way, I already added a JQuery tag at the "Quest Card" to link to any thread tagged with [data-quest-202207041052], and tagged this thread with it. I see.
Sasha: Sasha stealthily watches the quest center. Arx Dungeon is dungeon crawl, Arena is PvP, what kind of quests are those here? It can be a mix of any kind. The Book of Life quest would appear to be a crafting quest, but if you say you need a key ingredient from a dungeon then it becomes a dungeon quest. I think it is better if it sticks to a theme so we know what to expect. How about a familiar raising theme? No where else can have that theme to the fullness. Pokemon XD I don't understand 😅 I hope it is not too complicated.
Zoey: Zoey the Ranger greets Evelyn. "The roots of the World Tree reaches many realms through portals where you could reach many powerful things. But the quest itself is only asking for anything that would work until it is upgraded further. Zoey receives What is the real life thing that we are collecting in the directory? It is concepts. @Seed is taking over @Concept to list all the concepts. Arcacianism already has a mascot. So if you want to define Arcacianism, in RP you could be describing the Arcacian Princess Squirrel. That doesn't sound too bad 😃 Not everything has to be a creature. It can be a plant, a material, a location, etc. Just a comment on how to translate real life situation into roleplay: After identifying the challenge in real life, you could define a challenge in the game with analogus aspects.
Evelyn: Evelyn cleared an area at the quest board so people may pin their findings similar to how the quest board currently works. She knows this is probably not what people ultimately want, but she believes that more ideas will come when people start using it. Evelyn has started making Book of Life. Evelyn earns the recognition "The first step is the hardest." Evelyn receives For the ID, does it really matter what it is? The quest object has an ID "data-quest-yyyy-mmdd-hhmm". Do the other objects also need to be categorized? Would they need an ID like "data-xxxx-yyyy-mmdd-hhmm"? I think having no category is better. That can be an additional tag. Okay. I am convinced that we don't need "quest" in the ID. If we do that, it would only make sense if we put Quests and all other things in the same directory. and data-quest is a tag for querying. Will the content be RP or non-RP? I think RP. But you could use bubbles for non-RP information.
⭐
Ivy: Ivy passes by the quest center and shows Zoey and Evelyn a tome. "This is an example of what a directory might look like if you want some ideas." I am referring to the layout of Modzilla Developer Network, just as an example. I see many good features, like the scrollable sidebar on the left with expanable categories, the fixed section header on the right, and a search box on top. On the upper left there is a breadcrumb path that show how you could browse to the article from the root. Oh wow. I feel that we need this, for magic bakery.
⭐
Sasha: Sasha shows up and brings another tome for inspiration. What about the Pokemon index as reference? I hope we have enough pictures. 😄 The non-RP content is the most important for me. It should have concise definition if we will show it to someone.
Zoey: Let's start adding some entries to the directory so we have something to see while discussing. Zoey thanks all for answering the call.

202207062023|Scoreboard
July 6, 2022 Wednesday 8:23 PM
Arx Dungeon
⭐
This is Tanya calling for heroes to return to the Arx Dungeon for training. RP in CHAT and non-RP in BUBBLE, please.
Sasha: Sasha has arrived at the Training Dungeon. This time with proper sign in to avoid the drama. Sasha to Tanya: The page is seriously lacking pictures and colors. I propose that instead of doing the dungeon, we upgrade it first.
Zoey: Zoey arrives at the dungeon and sees Sasha at the check-in, there went the theory that they saw a doppelganger. Why didn't you keep the mystery going? XD I didn't feel like playing a fake Sasha, A fake Sasha would be nice, so that is kind of pointless. XD 🤣
LRRH: Little Red Riding Hood wonders whether she should open a chamber.
Evelyn: Evelyn arrives and hope that they could continue the training together. What do we want to do? Go for another story session or just update the page?
⭐
Sasha: Sasha complains about the bland interior and reception area of the Dungeon. "If you want to call this The Arcade, it has got to be more colorful and exciting." It has been 10 days since the last time we had a dungeon session. I'll leave it to the group to decide.
⭐
Evelyn: I heard that for us to complete the training, we need to gather enough points from the dungeon. There is no sign anywhere about this. Is it official? Should we keep track of how many points we have and how many points we need?
Sasha: Yes, we should have a sign about that.
Sasha: I am playing solo as a mage. I need 30,000 points total. I have 2,250 points so far. So I'm going to put myself on a scoreboard.
Evelyn: It looks really nice! 😍
Zoey: The bar is so long for Sasha XD
Ledia: "Amazing how a little bit of color makes so much difference." Ledia arrives at the dungeon.
Evelyn: Should we all put our scores on it, too?
Tanya: The heroes put their scores on a chart at the reception area. The bland entrance now has some color and was filled with happiness. Tanya awards merits to Evelyn and Sasha.
Evelyn: Maybe we should make it a habit that every time we come we make something a little better?
Ledia: Yes I agree. maybe that should be like the "entrance fee".
Evelyn: I think if it is not mandatory, people tend to do something better because when they do, they do it out of the joy of doing it. Evelyn receives
LRRH: Should we start or should we call it a day? I heard that if you get KO'ed in the dungeon, you could lose ALL of your points.
Evelyn: Is that official? 😅
Tanya: I am letting Sasha adjust the rules for training purposes.
Sasha: For training purposes, let's keep the rule where if you are KO'ed, including getting warped back from exhaustion, then you lose one item of your choice.
LRRH: Then I am opening a chamber since I don't have an item to lose yet.
Ledia: Um... but then what is the point of entering...?
LRRH: Right. 🐣
Zoey: Can we start in the morning or in the weekend then? I don't want to get KO'ed. It's already 11 PM. XD
Ledia: I second that.
Tanya: @StarTree, could you schedule an event for this weekend?
StarTree: Roger!
Sasha: As the heroes discuss the plan to regroup in the weekend, Sasha nukes a pudding near Ledia that had leaked through the door that LRRH was half opening. Even if you can lose an item by getting KO'ed, there is still a point in entering because you could get more than one item before you are KO'ed, or you could get an item of more points before you get KO'ed. Ledia and Red were both guilty of Slippery Slope. Sasha vaporizes the small group of puddings. Ledia and LRRH lose 1 hit point each. LRRH is KO'ed. 😅 I'll draw a Level 2 card because the pudding was from both Ledia and Red. Sasha gets a sack of gold for 750 points. Sasha's secret quest to nuke someone everyday is complete. Sasha signs out. XD
Evelyn: Evelyn signs out before it is too late. 😅 Tanya thanks the heroes for coming and looks forward to them returning this weekend.

👑 Top: 14 P4 13 Black 13 Zoey 13 Evelyn 9 Vivi 9 StarTree 9 Tanya 8 Ledia 6 Cardinal 6 James 6 Sylvia 6 LRRH 5 3B 4 Fina 4 Sasha 4 Arcacia 4 Casey 3 Helen 3 Ken 3 V 2 Vladanya 2 Kisaragi 2 Natalie 2 Ivy
Take good notes to save your future self.
At the Whale Nursery, we have been maintaining alchemist notes as MSW scrolls. Is there a better way? This quest is about finding better ways to maintain the alchemist notes.
🎪 Quest Objectives
⭐ Share and evaluate ideas to improve alchemist notes
⭐ Credit Category: EPIC I Innovation.
202303290023|🔮 Reference Writing
🍀 General Tricks
⭐ MS Project 20220624: Sasha: This is just a link on how to use MS Project. It seems like something that might help but we aren't doing.
⭐ HTML: Space for Text Search 202220629: Black: To help text search on HTML text file (as you are editting the HTML file directly as opposed to viewer the result on a browser), add spaces before and after a keyword within formatting tags to reduce the number of matches. For example, do <b> keyword </b> instead of <b>keyword</b>.
👑 Ranking Zoey's Quiver (HTML TSN) is an HTML document with a filter box and a cache area.
⭐ Advantages
⭐ Works well with other applications open
⭐ Quick Information
⭐ Cache Area This is our most efficient way. Put notes in cache area as they are used, then archive them to the proper TSN.
⭐ Search highlight 20220627 HTML TSN can use the built-in search word highlight function of the browser.
⭐ Allow Images 20220624 Although we are not using that feature, HTML TSN can easily include images without making the file big.
🐣 Disadvantages
🐣 Can't directly edit what you see
🐣 Can't open links to folder or file directly 20220630 There is a trick of using HTML to open a file dialog. There you could then paste the path and open files. But you need to store the path somewhere. We haven't found a way to set the path for the file dialog. 20221002 This isn't really a big problem since we still have Field Log.
💡 Ideas
💡 Remember collapsed sections 20220718 It would be better if HTML TSN can remember which sections are collapsed.
💡 Interface Upgrade 20220622 How about using the same search menu interface as w3schools
🏳️ Include 20220628 Tried using include but it did not work. w3schools
🍡 Evelyn's Staff
Evelyn's Staff is a spreadsheet where the columns are quest areas and rows are common subquests roughly ordered by when they should be completed. The HTML TSN is an HTML document with a filter box and a cache area.
⭐ Advantages
⭐ Easy to follow Evelyn's Staff captures the experience on what subtasks were done at other quest areas. This reduce the time on thinking about what to do.
⭐ More thorough checks Things worth checking get their own row on the spreadsheet. This prompts the quest doer to check

OLD TSN (Word)

TSN OneNote
2023-06: The TSN is implemented in OneNote, but so far it seems harder to use than the Word version. OneNote allows searching separated words, but the way it displays search result seems worse than Word. It is difficult to cycle through each match, perhaps the searcher needs to rethink how to use OneNote search to take advantage of the fact that it can match separated words.

Tanya's Field Map

Tanya's Field Log

⭐ Viewed on Spreadsheet.
⭐ Logs field activities An activity entry has a timestamp, quest, subquest, doer, and other info. It can be sort by time in either way, but it seems the most convenient when it is sorted in ascending order because when used with freeze plane, it is easy to show three regions all at once: Header, the log, and the footer.
⭐ Tallies field credits

Ideas

💡 Spreadsheet for storing information Use a spreadsheet with a column for the category. Add more columns as needed for sub categories. Add a link to a folder for attachments and documents.
💡 Flag Column if the data is listed rows, make column(s) and mark it with the rows you want to show on different views. Then use data filter feature to switch between them.
🏳️ Sasha's Planner
This planner was done in MS Word. It was convenient for reporting daily progress. But by August 2022 we stopped using is and use HTML TSN instead.
🏳️ Need Formatting This planner needs proper sizing for it to be convenient, but MS word does not remember the format for each document. It was inconvenient to set it every time it is opened.
🏳️ No Cache The Cache area of HTML TSN was more convenient.
🏳️ No Tallying Tanya's Field log in Excel has automatic tally. It is became our most advanced planning tool.
Updating node format. For Personal Knowledge Management, what about Obsidian? https://www.xda-developers.com/best-tools-effective-personal-knowledge-management-system/ 202208180954|⚓ Harbor 202207212151|🗃️ Guides {"id":"202207082239","parentid":"202301080924","parentname":"Writing Magic","icon":"🎯","title":"Alchemist's Notebook","subtitle":"Reference Writing","img":"https://cdn.pixabay.com/photo/2017/11/23/08/02/the-middle-ages-2972278_960_720.jpg","music":"","author":"Black","description":"","card-text":"Take good notes to save your future self."}
202207082340|Quest Formatter
July 8, 2022 Friday 11:40 PM
Whale Nursery
2022-0708-2340
⭐
This is just a quick announcement that the Script Maker has been updated to support generating Quest posts. At the script maker, you may now enter the title, url, and quest id, then copy and paste the HTML code. After confirming the format of a quest post, the plan for the next step is to use tags for javascript to do what the script maker does.
Black: So far, the necessary parts for a "Quest Card" seems to be: Title, ID, Objective, and Credits.
Black: The card itself will fetch comments tagged with the same ID.
Black: Therefore, once the quest card is created, if you want to talk about the quest, you could just go to chat and tag your thread with the quest ID. Then when someone reads the quest posting, they can see your message.
Zoey: This mechaniism seems related to Book of Life quest for World Tree. So I'm tagging this thread. 👍
Black: This thread itself is about the format of a "Quest Card". If we have a standard format, then we can do less each time defining a quest.
⭐
Ivy: I think the purpose of separating data from its presentation code is to make it EASIER to change the presentation as we like. So the order you described is the opposite. What we could do instead, is to start defining new tags. It is up to the formatter on how to format and display that quest data. Potentially there can be multiple formatters. Either the chapter or the viewer can select the formatter.
Tanya: This would solve a very important problem that I face. Sometimes I want to see a quest in RP format, sometimes I want to see it in non-RP format. If there are two formatters, then I can switch between RP and non-RP. Correct.
Black: It is not clear to me what initiates the translation.
Ivy: Maybe we need to create or upgrade a more general object viewer. The function of the object viewer is that you feed it an ID, then it fetches the data and represents it. You can then click on different format types and it will format the display accordingly. Seems like the way to go. Black thanks all for the great inputs.

202207090858|Arx Dungeon Day 2
July 9, 2022 Saturday 8:58 AM
Arx Dungeon
⭐
Arx Dungeon is calling for heroes to resume training. To recap, the heroes are asked to identify and defeat monsters in the dungeon. The scoring system currently serves no training purposes, and is for entertainment only. Please use CHAT for RP and BUBBLE for non-RP
LRRH: Little Red Riding Hood opened a chamber and saw some monsters. Refering to this thread at Twitter, where a poster said Capitalism has nothing to do with poverty.
LRRH: What do I need to do to get some points?
Tanya: First you need to identify the monster. Is this burden of proof? If you follow the conversation, they were making a claim that Capitalism lifted people out of poverty, but when questioned, they asked others to prove them wrong instead of proving that their claim is right. Burden of Prove doesn't have a monster yet. What would you like it to be? Something that weighs you down. A sand monster that can cast quick sand. How about mummies? Good idea! 🍰
LRRH: Little Red Riding Hood finds a mummy which is sprawning quicksand traps everywhere. Draw a Level 2 loot card please. It is level 2 because you identified a monster type that is new. Don't I need to defeat it before I draw a card? Spoting and avoiding it already worths a loot card. If you want to defeat it, it is additional. Then I draw a card. Avoiding the sand trap, Little Red Riding Hood got a sack of gold for 1000 points.
Ledia: Ledia arrives at the dungeon and sees the mummy and its sand. A general question: what does it mean to "defeat" a monster? If someone is using logical fallacies, are they really trying to learn anything or are they just pushing their view? Do we defeat them by ignoring all of them?
Evelyn: Evelyn arrives and casts Protect. The group now gains the effect of Protect. The speaker might be intentional to manipulate, but a bystander reading the thread could be manipulated. You could defeat the influence of their faulty logic by counter posting something. A response free of logical fallacies is worth 2 levels. I responded something. "In Monopoly, a player may own as many places they can and collect rent from those who have none. This mechanism creates poverty even if the players get $200 for passing GO. Any ism without rules against such mechanism creates poverty. Capitalism is just one of them." Then in terms of this game, you have defeated this mummy and you can draw another level 2 card. I'm really curious how Red fights a mummy.
LRRH: Little Red Riding Hood summons a giant pudding, which eats the mummy and mobs up the sand. The chamber is now clean. Little Red Riding Hood gets a Silver Ring of 2000 points.
Evelyn: Cute! 😊
Sasha: Meanwhile, Sasha looks into another chamber. In the posts leading to this, Repro argued that the record of how Egyptians moved rocks to build things were not enough to show how the Pyramids were built. This is No True Scotsman. In terms of monsters, I would say this is just a locked door or treasure chest. It is asking for the specific key to unlock it, which they know or presume to not exist. But there are in fact more ways to crack the lock. Good analogy.
Sasha: Sasha see s a locked treasure chest and nukes the lock. The treasure opens. Sasha gets a sack of gold for 1000 points. What's with Sasha and all the gold? Gold is all she ever gets! XD 1000 pts is almost as good as it gets for Level 2. Speaking from someone who knows all the loots. Evelyn thanks Sasha for setting up the dungeon. Sasha thanks StarTree for making an inventory of all the loots. Wee~!
Zoey: Zoey arrives. Seeing that Red and Sasha had already got some points, Zoey feels that she should really catch up. XD I think this is Composition/Division, because the argument appears to be, "All creatures can't be vegan in an ecosystem, therefore it is pointless for humans to be vegan."
Zoey: Zoey see another treasure chest, but could tell that it is a mimic, and fires a barrage at it. Too bad Sylvia had already decided the monster. XD Since it is the first time we see an example in the game, you may draw a level 2 card. I'll take that XD The mimic is KO'ed. Zoey gets a second Secret Door Card for 500 points. Why me? XD Jinx back at you! 😉
Zoey: Do these cards have a use in the training? If not they must be the worst loot. XD
StarTree: There is worse!
Zoey: No way! XD
Tanya: Having found two secret door cards, Zoey wondered if those cards are worth more than they appear. Zoey, I am open to suggestions if you want some other rewards or recognitions. I'll think about it. XD Whose turn is it? I think it is Ledia's turn. I opened a chamber but found nothing. 😖 I also opened a chamber but found nothing. I assume we just keep opening until we find something. Could we resume in the afternoon? The log for Dungeon has many links that do not belong to @Dungeon anymore. Yes we could resume in the afternoon.
🕰️ [3:28 PM] 🕰️
Evelyn: Evelyn returns to the dungeon and finds a Jack-o-Lantern. A loaded question was asked here: "How much philosophy do you have to ignore to pretend that "moral facts" are things that exist". Instead of asking a loaded question, the Tweeter could have cited references to philosophy claimed to be ignored, or ask the original Tweeter to confirm their intention.
Evelyn: I cast Protect III around the Jack-o-Lantern to contain it.
Tanya: Evelyn, roll with advantage for skill check please. This is the first time you use Protect III. Evelyn: ➕➕ 😃 Evelyn is recognized for Protect III, Rank A. Evelyn's Protect III trapped the flaming Jack-o-Lantern.
Evelyn: Ledia.
Ledia: I slice it with my great sword.
Tanya: Ledia, roll with advantage please Ledia: ➕➕
Tanya: Ledia's sword slices through the lantern in perfect halves, which exploded inside Protect III. A beautiful halo lighted up around the barrier where Ledia's sword passed. Evelyn spotted a new monster type and provided an alternative response. Please draw a Level 3 card. Evelyn obtains a Silver Ring of 2000 pts. Good job bringing the game back, Tanya.
Ledia: Ledia looks down the hallway. There seemed to be something lurking there. On the same topic that Evelyn cited, is there such a thing as "moral fact"? Given a set of moral rules that can be objectively judged, the judgment on whether a situation meets or violates those rules can be considered a fact. This is the same as saying having defined numbers a certain way, "2+2=4" can be considered a fact under those rules. Therefore, the OP's question could be valid. Fair enough.
Ledia: I got a better look at the hallway, and see what I believe to be Red fighting a monster. There was no sign of the monster left. Red had apparently defeated it. In this thread, the Tweeter that Red was responding to deleted their post. From what is left, it seems that the deleted post was using Slippery Slope or Composition/Division. Because the tweets were deleted, this doesn't make a good example for training. Please choose another example. Hmm... I sent that link to @Arena. I want to highlight somewhere that every time someone boasts about capitalism, they need to be asked whether taxation and national military are capitalistic concepts. What they call capitalism is at best a hybrid.
Tanya: So far Ledia had not been able to find any monster. Maybe this is not her day. Ledia, I give you one more chance to find a monster, else I think we should call it game today.
Evelyn: I bless Ledia to give her advantage to locate monster. I am assuming that the newer ones would be more relevant because before, @Dungeon was really @Concept, so Red would be tagging the Tweets for another reason.
Ledia: With Evelyn's blessing, Ledia opens the door of the next room. With or without monster, this should be the last room to clear for today.
Ledia: And she finds a group of Puddings and a Reaper. This is an example of Slippery Slope. The Tweeter argued that if work is voluntary, then people won't work because they prefer to relax. Therefore socialism won't work. But that situation is not different from a person stranded on a deserted island. Their desire to relax is kept in check by their needs to get food and supplies. In other words, they would work for themselves, as Red said in the response. And this is an example of Strawman argument. The OP never said that there would be profit, but the Tweeter assumed that there would be and created a situation that seems unfair in order to attack. Ledia, draw one Level 2 and one Level 1 card please. To not make a Strawman argument, the Tweeter could have asked, "Can people make profit in socialism? If so, how do you split profit in socialism?" Seek first to understand can also avoid the Slippery Slope argument. The Tweeter could have asked, "Are people eligible to welfare? If so, who provides welfare?" If the Tweeter had waited for a reply, they would learn that their assumption were incorrect. Someone knows how to play this game XD Draw a Level 3 and a Level 2 card instead please. How do I get a level 4 card? I would say if you give an actual reply, that would worth another level. But this thread is old and Red had already replied, so that doesn't apply in this case. Hmm... So it has to be current.
Ledia: As the Reaper swung its scythe at Ledia's shadow, Ledia summoned Tiamat to redirect the scythe to the Puddings. Ledia roll once please. Ledia: ➕ How do you want to do this?
Ledia: In one smooth swing, the scythe slices through the puddings and back at the Reaper. The Puddings and the Reaper are KO'ed. Ledia obtains the Reaper's Scythe (500 pts) and an ESP Medallion (500 pts). Those are literally the lowest value loots of Level 2 and Level 3, even if Tanya upgrade the Magic Sword to Reaper's Scythe. Watch out, Sasha, you could be next. XD
Tanya: With treasure to show for their progress, the heroes resealed the dungeon and returned to the lobby. Ledia and Sasha tie at 4000 pts, followed by Little Red Riding Hood and Evelyn at 3000 pts, then Zoey at 1000 pts. Until next time. Tanya thanks everyone for playing and setup.

LRRH: 3B, could you draw "WonderWoman". I was thinking maybe we could give her a picture for her birthday this month.
3B: Do you mean the kind that she could color? Yes.

Puzzle

You have been shrunk to the height of a nickel and is placed inside a blender. You mass is reduced so your density is preserved. The blender blades will start rotating in 1 minute. What do you do?

Source

📒 Are You Smart Enough to Work at Google? Page 4

202207270921|🧩 Puzzles 202207270913|📒 Google Puzzles
202207101129|Jumba You
July 10, 2022 Sunday 11:29 AM
Puzzle Land
⭐
Hey hey hey! It's your puzzle master Mikela! The style of this post is different from before because the layout at @Puzzle has changed!

PUZZLE

You have been shrunk to the height of a nickel and is placed inside a blender. You mass is reduced so your density is preserved. The blender blades will start rotating in 1 minute. What do you do?
Amelia: Wake up from the nightmare.
Ivy: Do we still use the buzzer if we are trying to answer the puzzle? Yes please! Ivy: I just jump out of the blender. Very roughly speaking, every creature that can jump can jump about the same height regardless their size. If you can jump out of a blender before being shrunk, then you can jump out of it while shrunk. This is a science puzzle.
Mikela: Congratulations! We have a winner!
Casey: Ivy that's too fast!
Ivy: You can still try if you haven't read my answer.
3B: I would remove the blades with metal bending. 3B is now recognized for the Alchemist skill Metal Bend.
Sasha: So is this a science puzzle? Since Ivy got it right away.
Mikela: That is up to you!
Evelyn: If it were me I would cast Protect III and hope for the best. 😖
P4: P4 will fly out and turn off the blender. Thank you! 😊
Ivy: Umm... you won't have the strength to push the button. P4 tries pushing the button but fails.
Sasha: I can really only do one thing. So I would nuke it. If I see the electrical cord I would nuke the cord. Sasha nukes the electrical cord. The blender is now disabled.
Zoey: Is Mikela the puzzle master or a dungeon master? XD Hey, why not both! Mikela is now recognized as a Puzzle Master and a Dungeon Master.
Evelyn: This feels like we were playing Adventurer Begins. 😊 Yes that was fun.
Sasha: So is Ledia inside the blender also? Can Ledia break us out?
Ledia: Actually who are in the blender?
Mikela: Let's say each person is in a separate blender lined up in a row.
Sasha: That's too evil. Us talking will take 1 minute already, and I can't nuke all of the cords in 1 minute. Evelyn thanks Sasha for the kind thoughts.
Ledia: If I just Tiamat out I should be able to cut at least a few cords.If they all line up I could probably cut all of them in Accel. Ledia Tiamats out of her blender and cuts all cords with Accel, just in time before the first blender would start. Ledia is now out of mana and is considered KO'ed.
P4: P4 will sing for Ledia to recover MP. The scene is not over yet, most of you are still inside blenders!
Natalie: I can fill each blender will water for those who can float. I don't want to get all wet though.
Casey: Natalie can fly, why not just bring us a rope? Good idea!
Ivy: Ugh... Natalie went looking for a rope but there was no rope to be found, and realized that a rope which she could carry back would really be a string instead.
Natalie: So... on second thought, I would fill the blenders with water for those who don't mind getting wet.
Zoey: If my equipment is also shrunk, then I could fire a rope arrow and climb out. Zoey successfully escaped the blender.
Evelyn: I don't mind getting wet.
Sasha: Hold on. Now we KNOW that Zoey has a rope, so no one needs to get wet.
Natalie: Then I take Zoey's rope and help everyone out!
Ivy: As you take the rope and climb out... OMG NO! XD What? That's what a GM says when something bad is about to happen! XD
Ivy: ... you feel that your body is suspiciously light. It was much easier than you thought.
Evelyn: I check if I left something behind.
Casey: I jump. How high can I jump? Casey discovers that they can jump higher than the blender.
Ivy: Now go read my answer. OMG Science! XD Mikela: The official answer is that E=mgh. The height a rocket can reach is its energy divided by its mass and gravity. When you are shrunk, your energy and mass shrink in the same proportion. Result: you can jump the same height before you were shrunk! Congratulations to Ivy!
Zoey: I imagine that Ivy got out of the blender while no one noticed and just watched our drama! XD I thought you wanted to figure it out by yourself.
Sasha: But this is so unrealistic, if it happened for real, I'm sure we would have tried jumping.
Casey: Um... no. not with the blender blades right next to you. You got a point. Pun intended. 😆
Mikela: After discovering that they could just jump out of the blenders, all heores have safely escaped! Congratulations! Until next time!
Zoey: Hold on, Mikela! Are we still shrunk or not?! XD
Mikela: Let's say it will wear off by tomorrow. Ciao! The heroes have escaped the blenders and obtained the temporary aspect "Shrunk" for today! Mikela is now recognized to have the Magician Stunts: Shrink Ray and Kaleidoscope. Mikela receives Welcome to the FATE club!

202210151642|Bookshelves

 202207101451|🍊 Extreme Ownership {"cmd":"PIN2","node":"202207101451","music":""}

Title

Extreme Ownership: How U.S. Navy SEALs Lead and Win

Links

Amazon | Author Webpage

Disclaimer

This book primiarily discusses solutions to issues that arise in an authoritarian command structure. Quest Society employs a flat command structure. Therefore, you may find some of the concepts contradicting to Magic Bakery values.

Lessons - Part I: Winning the war within

⭐ Extreme Ownership On any team, all responsibility for success and failure rests with the leader. The leader must own everything in their world. There is no one else to blame. The leader must acknowledge mistakes and admit failures, take ownership of them, and develop a plan to win.
⭐ No Bad Teams Only Bad Leaders If substandard performance is accepted and no one is held accountable, that poor performance becomes the new standard. When it comes to standards, as a leader, it's not what you preach, it's what you tolerate. Consequencees for failing need not be immediately severe, but leaders must ensure that tasks are repeated until the higher expected standard is achieved in a way that encourages and enables the team to utilize Extreme Ownership.
⭐ Believe To convince and inspire others to follow and accomplish a mission, a leader must be a true believer in the mission. If a leader does not believe, they will not take the risks required to overcome the inevitable challenges necessary to win. Leader must always operate with the understanding that they are part of something greater than themselves and their own personal interests. If they cannot determina a satisfactory answer themselves, they must ask questions up the chain of command until they understand why.
⭐ Check the Ego Implementing Extreme Ownership requires checking your ego and operating with a high degree of humility. Wanting to win and be the best is good, but when ego becomes destructive when it clouds our judgment and prevents us from seeing the world as it is. We can't ever think we are too good to fail or that our enemies are not capable, deadly, and eager to exploit our weaknesses.

Lessons - Part II: Laws of Combat

⭐ Cover and Move Team members must always help each other, work together, and support each other to win.
⭐ Simple Simplifying as much as possible is crucial to success. As a leader, it doesn't matter how well you feel you have presented the information or communicated an order, plan, tactic, or strategy. If your team doesn't get it, you have not kept things simple and you have failed.
⭐ Prioritize and Execute Relax, look around, make a call. A leader must remain calm and determine the highest priority task and execute. A particularly effective means to help Prioritize and Execute under pressure is to stay at least a step or two ahead of real-time problems. Don't let the focus on one priority cause target fixation. Maintain the ability to see other problems developing and rapidly shift as needed.
⭐ Decentralized Command Teams must be broken down into manageable elements, and each tactical-level team leader must understand from upper chain of command why they are doing, and be given authority to plan and execute other than asking "what do you want me to do?" This allows Junior leaders to be proactive rather than reactive.

Lessons - Part III: Sustaining Victory

⭐ Plan Leaders must identify clear directives for the team. A broad and ambiguous mission results in lack of focus, ineffective execution, and mission creep. The mission must explain the overall purpose and desired result, or "end state," of the oepration. By maintaining a perspective above the microterrain of the plan, senior leaders can better ensure compliance with strategic objectives and be the tactical genius identifying weaknesses or holes in the plan. The planning process and briefing must be a forum that encourages discussion, questions, and clarification from even the most junior personnel. Time is made for a post-operational debrief that examines all phases of an operation from planning through execution, in a concise format. What went right? What went wrong? How can we adapt and be more effective?

A leader's checklist for planning

Analyze the mission: Understand mission intent and endstate
Identify personnel, assets, resources, and time available.
Decentralize the planning process: Empower key leaders within the team to analyze possible courses of action
Determin specific course of action: Lean toward the simplest course of action; focus effort on the best course of action.
Empower key leaders to develop the plan for the selected course of action.
Mitigate risks that can be controlled as much as possible.
Delegate portions of the plan and brief to key junior leaders
Continually check and question the plan against emerging information to ensure it still fits the situation.
Brief the plan to all participants and supporting assets
Conduct post-operational debrief after execution.
⭐ Leading Up and Down the Chain of Command Leaders must routinely communicate with their team members to help them understand their role in the overall mission, and push situational awareness up the chain of command. Take responsibility for leading everyone in your world, subordinates and superiors alike. If someone isn't doing what you want or need them to do, look in the mirror first and determine what you can do to better enable this. Don't ask your leader what you should do, tell them what you are going to do.
⭐ Decisiveness Amid Uncertainty There is no 100 percent right solution. The picture is never complete. Leaders must be comfortable with this and be able to make decisions promptly, then be ready to adjust those decisions quickly based on evolving situations and new information.
⭐ Discipline Equals Freedom Generally, when a leader struggles, the root cause behind the problem is that the leader has leaned too far in one direction and steered off course. Awareness of the dichotomies in leadership allows this discovery and enables the correction.

The Dichotomy of Leadership

A good leader must be:
Confident but not cocky; Courageous but not foolhardy; Competitive but a gracious loser; Attentive to details but not obsessed by them; Strong but have endurance; A leader and follower; Humble not passive; Aggressive not overbearing; Quiet not silent; Calm but not robotic, logical but not devoid of emotions; Close with the troops but not so close that one becomes more important than another or more important than the good of the team; not so close that they forget who is in charge; Able to execute Extreme Ownership, while exercising Decentralized Command.
A good leader has nothing to prove, but everything to prove.

Credits

💬 Discussions

202207101424|Extreme Ownership
July 10, 2022 Sunday 2:24 PM
Reading Club
⭐
We got this book Extreme Ownership: How US Navy Seals Lead and Win for the reading club. I don't think we can read it during lunch. So this might be a weekend activity and I will try to summarize relevant lessons somewhere.
Black: I don't think this is a military book but a leadership book.
Ivy: I'm interested. And I think we should upgrade Chapter 26 to house reading club study notes.
Black: Depending on the content, it might be related to @Whale.
Ivy: I take that back. Let's put study notes at Page \ Data, and link to it wherever we want. But I think Chapter 26 should have a link to that data so we know what books we have regardless whether there are study notes about them. Okay. What should the ID be in Data section? Let's use the same ID code format as posts. Which is "P" followed by 12 digits timestamp.
Tanya: I'm also interested. For my understanding, why do we need the P code ID? I use that so I can refer to a specific thing without querying the group. But wouldn't you be able to do so as long as the data name is unique? As of now, data-####-####-#### is not unique, it will query all posts related to object ####-####-####. I am asking why do you need ID="P############" when you could have fetched the same item by using data-P############? It is true that you can get it either way. But there is an advantage of using data, because <jq> tag alreadys works if you use data. That might be the way going forward, if we put the content in Archive also.
Black: Let's visualize it a little bit. So we are saying that we will create a div in Archive without an ID, because @Book can fetch everything with the tag data-book without knowing what it is. But if we need to edit the content, we need to go to Archive and locate that. How do we do that? I see. I am convinced that we need the ID. The ID is for us to locate it in the Archive, and for the Viewer to show the content. Yes. I forgot about the viewer. This means we don't need to make another viewer for quest objects either.
Black: Let's confirm with some basic tests before going any further. I added P202207101451. How is it? Do we want it to show on Calendar also? It looks fine showing on Calendar. Maybe all posts should look like this actually. The viewer does not automatically expand and show the content. We can fix that later. It is fixed!
Black: Thank you, StarTree. Let's start reading so we have enough content to keep up with what the coding can handle. Black thanks all for studying.
Black: And here is the 202207101451|link to the quest. Black receives Tanya receives Ivy receives
Tanya: The tone of this book is quite different from Arcacian principles. Maybe we should make a comparison on how a similar principle would be expressed in Arcacianism.
Tanya: For instance, the concept on page 238 would violate Arcacianism. It reads, "But at the end of the day, once the debate on a particular course of action is over and the boss has made a decision--even if that decision is one you argued against-- you must execute the plan as if it were your own."
Tanya: In Arcacianism, there aren't mandatory leadership. If you don't agree with a plan, then don't join the team. Some of the presented principles are about an authoritarian structure, but Arcacian structure is flat.
Tanya: In a Quest society, a person would join a quest only if they agree with its purpose. That voluntary dynamic bypasses many of the issues the principles in Extreme Ownership try to address.
Tanya: In other words, Extreme Ownership is teaching people how to fix a systemic problem (caused by an authoritarian structure) through people, instead of solving it at the system level. Quest Society is a system level solution to those problems.
Black: I somewhat agree. Should we stop or finish reviewing all the principles?
Tanya: Let's finish the principles since we are already on 11 out of 12 principles. But we should add a disclaimer that this book deals with issues that arise in an authoritarian structure, which is not really relevant to Arcancianism that uses Quest structure. In Arcacianism, "Leadership" means something else and requires different skills.
Black: I will pin your comment. Let's finish it.
🕰️ [7:36 PM] 🕰️
Ivy: So we finished reading all the principles. That means that we skipped everything about military operations. Those interested can find the book at the guild bookshelves.
Black: Should there be a follow up quest about this book?
Tanya: What this book describes as "Extreme Ownership" is a given in Arcacianism because quests are voluntary. By design there is no one to blame. The dichotomy of leadership described in its last chapter are basic virtues that everyone should strive to have regardless whether they are leading someone or following someone. Those qualities are what make a leader in Arcacianism popular.
Ivy: I disagree with the last part of your comment. In Arcacianism, a leader can be popular just because they have a lot of rewards to give. You can totally bribe people to become their favorite.
Tanya: You are right.
Ivy: In terms of follow up quests, I think we have way too many unread books to focus on this one, and even more non-book quests to focus on reading. I think what we need is to start creating an inventory of the books we have, so we can Prioritize and Execute. Being true to our Quest Society structure, and having no leader telling us what to do, I propose updating Chapter 26 @Book to have a quest that includes a routine that inventory books we have, prioritize a book that can help us the most, and read and procedue a summary like the one we did.
Black: I see Ivy is communicating the intent of the quest quite clearly.
Tanya: In Quest Society, if your quest is clear and simple then people can understand and do it. The Quest Giver doesn't take all the credits because each Quest Taker gets their own credits. The person that ends up having the most credits is not necessarily the Quest Giver. I feel that principle that Extreme Ownership mentioned are built-in to Quest Society. In a Quest Society, you don't need to be taught how to be a leader. the Quest System drives a person to take responsibility of their actions. Tanya, I think we understand that. You are just repeating yourself now. I'm sorry.
Black: I think we have done enough for this topic. Thanks again for reading. I think we did a good job noting that might be helpful to us. If we need to refer to it, we can now link to 202207101451|P202207101451.

Amid the mountains of board games that people think Sasha should work on to reduce, Sasha decided to tackle a problem that is the most current, which is to populate the profiles of the champions at Philosophy Arena.
