I think the cards are supposed to be in boxes, but we got this as a used set where the cards are all mixed. We need to somehow sort them back into the boxes... This game is a contest of imagination judged subjectively. According to BGG, this game isn't really playable because there is no rule on who shall win. It is up to the rotating judge. I got it for the ideas on the cards. Maybe we can redraw them. At this point I consider Sasha a board game collector, collecting different board game for the sake of variety. I don't think Sasha needs to justify her picks. XD I don't just get every game. I am choosy. This set is complete with 112 cards. 50 Warriors (+6 blank), 25 Items (+3 blank), and 25 Battlefields (+3 blank). The rules actually seem quite playable. Especially for us because we normally roll dice. So this game is compatible to how we would normally play games without losing anything that the game crafted. I am adding a "try" attribute to rank the games on how badly I want to try them. I'll try. If we are using house rules anyway, then there is no rules to learn. That's a plus. Let's play Story War. @Cardinal please run the game as a co-op. A new dungeon has been discovered. What treasures and monsters await? I arrive to explore the dungeon. Casey is at the foot of a giant beanstalk that stretches up to a palace in the sky. Winged Unicorns are circling the beanstalk in midair. Am I supposed to be able to solo this? It seems extremely dangerous unless you can fly. Do you have a jetpack? Can I use a mech to pull on the beanstalk and pull the palace in the sky down? Please roll 3+. ➕➕➕➕➖ Casey observed that the palace in the sky seems light and decided to pull the beanstalk with a mech. The plan seems to work, Casey thinks, as the flying unicorns fly down to attack the mech. Anyone on scene please describe what you do in the situation. I cast sleep on the unicorns. Ledia recognizes this as a best them to ride them situation. She lights up her great sword in unicorn color and provokes, "Let's see who's boss!" I switch to cast protect instead. 😅 I use illusion to camouflage the area with flourishing flower petals and starts casting a nuke in case Ledia fails. Pretty! I fly to the palace discretely under the cover the flower petals. I try to tame a unicorn with a lasso. XD I ride up the beanstalk. I connect to the beanstalk. We're going to convert everything! XD Natalie please roll 3+, everyone else please roll 2+. Flying: ➕➖➖➕➖ Protect: ➖➕➕➕➖ Challenge: ➕➕➕➖ Camo and Nuke: ➕➖➖ Lasso: ➖➕➕➖ Climb: ➕➕➖ Connect: ➖➖ Pull: ➕➖➕➖ While they may look impressive to the team, Sasha's new skill is ignored by the unicorns. Sasha is being chased down by unicorns and is out of breath. In the chaos, Zoey lassoed one unicorn, which is growing to like this ragdoll it gets to thrash about. It is not clear if holding onto the lasso will have the effect Zoey expected. In the center stage, Ledia and a distinctive unicorn are having a mutually respectable match and attracted a ring of unicorn spectators encircling both Ledia and Evelyn. It appears that unicorns matches fight in pairs with one champion and one support. As the champions fight, the support is supposed to walk around casting support magic on their champion. The support champion in the ring paces around Evelyn taunting her to use better support spells. With the support unicorn, Ledia is slowly but surely losing the match if nothing changes. With everything going on, Casey could mostly continue pulling down the sky palace undisturbed. Casey please roll 1+. ➖ Casey did not notice that StarTree who was trying to connect to the beanstalk nearby had disappeared in the spool of beanstalk. StarTree is considered KO'ed. Friendly fire! XD 🏳️ Up in the sky, Natalie was first to arrive. She sees three items: 1) a Mega Mushroom; 2) the Excalibur; and 3) a Thunder Hammer. Red arrives second and sees two items: 4) a Time Bomb, and 5) a Wishing Star. Adventurers (including StarTree or any new player), please describe your next move to be continued in the next session. To be continued. XD Good session. I am wondering if sky palace is supposed to be free floating without the beanstalk. Is the relation between the palace and the beanstalk obvious as I look around the palace? I will wait for others to describe their thoughts, then we roll to see whose context is correct. I think we are supposed to defuse the time bomb. But then it would mean someone was trying to blow up the sky palace. For the game, what skill should I be using to help win? How does that fight work? The supports are healing the fighter but the fighters and the supports don't attack the supports. Wouldn't that just be a really drawn out fight? XD Can I have a skill that let someone use my XP to boost their stats? So if Evelyn is level 100 and Ledia is level 80, giving her all EXP would boost Ledia to level 128. How many advantages is that worth? I think that skill in general should not be worth doing unless Ledia must solo, or there is some skill only available to her at a higher level. What are high level cleric skills? If clerics are similar to WHM, then they can also use nature/elemental magic. I thought the context is support, not nuking. The other unicorn is not nuking Ledia. I think Ledia is stronger by herself if we don't force her to tag team with me to combo. I want a skill that frees Ledia to solo. 😖 If Evelyn uses a skill that raises my level, I will unleash a purified version of Tiamat power and keep it purified even after her skill ends. For those who have described their action, please roll 3+. For those who have not and want to continue, please describe and roll 2+. The delay was too long. I was still lassoed to the unicorn with sheer determination: ➖➖ XD I am pretty sure that by now I had pulled the sky palace down: ➖➖ 🤷‍♀️ I do the skill where I boost Ledia with my mana/XP. ➕➖➕➕➕➖ When in doubt, I quickcast and nuke: ➖➖ StarTree connects to the beanstalk: ➖➕➕➕➕➖ I find someone at the sky palace and learns that the beanstalk entangled them, and get the Excalibur to free the palace. ➖➕➖➖ I defuse the time bomb. ➖➕➕➖ P4 wants to get the Wishing Star. ➕➕➖ I unleash my Levelyn Power. ➖➖➕➕➕➖ It's Fusion! As Zoey, Casey, and Sasha are getting KO'ed by the unicorns, Evelyn activates her new skill to boost Ledia. The illusionary flowers that Sasha made turned real and let everyone willing to join the boost. How would you like to do this, Ledia? I give the boost my Tiamat mana and transform into Levelyn form, which is a winged unicorn style outfit with two braids. The unicorns like this new form and want to learn how to transform. Meanwhile, Natalie couldn't find anyone at the Sky Palace, but was able to get the Excalibur to cut the beanstalk. But at that point, StarTree had already taken control of the beanstalk and found that... The beanstalk absorbed Mega Mushroom spores and turned into a Mega Beanstalk, which snatched the passing sky palace. Seeing that their home is freed, the unicorns fly back to the sky palace. To catch up to the other migratory palaces, they dropped various items left by invading adventurers: 1) An abandoned Thunder Hammer that can be repaired and recharged. 2) A Pumpkin Chariot that only runs at night up to midnight. 3) A Tempest Teapot that can summon a storm. 4) A Winter Wand that can freeze things. and 5) A Mirror Shield. Does everyone get something? What is the catch of the teapot, wand, and mirror shield? They look like normal items. They weren't treasures, they were abandoned and we don't need to return them to the adventurers for a reason. I get the Pumpkin Car. Sasha now has the Pumpkin Car, which, during the day just looks like a normal pumpkin. Since I am a mage, I am going to enchant it so it basically functions as an RV for the night and it can move up to midnight, but doesn't turn back into a pumpkin until... whenever I get up in the morning... Sasha can feel that she is stretching it, the success rate of this enchantment is about a roll of 2+. On a fail the pumpkin could just blow up. Maybe you can give up the part where it can move? Why does it need to move when you can carry it around as a pumpkin, which you can just store in inventory? XD A moving house is cool. StarTree will help Sasha do the enchantment! The success rate is now 4+. Sasha please roll. ➕➕➕➖➖ StarTree please roll 2+. ➖➖ With StarTree's help, Sasha got what she wanted plus a little extra. The pumpkin RV can hold itself into brunch time or until it drains Sasha's mana. StarTree is out of mana and is considered KO'ed. Thank you! I want to get the mirror shield. The mirror shield has a rather unsighty crack. But it is supposed to reflect damages. Since I haven't actually done anything as Levelyn, I will use my unicorn healing and transformation power to turn the cracked mirror shield into a winged mirror shield. Levelyn please roll 4+. ➕➖➕➕➖ Levelyn succeeded and obtained a winged mirror shield. I will get the hammer. Casey would have no trouble fixing it, except the hammer is huge: too big for Casey, and too small for the Mech. I will think about what to do with it later. Casey now has the half-giant Thunder Hammer. The most useful thing to me seems to be the Winter Wand. What is the catch? Zoey's elemental inspection tells her that the wand is supposed to freeze objects at range, but it instead does AE freezing centered at itself. I will fire it like an arrow. XD Zoey obtained the AE Winter Wand. I want to be able to summon the Excalibur. Natalie discovers that ever since she picked up the sword, she has been having this feeling that people all over the world are trying to borrow the sword, but the sword had chosen her to be the lady of the lake to hand it out. It would appear the the previous lady of the lake had had enough. XD With pleasure. Natalie is now the custodian of the Excalibur. Am I actually somewhere or am I part of Levelyn? Everyone sees Levelyn asking that question. 😅 The Levelyn effect lasts for one week or until Levelyn is KO'ed. That effect seems too long. You could just use up your mana. I give mana back to everyone. Everyone else on scene now has full mana. StarTree is no longer KO'ed. Levelyn is still Levelyn. I think I still have a time bomb that can "blast you into next week." Okay The countdown timer of the Time Bomb was 1 week. But since Red defused it, the current countdown is only 10 seconds. The bomb explodes on Levelyn, and Ledia and Evelyn emerges from the smoke, along with a rainbow colored Philosopher's Stone which is a transformation catalyst. Evelyn gives the pretty stone to Red Cute 🌈 StarTree stores the Mega Mushroom ability. StarTree succeeds with no difficulty. P4 currently has the Wishing Star, and the Teapot is left untouched. Does anyone else want the wishing star? What is wrong with the wishing star? The wishing star is an orb that looks like a Dragon Ball. If you make a wish while holding it, the wish may come true in an ironic way. That could be the worst item. XD Not if you use it on a villain when they make a wish. Then P4 will keep it. As P4 says so, the wishing star fuses inside her such that she is forever in touch with the wishing star and no one else can touch it. No!! Not to our lucky girl! XD I wish it to come back out. That wish had no effect. I shadow clone. Each of the four P4 has a wishing star inside. Now P4 just need to be able to make a total of seven clones. XD 💟 Unless they are all one-star. XD Meanwhile, the Tempest Teapot is rattling and spinning. It seems to be a piece of trash that is worse than a piece of trash. It seems to be powerful, but in the most annoying and uncontrollable way. I think we can't leave it there. I can't think of what to do with it. But I can lock it up in Arx. The philosopher's stone can transform something into something else. Maybe it can transform the teapot into an orb, then it won't be rattling. It seems like a waste since the resulting orb doesn't seem useful either. A catalyst means that it can do its job without being used up. Just saying. You never know what happens when you fail a roll. XD I deploy my pumpkin RV for everyone to have a sleep over. Cute!!! The pumpkin RV is effective at blocking the clanking of the tempest teapot. This puzzle is too hard. Why don't I just freeze it with the winter arrow then smash it? XD But if it keeps spinning it is at least a perpetual energy source. We don't really need perpetual energy source in a fantasy world. XD Maybe it can be used as a water magic training tool. If your water magic is high enough, you can keep the water calm. Evelyn hasn't gotten an item yet. I say this is destiny. Of all people only Natalie and Evelyn would have a chance of 1+ to calm the teapot. Sasha feels offended because she can summon tsunami. That is not water control. XD I guess I will try. But why turn something energetic into something calm? Maybe we can make it energetic in a convenient way. Turn it into a teapot that can brew any tea endlessly. To be honest that was probably what it was before it was broken. I cast protect and try: ➕➖ With effort, Evelyn is able to make the teapot not make a sound while it shakes. But the rattling resumes once she takes her mind off it. My turn: ➕➕➖ With mild effort, Natalie is able to make the teapot not shake at all, and it remains not shaking for a few seconds after she takes her mind off it. Evelyn is recognized for Fine Water Control Rank A. Natalie is recognized for Fine Water Control Rank S. P4 thought of licking some Mega Mushroom then swallow the Tempest Teapot. Then it would be so annoying in her body that the Wishing Star will come out. But then P4 thinks that the Wishing Star will be lonely so she doesn't want to do it. As she says that out loud to the group, the Wishing Star comes out and rolls into the Tempest Teapot. The Tempest Teapot becomes quiet as the Wishing Star is spun by it inside. It seems that P4's feeling was not reciprocated. It is a Wishing Star of Rebellion. I give P4 a hug. おかえり。 💟 As such, the party is able to have a peaceful night at the pumpkin house. Until the next adventure. GG XD Good ending. Evelyn is recognized for the Cleric Skill Level Fusion Rank A. For Red. The card style looks nice. I like how this deck has traditional drawings of the characters instead of just photographs. This set is missing the instruction, one frog token, and the hourglass. This game is rated 8+. I think this is an area control war game. But that might not be how we play it. Not really a fan of this art style. The last two Exit games with 2/5 difficulty rating was a little too easy. This one is rated 4/5 in difficulty. This is a detective game where each player tries to convince others that they are innocent waking knowing if they are. I don't know if this game has an English version. Interested. This game is quite fun to play. When playing with just a few players, we removed the rules on speaking and just let everyone speak any time. I think the randomly assigned character and character settings cards help the players not get so invested in their character and play for a fun story. Although the theme won't pass Magic Bakery requirements, this is closer to Detective Conan level of violence. For the Law Game. I think this will be a good story game for us. Interested. Let's play this. These games are rated for 10+ year-old, but this one has a skull key and could be scary. This one is a little easy. The chance and chest cards are funny. "Forget real estate. You can't afford it anyway." XD This is like Apple to Apple, but the prompt is a job title and the players are choosing whacky qualification cards to give a compelling story for the interview. This deck of playing cards use the house symbols as the suits! Yes please. What is the order of the suits? idk... XD We don't have this game, but we talked about it a little bit. 202403161819 Daggerheart session 0 https://www.polygon.com/tabletop-games/24099460/critical-role-daggerheart-character-creation-one-shot Combat in Daggerheart https://www.thegamer.com/matt-mercer-critical-role-new-ttrpg-not-dnd-dungeons-dragons/ Daggerheart https://darringtonpress.com/playtest-daggerheart-right-now/
In Daggerheart, a turn is called a "Spotlight." It’s when the focus is on one player to act or make choices, fitting the game’s story-driven style. Daggerheart is a narrative-focused tabletop RPG that stands out from D&D with a brilliant mechanic: the use of Hope and Fear tokens. These tokens reward roleplay and add drama, encouraging collaborative storytelling. With a lighter ruleset and emotional focus, it appeals to players who want less combat and more character-driven stories. https://www.gamesradar.com/tabletop-gaming/ive-been-playing-d-and-d-for-years-but-daggerheart-might-make-me-defect-for-one-simple-yet-brilliant-mechanic/ Critical Role plays DaggerHeart on May 29. https://darringtonpress.com/daggerheart-age-of-umbra-mini-series-coming-to-critical-role-may-29th/ This figures look cute from a distance. The kids like this. This puzzle is cute and easy. This game takes longer than exploding kittens because a dead player may come back. I like the graphics on this deck better than exploding kittens. This is quite playable. This is a game to feel like a caveman. This could be a game for us. I think this is still among my other board games I haven't unpacked from moving. This game looks really pretty! Maybe we need a category of self-help games like this. I got this because I am a witch. Our the box and paper components of our set (not the one in the link for reference) is pretty beat up. Got on sale. The one we have has a white box like this: https://www.ebay.com/itm/124734599030 For collection and decoration. Got this onsale and this set came with two dice instead of one. Set up the pieces according to the book then use the fewest moves to cover each cat. So cute! On Junior puzzle #16 😖🙏 We don't already have this, do we? I thought we do. Not according to the list. The list is probably not complete. XD I got this game because I have never seen it before and it looks cute. I think this might be slightly better than Love Letter but it is always very hard to strategize. These dice have 4 blank sides, 1 plus side and 1 minus side. (They are not Fate dice.) It is said that this game is not like Risk at all. This set is not complete. When I got it there were only 39 out of 55 cards left. The box was kind of beat. Taped it back. This set is at the ice house. This is the set with a case the looks like a book. It is complete.
A Knight's Tour https://x.com/Rainmaker1973/status/1996291498552238336 It seems that this game is rated 18+ because the theme is about wine. This is a lite version of Dungeons and Dragon. The components are nice and we played it with Magic Bakery RP rules. Reference for our Quest Cards design: Adventure Begins https://149455152.v2.pressablecdn.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/09/DDAB_scenario-cards.jpg Where did we play Adventure Begins? That was 20220123 at the Deer Crossing node at BlogSpot! That node is huge! XD 😖 This could be a good game night game.

 We have two of this set.
Let's try playing this in our rules. @Cardinal. The campaign is "Lost Mine of Phandelver", and it has four parts.
You are on a quest to escort a merchant's wagon to town. If you are part of the quest, please introduce yourself. The template has five level 1 characters: two fighters, one rogue, one wizard, and one cleric.
Detective Sasha is resting inside the wagon, disguised as a beginner adventurer. She is following a lead about a certain discover. Evelyn the cleric joins the quest. Evelyn is walking along the wagon animals and watching around. P4 is patrolling ahead and around the wagon. StarTree is driving the wagon. Wee~~! I guess I'll be the other fighter. I walk along the other side of the wagon. The game asks the DM to ask the players for their marching order, but you have described that in your introduction. In fact you took all the suggested positions. Is it too late to join? I want to play with StarTree. XD Zoey the Ranger sees the wagon. She could tell that it is an escort quest. I let the party know that I joined the quest and I am scouting ahead. Wee~~! Scouting ahead, Zoey sees two dead horses with black feathered arrows stuck on them. Are we level 1? I want to hide and use a familiar to investigate, while sharing the news with the party. Every player is level 5. You may import skills worth 5-stars for free, then you need to spend inspirations to import more. Then I import: Stealth, Party Telepathy, Familiar Sense. I set familiar sense to be a two star skill because you are both summoning a familiar and remoting to it. That's fine. What do you do now? First I use Party Telepathy to let the team know, so that everyone can act, then I use familiar sense to have a crow investigate. P4 look for ambushers. Please describe the skills you use with that. Heat sense, hearing, invisibility. I would weigh invisibility to be 3-star. You have no free import left now. Zoey has a crow flies to the horse and shares its view with the party. I check the scene with my 🔮3 The party except Zoey recognizes the two horses. They belong to the quest giver and his body guard who went ahead of the wagon yesterday. Sasha can tell that the horses have been dead for one day, and the small arrows are the kinds that goblins would use. Should StarTree stop the wagon or keep going? StarTree cannot see the dead horses because they are around the next corner. She can only see them through party vision. I think we should keep going and pretend we didn't know. But a discreet Protect would make sense. What if I cast Sleep after P4 finds the ambushers? Meanwhile, P4 finds three goblin ambushers on the right side of the road, which is on the opposite side as where Zoey is hiding. I fly the crow off and circle back to check my side of the road from behind. Zoey finds three more goblin ambushers on the left side of the road. The two groups are too far apart for a magic spell to cover both. What should I do? Cast Protect around the wagon, or use Bless to give inspirations to Zoey and P4, then use Light to blind the ambushers to let P4 and Zoey KO the goblins? I would like more inspiration. Can each of you take on three goblins? Am I supposed to join the fight? Is StarTree immune to arrows? If they kidnapped the quest giver, we need to find where they took him. I think we should make one of the flee back to their hideout. The wagon is approaching the corner. It is the last chance the cast Protect without the Goblins seeing the glow from spell casting. I cast Protect on the party at the wagon and bless Zoey then P4 if I can. Don't cast bless. It will disclose where Zoey and P4 are. When I see the horses, should I walk up to it to prompt the goblins to attack? Then Zoey and P4 and surprise attack them while they are focused on me. Evelyn please roll for the cast of Protect. The spell is ❤️3, I first rolled two white stars and a treasure. Because that is not enough stars, I roll a fourth die and got a yellow star. Sasha, StarTree, Casey, Evely, the Wagon, and the Wagon Animals now have protect. This Protect works like a mana shield. When you get hit, you may choose to use it or your inspiration to absorb the damage. Then may I roll my fifth dice to see if it also give inspiration? Yes. I rolled a key. Evelyn reflects briefly that the quest giver and his brothers have found something big, and was in a hurry to bring the wagon with supplies to the rough town of Phandalin. The wagon is turning the corner and the dead horses become visible. Zoey and P4 can see that the ambushers are observing the wagon's reaction. Zoey please roll for Familiar, then Stealth. P4 please roll for Invisibility. Familiar: White Star, Yellow Star, Dragon. Stealth: :TreasureChest:. I spend one inspiration to roll for stealth: Dragon. Uh oh. Invisible: White Star, Black Star, Treasure. It seems that you two imported the skill but are too low level to use them successfully. Shouldn't I know if the skill didn't work? You wouldn't know until someone sees you. Could I roll for Telepathy to see if I got another inspiration? Okay. Then P4 also roll for Heat Sensing and Hearing. Telepathy: White star. XD Heat Sensing: White Star. Hearing: Treasure. I spend the inspiration to add a star to my invisible roll. Zoey didn't need to roll for after rolling treasure for stealth. She could have just spend the inspiration to add a star like P4. The dice was cast. As Casey approaches pretending to check on the horse, the goblins noticed something was off. Casey, make an acting check. Is that a trick question? I am not pretending to be a guard. I am a guard. Although I didn't have any trickery proficiency. And no inspiration. The goblins noticed that Casey approached the dead horses as if they were already seen.. They decided to attack earlier than P4 and Zoey thought they would. We shall continue after the goblins get their combat algorithm on a card. Roger! It seems quite playable so far. Gotta remember how inspirations work. You could just spend it and get a star if it is too risky to fail. I would suggest that each day normally starts with some inspiration. If I had been marching I could have been whistling and inspired myself. In this case it seems that the party was slightly overconfident and didn't pay enough attention when their marching spirit wasn't as high as they thought they would be. The goblin AI card is ready. Are you? I am going to have armor, shield and a hammer. Does my character just have them? Or do I need to spend points to import them? You don't need to spend points to import your starting equipment. If it breaks, then you can spend treasure to draw a spare. How do armor, shield, and a hammer work? Updated information. The goblins saw something wrong with the way Casey reacted and fired their arrows earlier than the party expected. Six goblins fired their arrows at Casey at about the same time from two sides of the road. The spotlight is on you, Casey. What do you do? I use the dead horse as a spring board to leap, evading the arrows, and to land a hummer strike at the goblins on the right side. Please make a combat roll. Three stars to succeed. Dragon, yellow star, black star. Then Dragon and Black Star. Casey deals 2 damage to one goblin but was also hit twice by arrows. The goblins didn't fire the arrows all at the same time. Casey was hit once in the leg and once in the back. Dealing 1 damage each. I am armored, so those hits just bounce off. I spend a dragon to react with the goblin next to the one that was just hit. As Casey made a strike on one of the goblins, the one next to it took an opportunity strike against Casey, dealing two damages, which Casey could not react to. P4 had tied that goblin with a string, pulling it off balance and miss, and then ties it up while it is down. There were three goblins and you tied one. Your strength is less than a goblin. And I think it is difficult to tie a rope on a goblin even with invisibility. The difficulty is at least the same as what Casey attempted, but you also have five relevant dice to roll for three star. Black star, Treasure. Treasure.Key. Yellow Star. P4 spends one inspiration from last time to succeed. P4's tripped the correct goblin from intuition and proceeds to tie it up. As P4 breaks invisible to tie up the goblin, she notices that another goblin is eyeing the items she is carrying. I spend four threats to fire another volley of arrows, four at StarTree this time. StarTree pretend not to notice so that the goblins don't feel threatened, so that Zoey can take good aim at them. I shoot all three on my side. StarTree please define what proficiency you have to appear non-threatening. StarTree just doesn't look threatening. StarTree has no weapon and sits like a doll. I'll consider that a 1-star trait. Please roll one die to see if that worked. Black Star! StarTree looked like an easy prey to the goblin as they took aim. Zoey, to shoot three goblins, are you shooting them one by one or three at the same time? Three at the same time from my cover. From my standpoint they lined up perfectly. XD Without importing any archery skill I think this harder than what Casey did. The shot itself would be 2-star with advantage from StarTree, but you only have one combat die. Do you want to spend two inspirations to have +2 dice in archery? So that when you use a bow to attack, you roll three dice? Sure. Zoey listens to the DM. XD Dragon and white star x2. XD As the goblins took aim at StarTree, Zoey fires a triple shot dealing 1 damage to each, causing them to lose aim. On the other side, the fourth goblin fired at StarTree, dealing one damage. StarTree uses the trait "living dummy" to take the a piercing damage without losing HP, and pretends to collapse on her side. I spend a dragon. 🐉3 As StarTree Takes the hit and falls to her side, she learns that the arrows are poisoned. StarTree uses her traits "compartment" and "decomposition" to isolate the affected area and analyze the poison while breaking it down. An imported trait is not a roll. When you import a trait, it gives you a bonus when you roll. So StarTree should still be rolling unless the DM wants to keep the game faster. I'll say Decomposition is a two star trait, because it is both analyzing and breaking down the poison. To tell if the poison is faster than StarTree's defense, roll for two stars. You can roll three dice just from your traits. My may roll one more die for each Alchemist proficiency you instantiate. Treasure, Key, Key. Alchemy 1: Treasure. StarTree thinks that this means the roll failed because StarTree has no inspiration. Even if the fifth die is a star, it is not enough. As StarTree starts her routine to decompose the poison, the party noticed from telepathy that she fell asleep as her speech became unintelligible. As StarTree's consciousness drifts away, she dreams that the poison was too pure to have been made by goblins. I spend two threat to move goblins. As P4 tries to tie up the goblin, the other two goblins from the right side attacked her with swords at the same time dealing 2 damages each. I cast chain lightning on those three on the right discreetly from the wagon near Evelyn without showing the goblins on the left that I was there. To hit only the goblins without hitting P4 would be 4-star and may use 🎩 dice for fine control. I first roll my 🎩2: Key, Dragon. On second thought, Sasha decides to cast Magic Missiles instead. For magic missiles can be cast with 🔮 or 🎩, but for changing your mind you lost some time, so the difficult is 4-star to hit all three before they could land a strike. Star, Black Star, Dragon, Gold Star x2. Sasha's magic missiles hit and KO'ed the goblins trying to attack P4, allowing her to tie up one goblin. Sasha gets two bonus inspirations for the KO's. P4 gets one. I turn around and chase down the three from the other side. Those three goblins set their sight on P4, they are running across the road to attack her with blades. I tackle two goblins and swing at the last tone. Roll for 3-star to see if you hit all three. Star, Dragon, Key, Star, Key. I spend one inspiration. Casey body slams two goblin dealing one damage to each, and send the last one flying dealing two damages. Casey KO's one goblin and gets one inspiration. The remaining two goblins gave up on P4 and changed to attack Evelyn near the wagon instead. I move back defensively while casting bubble prison on them. The difficulty is two stars per bubble. Black Star, Key, Black Star, Key, Gold Star. I spend one inspiration. I spend two threats. Evelyn was able to cast the spell in time levitated the goblins, but almost immediately, the goblins sliced through the bubbles to attack Evelyn from both sides dealing 2 damages each. I slam one with my shield against the wagon. I shoot one from behind the wagon. The goblins are fast and agile. They are also very close to Evelyn. To succeed without also hitting Evelyn would be 4-star. Treasure, Black Star, Key, Star, Treasure. I spend one inspiration to make the hit. Dragon, Black Star, Gold Star, Star, Dragon. I spend one dragon each goblin keep their positions. As Casey slams one goblin onto the wagon and Zoey's arrow forced the other to parry, the goblins each tossed a fizzling black sphere under the wagon. One under where Sasha is, the other under where StarTree is. Does StarTree wake up? That would be a constitution check. To be awake enough to know there are bombs under the wagon would be 3-star. StarTree has Alchemy-based constitution: Gold Star, Star, Treasure, Dragon, Dragon. StarTree woke up from Casey's body slamming the goblin, but not awake enough to know what has transpired to react to it. Through telepathic party vision I can see where the bombs are. I use invisible hands to slap them to the direction with no one. 4-star to do both. Treasure, Key, Dragon, Key, Star. I spend one inspiration. Sasha tried to slap both bombs away but one of the them hit the axle of the wagon and bounced back. It is still under the wagon. The bombs explode, what do you do? I cover it with my shield then put my body on it. I shoot water in the direction of the bomb under the wagon, to extinguish the fuse and make the area wet so things won't burn. P4 attacks the goblin closer to her. I step away from the wagon and shoot the other goblin. I cover my ears, roll into a ball at the opposite corner of the wagon. Maybe we shouldn't try to do anything. The more you try, the more dragons you may roll, and the bigger the explosion it will be. Is that how the game works? When StarTree made the AI sheet, StarTree tried to make the bomb damage based on the number of dragons, but it wasn't legible so the current version deals a fixed damage. The DM can normally adjust the strength by spending Dragons. Your party is almost unscathed. How many dragons are there left? 11 Dragons and 12 Threats. I think Cardinal isn't using them fast enough. Evelyn, to make the bottom of the wagon wet in time only takes two-star. To extinguish the bomb will take five-star. You may decide how many dice to roll. I roll one by one: Dragon. Treasure. Black Star. Gold Star. Treasure. Almost worked! Evelyn had two inspirations so it only needed 3-stars. XD Evelyn desperately tried to extinguish the bomb but it was too late. Seeing that there is nowhere for the bomb to go, Casey covered the bomb to protect everyone. The bombs explode and deals 6 damages each. Casey's shield was destroy after absorbing 2 damages. Casey, how do you take the remaining 4 damages? I will let protect break taking three damages and my body armor take one. The explosion sends everyone's ear ringing, except Zoey and P4 would receive one hearing damage each. We had no protect. XD P4, you see one goblin trying to flee into the woods. If you make a ranged attack, the difficulty is 3-star. Black Star, Treasure, Key, Star, Dragon. I spend 1 inspiration to make the hit, and spend 2 treasure to use a +2 Shuriken to deal three damages. P4 KO's the goblin and regain one inspiration. P4 noticed that the fleeing goblins drank potion. Zoey, the difficulty is three for you to hit the last goblin. I don't think I can hit it without inspiration. XD I kept my bow drawn and watched the goblin escape while sending the familiar crow to follow it. The last goblin escapes into the wood. Casey crawls out from under the wagon with soot stain from the blast. The area seems to be safe at the moment with four goblins KO'ed and one tied up. Please discuss what you would like to do. We will continue when a decision is made. Should I have tried to shoot the last goblin even when the chance to hit was only 13%? I didn't have enough inspiration to make the hit, but if I do nothing I don't gain inspiration. But if I did something I would risk the encounter not ending the DM decides to use the threats. XD These are your characters and imported skills. I am tracking the number of dice rolls for each player to help tell if someone is low on interaction.

 Level 5 Ranger

 This set has a square score board. This set has a rectangular score board. This is the one with the submarine. Retrieve EXIT: Tomb game to write the story. I know we have one already but it is not at HQ and it is on sale. I think we shouldn't get it just to save it from trash. Why not? I think we don't have this version. Then we have three! XD We should at least play it once. So we have been secretly designing a card game but I think it is time for some review/input by the guild. The inspiration was Battleline: Medieval. But we want a version that can be played on a normal deck of playing cards, and stick to simple rules. Theme: We play tested with the WWII spotter cards, although there could be a cuter theme for Magic Bakery and Quest Society. Theme: We used war planes but conceptually there is no dying because there is no discard pile. But we designed the suit abilities based on warfare: Rebel, Recruit, Ambush, Recon. The biggest "challenge" of this design is how to translate it to a Magic Bakery / Quest Society game. The main mechanism to evaluate strength is based on probabilities of the three card patterns. What is equivalent to a straight or three of a kind in questing? Also, we have six archetypes. Does it mean there should be six suits? If we stick to four suits, what should the suits be? The four suits can represent End goal (Diamond), Policy (Spade), Infrastructure (Club), and Community (Heart). In co-op mode, The cards on the quest side represent the difficulty of the quest. (It is up to the players to imagine what those quests mean). To clear a quest, you either play enough cards to exceed the sum in each suit, or you use "ingenuity" which is the common card-suit patterns. It is up to the player to imagine how that ingenuity overcomes the challenge. Any special pattern that forms on the quest side represents complications. In this case can the player side have more than three cards? I think the Solo Mode should alternate between playing a card face down and up, or only play a card face down if there is no card facing down. I think we need to play test to see if we still need to set a max of only three quester cards per quest. It is more thematic if more questers can do a quest. I don't think you should have to wait for a quest to have three cards before you do it. So if you are a 10, and you see the quest has one revealed card with low or no number, you could attempt the quest with a club card (Using just the raw numerical sum) and brute force it. I don't have a problem with some quests being soloable. I just want to make sure that if the cards show characters, the low value characters can still make important differences. A 2 beats an Ace already. I think that is covered. The way it is described so far, you would always try to use the lowest level quester to complete a quest, so you can save the higher level cards for tougher quests. Wouldn't that mean A, K, Q, J are "useless" normally, since they have no numerical value. It is like they are artifacts. I think if they have no value on the quest side, the game will be too easy. Maybe the quest shouldn't be available to be completed until it has at least one number, or to complete a quest with one of those cards, you need a team of three. How do we simulate the part where we break down a complicated quest into smaller chucks? Maybe a spade card can be used to split quests. What should be the rule about splitting quest? And what should happen to the rule of losing a lane when a quest has 5 cards already? I think we don't need to lose a lane when the quest gets complicated. The questers are still there they just need more help. I don't think there are enough cards to handle splitting quests. If you can split them, can you also combine them so the same team can effectively do two quests at once? How many quests should there be "on the board"? Maybe the rule is that when you place a spade card, you may move a quest card to another open quest slot. Then you take a card and the AI places a card. What do Heart cards do? When you place a heart card, you may take a completed quest card from that column. Diamond: When you place a diamond card, you may reveal any face down card in that column. (I think there is no need to have neutral cards.) I think the divider line helps to show the quest slots. Unless you play to have quest cards to always be shown sideway. I think incomplete quests always have a face down card, that card can be sideway and be used as a divider. If you scout and reveal that one, when you place the AI card the AI card must refill that slot. When you try a quest but you fail, where does the trying card go? I think it should add to the quest as part of its difficulty. And then you take a card, and the AI places a quest card. Is it possible to completely scout out a quest before attempting to complete it? It seems that the steps are: You place a card on your quest team and indicate that you want to complete a quest. Then the hidden cards of that quest are revealed one by one. If your quest team fail, the card you tried with goes to the quest side. Then you take a card somewhere, and the AI places a card. If the quest has no hidden card, the AI will place it there. Then does it mean it is never possible to completely scout out a quest? Wouldn't you just scout the draw deck? Then when the AI takes that card and place it face down, you already know what it is. I think it is too hard remember that card. Could the rule just be that the AI will take the card on the top of the draw deck and place it for a quest, without flipping the card? Can a player play a diamond, use the effect, then take the diamond card back? If there is allowed it makes a boring role for that player. But the result is that the AI won't get any face down card. We need play test for that, but I don't want to add a rule just to prevent the players from playing it in a boring way. I think the disadvantage of doing that outweighs the benefit because when you take the diamond quester back, you haven't made progress by adding a card to do the quest, when the quest is getting harder. That tactic doesn't work because you get a new AI card for each player. Unless all players are doing the same, there will be a face down AI card. The tactic wasn't for every player to do it, but to assign a player as the scout. And the next player be the finisher. But in that arrangement, only the finisher player will get new cards, the scout player will be playing and taking the same move over and over. I don't think that tactic would have much effect. When you place a diamond and reveal a quest card, can you then decide not to finish the quest? I think there shouldn't be so much penalty in trying but fail. When you fail, there is already a penalty that the quest is getting harder, and you need to reassign questers. There are five frontlines, the first to control three fronts wins. Play 1 card from hand then draw 1 card. When you already have 3 fighters, you play a fourth card to attack, and place it facedown side way on the losing side. After playing a card, if a group has too many cards you must remove a card you control from that group. You must play a card during your turn, even if doing so will make your group weaker. The card you remove cannot be the same card that you have just played. The card you remove can* be the card you have just played. You may play the card into any slot that is neutral or in your control. You may remove a card from any slot that is neutral of in your control. Basic Rule: When you attack, the defender may choose to review the neutral card and use that as their fourth card. When you start an attack, the fourth card can be one of the three cards for the attacking group. Advanced Rule: Suits: You may play a heart card to an occupied zone, then take a card from that zone. Advanced Rule: Diamond: When you place a diamond card, you may look at any faced down card (You may normally look at your own faced down cards.). Can the diamond ability not be a peek but a reveal. It is hard to remember what it is. There is an advantage when you scout out a neutral place. Then you don't want the enemy see it. If both sides agree they can always just reveal a card and make that card public knowledge. The problem is that when there are too many cards facing up, the game area looks confusing. Advanced Rule: Suit: Club: When you use a club card as your attack card, the attack is an ambush and only the numerical strength of the cards are used to determine the victor of the battle. A, K, Q, J have no numerical value in this case. Advanced Rule: Suits: Spade: You can play a spade card at an occupied slot that is not already full, then take the occupier card. By doing this you regain control of that airfield. Advanced Rule: When you play a card, you may always choose to play it face up or down. To use a suit ability, you just need to show it to confirm the suit, then you can still play it face down. Support Competitive Team play: In a 2 vs 2 game, each player has their own hands. The players are only free to discuss openly. So if you strategize with your teammate, your enemies will hear that too. Is there a cooperative mode to this game? Cooperative Mode: The players play against a dumb AI to clear all fronts. At the end of a player's turn, the player draws a card from the deck and place it face down for the AI enemy. to a zone that has the fewest AI cards. AI mode difference: Unlike a player, the AI can have more than 3 cards at their airfield. When the AI tries to place a sixth card, the players automatically lose that column and the sixth card is placed face down to occupy that column. Interesting. The coop/solo mode. AI Mode: If the AI tries to draw a card but the draw deck is empty, the players lose. This game is played with an app. We played this once and we lost. How to play Catan online https://boardgamegeek.com/blog/16348/blogpost/173610/my-experience-with-online-catan Could this game be fun? +1 We have two sets. The components of this game are very cute. +1
This set is complete! We played this. It was a good game. Quest: Clear the Programmer's conviction of the Research station leader. He claims that he was framed. Look for the Motive, Means and Opportunity. The suspects are the eight members of the research team: Leader, Doctor, Mechanic, Cook, Biologist, Electrician, Glacialist, Programmer.
    The Biologist said the Cook spent all his money on the expedition and wanted his money back, but the leader declined. The doctor is married to the Mechanic but seems to have an affiar with the Leader (?). The Doctor didn't want the leader to tell the Mechanic becahse she thinks that the Mechanic is short-tempered and unpredictable. The Doctor might have a murder motive to hide the affair. The mechanic is married to the doctor. The mechanic might have a motive of the murder out of anger about the affair. He has a motive for the inheritance.
    There is a change to the dosage at about 9:09pm. It seems that the Programmer might be an addict and hide a syringe, but did not commit the murder. Do we know the programmer's birthday? In any case it can't be 1803. That question is just to see an honest No repsonse. This letter is addressed to the Glacialist. From his grandma how has the same last name as the victim leader about inheritance. The gum used to cover the camera had an ingredient that the Programmer would be allegic to.

Timeline

0904: An argument and fight between the leader and the programmer. 0905: Leader murdered. 9pm: Biologist: 9pm At bunker to sleep. The programmer seemed to be asleep. 9:09pm Change of dosage. This is a set of clear plastic puzzle. There is no image but the box cover shows where the pieces should be. 20 years to transform our world https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/stop-drop-and-roll/earth-rising-20-years-to-transform-our-world/faqs Let's try Friday. Can we make it multiplayer? From thrift store. The box says the difficulty is 2 out of 5 and is medium. Shouldn't medium be a 3? This is a set of 6 episodes but the keycode for one of them is missing. I think you can find the solution on a youtube playthrough. I think this game could be a good game night game.
1 Center Square Board | 6 L-shaped Boards 60 Hero Deck Cards | 60 Zombie Deck Cards | 6 Turn Order cards | 2 Crawling Torso Cards 8 Hero Boards | 5 Scenario Boards | 1 Sun Tracker 1 Rulebook | 1 Grave Weapon Rulebook 1 Theme Music CD 16 Tiny Dice 8 Heros | 7 Green Normal Zombies | 7 Orange Normal Zombies | 7 Green Grave Weapon Zombies | 7 Orange Grave Weapon Zombies 4 Taken Over | 3 Lights Out | 1 Sun Track Circle | 1 Red Circle | 4 Spawn Points | 18 Wound | 7 Number Tokens (Missing "2") | 6 Townsfolk | 4 Zombie Heroes | 2 Crawling Torso | 4 Gas Marker | 2 Bio Canister | 1 Old Betsy | 1 The Skull | 1 The Book | 1 LNoE Circle | 1 Truck Token | 1 Tractor Token These are suggested gamerules to play the game faster and in co-op without a zombie master.
Move the Sun Track Marker Each player draws a zombie card and may not show or discuss them with other players. Each player should try to use up their zombie cards. Keeping them in your hand gives your character disadvantages. The player may not use a zombie card in a way that does not give the zombies the apparent best advantage.
The player has a zombie card that can re-roll the hero's fight dice and during a fight, the zombie's roll is 3 and the hero's rolls are 2 and 6. If the player decides to use the zombie card, the player may only choose to reroll the hero's 6 to maximize the chance for the hero to lose the fight. This step is skipped. Each player move the zombie(s) near where they sit. The fight is resolved as one event no matter how many zombies are fighting and the zombies have a fixed initial roll. The zombie's initial roll equals the number of zombies fighting the hero plus the number of zombie cards the hero has in their hand. If the hero wins, the hero may discard 1 zombie card in hand. If the hero has no zombie card, wound 1 zombie in the fight.
Two zombies are fighting a hero who has 1 zombie card. The roll value of the zombies is therefore 3. The hero still needs to roll dice, and the game proceeds as if the zombies rolled a three. Always spawn a zombie at each spawn pit. For each pit that has no zombie model left to spawn, discard a hero card from the hero deck. The heroes lose if the hero deck is empty. A hero with 5 or more zombie cards cannot move or search. Discard 1 zombie card and skip to step 2. Ready Stance: A hero may choose to skip this step to discard 1 zombie card. A hero may choose to move 3 spaces or search (at a searchable location). If the hero has fewer than 5 zombie cards, they may also choose to draw 1 zombie card and move 1 more space or do 1 more search. (No change) (No change) The hero may target one zombie, and the zombie's initial roll equals the number of zombies on the tile plus the number of zombie cards the hero has. If the hero wins, the hero may discard 1 zombie card in hand. If the hero has no zombie card, wound 1 zombie in the fight.
Played the defend the mansion with the quick rule but we lost with six players after 3 hours. It went from the sun tracker at 17 down to 6. https://boardgamegeek.com/filepage/154398/solo-system-for-last-night-on-earth https://s3.amazonaws.com/geekdo-files.com/bgg209306 We donated this game along with some expansions years ago, but got another copy along with two crawling torso Grave Weapon cards mixed in) and its CD at a thrift store. Checking components. The grave weapon parts are complete. It is the miniature set, which only has two grave weapon cards. The other grave weapon cards come from the Surival of the Fittest set. Coffee Time game. +1 This game might have too much reading for a casual game night. This game is about the Trolley Problem https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trolley_problem https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZXd0LN7g3GE This game looks cute. We should play it. WIth Chaos Pack Expansion. July 13, 2022 Wednesday 10:15 AM
Pirate Bay
⭐
Since the person who is the biggest stakeholder is not taking charge, I am going start doing what we think should happen. We are going to make a list of all the board games we have and keep the ones we really want.
Sasha: You may make a list, but I don't want you to get rid of anything. We shall see.
Casey: I am just going to first list everything I see here: 202207131009|Board Games
Casey: Starting at the DINNING TABLE, UNDER THE TABLE, and around almost EVERY WALL of the DINNING ROOM. Board games make pretty good decoration even if you don't play it.
Casey: Here is what I see: Camp, The Worst-case Scenario Survival Game, Dungeon!, Stories of the Three Coins, Calendea, Risk Battlefield, D&D Starter Set, Monopoly, Axis & Allies, Ikusa, Agricola, Chess, Fast&Furious, Wonder Woman, Cathedral, Bob Ross Art of Chill, Crack the Case, Sherlock Holmes Consulting Detective: The Baker Street Irregulars, XCOM, D&D Adventure Begins, Princess Bride, U-Build Monopoly, Sherlock Holmes Consulting Detective: The Thames Murders & other cases, WWII Spotter Deck, Angels and Ancestors Oracle Cards, MM: The Carver, Eight-Minute Empire, MM: Fortune, MM: Midnight Dreary, MM: Pandemonium, MM: Quarantine, MM: Freak Show, MM: Eight Deadly Sins, MM: Underwood Cellars, Clue Junior, Mission Command, Break the Safe, Deception, Chronicles of Crime, One Night Werewolf, Day Break, Chutes and Ladders, Labyrinth, Hellapagoes, Monopoly Gamer, Sakura Arms, The Game of Life, Einstein, King of Tokyo, Risk: Plants vs Zombies, Pirateology, Dread Pirate, Magic the Gathering: Arena of the Planeswalers, Zombies!!!, LIFE: Pirates of the Carribean, Here Kitty Kitty, Pandemic. See? It is just a modest collection. It is not anything worth worrying about.
Evelyn: I wonder if this is a good time to do this on a Wednesday? 😅
Casey: I got the list posted, so we can think about it when we have time. Casey has completed a quest objective to create a list of board games. Sasha thanks Casey for making the list.

Charter 28 v4

Freedom Plaza
The purpose of this quest is to define the focus of Chapter 28 @Freedom, aka the Freedom Plaza. My overall vision is to establish Freedom Plaza as a main gateway from the outside world to our guild without Roleplaying to maintain the highest clarity. ~20220714

Version Up (20220714)

With @Concept changing to @Dungeon as a training ground, many items, most news events, previously logged there were recategorized to @Freedom. @Freedom decides to take charge of presenting those news events and take the unique role in the guild as a news hub. As such, housekeeping quests are now at odds with that focus. Those quests are up for adoption by @Guild or @Board, or @Treasure.

Objectives

⭐ Create a section for world events: News Hour, Freedom Watch
⭐ Create a philosophy/metric section, link to Freedom House Index
⭐ Maintain a ch28 quest list

⏱ Exercise Routines
Arnold 10/10
Start with one squat and one push up, then repeat with one additional squat and push up each time until you have done so for 10 minutes. Follow that by a 10 minute walk. {"cmd":"url","url":"https://www.insider.com/arnold-schwarzenegger-10-minute-bodyweight-workout-routine-2023-10","desc":"[link]"}
⏱ Healthy Practices
Turning routines into more flexible practices could save one broken link from disrupting the entire chain. Routines are what you do. Practices are what you do and who you are. {"cmd":"url","url":"https://www.inc.com/jeff-haden/want-to-be-more-successful-embrace-practices-not-routines.html","desc":"🔗"}
🧱 Exercises with Weights
{"cmd":"url","url":"https://www.fitandwell.com/news/an-expert-trainer-says-you-only-need-these-three-exercises-to-build-full-body-strength-at-home","desc":"🔗"} Goblet Squat

202207131009|Board Games

 202207142259|🧀 Mice & Mystics {"cmd":"PIN2","node":"202207142259","music":""}
Mice and Mystics is a super cute-looking game that Sasha suggested. She made a wish that we could play it together and have a rewarding experience. You are invited to play this game with us. ~ 20220714 LRRH: However, I heard that this game is very hard and we will probably play it very slowly as we will be discussing and most likely applying all kinds of new rules. So if you are looking for a game that will be played faithfully to its rules, this is probably not the group.
About this Game

Links

Rule Book | Story Book Chapter 1 | Lost Chapters | BoardGameGeek | How-to-play video |

Expansions

BGG: Heart of Glorm | BGG: Downwood Tales
🧀 Story driven game with 11 chapters: Save the kingdom from an evil queen who has brought illness to the king.
🧀 1-4 Players, but with 6 player characters. In some chapters not all of them are selectable.
Optional Subquest
🧀
Summarize how to play the game as needed to help others play.
Credit Schedule
🧀
EPIC Category C: Community. You may earn peacemaker credits for helping others have fun through this game.
Game Signup Sheet

💾 Save Points

Team Cheesecake
202207312301|🧀 The Sewer 202207312228|🧀 Kitchen Tunnels 202208072224|🧀 The Kitchen

✏️ Draft Area

Step by Step Guide

Chapter 1

Preparation

Preparation Step

We put this step in the beginning because it can be done before players gather at the table.

Game Clock

Set the game clock ("Story Control Board") with the hourglass token on Page 1 and "The End" token on Page 6. When the enemy gets a piece of cheese during the game, place that cheese on the clock to represent 10 minutes have passed. When the clock is full, clear the cheese and advance the hourglass. The heroes lose if the hourglass reaches "The End".

Encounter Deck

Take the nine Standard Encounter cards from the Encounter Deck. Shuffle and place them as a deck faced down on the Story Control Board.

Search Deck

Gather the heroes character cards. From the Search Deck, get the Starting Equipment for each hero. Give each hero their Starting Equipment. Shuffle the remaining deck and place it face down on the Story Control Board.

Chapter 1

Read Prologue (page 1-2)

TLDR

Prince Collin and friends were in prison for conspiring against Evil Queen Vanestra. To avoid certain execution, they stole some of Vanestra's magic and transformed themselves (along with a cellmate Filch) into mice.

Chapter 1 Tile 6

The Sewer

⚔️
The heroes fought the Rat Warriors in the Guard Room and then went down to the Sewer, falling into the running water. They must defeat the minions then exit to the North.
⭐ Use Tile 6: The Sewer Which is the flip side of the Tile 5: Guard Room
⭐ Place the heroes at the hook/turnover symbol. The heroes will start in the water.
⭐ Draw an Encounter Card.
⭐ Draw Initiatives
Movement in Water

Manual: Page 10

💦 Water does not affect minions in any way.
💦 If a mouse moves into water space, its movement ends.
💦 Mice CANNOT move aginst the water current.
💦 Mice CANNOT use free action in water.
💦 The water current carries each mouse one space along the flow at the end of its turn.
💦 A mouse is CAPTURED if the water current carries it off the tile
💦 To get out of water, spend the entire move and roll one die for Crit (D2). On success, the mouse is out of the water on adjacent land space. On failure, the mouse does not move.

Special Search

In Water Spaces: Fishhook and Thread
The firsthook and thread allows mice to get to high places quickly. This item is required if the mice wants to return to the Guard Room from the Sewer. Party items such as this are not saved at the end of a chapter.

Special Rule

Leaving the Sewer
All mice must be on the same side of the water to leave the sewer

Chapter One - Flight to Barksburg

🧀 STEP 3: Choose four mice, get one ability card each

Details

Lily cannot be chosen because she is not in the story yet. For each chosen mouse, inaddition to their starting equipment, choose one ability from the Ability Deck. Note 1: Most of the abilities are tied to specific class(es). Note 2: Cheeses are the cost of using abilities. The heroes can randomly get cheese when they roll dice.
In our group, we decided that after casting the transformation spell that Maginos could not cast without borrowing Vanestra's power, it would make sense if Maginos is out-of-mana. Therefore we have him be the odd one out.
📖 STEP 4: Read the Story and Victory Condition (Page 3)

TLDR

The heroes quickly retrieved their equipment in custody before the transformation spell ended, but Vanestra discovered their escape and transformed the prison guards into Rat Warriors to chase them.

Victory Condition

Clear Tile 1 of all enemies and move all mice to the Tree Hole at Tile 1 within 5 game hours.
🗺️ STEP 5: Get Tile 5 and place minis
Place the four heroes and three Rat Warriors as shown. This tile has these aspects*:

What a shiny helmet

Any hero searching at the helmet may choose to get the card "Tinkerer's Treasure" from the deck instead. Note 1: That card allows a Tinkerer Hero (Nez) to get any armor piece from the Search Deck when they search.

Can't Avoid Fighting

The heroes cannot escape down the sewer unless the tile is clear of Rat Warriors.

More Guards Are Coming

Spawn two more Rat Warriors on this tile every game hour. Note: The two Rat Warriors we placed outside the two doorway represent more guards are coming. Going through those doorways is not part of this chapter.
Aspect is a term from FATE-CORE. I think it simplifies the explanation.
⭐ STEP 6: Draw Initiatives

Turn Order

From the Initiatives Deck, get the card for the four heroes and the single Rat Warriors card. Shuffle these five cards and place them from slot 1 down . This represents the turn order of the minis. The Rat Warriors will act one by one during their turn. Note 1: The Rat Warriors initiative card has their battle powers and special ability: They move up in intiative after taking their turn!
⚔️ STEP 7: Defeat the Rat Warriors
In this step, the characters will take their turn according to the turn order. The character may move then act, or act then move.

Hero Move

Move Value + D3
Roll one die and add the number at the corner to the Move Value of the hero to get the number of spaces the hero may move this turn. The icon on the dice is irrelevant for movement.
Each die has two 1's, two 2's, and two 3's. A character's movement is their Move Value plus D3. Note 1: Each space can fit four small minis and one large mini. Note 2: When a hero moves into a space that has an enemy, the hero's movement ends. Note 3: When a hero's space has an enemy, the hero can't start moving unless there are more heroes than enemies on that space.

Hero Action

Scurry
Scurry allows the hero to take another movement as their action. The two movements use separate die rolls.

Hero Action

Battle (Melee)
???

Rat Warrior Move, Attack

Move: D3 | Attack: 2 dice

Who Controls the Rat Warriors?

The player with a hero lowest on the turn order control the movement of the Rat Warriors. However, each Rat Warrior must move to a hero closes to it. If there is a tie between two heroes, the Rat Warrior will attack the hero higher on the turn order. After moving as need toward its target hero, the Rat Warrior will attack if its target hero is in an adjacent or same space.

Attacking a Hero

Roll two dice for the Rat Warrior (since its Battle Value is 2), and count the swords. Roll the number of dice equal to the Defense value of the hero (plus any bonus from armor), and count the shields. Each shield cancels a sword of the Rat Warrior. The hero takes a wound for each sword not cancelled.
Each die has three swords and two shields.

Cheese 🧀

Rat Warrior rolling cheese has no effect. But if a hero rolls cheese while defending, the game clock advances 10 minutes once immediately, and the hero gets one cheese for each cheese they rolled.

Bow

The bow symbol rolled by a Rat Warrrior or a hero doesn't have any effect in this case.

I already played Adventure Begins... 😅 Evelyn signed up Little Red Riding Hood in Team Deus Ex Machina. Evelyn has signed up and added some info about the game.

Sasha: I suppose after we played it we could GM it for you. Then I look foward to your team playing it. 👍 Sasha signed up Black in team "Wish someone could GM" Black adds the credit category. Black thanks the team for trying the game.

SCOREBOARD

Video
{"cmd":"url","url":"https://www.youtube.com/embed/Mjoxh4c2Dj0","desc":"📺"}
Encounter
⭐
I was referred to this book while discussing at Twitter. It might be related to Stewardism, so we should examine and compare the concepts.
⭐ Following that, there was another link to the Tweeter's Blog.
⭐ More follow up: Sacred Economy

Principles (and comparisons)

See Chapter 3 - Interbeing
1 That my being partakes of your being and that of all beings. This goes beyond interdependency—our very existence is relational. That, therefore, what we do to another, we do to ourselves. This is not a principle in Arcacianism. Arcacianism cares about beings that are not part of ourselves.
2 That each of us has a unique and necessary gift to give the world. Arcacianism does not prescribe necessity nor uniqueness. It is about helping, not looking for something special about yourself (although you could very well find it while helping others.
3 That the purpose of life is to express our gifts. Arcacianism does not prescribe a purpose of life. Gifting is a choose. A person may choose to adopt that as their purpose of life.
4 That every act is significant and has an effect on the cosmos. Arcacianism does not prescribe such importance to every act. Arcacianism recognizes that small acts can build habits.
5 That we are fundamentally unseparate from each other, from all beings, and from the universe. Similar to principle 1, the truthfulness of this condition is irrelevant.
6 That every person we encounter and every experience we have mirrors something in ourselves. In Arcacianism, a person can find new paths and experience. It is more about accepting new encounters
7 That humanity is meant to join fully the tribe of all life on Earth, offering our uniquely human gifts toward the well-being and development of the whole. Similar to principle 3, there is no prescribed meaning in Arcacianism. You don't have to live a life aiming to offer something unique to others. You could just enjoy or share the good things you encountered.
8 That purpose, consciousness, and intelligence are innate properties of matter and the universe. Arcacianism prescribes neither of the three.

Chapter 32 - Miracle

⭐ When one is aligned with the purpose of service, acts that seem exceptionally courageous to others are a matter of course.
⭐ When one experiences the world as abundant, then acts of generosity are natural, since there is no doubt about continued supply.
⭐ When one sees other people as reflections of oneself, forgiveness becomes second nature, as one realizes “But for the grace of God, so go I.”
⭐ When one appreciates the order, beauty, mystery, and connectedness of the universe, a deep joy and cheerfulness arises that nothing can shake.
⭐ When one sees time as abundant and life as infinite, one develops superhuman patience.
⭐ When one lets go of the limitations of reductionism, objectivity, and determinism, technologies become possible that the science of separation cannot countenance.
⭐ When one lets go of the story of the discrete and separate self, amazing intuitive and perceptual capabilities emerge from lifelong latency.

Objectives

⭐ Survey the concept in this book and take notes. Is it its own ideology? Did we find something new?
⭐ Update Seed of Peace with link if we found something new or find a good reference to an existince concept.

Credit Schedule

⭐ EPIC I: Innovation/Ideology. You may get credits exploring ideas related to peacemaking.

Follow up

⭐ Sacred Economics

📯 Quest Info

Objectives

⭐ Review failures of guild activities for improvement

Process

⭐ Tag failures in field log with the tag "[FAIL]"
⭐ Review failures in discussions with tag "fail"

Credit Schedule

⭐ EPIC P :Production/Performance. You may earn credits for reviewing production performance to identify areas of improvement.
📌 Monthly Todo List

Field

1️⃣ Assigned solution code to each failure.
2️⃣ Complete countermeasure for all failures.

HQ

📌 Explain the situations in the TPK section
3️⃣ Process entries with code HQ.
4️⃣ List all Failure Analysis codes.
5️⃣ Check MIS/MET for all quest sites.
6️⃣ Categorize Failure Area and Cause for all failures.
This includes lack of research. This includes not paying attention or distracted. Update the quest checklist to include missed checks. How to use CHK: While using, if you think of something that should be checked, make a list. If the CHK list doesn't cover those items, add them to CHK.
Count: 60/210 Periodically review protocols as a refresher. Queuing tasks so that they are not forgotten. Need to count repeated failures where a previous countermeasure didn't prevent reoccurrence.

Before discussion, it was marked 'HQ'.","type":"Countermeasure","start":"20230416 ","author":"Tanya","cmd":"pl2"} {"icon":"🌳","desc":"MAP","qty":"3","outof":"210","note":"Update the quest map to share experience across different quests.","type":"Countermeasure","start":"2022","author":"Tanya","cmd":"pl2"} {"icon":"🌳","desc":"MST","qty":"12","outof":"317","note":"Master Template for documents.","type":"Countermeasure","start":"20230318","author":"Tanya","cmd":"pl2"} {"icon":"🌳","desc":"PRC","qty":"28","outof":"210","note":"Upgrade the checklist by grouping check items by stages to faciliate earlier checks. 202302041643|💬","type":"Countermeasure","start":"20230130","author":"Tanya","cmd":"pl2"} {"icon":"🌳","desc":"PTC (Protocol)","qty":"2","outof":"330","note":"Define a protocol for participants to cooperate. 202305012048|💬","type":"Countermeasure","start":"20230130","author":"Casey","cmd":"pl2"} {"icon":"🌳","desc":"RCD (Record)","qty":"?","outof":"???","note":"Update the record for future reuse.","type":"Countermeasure","start":"20230802","author":"Casey","cmd":"pl2"} {"icon":"🌳","desc":"RGT (Regression Test)","qty":"?","outof":"???","note":"Keep a list of check items to confirm there is no unintended changes.","type":"Countermeasure","start":"20230830","author":"LRRH","cmd":"pl2"} {"icon":"🌳","desc":"RVW (Review List)","qty":"?","outof":"???","note":"Checkitems for design review.","type":"Countermeasure","start":"20230830","author":"Black","cmd":"pl2"} {"icon":"🌳","desc":"SGN","qty":"2","outof":"317","note":"Placing signs and place them where people would be reminded to do something.","type":"Countermeasure","start":"20230416","author":"Evelyn","cmd":"pl2"} {"icon":"🌳","desc":"TSN","qty":"110","outof":"317","note":"Update the quest notebook with useful information.","type":"Countermeasure","start":"2022","author":"Zoey","cmd":"pl2"}

No lasting impact to the quest but causes inconvenience to other teams. The code MTG is for marking meeting that are ontime.","type":"Severity","start":"","author":"Evelyn","cmd":"pl2"} {"icon":"⚡","desc":"RWK (Rework)","qty":"?","outof":"???","note":"Failures where a process needs to be repeated or a part needs to be remade.","type":"Severity","start":"","author":"Casey","cmd":"pl2"} {"icon":"⚡","desc":"SFT (Safety)","qty":"1","outof":"330","note":"Failures where an injury was or almost occured.","type":"Severity","start":"","author":"Ledia","cmd":"pl2"} {"icon":"🔥","desc":"TPK","qty":"4","outof":"317","note":"Total Party KO. This signifies high priority situation to avoid in the future.","type":"Severity","start":"","author":"Evelyn","cmd":"pl2"}
Late for Routine Meeting ⭐

Problem

Being late for routine meeting.

Goal

Not being late.
Causes

☁️ Lack of habit

The meeting is at the same time in the morning every week, but earlier than quester's random start time.
Solutions

🌳 Reminder

Add a prominent reminder that there is such a meeting.

☁️ Remote Meeting

Get use to joining the meeting remotely

🧱 Start Earlier

Change the habit to start earlier every day. Evelyn: This might not work if you are covering a later shift every day.

☀️ Earlier reminder

Change the default reminder from 15 minutes to 30 minutes.

☀️ Set the clock earlier

Set the clock 20 minutes ealier.

202207152016|Game Arrived
July 15, 2022 Friday 8:16 PM
Mice & Mystics
⭐
Mice & Mystics Chapter 1 Go Go Go! Friday Night Game Session XD I'm following Tanya's format of using CHAT for RP and BUBBLE for non-RP. Starting now. For this playthrough, I want to use the official game rules first, until we decide that we have something better and we won't miss some surprising mechanics or plot that the designer planned. So who is reading the story? We take turn reading it? It is not that long. 202207142259|🧀 Mice & Mystics
Zoey: I can't believe that there IS an explanation to why the mice have armor! XD Are we RPing? After reading the prologue and start of chapter one, I have no idea how to RP. Let's just talk normal for now and play the game. To summarize, we were planning to revoke against Vanestra the evil queen. We were caught and thrown into a dungeon. To escape, we permanently transformed ourselves into mice, but Vanestra found that out also and transformed some guards into mice to chase us. Our immediate objective for Chapter One is to escape to the courtyard before time runs out. We will need to defeat all enemies at the last tile and reach the exit space before time runs out. It says Lily the Archer is not in Chapter One! XD 😅 I guess I'll GM this time! XD We still have one extra. I'll sit out and co-GM. But you are Collin, if you don't play, wouldn't that make the game really hard? I think that makes the first Chapter a good chapter to sit out. I guess.
Zoey: (GM) So I will use the CHAT to talk about game. We are about to start Chapter 1. The players we have are Sasha (Maginos), Evelyn (Tilda), Casey (Nez), and P4 (Filch). I have an idea. I should sit out as Maginos. He was the mage who had just transformed everyone into mice, which was supposed to be a huge feat. He should be out of mana. Tanya receives That does make a lot of sense. Then, I play Collin? 🤔 I think P4 should play Collin. You should play Filch. From the story, you are more like Filch. It might be easier to RP. I don't feel strongly either way. If I play Collin could we swap stats and class? That's way too confusing. I'll play Collin. Let's try first. If it doesn't work, we can change it next time.
Zoey: (GM) Players, after choosing your mouse, you may look at all the ability cards and choose one. Please consider the abilities your mouse already has when you choose. Does it mean that Tilda as a healer can't heal anyone without getting First Aid as an ability? 😅 Correct. Evelyn (Tilda) chooses First Aid as her ability. Some of the abilities are tied to a class, let's separate them. There are actual Healer class abilities. May I change my choice? Yes you may, until everyone is ready. Evelyn (Tilda) changes her ability to Cure. P4 (Filch) chooses Dodge as her ability. Casey (Nez} chooses Protect as her ability. Sasha (Collin) chooses Give Order as her ability.
Zoey: (GM) Now I shuffle the Encounters deck and place it on the story control board. As I do so, I notice that the encounter cards have two types. Some have a black E on the back, the others have a red E. We will find out what they foretell.
Zoey: (GM) Next I set up tiles 2, 4, 5 as shown on the manual. The manual didn't show the tile numbers. I think it would be quicker if they did.
Zoey: (GM) The mice will start on Tile 5: The Guard Room. There I also place 3 rat warriors. The rules on page 14 says when placing small melee minions, they should be spread out as much as possible. Therefore, in this case there is only one way to place them: One on each spawn point.
Zoey: Helmet is special if searched by a tinkerer. And this tile will only spawn more rat warriors (2 each) time when the story timer advances. I think this game would play better with a GM, because the GM can deal out the tiles one by one and hide the details about the rules.
Zoey: (GM) As GM, right after the part Maginos said, "Quick, to the Sewer", I would present the first tile (The Guard Room) with the minis already set up. I think that would be quite dramatic. 😊 I have an idea, why don't we write a step by step script for a GM to present the game? Right now the Story book doesn't do so because it assumes the reader already read the rules. It doesn't even have hints that the player should be doing something standard.
Zoey: (GM) Take the one Rat Warriors initiative card and shuffle it with that of the four selected heroes. Then reveal them on the initiative tracker starting from the top. The order is Rat Warriors > Nez > Collin > Filch > Tilda.
Zoey: (GM) Since Collin is not first, his Born to Lead ability gives him one cheese.
Zoey: (GM) The rats go first. For each rat, roll one die for movement and move the rat toward the closest hero. Then attack anyone within its melee range. In our case the bottom rat willl attack Nez because he is higher on the scale.
LRRH: The gaming session ended because it was late. To be continued...
