Guide

What is a Post?

202207160804|PIN 202207160804

At the Blogger at Magic Bakery, a Post is dashboard layout to display contents stored in an archive. This is not the typical way Blogger sites work, but we are mimicking the intended organization when the site is upgrade to use databases.

What is a Dashboard?

⭐ A dashboard is something that each user may create to view contents of the archive.

What Should be a Post?

⭐ Chapters
⭐ For now, also Calendars

How do I reference something in the archive?

⭐ Each data object in the archive has a unique ID and labels.
⭐ The tag [jq] can reference the data.
⭐ To reference a specific object, such as this guide: [jq]#P202207160804</jq>
⭐ To reference all objects with a label such as all "guide" objects: [jq][data-guide]</jq>
⭐ To reference all objects with multiple labels: [jq][data-quest][data-ch18]</jq>
⭐ To reference all objects with either label: [jq][data-book],[data-charter]</jq>
202207160808|What is a Post?
July 16, 2022 Saturday 8:08 AM
Help Desk
⭐
At the help desk, StarTree is supposed to explain how the Blogger site works. The first question is "What is a Post?" So. 202207160804|What is a Post?
⭐
Ivy: For blogger sites in general, there are two main ways to display content: Post and Page. A Post is the default way blogger gives us to post content. A Post shows a timestamp, it is automatically listed on the front page and calendar control, and it allows labels. A Page is does not have a timestamp and is not automatically created. The admin would need to create a link to link to a Page. Typically this is done at the top navigation bar or at the side bar.
Ivy: For OUR blogger site, we use Post and Page quite differently. So what I will talk about below is more like a proposal.
Ivy: I propose that we separate the data and the layout by using Post as a dashboard layout, and Page as data archive. I think this will make it easier when we migrate to a website that has a database.
Ivy: In Magic Bakery terminology, only Chapters and perhaps Calendars should be a Post. Everything else should be in a Page.
⭐
Zoey: I have a question about this arrangement. If we organize it like this, does it mean there can only be one archive? Do we end up having a centralized system?
Ivy: My answer is we want to have a decentralized system, but first we need to know how to make at least one system with database. In a decentralized and cross-checking system, which is the way Quest Society data should be maintained, each data center is making copies of the data of another center, but each center by itself can be considered a centralized node.
Tanya: What is the plan to handle object ID's? Will they still be unique across all nodes?
Ivy: That will be handled the same way as URL. Each node has its own unique domain ID. The full unique ID of an object would have the domain path.
⭐
Ivy: So even though we know that the final Quest System would be decentralized, I propose that our current focus to be on a single centralized system. We are trying to make one node first, then we will make it work with other nodes in an alliance.
Tanya: I see. I think this overall plan should be somewhere.
StarTree: StarTree will add to 202207142015|🎪 Quest Board!
Evelyn: If a post should only be a dashboard, does it mean there should only be about 30 posts right now?
Ivy: Correct. Of the 99 posts now, most of them should have its content sent to an archive.
Evelyn: The archive is becoming slow as it gets bigger. Is there something we can do about it?
Ivy: We could split it into multiple archives based on the data date. But that mechanism is only for Blogger. When we migrade to a database, that would be implied. For now I suggest just using VSC or other editor to edit it locally. Then just copy and paste to the archive.
Evelyn: But if I am stitching data back to the archive, I would need to keep the Archive open for a long time, then no one can edit anything.
Ivy: If you just want to test the HTML before adding it to the Archive, you could test it at a temporary post first. Then when you are done, you could copy that to the Archive. Then if something breaks in your code, it won't break the entire site.
Evelyn: Okay. I think I can do that. Evelyn thanks all for the guide.
This node was the game version of the Bias node, but maybe the two nodes should be merged. Change the layout format to the new style. No longer need the three column style. A woman will base 55% of her initial impression of a man on his appearance https://twitter.com/Fact/status/1427237706887798786 The items on the sighting and staging areas can go to the inbox of the corresponding node. Added the search list section.
🎪 About
📕 Fallacies Listing
🎃 Monsters Listing
🍀 Sighting Board
🍯 Staging Area
🎯 Deployment
🔮 Operation View
👑 Scoreboard

Objectives

⭐ To train guild members to avoid logical fallacies by representing fallacies as monster for them to spot and defeat in dungeon.
⭐ To log the current best practice of how to run the @Dungeon quest to promote participation of the quest and quality of the content, so that as a guild, we could help people in an efficient and enjoyable manner.

Credit Schedule

⭐ EPIC C: Community/Communication. You may earn credits for helping a community improve their communication skills.

How to Participate

⭐ Catalog: Currently there are about 25 fallacy types identified. Define each of them as a fallacy in the archive with label "data-fallacy"
⭐ Catalog: When a monster representation is conceived/mentioned in a post, define each of them as a monster in the archive and tag with "data-monster" and the ID of that fallacy
⭐ Spotting: During any discussion, if you spot a monster, you may link to that monster's ID or the fallacy ID

Game Scoring (Not Guild Credits)

⭐ Spotting a fallacy monster.
⭐ Spotting for the first time a fallacy type that has not been spotted is worth an additional point.
⭐ Protection Discuss alternative way to communicate without using the fallacy.
⭐ Countermeasure Discuss best practice when the fallacy is used against you.
⭐ Counteraction Responding to a thread in best practice where a fallacy was used.
⭐ Awareness Promoting this game to spread awareness as appropriate.

Treasures

⭐ You may draw a treasure card (based on Dungeon!) at a level based on the score. Each card has a credit value. When the total of your score reaches the amount you need for your class, you are considered graduated from the training.
⭐ Graduation credits required (based on Dungeon!): Cleric: 10000 pt | Rogue: 10000 pt | Fighter: 20000 pt | Wizard: 30000 pt

202207161150|Mode Change
July 16, 2022 Saturday 11:50 AM
Arx Dungeon
⭐
I am changing the mode for Arx Dungeon because, of the 100+ items currently tagged for @Dungeon, most of them should be in @Arena. So I have just set all of them to be Arena. If you spot a fallacy, please tag it for @Dungeon. Other than that, @Dungeon will focus on upgrading the interface on weeks where no 202207161025|fallacy is identified.
Tanya: Please support @Arena in clearing all those quests.
Zoey: Hmm... And @Arena should also be a quest. So we should also define that as a quest.
Tanya: I think we need to do that, and define profiles of the contestants as ID'ed objects. Right now they merely have an ID, but they are not objects in the archive.
Evelyn: How many different objects do we need to make?
Tanya: A philosophy can have multiple champions. So each philosophy should be an object, and each champion should be an object. After that, each challenge, contest, and combat should be an object.
Sasha: I don't think we should spend a lot of time juggling data. Otherwise we would be spending time making new bins instead of adding substance to the bins.
Tanya: Then let's try continue in the new way when we add something.
Evelyn: I think we should define @Arena as a quest, otherwise it won't show up on the quest list.
Tanya: @StarTree, could you define @Arena as a quest object?
StarTree: It is now defined! 202207161224|🏛️ Philosophy Arena
Tanya: Next let's define an ideology object. Since StarTree is here, let's define Veganism. Roger! 202207161233|🥦 Veganism
Tanya: Now add StarTree as a Champion object. P02207110924|🏅 Veganism
Tanya: Next, add flex containers to list all philosophy and champion objects in arena quest. Done! This looks kind of fun when you don't need to do it yourself. 😅 StarTree is happy for helping people! StarTree, I think the object picture of the champion should be you. Hai!
Tanya: Since there was already an ID, the object should use the same ID. And on the category title, instead of "Champion", please say "Veganism". And add back the four hearts. Roger! And make the picture 100 px please. StarTree is cute! Wee~~!
Tanya: Now at the original arena page, add jq tag for Champions and remove the original StarTree.
Sasha: Hold on. If you do that, the ranking order won't be preserved.
Tanya: I think we shouldn't manually set the ranking order. The ranking order should be automatically done with javascript. StarTree please add a todo item for Arena to implementing ranking order code. I think we should also add the other todo items. StarTree please add those with priority above the ranking code. Done!
Tanya: Since we will be making the other champion profiles similarly, let's complete StarTree's profile first. What should go in StarTree's profile?
Ledia: I think that will be her moves. But that is irrelevant until we fight.
Zoey: Actually what is the intended difference between Philosophy and Champion?
Tanya: Philosophy is non-RP. So if I want to talk about it on discussion, I could just link to that without the gamification.
⭐
Zoey: Then should StarTree's champion profile have a link to Veganism? Yes it should.
Tanya: StarTree, please add Veganism as something you are a champion of. Can a champion be a champion of multiple philosophies? The way we define it now, no. Since each Champion profile has hit points, and hits points are tied to a specific philosophy. But I think the "Champion of" section can have multiple items.
Ledia: StarTree could you replace the picture for Veganism? And in Veganism, I think there shouldn't be StarTree's photo.
Evelyn: Shouldn't there be a "Championed by" section in Veganism?
Zoey: I agree with Evelyn. But I think Tanya will object.
Tanya: I prefer having something that is completely factual. By that I mean everything on Veganism should be about Veganism itself. So I agree with Ledia.
Zoey: That has to do with how Tanya intends to use it. We had many debates about that regarding Albatross. But, Tanya, if you are okay with Logical Fallacy Dungeon to have gaming elements in it, why is it not okay for Philosophy objects to have gaming elements also?
Tanya: I think the audience that would read the philosophy object would be the more serious type. If you keep one to be more serious, then you could choose to use the Champion link or the Philosophy link. On the Philosophy object I plan to link to more URL and articles outside Magic Bakery.
Zoey: Okay. I understand. That might be a good way to separate RP and non-RP content. Ivy's request to create reference links for profiles is complete.
Ivy: Well I marked that done because you bit the bullet and made the actual object. So we don't need the reference link anymore. Congratulations. Ivy thanks all for the upgrade. Could we not use that idiom? It has a very graphical and unpleasant origin. 😖 Apologies.
Ledia: We're just about done with the garnish. Let's add the definition from Wiki: "Veganism is a philosophy and way of living which seeks to exclude—as far as is possible and practicable—all forms of exploitation of, and cruelty to, animals for food, clothing or any other purpose; and by extension, promotes the development and use of animal-free alternatives for the benefit of animals, humans and the environment. In dietary terms it denotes the practice of dispensing with all products derived wholly or partly from animals." Ledia and StarTree got EXP for adding a definition of Veganism.
Ledia: I think we should also add links to the Vegan Society and wikipedia. Now that looks minimally complete.
Evelyn: Should there be a collection of philosophies for people to browse to another philosophy node on our site? I would say yes. If an object belongs to a collection, having a link back to its collection should be required. StarTree please make a Collection object for Philosophies, and create another object that is a Collection of Collections. Tanya receives

Veganism is a philosophy and way of living which seeks to exclude—as far as is possible and practicable—all forms of exploitation of, and cruelty to, animals for food, clothing or any other purpose; and by extension, promotes the development and use of animal-free alternatives for the benefit of animals, humans and the environment. In dietary terms it denotes the practice of dispensing with all products derived wholly or partly from animals. https://www.vegansociety.com/go-vegan/definition-veganism
Different axioms of veganism https://x.com/LRRHBakery/status/1844404543519605233 If you eat meat to survive, you are not necessarily practicing non-veganism. Non-veganism means that given the choice, you would rather exploit animals for yourself than just let them be happy. https://x.com/LRRHBakery/status/1555232363189137410 This is a definition from Animal Ethics website https://www.animal-ethics.org/veganism/ Veganism is a moral position that opposes exploiting and otherwise harming nonhuman animals. This includes what we do directly, such as hunting or fishing. It also includes what we support as consumers, which affects many more animals. originally the word "meat" meant any kind of food or sustenance, not just animal flesh. https://x.com/maaanandamayi/status/1842293547913146661 {"cmd":"url","url":"https://twitter.com/veganfuture/status/1546620347184734215","desc":"🔗"} Lobsters can feel {"cmd":"url","url":"https://twitter.com/veganuary/status/1535698379375685632","desc":"🔗"}. Collection

Collections
202207161422|PIN 202207161422
Load all Collections
This section shows philosophies in categories as we analyze them for their intended role in world peace.
In this website we define things so they can be discussed. Good definitions should be easy to understand to help people discuss philosophy. For precision we could use compound nouns to avoid defining a word to only have a narrow meaning. https://x.com/LRRHBakery/status/1896243693797142917
⚖️ Impartial ⚖️
Impartial philosophies follow Reciprocative Justice "You may treat someone the way they treat others" and establishes an unbiased basis of conflict resolution that makes them especially important for world peace as a foundation of inter-society arbitration.

Criteria

✅ Free of preferential commitments
✅ Arbitrates inter-societal conflicts
💗 Preferential 💗
Preferential philosophies, while possibly peaceful, have additional commitments that could be beneficial but not strictly necessary for world peace. They can serve as role models but they need to let people quit for other peaceful societies they prefer.

Criteria

✅ Has preferetial commitments
✅ Forces no one to accept membership
🗺️ Complete 🗺️
Complete philsophies aims to prescribe the entire constitution of an organization including any rights and obligations, and conflict resolution methods in all areas among its members.

Criteria

✅ Defines all societal functions
✅ Defines diplomatic decision process
🧩 Component 🧩
Component philosophies do not aim to envision how an entire society function, but focuses on principles that a society shall follow.

Criteria

✅ Passes objectivity test
✅ Net positive effect on world peace
✅ Can be adopted by other philosophies
🎠 Stable ♻️
Stable philosophies are sustainable and can withstand internal and external stressors to a good degree for perpetual peace and happiness. The basic requirement is sustainability.

Criteria

✅♻️ Sustainable
✅🕊️ Long lasting peace
✅🎠 Perpetual happiness
⚠️ Unstable 💣
Unstable philosophies drive the world toward conflicts and extinction by the dynamics of the philosophies themselves, or by their inability to handle variation and natural stressors.

Criteria

✅⚠️ Unsustainable
✅⚔️ Conflict inducing
✅💣 Extinction inducing
⚖️ Impartial Philosophies

🗺️ Complete Philosophy

Last week I sketched 202207092054|Wonder Woman but didn't get any feedback. Maybe they don't really care about it. Does anyone want me to draw something else?
Tanya: 3B could you draw Melody? Melody doesn't have an avatar. Actually I drew her already. Let me make the avatar. ... Yellow doesn't look very good does it? I made it black and white.
Sasha: That's isn't really drawing anything. If you just want to look up pictures, even I can do that. I didn't know it existed.
Evelyn: What about Skyle? She has a picture also. I know this is not a drawing request but I want to see their avatars also. 🙏 Let me find it. Uploaded. Thank you! It bothered me that we heard her singing by she didn't have an avatar. 😊
Sasha: Could you find Albatross too? I mean... that's the last one without an avatar on the Roster... I though that was supposed to be a LEGO model. Let's say if the person that be got around building a LEGO model, we would gladly replace the avatar with a picture. Ok. 😅
3B: I uploaded a flag... because I haven't found the picture yet.
LRRH: It better be somewhere. I found a 48x48 px sprite. CUTE !!!! 😍 That is SO old school. I thought there is also a drawing. I don't see it. But I found an energy bar. {"cmd":"img","src":"https://magicbakery.github.io/images/Albatross%20-%20E12.png"}
Sasha: I have an idea. Why don't we replace all the emoji we use with sprites? Because they don't look the same on different browsers. It is a lot easier to draw sprites that look complete.
3B: I think unless everything is drawn with sprites, the style would clash. If you say so.
Sasha: Then could you draw Chibi characters for @Arena? We are gearing up toward having fights between philosophy girls.
3B: I think I can. Who will fight first?
LRRH: I think Capitalism and Socialism should fight first. They fight all the time at Twitter, there is a lot of material.
Tanya: We don't yet have a champion for Socialism.
3B: I could just add one if you want. Who? Naomi of Reclamation Corp.Chapter 10 page 21. That is a really nice picture. Please add her. A champion for Socialism is found.
Tanya: I think now we met the minimum requirement to start having a fight. I still don't know what the rules should be. Are we trying to compare military strengths? What is a fight? I thought a fight is a debate presented as a duel. But the content of the debate allows the champion to use military force. For example, if the debate topic is territorial dispute, Imperialism could say they will take over the territory by force, and the opponent can't just say, "If you do that the other nations will attack you" because the assumption is that the two philosophies are on a duel arena--There are no other forces. I would take that explanation, but I think it should be pinned. It is hard to remember such details.
Tanya: StarTree, could you make a collection node of games? Done! And a game 202207162110|"Philosophy Duel" Tanya added the explanation and updated @Arena.
Sylvia: ** Plays Fort Arsenal **

202207161422|🐿️ Collections
Philosophy Duel pits the philosophies against each other in scenarios where they can interact with no higher power to enforce any rule. Everything goes. This is not about who is right, but who will be left.

Objectives

⭐

Credits

⭐

Plan

⭐
Duel: Capitalism vs Socialism. This duel should be first because there is a lot of debate at Twitter.

Design

⭐
Philosophical Duel is a debate presented as a a duel. Depending on what arguments are allowed in the debate, the content might include the use of military forces. For example, if the debate topic is territorial dispute, Imperialism could cite its policy to take over the territory by force, if the opponent reply "The other nations will protect me" or "the nations will counter attack you,", then the response would be an ineffective defense because there are no other nations to help in the context of a duel.

Government

13 SW Lestrade Has official reports and information from the London Police.
38 EC Medical Examiner Performs the autopsies on all bodies found during the investigations
22 SW Ciminologist Analyses all items and substances found during the investigations.
17 WC National Archives Somerset House. All archives: births, deaths, marriages, wills, land records, and old criminal records.
5 SW Librarian Works at the London Library. To be consulted for any encyclopedic research.

30 EC Ellis (Times Reporter)
2 SW Pike (Social Columnist)

SCOREBOARD
🥨 Players: 3B, Evelyn, Ivy, Ledia, LRRH, Sasha, StarTree, Sylvia, Tanya, Zoey
⭐ Merits: 3B, Ivy, StarTree, Tanya, Zoey
🏅 Recognitions:
💟 Kudos: Evelyn, Ivy, Ledia, Sasha, StarTree, Tanya, Zoey
💗 Awards: Arcacia, Evelyn, Sasha, Sylvia, Tanya
👑 Top: 17 StarTree 13 Ledia 13 Tanya 11 Evelyn 8 Sasha 8 Zoey 8 3B 7 Black 5 Sylvia 4 Arcacia 4 LRRH 3 James 3 Ivy 2 Fina 2 Kisaragi 2 V 2 Natalie 2 Casey 1 Cardinal 1 Vivi 1 P4
Peacemaker Archetypes celebrate different approaches and skills on peacemaking. Below are six archetypes we use to classify skills. What is your peacemaking style?
202207170956|🍀Alchemist 202207252131|❤️Cleric 202209031718|📯Herald 202209082209|🎩Magician 202209142228|🔮Oracle 202209192238|:Paladin:Paladin Make the layout intuitive for navigation. There are many changes to the layout of the archetype nodes, I think it would be helpful to share our thoughts on which layout is the best. Word of caution: When you read these kudos, please consider whether the concept is the best for your node. Maybe there is something even better and we would like to see it. I think the beauty of EPIC is that you can be EPIC in different ways. The headings don't need to be standardized. I think we will need a triage node to index what skills can solve what problem.
[1] Evelyn
I think right now Paladin has the best layout. I especially like the sections in Practical Skills where the sections are listed in the order a person would want to follow as a curriculum. [1] Tanya
The Cleric node is moving toward: curriculum \ ordered topics \ star self-assessment. I think the self-assessment section is new and useful. I plan to use a similar structure but I might use topics instead of RES at the topic level so that the individual skills within self-assessment can be RES objects. [2] Evelyn, Ledia, Arcacia
Zoey started the Exemplar section that lists different titles of herald roles. I think that is helpful for newcomer to tell if what they aspire to do is a herald role. I also liked that section in Herald. Maybe I can add it in compatibility section in Paladin. I like how the roles can be sorted by the medal icon search button. [1] Arcacia
Setting the main sections to be Essence, Principles, Infrastructure, and Catalog of Skills. Hiding text with About note sections. [1] Evelyn
I like how the Magician practical skills section is more compact by using notes instead of topics for the first level. I think it would be better yet with introductory text like the section at Paladin.

Adoptions

This is using topics for the top level menu, notes for the second level, and Res for the third level to keep the layout more compact. RES entries have a section for people to report that they have the skill by marking with a medal, which is added as one of the search buttons. I think the specific spells listed for Combat Magic should not be classified as Paladin skills. What do people think? @StarTree please reformat as poll. Done!
[3] Sasha, Casey, Ivy
[-2] Ledia, Tanya
I think only the combative intention or Use of Force is the paladin skill. The catalog of individual spells should be in either Alchemist or Magician nodes. But I would add spells like fireworks and sparkers to Magician because in those cases they are entertainment use of force. So I thought combat use of force should go to Paladin. I think it should mean more when a paladin says the skill shouldn't be in the paladin section. Maybe we should give Ledia's vote a -1 also? Done! I think I would side with Sasha that the combat use of an element is a Paladin skill. The Alchemist/Engineering buildings don't have combat training room to practice those skills. That hall would be a paladin hall. True, but I think it is appropriate to put healing skills in clerics even though we use them in combat and they train with us. What is the issue if combat magics are listed in Paladin? Using tools for crafting would be in Alchemist, and using tools (weapons) for comat would be in Paladin. Why should it be different when the tool is magic? In this case the spell is the same spell. Why would you list fire cone twice depending on what it is used for? We could just classify energy conjuring in general under Alchemist, and "Tactical Use of Force" under Paladin. And in Tactical Use of Force, we talk about their functional roles in combat such as protection, barrier, incapacitation, elimination, etc. They answer the question from Paladin perspective, "If you need to form a barrier, what spells can you use?" or "What spells are effective against what kind of opponent?" So the Paladin section will also talk about magic but refer to Alchemist or Magician training if the specific person wants to learn more about how to use that particular element. I think Ledia's explanation is clear and I agree. Please feel free to update and move the content to Alchmist. I am not sure how to word them. [6] Ledia, Tanya, Sasha, Casey, Ivy, Zoey
Since @Sasha didn't add them to Magician, maybe they should be in Alchemist. I am fine with the spells being listed in Alchemist if they don't have combat names. And the section should be something like "Elemental Mastery." Combat skill naming can be a paladin skill. When the skill gets their own node, both Paladin and Alchemist nodes can refer to that node, and the elemental skill node can list the Alchemist name and the Paladin name. It will be a Chuunibyou translator! XD https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Main/CallingYourAttacks I like the sound of elemental mastery, but I wouldn't think twice listing Herald elemental skills under Herald. XD I don't think I am very off. Why would an Alchemist cast "Magic Missiles"? They won't. It is distinctively a combat use of force. Same as Frost Ray. Maybe "Fire Cone" was just not combative enough. Fire cone is like a flamethrower. Not all kinds of "combat" use are "paladin" uses. But you would use that in pest control. I would consider pest control an Alchemist use of force. Okay I concede. Good rebuttal. I was inconsiderate to just add Fire Cone as an example in Paladin. A nuke is a nuke to a nuker! Good discussion. XD [1] Ledia
[-1] Sasha
 Archetype normal name: Production, Empathy, Action, Communication, Enjoyment, Warning
Make node for each skill. Make node for each skill and list with star rank. Each identified failure type should be a card. Each bias should be a card Check each Skill RES and send additional information to the node. Keep each Skill RES as short as possible. Knowing the six archetypes helps build teamwork. https://x.com/LRRHBakery/status/1932133449315586502 Putting the links to the card. Putting the links to the card. 202209142228|🔮 202207252131|❤️ 202209192238|:Paladin: 202209031718|📯 202207170956|🍀 202209082209|🎩

What is your
peacemaker style? {"id":"202207170113","parentid":"202212090038","parentname":"Adventurers' Hall","icon":"🏅","title":"Peacemaker Archetypes","subtitle":"Orientation","kids":"archetype, skill","img":"https://cdn.pixabay.com/photo/2017/05/28/18/59/group-2351896_960_720.png","music":"","author":"Ivy","description":"","card-text":""}
#P202207170956
🏅 Alchemist is one of our six peacemaker archetypes. This node should introduce alchemist in a way that helps the reader recognize whether they are interested in making peace as an alchemist. Alchemists make peace by creating abundance through studying and production. "Alchemist" roles can be hands-on or intellectual. Here are some examples. Makers include those who supply essential things (e.g. farmers) and those who make desirable things (e.g. jewelers).

Guild Makers

2022-01-10: StarTree (Magic Bakery) 2022-01-08: Casey (Defense of Deer Crossing) 2022-01-08: Arcacia (Defense of Deer Crossing) 2021-12-30: Fina (Cooking in Defense of Deer Crossing) Scholars study and explain things.

Guild Scholars

https://panarcana.blogspot.com/2021/12/heroes-choice.html The scholar core is for people who just want to understand and obtain knowledge about the world regardless of the practical use of that knowledge. With proper teamwork, their research results can be applied to practical uses by makers. Makers are happy when they make things. If you enjoy making things, you might have a creator core of an alchemist. If you don't know whether you like making things, here are some areas to try:
202407280015|Origami 202310222347|Cooking | 202209282350|Homemaking Do you enjoy finding ways to make things better? If so, you have have a designer core as an alchemist! Alchemists with this core strives to improve and seek knowledge to do so. This concept includes incremental improvements also known as Kaizen, or sharpening your saw. Showing your love through what you make! https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Icx7hBWeULM These policies are related to peacemaking through resource management. Housing solutions liberate people so that they can choose to help others. A wish list can stop a fight if the other party is fighting just to be heard.
These are skills related to wish taking to help people express what they actually want versus what they think they want at first. This is a thing that lets people share findings and knowledge on how to do things. An example would be Wiki How, but expand that enough to cover local processes.
This is for keeping a record on what decisions or actions were done to a specific thing. The skill tree helps people know and learn the skills they need.
The skill tree includes skill discussion forums for people to ask questions. Within the skill three there is tutor and mentor pairing to help learners learn. A progress tree or a tech tree shows the list of technology that would help humanity overcome challenges. This tree is very similar to a skill tree, but its focus is not on tracking what a person knows, but what humantity needs to make progress. The tech tree allows a person to start from a problem they face, and trace the tree to identify the component humanity needs to develop to solve the problem. This is a place where you can get supplies like a local grocery store, but more homey like a cooperative warehouse. https://x.com/LRRHBakery/status/1885420518775677364 This doesn't mean that you could just take stuff. There might still be scanners for accounting inventory control. For someone trying to solve a problem and know our star rating, when they go to the catalog they would first want to click on the star rating, then browse for a solution in principles, infrastructure, or culture. Should that be the order? If possible please try on the node of the archetype you are most familiar with first. I asked here because the skill I am trying to add is an alchemist skill. But I will add it to Magician first. If you want to browse by star rating you can view the RES list and sort by stars. Okay I tried at Magician, but 1) It doesn't look good to have stars as category titles; 2) It is not intuitive. It seems better to actually have a curriculum section where a viewer can follow it in a logical order and be introduced through all the skills. This concept is about valuing, capturing, and fulfilling creative sparks instead of letting them go to waste. This concept is also known as Kaizen, which is being welcoming in making improvements big and small. This skill is about being familiar with how you learn the best so that you can learn effectively.
As you learn how to do something, take notes and summarize the best way for you to do it so the next time you can follow the recipe. This is the skill of always having a notepad or something in hand to draft if that helps you understand or explain something. Being able to tell the function of something you encounter. If you can't tell what something is for, this is the awareness that knowing its function could be important to understand first. This is a self-check on how well you understand something. What would you need to know to consider yourself familiar with a subject? The more you know about sciences, the more methods you will know to make something happen.
202403301836|🐸Biology 202403311838|📐Mathematics Arcacian Call is a meditative skill to help bring eureka moments. +1 I think this is a two-star skill because you have to let it happen. Maintain an organized workspace especially when the space is shared. Pollution awareness https://x.com/Unpop_Science/status/1857284032599396536 This is the skill to make something move. I think it is 4-star because it makes things work by themselves and frees up people's time. This is the skill of making something tasty. It could make people want to stop fighting. This is the skill of making a copy of something so people don't have to fight over it. I think this is a five-star skill when a fight happens because there isn't enough of something for both sides.
Photocopy prank https://x.com/tradingMaxiSL/status/1883369880529092714 Snow clearing. This is the skill of creating material or energy for use. This is the skill of bringing a specific energy or material into existence.
This is the skill of creating light (such as for illumination). This skill creates space, which can resolve fighting cased by scarcity in space. This is the skill of designing things in a way so that it can be upgraded in parts instead of having to make encompassing changes on many things. This is the skill of designing things so that a lot of it can be upgrade at once. This is a creation method or outcome of building something bit-by-bit toward something long-lasting. I think this skill can be 5-star if you can make an enemy realize the effect of their action to the crystal. This is the skill of making it clear what is being created so that others can help effectively. I think this should be herald skill. XD Some skills overlap, they can be referred to by more than one archetype. Enchantment attaches meaning that resonates with its user or viewer. High level enchantment could stop a fight when its meaning resonates with an attacker, making them wanting to protect instead of destroying it. This skill upgrades something to gain extra attributes. This skill transforms energy from one form to another. It could stop a fight if people are fighting over a shortage of some form of energy. This is the skill of turning a friendly creature into a familiar. This is the skill of a soul taking over a body. Prototype is the skill of making a simplified version of something in order to answer design questions. Safety designs of a space heater: tipover switch, overheat switch, lock out. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HnMuNCl7tZ8 This is the skill of creating an easy to follow format so a person can use it as a base to make something similar. I think this is a four-star skill because it could bridge the gap for someone who wants to but is reluctant to make something. This is the skill of turning a material into another material. Requirements, simplify, optimize, accelerate, automate. https://www.christianscheb.de/archives/893 https://modelthinkers.com/mental-model/musks-5-step-design-process
This step is about being aware of how a design and the design process should be evaluated. If you start this early in the begining, you could avoid pitfalls before they happen. This is a collection of how the thing to be designed will be used, so that the thing can be designed to support all uses. Making multiple prototypes to compare how well different ways solve a problem. https://freakonomics.com/podcast/how-to-have-good-ideas/ This is the skill of using the experience/record/simulation to estimate how long the process will take. This might be more of a Herald skill as part of project management, but the original framework that can do the estimation comes from people who know the Alchemist process. This is the skill of knowing and applying design principles so that people who see it or use it would notice and be impressed by the care and thoughtfulness of the design. Safety, Quality, Cost & Delivery.
Safety considerations
This is a design implementation so that when a system fails in a way that is foreseeable, it will fail safely. Includes physical and cyber securities. This is a way of implementing a change so that it can be undone easily if it is found to be faulty. This means that the system can handle usage and input meant for an older version of itself. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Backward_compatibility This means future version of the system can still work with the current usage and input types. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Forward_compatibility Cradle-to-cradle considerations. Is the design easy to maintain? What are the maintenance costs? Can a thing be designed to serve multiple purposes to cut cost of having two different things?
Chair helmet https://x.com/VertigoWarrior/status/1881323108017344972 Can the design be simpler by noting that it can be used with something existing and available? This skill is about designing with enough considerations that applicable to the scale of a world. This concept should not be mistaken as trying to be authoritarian. A World Builder has the skill to build a world that people want to live in without forcing them to do so.
A designer city is a precusor to world builder where the city typically still need resources from outside. A designer city could be a "coin-operated resort" where you have to insert a coin for it to provide the hosipitality. This is the skill of being able to solve problems effectively.
This is the skill of identifying the root cause of an issue. Solutions solve a problem in a way that it or similar problems do not re-occur, and with no upkeep to maintain the solution. Countermeasures do not solve the root cause of the problem, but reduces its occurence or cost. Three levels of countermeasures: Foolproof, fail-safe, fail-soft. Without countermeasure the result would be a hard fail. This is the skill of using the scientific process to obtain knowledge from data and experience.
The scientific method normally does not "prove" anything but rejects hypotheses. And generic knowledge does not override the actual effect on a subject.https://x.com/LRRHBakery/status/1882861054151258159 These are ways experiments are conducted. Knowing them means knowing what they can and cannot do. This skill is about deriving patterns and information from data for answering question. This skill includes statistics.
Updating the layout. Steps to Add or change chapter name

202207171241|Demo Case #2
July 17, 2022 Sunday 12:41 PM
Magic Bakery
⭐
Hey hey hey! For this week let's try the detective case Demo No2! Too much words XD 202207162305|Consulting Detective
Evelyn: I suppose this can count as daily reading. 😅 XD
Tanya: In summary, a notorious thief and master of disguise Gilly Niles has been seen casing the area around Trafalgar Square. We are to figure out what she is up to and stop her plan. Material available are a directoy, map, informants and newspaper.
Sasha: Do we just read everything at the leads? If we want to pretend that we are actually doing this, there would be a cost for each lead we choose to follow.
P4: Where is Trafalgar Square? It is not a number place but south of National Gallery 24 WC. 💟
Casey: If she has been casing around there, I suppose what she wants to steal is somewhere nearby.
Sasha: The newspaper says someone probably has a diamond at Grand Hotel 22 WC. I think that is probably the target. There is also a painting at the National Gallery, but it has been there since September 1st. So I think the target is the diamond.
Casey: Since she is a master of disguise, potentially she would pretend to tbe the anonymous buyer of the diamond and just take it without paying.
Tanya: We need to pick a location for the first lead. Do we agree that we will go to Grand Hotel? I want to visit the Unicorn 19 WC. I want to know what it is. Let's visit there after solving it. 😊 💟 The Unicorn is probably a pub, you can't get in XD Oh 😖 I nuke Zoey for using a mimic. There was no age limit for drink back in 1880. I wasn't talking about the age limit, I was talking about Evelyn XD But you all went to a tavern back in Adventurer Begins. That was because that was the only choice. There was no inn that wasn't also a tavern and we needed a place to lodge. XD Zoey, are you counter attacking Sasha? No, I'll just brush it off. I don't want to KO Sasha. XD Zoey you may draw a Level 3 treasure card. Zoey obtained a Gold Ring for 3000 points. 202207161025|🎪 Log Fall Dungeon Evelyn casts Protect. Just in case something happens. 😅
Black: Could we wait for Amelia? She also wants to play in about an hour. Let's wait so we can play together. 🤗
🕰️ [2:13 PM] 🕰️
While we are waiting... will we finish playing Mice & Mystics? Wasn't it too slow? Not particularly, since it was our first time playing it. I thought if it is too slow, we could change the rules. And I want to know the rest of the story. Someone still has faith in the game 😂 The last thing I remembered was the rat was attacking us, but we didn't know how attack works. I think the game manual is long and didn't do a good job summarizing the actions. I think a step-by-step guide would help. Let's make an outline of the steps.
202207171841|202207171841
Mice & Mystics
First Fight
202207152016|◀ 202207142259|▶ July 17, 2022 Sunday 6:41 PM
Guard Room
⭐
The newly transformed heroes found themselves being chased by guards transformed into rat warriors in King Andon's castle dungeon. To escape, they must escape down the sewer through paths yet unknown to them to where they could get a breather. This is a continuation of Team Cheese Cake's play through of Mice & Mystics. This post follows Tanya's RP convention: CHAT for RP (plus GM Narration) and BUBBLE for non-RP.
The initiative order since last game is Rat Warriors > Nez > Collin > Filch > Tilda.
Cardinal: In the Guard Room with the heroes are three Rat Warriors. Maginos, who was instrumental in giving the heroes a slim chance of escape, is out-of-mana. He could not fight but is well awake and aware of the situation.
Cardinal: Meanwhile, one Rat Warrior had caught up to the heroes and swung its sword at Nez, the Tinkerer Warrior. Cardinal: (Rat Warrior) ⚔️⚔️
Cardinal: The Rat Warrior swung its jagged knife as hard as he could toward the group, even if that left him wide open for counter attacks. @Casey, roll for your defense. (Nez the Tinkerer) My defense value is only 1, and I have no armor. Does this mean that I will get one wound no matter what? Seems like it. (Tilda the Healer) I'll heal you. 😖 Casey: (Nez) 🤍 🤷‍♀️ Nez takes two wounds from the heavy blow.
⚔️
Casey: Nez didn't finch. In fact he had had enough seeing Queen Vanestra's ruffians around the castle he had fought hard to defend as a former soldier. I think I am the strongest here, so I will just attack. Casey: (Nez) ⚔️⚔️⚔️🏹
Cardinal: Nez's long hammer hit the Rat Warriror and sent it flying to the end of the wall. The Rat Warrior is KO'ed. Casey: D3=2. My character moves kind of slowly so I will move directly toward the sewer.
Casey: Nez moves to the next Rat Warrior in front of the sewer, blocking the swing from it with his long hammer. (Tilda) Sorry, I got cheese when Nez was wounded. 😖 ** Gives Tilda one Cheese. ** Does cure actually heal wound? I think I read the card wrong and should in fact get First Aid as my ability. 😖 You may change it if you want. Thank you! 🙏 Evelyn's starting ability is now First Aid. Clarification about the rules: Only one of us need to be at the sewer space to flip the tile. The rule about all mice must reach the target only applies to the Tree Hole. Alright. I think my character is pretty strong also so I will fight a Rat. Sasha: (Collin) D3=3. I move to the same space as Nez and attack the Rat there. Sasha: (Collin) ⚔️🧀🧀 Cardinal: (Rat Warrior #2) 🤍
⚔️
Sasha: With its sword momentarily knocked aside by Nez, Collin the Young Princess lands a swift strike. It is just the way the Prince had trained with Nez. I give Nez one of my cheese as a free action.
Casey: Nez is pround of the Prince. He will do everything to help him defeat the Evil Queen. Great RP! XD P4: (Filch) D3=1. I want to get the special card for Nez. Do you want to try RP'ing that? Ok. Is it okay if Filch is a nice person? Filch's story says he has continued "inability to ingratiate himself to those who would provide shelter." What does that mean? It means he doesn't do anything nice to others even when they do something nice to hiim. 😟 If we want to keep the roleplay by the book, I suggest Sasha and P4 trade character. I see what you mean now. But I already took a turn. But that was just the first turn. Should we rewind and let P4 play Collin? I am okay if P4 wants to play Collin. I want to play Filch but I don't want to be mean. I hope Filch will change into a good person during the story. It says he is unable to be grateful, maybe he wants to be grateful. 😅 P4, how about you are playing the better self of Filch? You are the good voice in Filch tell him to do good, but Filch also has a selfish voice telling him to care for himself. For every decision that is kind, you will roll one die. If you win it works as you intended. If you lose, I decide what Filch will do instead, but you get one cheese. Cardinal receives Okay 💟 Wow, interesting. That's a FATE Cheese XD
⭐
P4: As soon as the fight started, Filch sneaked aside trying to steal something before leaving the guard room. But a voice tells him to be kind and get something to help the big tinkerer mouse who just got wounded. "Get that piece over there," the voice said, "it will help him make armor." P4: (Filch) D2=⭐. 😃
P4: Filch says to himself, "This is so nice, of course I'll take it." and takes the material he knew only the tinkerer could use. Filch obtained Tinkerer's Treasure. 🤗
Cardinal: The third Rat Warriror sees Filch searching through his stash of confiscated treasures and decides getting it back was more important than following the Queen's order to stop the prince from escaping. XD Filch is now closest to the last Rat Warrior. 😖
Evelyn: Although Nez was wounded, Tilda worries about Filch, who seems to be unaware that the third Rat Warrior was about to hit him. Evelyn: D3=2. I move the same space as Filch and search. Tilda became a looter! XD But there is nothing else to do 😅 I will think of the RP reason if I actually get a card, since searching only works half the time. We missed a rule. P4 still had to roll for search to get the Tinkerer's Treasure card. 😖 I'll let that slide since P4 did roll for something and got a star. Evelyn please roll for Search. Evelyn: D2=⭐. 😃 First draw of a Search Card is Magic Bakery History. This better be good! ** Draws a Search Card. ** Evelyn obtains the Scorching Wand. I can do ranged attack 🙏 Congratulations! What is the RP explanation that Tilda just took someone's stuff.
🔥
Evelyn: (Tilda) With rising resolve, Tilda quickly warped bandage around a twig to make a makeshift wand. "This shall do," Tilda said to herself and points the glowing tip of the wand at the last, approaching Rat Warrior. I use my free action to equip the wand. Dual wielding healer XD
⚔️
Cardinal: The Rat Warrior swings its jagged blade against the apparently distracted Filch. Cardinal: (Rat Warrior Attack) D2x2 = ⚔️⬜. P4: (Filch Defense) D3x2 = ➕➕.
P4: Filch deflects the swing with his tail dagger.. Casey: (Nez Move) D3=2. Casey: (Nez Attack) D2x4 = 🏹🏹🧀🧀
Casey: Nez took two steps toward the last Rat Warrior thinking he could finish him, but missed the swing. "Come On! Let's go" Nez said something to hide his embarrassment. Sasha: Should we keep looting or? It doesn't make a lot of sense if we keep looting. 😅 (Collin Attack) D2x3 = 🏹⚔️⚔️.
⚔️
Sasha: Collin steps in and KOs the last Rat Warrior. "We must hurry." P4 can make it to the sewer for sure, but if we do that we waste a chance to loot. What would Filch do? I think we should loot. Even if we end the round with no enemy, it would only add one cheese to the clock. It has only been 20 minutes in game time. I think Tilda walked too far from the sewer. If she was one space closer, we would be sure that the party can exit before the round ends. I am proposing let's all take maybe 20 minutes to loot. We can loot around the sewer entrance and Tilda will explore the round after next.
Cardinal: As the heroes gathered around the last KO'ed Rat Warrior, it was apparent that that Rat had a huge stash of equipment shrunk to the same size by Vanestra's magic spell. Perfect excuse XD I want to move to Nez's space, give him the Tinkerer's card and then search again. Roll one die to see if Filch is willing to share. P4: (Filch Kindness) D2=💟. P4: (Filch Search} D2=⭐ Isn't someone lucky today XD ** Draws a search card **
P4: Filch shoves the pack of material he knew to be invaluable to a tinker to Nez, then use both of his paws to pull out the a blade he spotted from the stash. Filch finds a Mystic Blade, which is usable by a warrior. Evelyn: (Tilda Search) D2=⬜. 😅
Evelyn: Tilda heals Nez by one wound. The time is now at 30 minutes. It is Nez's turn. I give Tilda two cheese, move as close to the sewer as I can and search. (Nez Movement) D3=2, (Nez Search) D2=⭐ Blank Check! XD
Casey: Not knowing what Filch shoved at him at first, Nez started walking toward the sewer. They he realized that what he was holding was... So. What do we want? I can get any armor or weapon. Maybe something for Filch? He got us two things already. He got the group one thing so far. The sword. Right. Sorry. 😖 I found something truely unique so I think I should get that for Nez. The Shifting Cloak, which allows the wearer to teleport to any normal space after blocking all hits from a minion attack. Why Nez? That looks like rogue gear. Nez is slow, so if he could teleport it would be awesome. You can't wear it. It is for Mystic or Scamp. How did I miss that?! XD So it ends up being something for Filch. 🤗
Casey: Nez's hope that maybe there was some good in Filch vanished as he was convinced that Filch handed him the cloak only because he knew he could not use it. Filch's kindness is being misunderstood. XD Sasha: (Collin Search) D2=⬜
Sasha: Collin reaches the sewer, It was very dark beneath. He wonder if he could find a torch. P4: I want to give Collin the sword then search for something. (Filch Share) D2=⭐. (Filch Search) D2=⭐. Super Lucky! XD ** Draws a search card ** Filch learns the trick Desperate Rescue. Are you going one more round of search? Yes. Evelyn: (Tilda Search) D2=⭐.
Evelyn: Tilda heals Nez to full health, and in the meantime, gathered the ingredients from her porch to create a Heal All scroll.. It is 20 minutes before the first surge. Casey: (Nez Search) D2=⭐.
Casey: Nez looked down the dark sewer, the foul smell, mixed with the smell of herb from Tilda's ingredient porch was just enough to make him feel ill. Nez rather rudely shoves the cloak back to Filch and threw up down the sewer. Nez takes one poison wound. I hope most of the search cards are good. Right? 😅 Sasha: (Collin Search) D2=⬜
Sasha: Collin fastens the sword Filch gave him as Filch puts on the cloak. P4: (Filch Search) D2=⭐. Filch obtains a Button Shield.
Evelyn: Tilda points her Scorching Wand down the sewer as a torch. In the dim light, they could see a ditch with flowing water right below. The water will break their fall, but the flowing water seems troublesome. "Maginos, which way should we go?
Tanya: "North. Through the kitchen." Maginos says. And the hero mice jump down to the sewer.

LRRH: I want the header to be chocolate dipped. I totally understand what you mean. XD Ivy updated the Quest Board Quest to prioritize chocolate dipped header. Wait what? XD

Goal

Create a fun, free, and family friendly puzzle book to entertain and help promote the guild.

Activity Types

🐇 Gather puzzles, link to hints and answers as needed.
🐇 Rank the puzzles and keep the best ones at the top.
🐇 Let guild members try and comment.
🐇 Story mode allowed.
202207270921|🧩 Puzzles

Plan

📌 Add back a greeting.
📌 Separate 1 star and 2 start puzzles
📌 Add back story mode puzzle list
🐤 Merge the Old Chapter (Page 33 to 45 remaining)
🐤 Add new puzzle
✅ Need a JQuery list of puzzles
✅ Need puzzle format script

Help Wanted

(1 EXP for each of the following)
📐 Formatting (Mikela, Ivy, + )
🧩 Puzzle Gathering ( 1 EXP per good puzzle )
🌠 Changing or making new puzzle ( + )
📐 Stylized hints and comments ( + )
🎨 Supportive artwork ( + )

[202207192035]
Quest Board Quest
202207192035|Saving Evelyn
202207182013|◀ ▷
July 19, 2022 Tuesday 8:35 PM
Magic Detectives
⭐
There is a quest that will greatly affect Evelyn's life. So I want to get our attention to complete it ASAP. 😅?
Sasha: As you might notice, recently Evelyn had stopped importing the old post to Blogger, and the Great Detective Sasha determined that the reason was she didn't want to import twice if we are going to change the format as Ivy had suggested multiple times. I'm sorry! I'll start again tomorrow. 😖
Sasha: My point was you were right waiting. The blog needs to catch up so that you won't waste your effort. The long the blog isn't upgraded to the intended format, the more work there would be for Evelyn. So I say, Let's do it. Right now. Let define the new format, and write the code for it.
Sasha: So, this morning I made a post, or I should say, a node in the archive. Its ID is P202207191024. If you try to display that in Viewer or JQ that node, you will see that it has not format at the top and it is missing a frame around it.
Sasha: Now, if you look at the node at the Archive, you will see that the unformatted parts have tags: "node-category", "node-title", "node-img", "node-content". I took the liberty to define them because I think they are easy to change later if we want.
Sasha: What I understand is that when the Javascript code is told to read data from the node, if the content is tagged properly, the JS code should be able to construct the clickable title that we are familiar with.
Sasha: Does anyone understand what I said so far?
Tanya: Then depending on what is trying to display the content, the "Dashboard" can decide to show a clickable row, a small thumbnail, a big thumbnail, a game card, or a tarot sized card. Yes. But that is as far as I understand. What do we need to DO to make that happen?
Tanya: We need to edit the JS function that fetches the data from the Archive. @StarTree, what function is that?
StarTree: There are about a dozen! You can find them because they all call the function "Macro"! "Macro" is the function that reads "jq" tags get the data, then replace the "jq" tag with the actual data!
Evelyn: Does that mean we only need to change "Macro"? StarTree is not sure! Macro scans the specified section for tags, then calls "QueryAllReplace", which does the magic for each tag that Macro found!
Tanya: It looks like QueryAllNext is what we need to change. QueryAllNext doesn't call anything else made by us.
Evelyn: StarTree, why are there so much commented code in that function? Ivy tried to added an alias feature so that a node could be referred to by multiple names, but it didn't work. If Ivy couldn't make it work... 😅
Ivy: What I was trying to do was different and not as important as so I just left it alone. If you want to try something, I suggest you make a copy of QueryAllReplace and just specifically run that at the Test Area. Then you won't break the rest of the while testing. Understood. Tanya created QueryAllReplace2.
Sasha: How do we actually run this function by itself at Test Area? You could make a button and call it with fixed arguments. Wow, I feel like I asked something really basic! If you don't want the button itself to disappear, then comment out the part that removes the button. Then you don't need to refresh the page just to test again. It is spamming Sasha's charter XD
Tanya: Let's try making some changes instead of just pushing the button. Okay. Enough spamming.
Tanya: Let's confirm that we can alter the content of what it displays. Tanya confirmed how to set what is displayed. Good job, you are halfway there.
⭐
Sasha: Wait, I want to do something also, let's take turn making a change. Baker Street Irregulars style. Ok. 😊 Hmm...
Zoey: May I make the change first? I have an idea XD Yes you may. That is Cardinal Style Initiative. That's a lot of changes. 😅 I'm just adding the button back. Okay let's try it. It's not loading. OMG I broke the site XD It is the quotation marks. You can change them to single quotes. It's working! Zoey enabled the button code.
Sasha: It is clear that we need to replace the dummy "TITLE" and "CONTENT" with the actual content from the node. How do we read the tags? Google is where you could ask that kind of question. But I'll just show you this, then I think you can finish it. Ivy demonstrated how to read values in a tag.
Sasha: Now I take my turn. It's working! 😃 Sasha replaced the placeholder with actual values from the node.
Sasha: Now Evelyn can continue!
Tanya: We formatted the wrong thing. We made the format for a conversation post, but P202207191024 is a charter, so the button should not be plain text but with a thumbnail. But Evelyn is mostly going to import conversation posts. We need a way to know that the content is a conversation post... Actually, we need a way to tell if our code should be used at all. If we just add this code back to QueryAllNext, the rest of the archive can't display because none of them have the "node-content" tag. Then I shall take my turn and protect the rest of the archive. Evelyn receives
Evelyn: I'm trying to make it so that we don't alter the content unless we see "node-content". I am adding a test button so show what happens if we run QueryAllReplace2 on an old post. The old post doesn't display at all 😖 🏳️ Tanya's new test button caught the error. Ivy fixed the error.
Ivy: When "get" finds nothing, the result is not null. It is a zero length collection. Thank you.😖 Evelyn's IF statement can now distinguish the old type and new type.
Zoey: If you compare them side by side, it is clear that our function is missing the icon and the participants list. So I'll add the icon with a new tag "node-icon" for my turn. CUTE !! 😍 Thanks for using the pudding. Zoey brought back the icon. Next is the participants list...
Ivy: I think it is time to wrap up. If you want, you could add your changes back to QueryAllReplace, so Sasha's charter can at least display like a story post at the calendar. It's not working. The calendar doesn't call QueryAll Replace. Apparently the calendar does something different. It doesn't even have run Macro. And if you add Macro it won't do anything because Sasha's node doesn't have any jq tag. I see. We need to make what added as a function and let calendar call it by itself. And that is not working.
Ivy: The problem seems to be that the calendar doesn't fetch a node one by one. This means that your function would not work if the input argument is not just a single node but a list of nodes. What is the query string that calendar uses? It is "[date='20220719']"! Tanya added the test to Test Area and confirmed the issue.
Tanya: We need to loop through each top level object. Tanya created the looping version of "NodeFormatter". We could change the name later.
Evelyn: It is working! 😃 Impressive. 👍
🕰️ [12:00 AM] 🕰️
Sasha: And so Team Detectives made significant progress. What will be the next challenge they overcome? Evelyn thanks all for the improvement session.

👑 Top: 29 Zoey 29 Ledia 16 Evelyn 8 Sasha 8 Sylvia 7 Cardinal 7 Vivi 7 P4 7 StarTree 6 Black 5 Tanya 4 Ivy 4 Arcacia 3 Fina 3 V 3 James 2 Natalie 1 Neil 1 Mikela 1 Kisaragi 1 Casey 1 LRRH 1 3B
Honorism refers to a moral view of promoting and celebrating honorable deeds and those who act honorably. This concept does not prescribe if there are rewards other than acknowledgement and recognition.
⚖️ Honorism is...

Honorism is...

✅ Voluntary commitment
✅ Helping others is honorable choice
✅ Not helping earns no honor
✅ Not helping is not dishonorable
✅ Doing harm is dishonorable
✅ Pro Autonomy
✅ Pro Intervention of Harm
✅ Pro Empowerment
✅ Pro Truth Seeking,
✅ Pro Whistle Blowing
✅ Anti Corruption
✅ Anti Exploitation

Honorism is not...

❌ "Everyone should help"
❌ "Helping is an obligation"
❌ "Not helping is a crime"
❌ "Non-helpers are part of the problem"
❌ "People should protect themselves"
❌ "Everyone should make the world better"
❌ "You have no jurisdiction to intervene"
❌ Monopoly of power
❌ Loyalty to a group
❌ Only follow hierarchical orders
❌ Hiding the truth to protect image
❌ "Exploitation is part of business"
🧭 Cardinal Virtues
⭐ Voluntary Commitment
Honorism views virtues as personal voluntary commitments. People who do not commit to practicing those virtues are considered neutral.
2022-11-13 @Twitter
You may commit yourself to helping others. But when you declare that "others have a responsibility to help", you cross a line where you no longer view those people as neutral. Your ideology becomes a control paradigm that attacks the neutral by branding them as irresponsible. 🐣

Grammarly Tone Rating: Informative
Q & A
Q: Why can honorism stop aggression when it claims that does not subscribe others to the same commitment? Natalie: When an aggressor subjects others to violence, the aggressor subscribes itself to an arena where opposition by force is okay. Honorism can then join that arena to intervene.
Q: What if the victim is at the brink of extinction? Can they force others to help them? Natalie: Honorism: Victims surviving by dishonorable means are less guilty than the aggressor creating the conflict. It is unjust to act against the victim without acting more against the aggressor.
Q: How does joining a conflict and opposing aggression make peace? If the victim concedes then the aggressor won't need to attack and no one dies. Natalie: Subjugation is seizing one's free life. Lack of opposition allows aggressors break peace.
Q: How does joining a conflict and opposing aggression make peace? If the victim concedes then the aggressor won't need to attack and no one dies. Natalie: Subjugation is seizing one's free life. Lack of opposition allows aggressors break peace.
Q: I am a bystander, but the aggressor threatens to harm me if anyone tries to stop it. Can I hold people against the aggressor accountable for the consequence affecting me if the aggressor goes berserk? Natalie: Holding helper (instead of the aggressor) accountable is anti-help, that is beyond being neutral (Neutral: I don't help, but if the aggressor goes berserk I blame the aggressor.) Anti-help is not neutral, it is a form of aggression.

Other Advocates of honorism

202207202206|☀️ 24 Ideals of Honorism

202207202118|☀️ Honorism

HonorBuilt

24 Ideals of Honorism
202207202206|PIN 202207202206

Link

Encounter

Natalie: I found this website while looking up references for Honorism.

Ideals

⭐ #1 Don't stop until you're proud
⭐ #2 We make it happen - no matter what
⭐ #3 There is no shortcut for the extra mile
⭐ #4 Let's build something great
⭐ #5 Measure twice, cut once
⭐ #6 Do everything in love
⭐ #7 A good name is more desirable than great riches
⭐ #8 Stretch and fear not
⭐ #9 Measure twice, cut once
⭐ #10 Relationships determine results
⭐ #11 Success fades, but excellence endures
⭐ #12 Speak the truth in love
⭐ #13 Attitude determines altitude
⭐ #14 We are all equal in God's sight
⭐ #15 We rise by lifting others
⭐ #16 Drive it like you own it
⭐ #17 Practice an attitude of gratitude
⭐ #18 Perpetual optimism is a force multiplier
⭐ #19 Manners cost little but return much
⭐ #20 Promises made are promises kept
⭐ #21 Thanks for playing
⭐ #22 If you can't find a way, make one
⭐ #23 Let the wise listen
⭐ #24 Do ordinary extraordinarily

202207210907|🎁 Gift Ideas
A rewards inventory is a facility that stockpiles gift items as rewards for questing. Are you giving something that others would value more than you do? Can I introduce someone to something they might not otherwise know about? Can I get them something nicer than they would buy for themselves? Can I make them feel seen? If you want something that you can't readily get from the reward inventory, you could post a quest for it to be made. Your posting could specify whether you are providing the material. https://freakonomics.com/podcast/how-can-you-give-better-gifts-replay/ You can craft the requested rewards and submit them for credits. In cases where there is a risk of duplicates, you should reserve the quest and complete it in reasonable time. In general it is okay to submit extra quantities because someone else might want the same reward once they see that it is available. When you offer unrequested rewards, you get credits when someone claims them. If you have a used item, can you offer it as a reward? If someone wants to claim it, how many credits should you get since you didn't spend time making it? I think you would get the standard listing time, and delivery time if you would also deliver it. If people end up bidding on it, should you get a portion of what they spent bidding? I think the winning bid helps gauge how scarce the offer is. The more scarcity you help alleviate, the more credits you should get. Would that instead encourage people to hog resources to create scarcity so that they could earn more credits by offering the item when someone would bid on it? Like diamonds. I think artificial scarcity might also happen just from rewarding people for offering the item. If a person could earn credit by re-offering it, a person has incentive to form a loop with fellow reofferers to get credits from doing nothing. When you offer an item for credits, you can't guarantee that your loop buddy would claim it. A legitimate keeper might claim it, then the loop is broken. The cheating behavior is on public record. A detective could see the fraud. Instead of relying on detectives to detect frauds, can we change the rules so that legal moves would not contribute to a fraud? I don't think you need a loop buddy for the "fraud"/undersirable milling to happen. A miller just claims an item and reoffer it. Doing that earns them listing credits. But the miller spent time listing it. Is this a worthwhile activity? Wouldn't the miller have better use of the time than just claiming an item and re-releasing it? The miller is not getting credits for their time spent shopping. But the miller is not really shopping. They could just randomly claim anything since they are free for them to get. But if a legitimate claimer claims it, they have to yield/deliver it. I think Ivy is saying that the miller doesn't actually earn anything because the standard credit is tuned to match what people spend for that task. But the miller would get gold credits for relisting when there is no demand for their effort. What if the rule is that items that tend to be relisted over and over become a library item? You don't get credits for simply returning an item or sending it to the next person. I think that is closer to the correct answer, but is there a better explanation of why returning a borrowed item is not worth any credits? It would seem it should worth community credits. The original context was that the claimant could have chosen to keep the item. But they chose to release and share it. Why shouldn't they get credits? If the principles are wrong, it is possible to have a situation where the principles say you should get credits, but he result is that the system would break. If that happens, it is a proof by contradiction that the principle is wrong. Or another principle is missing. What do people think about the context of returning a borrowed library item? Should it worth credits for the effort to return a library item? If the item has a hold on it and you don't return it by the due date, I think your credits would get deducted. So maybe when you borrow, you should pay a small amount of credits as deposit. When you return it in time you get your deposit back. I want to move away from the library analogy if possible, because in Gaia distribution, you are supposed to be able to just opt out. Opting out means you can checkout a resource and not return it. I don't think you need to move away from the library view. Once you have paid the deposit, it applies every time you check something out. You could assume that you are actually always paying the full price of the borrowed item, but if you return it, you are refunded the full price. Until you withdraw from the library society, borrowing seems free but it is not. You paid a refundable deposit. I am okay with a community inventory system where you need to have enough quester credits to borrow expensive items. It still feels free and reasonable, in addition to knowing how to use that expensive equipment safely. If you don't have enough credits to borrow it, you would need a sponsor to borrow it for you. That is how Arx works. But what about rewards? Why do you need enough quest credits as deposit if you are supposed to keep or consume the reward? If there is enough of that reward for everyone, it could be free. Otherwise, you still need to spend credits to bid for it. To simple commonly scarce rewards, the reward could just have a credit price. For example, a new reward added to the inventory could start at a credit price of 1000. If no one claims it, its price can drop regularly. I want an argument on whether the reward market should behave more like a transactional market where the winning bid amount goes to the crafter instead of simply distroyed. This situation is different from puting up a free land for auction, where the winning bid is destroyed (goes to Gaia) since the free land has no owner. +1 I also think that is more fair. According to your current forumlar, the crafter would get standard time credit for the crafting, plus benefit to society. In the case of a reward item, we are saying that the amount a quester is willing to bid for the reward is the goodness driving power of the reward item. It was thanks to the reward crafter that the quester was willing to help other people. By giving the credits back to the crafter, the society now knows WHO powered the system. +1 It may be correct to transfer credits to the crafter for this reason, but it is a little confusing since normally you only get credits for creating abundance, but in this case the crafter got credits for creating a scarcity. I don't think the crafter created a scarcity, the crafter merely created a new object of desire. The crafter didn't cause starvation. Good thoughts. I am leaning toward the crafter should get the winning bid. And for abundant reward that no one needs to bid on, the crafter still get credits based on standard crafting time. Then would it make sense that for rewards that do not require bidding, the claimer still needs to pay for the standard crafting time? I think that violates Arcacian principle. If the law requires the claimer to pay, it prevents the crafter from distributing free rewards. Back to Black's original question: If you claim a free reward then reoffer it at a time when people would bid on it, you would be earning the winning bid (plus perhaps a minimal standard listing credit). Is this a problem? Or is this particular case good because it means you helped the socity preserve something common for a time when it is out of stock? It seems that your action should worth credits unless the item went out of stock because you hoarded them when they were free. If you didn't hoard them, those items could still be sitting in inventory for people who need them. I think you can draw a difference between a functional item and a reward item. A foreseeable functional item shouldn't be hoardable. You simply can't check out/claim all the armor sets in the armory. I think the answer to that question might be Gaia resource accounting. Even a reward item might have a material component list. A person can't claim an item if they don't have the corresponding Gaia capacity to receive it. I am okay with that idea. I think that also aligns with artifact laws. If a crafter created a unique item with rare material, the item becomes an object of custody and it could require the sponsorship of more than one person to maintain custody (they have to pool their rare material rights to claim the artifact). I think it is easier if we don't have to distinguish between functional or reward items. So I would think component limit is better because it is more objective. That is not sufficient because wooden shields are just "wood", but you can't discount the crafting time went in turning trees into shields. Only accounting for the material will still allow someone create a dangerous shortage of shields. +1 If an item is part of emergency or production item, then there should be more regulations against hoarding beyond the material limits. We need to classify the items to the best of your knowledge. A "reward" item can be defined as something that is not listed as part of the quantity needed for emergency or production reserve. Then once the reserves are filled, an extra shield can be a reward item. The determination is not merely based on what the item is, but whether it is reserved. Outside the reserve amount, you can do the "buy low sell high" operation. But even if you try this, you can't price gouge others because a person could just check-out equipment from the reserve. I would consider Black's concern solved. What about credit milling? You claim a reward item. Do you get credits for just listing it back? If you set the return credit too low, there might not be enough people returning things. Set it too high and you get people milling for credits. Since emergency and production items have reserves, I think the standard listing credit should almost be zero, if not exactly zero. If you list something and no one bids for it you get nothing when someone claims it. You merely let it go and regained some of your Gaia capacity to acquire other items. I can understand the freeing up capacity explanation. Suppose you find an artifact from your attic. You can't use it but thinks that someone else might. Should you get credits for listing it? Could you specify how many credits you want to part with it? You should get credits for registering the item so it has an item ID in the system. I think it is okay for the offerer to set a credit price. I think this is the same mechanism for a restaurant to set prices, especially for items it knows would run out if they are offered for free. If you offer an item for sale for credits, then you shouldn't also get credits "helping", since you are already compensated. The system shouldn't create more credits for you. I think it is okay if that is just the crafter's choice. The crafter knows how much they would get based on standard credit. If they think that is too low, they could use a different price. But I think the crafter could choose to claim both. There is the standard credit that the crafter is giving out for free, and the non-standard quality that the crafter wants more credits for. Should this just be the crafter's choice, or does this need auditing? When the crafter claims quest credits, the crafter could be required to disclose if they are also collecting credits. Is it okay as long as they declare truthfully? I am okay with letting people just declare. I think it should be normal for a re-crafter to get a free stave, enchant it, then re-offer it as a +1 stave at a higher credit. The system might not understand a need for a +1 stave, but a buyer would. When the buyer buys the stave, the system pays the crafter for the registration, but does not pay for the enchantment because the system doesn't yet know a value for that. But the buying record helps the system understand later what the value of the enchantment is. The system can retrospectively understand that the enchanter sold it for too low and compensate by giving the enchanter more credits. I think we now have the correct mechanism to power the valuation system. Which is just the same as normal free trade. So we know to some extent this will result in correct valuation. The extra amount requested by the crafter is a guess of the gap between the standard worth and the true worth of the item. A buyer can second that guess by buying the item. The system might retrospectively compensate both the seller and the buyer (e.g., The buyer was merely trying to get a more powerful stave to help people. She had to pay for it because at that time the system didn't compensate the crafter enough. Therefore the system reimbuses the crafter and/or the buyer for the temporary sponsorship. The honor that the buyer spent can be restored.) +1 When you buy a reward item, do you pay silver or gold? How would this not lead to accumulation through inheritance? Alhtough it came with blank brown paper. This means every kid can normally afford to get toys, so even though someone still has to make the toy for them, it is more similar to "being able to afford a toy" than "being given a toy they couldn't afford." But similar to a toy drive, when a kid gets a toy through the rewards inventory, the kid might not have paid for the labor cost, since the crafter got quest credits instead of getting paid. A peace paradigm is supposed to make peace, including resolving wars before they happen.
This is a quest to monitor and bring awareness to unresolved world conflicts that 202209242119|peace concepts are meant to solve.
⭐ Monitor world news related to freedom.
⭐ Increase awareness to situations and policies that harm freedom.
⭐ Empower people to stand for their freedom

 202207211958|📜 Charter 22 {"cmd":"PIN2","node":"202207211958","music":""}
QUEST CHARTER XXII
Magic Bakery Book of Wishes
@Wish ver.1
November 7, 2021

Goal

⭐ Promote a wish-fulfilling culture using stories and fulfill everyone's wish.

Credits

⭐ EPIC C Community: Get credits for helping each other.

How you may help

Participate in the wish-fulfilling culture by:
1. Making a wish to let others help you fulfill
2. Gather and share people's wishes
3. Fulfilling someone's wish
4. Tell a story about fulfilling wishes.
5. Specifically on this blog: tag a post with data-wish when someone makes a wish.

Plan

⬜ Fulfill wishes in the Guild Log Wish section
✅ Import old wish stories

History

🍮 JUL 21: EXP 12 | 0 days cycle | 0 Issues | 17 Quests | Boss Lv 21 |
JUN 20: EXP 10 | 0 days cycle | 0 Issues | 18 Quests | Boss Lv 20 |
MAY 14: EXP 6 | 8 days cycle | 0 Issues | 26 Quests | Boss Lv 20 |
