{"id":"202207211958","parentid":"202208180941","parentname":"Wishing Tree","icon":"📜","title":"Charter 22","subtitle":"Charter","img":"https://cdn.pixabay.com/photo/2013/07/18/08/03/birthday-163362_960_720.jpg","music":"","author":"LRRH","description":"","card-text":""}
202207212021
Wishcraft 101
202207212021|Wishcraft 101
▷ July 21, 2022 Thursday 8:21 PM
Magic Bakery
⭐
Wishcraft 101 is a discussion series about how to collect and fulfill wishes. Those were the founding purpose of Magic Bakery. This particular discussion thread will be especially disorganized because I want to talk about the outline and also a wish that we could fulfill at the same time. I think that it would be easier to brainstorm if we have a wish as a context. So here it goes.
LRRH: Wishcraft 101 should cover collecting and fulfilling wishes. But there are different kind of wishes when people say "I wish something something."
LRRH: For example, say someone says, "I wish to finish this jigsaw puzzle." That can mean to things:
LRRH: 1) They wish that the puzzle jigsaw puzzle is complete, regardless who does it.
LRRH: 2) They wish that they themself have the leisure time to finish the puzzle by themself. They could mean that they wish that they don't need to work so they have spare time to do whatever they want to accomplish.
LRRH: Both wishes are valid wishes, a wish collector needs to tell the difference.
Vivi: So in this case, do you wish to write this guide by yourself, or do you wish to have this guide written regardless who writes it?
LRRH: You are a little bit ahead. To answer your question, my wish is that the guide is written regardless who writes it. But what I was getting at, is that at Magic Bakery, That is the default meaning of a wish.
LRRH: The Magic Bakery definition of a wish is a desired for an outcome regardless who fulfills it. That is the only way that Arcacia could get the permission to be involved. Little Red Riding Hood receives
LRRH: For example, if you wish for "world peace', it means that you let go of wanting to make a difference in the world and let the world make itself peaceful. This doesn't mean that you should do nothing when there is an opportunity to help. But it means that you are not trying to be important as you do it.
LRRH: If you try to do it yourself, Arcacia would pick up that desire and let you do it by yourself.
⭐
Vivi: Then why can't I just wish for being important in getting immediate good result?
LRRH: I suppose you can.
Evelyn: I think this concept is similar to the visualization technique. There you would imagine the good result in the present tense. But that technique is also different from making a wish, since the practitioner is thnking in future tense.
Evelyn: So, for example if you wish that the world is peaceful, what you condition yourself is "I am a peacemaker." You are not prescribing or predicting what you will do to make the world more peaceful. Instead, you just declare that that is who you are, without judging whether the world right now it peaceful or not.
Evelyn: Then in every challenge you face, you act in accordance to your self-assigned role as a peacemaker.
Vivi: But that means that the first lesson of making a wish is to NOT make a wish. Then we don't need Wishcraft 101, we need Selfcraft 101. 😅
LRRH: Let's have a comparison. In Guild Log, Black said he wanted to learn Japanese. @Black, is that wish current?
⭐
Black: It is current but low priority. And when I say wish, I am using the Magic Bakery meaning. I wouldn't mind if I just wake up tomorrow and be fluent in Japanese.
LRRH: According to Evelyn, instead of thinking that "I wish that I will become fluent in Japanese", you would instead say, "I AM a fluent Japanese speaker."
Evelyn: It is slightly different 😖, the present tense-role should be "I am a Japanese learner." In that declaration, you are telling yourself that, since you are a Japanese learner, you would do what a Japanese learner does. For example, if you don't know how something is said in Japanese, you would look it up. If you find an opportunity to practice Japanese, you would take it. It ties into Atomic Habits, where the identify is the trigger.
Ivy: That doesn't just tie into Atomic Habits, that IS Atomic Habit. Atomic Habit and Visualization overlap in that regard.
Vivi: And what is the comparison? What would Red did in terms of Wishcraft?
LRRH: In Wishcraft, we would post that as a quest for people to gather the best learning materials and methods. We would give credits for studying Japanese because that is no different from say the reading club. We will present the best resources we gathered and present it in a cute way.
Vivi: So the difference between Wishcraft and Visualization is that in Visualization, you are basically telling the person to go fulfill their own wish. While in Wishcraft, you would take that wish and create a supporting community to not help not only the person making a wish, but anyone else who are interested in learning or to help.
LRRH: I thought I lost but Vivi made it sound so awesome. 🍰🍰🍰
Ivy: They don't contradict. You can do both at the same time. You are creating a supportive community for learners and help them see themselves as proactive learners.
Vivi: So what is the best resource we know?
Ivy: There were some training methods where you get a list of most common phrases and keywords and just memorize those. As you use it daily, you would pick up the rest, including correcting the errors.
LRRH: I think how best to learn will evolve and could be different for each learner. But in Wishcraft, the first step is to create a place for people to share. That means we make a quest for it. A guide quest. And by now we have a few guides in the making so we should also have a collection node for guides.
Vivi: @StarTree.
StarTree: Hai! StarTree created a guide quest "Learning Japanese". 202207212155|🏯 Japanese Club That's not how StarTree should talk to Queen Vivi XD スターツリーが悪い？(Is StarTree bad?) That's too casual if you talk to your queen XD
Vivi: I don't want StarTree talking to me in Japanese all of a sudden.
Zoey: Then where can we do Japanese? Isn't that what the guide section is for? I thought it is fun if we talk in Japanese sometimes. No it is not. If I start seeing language I don't understand, I assume I am not invited and would stop reading it. It is off-putting because the site itself is not about Japanese. StarTree is doing it wrong! 😭 It is fine if the post is about Japanese. I agree that we should be kind to the reader. The reader is our guest. Maybe if we use other languages in normal discussion, we should at least put the translation next to it? Added 😭
Zoey: Do we get a daily quest for adding to the Japanese guide or practicing? Like the reading club?
LRRH: Yes, but the Japanese Guide quest is on EPIC C schedule. So please keep in mind that the credit is for helping, not for adding each word. So if you add a hundred words a day, you still only get 1 point. Yes I know, we don't count how many pages we read either. XD
Zoey: So who would join if it is a daily quest? I would, if it is quick. I would join too. 😊 StarTree wants to learn! I might. Depending on what you do there. I'll join.
Zoey: That's enough people, we'll decide what to do XD Learning Japanese is now set as a daily quest with a roster of "interested."
LRRH: Okay. I think that is all I wanted to discuss for now. I have a question. What is the plan for this weekend? If there is a birthday party, then going is the default unless we can't afford to. What about COVID? You don't have to eat anything or eat while others are eating. What is this about? Amelia wants to know if Black would attend a birthday party. But it is not in Black's habit to go anywhere. The target looks for arrows willing to reach it. Arcacia receives Okay. I'll plan to go. Evelyn thanks all for sharing.

202208180941|Wishing Tree

 202207212025|⭐ Wishcraft 101 {"cmd":"PIN2","node":"202207212025","music":""}
"We collect and fulfill wishes. When everyone supports one another, every wish comes true, every story comes to a good end."
~ Magic Bakery
Welcome to Wishcraft 101, an orientation on collecting, sharing, and fulfilling wishes, as applied to Magic Bakery.
⭐ Making Wishes
🏅 Do you want it done or do you want to do it yourself?
2022-07-12: LRRH
 There is probably no wrong ways to making wishes, but at Magic Bakery, a wish is something someone wants to happen regardless who makes that happen. However, since this is not a wide-spread understanding, it is okay if the wish maker is ambiguous. The wish collector is supposed to confirm the meaning so that they don't beat you to what you want to accomplish by yourself.
💌 Send us a wish here.
2022-10-05: LRRH
 This is a form if you want to make a wish for us. [Google Form]
🎀 Collecting & Sharing Wishes
This section mainly pertains to members only. 💟
👉 Wishes that don't look like wishes
2022-10-05: LRRH
 Sometimes wishes don't look like wishes. For example, someone is angry and complaining. But behind what they are angry at, there is something they want to happen instead. Collecting wishes is about identifying those desired outcomes even when the speak is not using the magical phrase, "I wish something something happens."
💣 Wishes that aren't wishes
2022-10-17: LRRH
 Sometimes people wish for others to get hurt. At Magic Bakery, those types of wishes are called curses. You could try to create a wish out of it. Other than that we don't have a policy of dealing with those. It is a potential discussion topic.
🖥️ Log it at Guild Log
2022-10-05: LRRH
 When a wish is collected, please first log it to the guild log unless you can finish it within a week.
📌 Pin it on our board
2022-10-05: LRRH
 Every member may choose one wish they want to focus on and pin it on the board. You may pin another wish over your existing one. The point is your posts shouldn't take up all the spaces.
🎁 Fulfilling Wishes
This section mainly pertains to members only also. 💟
🕊️ World Peace
2022-10-06: LRRH
 Many people wished for world peace. So we are not going to do that. We'will hand it to Arcacia because if we try to do it she would stop. Instead, we would just play and learn about what others do. It's all yours, Arcacia. LRRH receives
🎪 Make a Quest
2022-10-05: LRRH
 For wishes that would take considerable time to complete, please make a quest node.
💬 Discussions

202207161422|🐿️ Collections
Come get some sakura XP! 🌸 Welcome to our Japanese Learning Club! This node is for sharing learning resources and getting sakura XP! Why do you want to learn Japanese?
So that I could understand anime without subtitles.
Because I can get guild credits.
Language learning is classified as EPIC C Community in peacemaking relevance. It means you get guild credits for helping others learn Japanese, but not for learning it by yourself. Because Japanese is cute.
Because I know people who speak Japanese.
Because I know someone who is learning Japanese.
Because knowing Japanese helps on some quests.
So that I can understand and sing Japanese songs.
Because learning foreign languages is stimulating in general. Should have learned Katakana before Hiragana. If I can read Katakana I can know what the word means. The mini diary collects things you said or want to say in Japanese. List common vocabulary that we encounter at our Google Sheet. Cleaning up this node because some links don't work.

J-Idols Project

Nice and not too party-ish.

Powerplay Kawaii

More party-ish.

Radio Kishiwada

Talk radio.

Other ways to Learn

Duolingo
Take formal class
Speaking practice
Immersion
hiragana/katakana/kanji
 https://japanese.stackexchange.com/
Keigo
When spelling and pronounciation don't match
 https://www.youtube.com/@StudyInDailyJapanese
